text: Prior to graduation, Bowman toured with the musical Fame in China and performed as Rizzo in Grease in Turkey. She was also a performer with the Young Columbians. Shortly following that, Bowman returned to the states as Lady of the Lake in the Spamalot national tour. Very shortly after the Spamalot tour and her move to New York City, Bowman was offered an ensemble role in Wicked, understudying the lead role of Elphaba. Bowman left Wicked to take part in originating a new musical Kinky Boots in 2013. However, she exited the company shortly after its Broadway premiere to join the Evita national tour as Eva Perón.

After filming for four days in 2013, the films duration was too long to be a short film but too short to be a full feature. The short film soon turned into a feature film with a plethora of Scottish cast coming on board the production. The filming occurred within 2 weeks over a one-year period. Production took place in under three weeks over 2013 and 2014. Sponsor contributions made up £5,000 production budget. The release date was put back as Dewar and Grieve appealed for assistance to get the project finished.

After the Spanish Civil War the site of the palace is occupied by a building that would be the Secretaría General del Movimiento (General Secretariat of the Movement) until 1977 and whose facade facing calle de Alcalá highlighted the yoke and arrows of great size that was its symbol. The garden was then used as parking. Then passed to the current office building of the Calle Alcalá No. 44, which can also be accessed through the garden from the calle Marqués Casa Riera. The office building reflects a simple structure, elegant and modern, with stone, marble and metals. Inside are touches of greenery and water, as continuation of the original gardens as well as a fountain of contemporary design in the center of the courtyard.

Clenbuterol, a β2 agonist, is often used by bodybuilders during cutting cycles for its fat-burning and muscle-preserving properties. Publicized for off-label weight-loss use by celebrities like Victoria Beckham, Britney Spears, and Lindsay Lohan, it is similar to sympathomimetic amines like ephedrine. Despite its popularity, there is insufficient clinical testing to support or negate its efficacy for weight loss. In 2021, Mexican fitness influencer and bodybuilder Odalis Santos Mena tragically died of cardiac arrest while undergoing a procedure for underarm sweating. Reports linked her death to a reaction between anesthesia and medications, including clenbuterol, administered by an untrained anesthetist. The coroner attributed her death to cardiac arrest and respiratory failure caused by this drug interaction.

The Frank Tea and Spice Company was founded in 1896 in Cincinnati, Ohio, by Jacob Frank and his brothers. In 1917, Jacob Frank contracted with the Estilette Pepper Farm in New Iberia, Louisiana, partnering with Adam Estilette. Together, they mixed aged cayenne peppers, vinegar, garlic, and spices, creating Frank's RedHot, which debuted in 1920.

On October 1, 2023, the US Navy issued a "Memorandum for distribution" defining the scope of reports related to the USS John S. McCain and Alnic MC collision. In the interest of transparency, the memorandum authorized the release of two annexed reports: one detailing the USS John S. McCain's actions and another addressing broader operational factors. The reports, intended as factual and objective, exclude examination of the Alnic MC's actions. The USS John S. McCain report specifically details navigational errors and procedural failures.

Olavo de Carvalho dismissed global warming as a hoax, basing his claims on the 2009 Climategate episode, where hacked emails from the Climatic Research Unit (CRU) at the University of East Anglia were misrepresented by skeptics to allege scientific misconduct. Occurring shortly before the Copenhagen Conference, Carvalho argued that Climategate exposed a globalist conspiracy led by the "new world order" to impose a "worldwide socialist dictatorship." He linked this alleged conspiracy to other campaigns, such as climate change activism, and criticized the Obama administration's proposals for regulating greenhouse gas emissions, framing them as part of the same agenda.

Torres was born in Xalapa on 19 November 1948. Her parents were Guillermina Cerdán, a housewife, and Abel Torres García, who was variously an itinerant coffee-seller, bricklayer, waiter and eventually restaurant owner. She credits meeting the chef Angel Objeco, who worked at her father's restaurant of La Parroquia, in inspiring her interest in cuisine. She initially worked as a civil servant, but made the move to study Anthropology at University of Veracruz in order to expand her knowledge of the food of the indigenous peoples of Veracruz. The accumulation of this knowledge and its preservation has become an important aspect of Torres' career.

The Ladies of the House received a positive reception from the horror review websites Dread Central and Bloody Disgusting. Bloody Disgusting wrote that while the film was not perfect, that "Wildman and Walford have their heads and hearts in the right place and can only get better from here. A solid first effort even if it wasn’t my cup of tea to start with." Twitch Film wrote a positive review for the movie, which they felt "succeeds in making grindhouse both intelligent and sexy".

The Mountain View train collision occurred during the morning rush hour on [date not specified in the provided context], involving two passenger trains near the City of Tshwane. Approximately 800 passengers were on board the trains at the time of the accident. One person was killed, while more than 620 sustained minor to moderate injuries. Two critically injured victims were airlifted to a hospital via helicopter. Emergency workers responded promptly to treat the wounded and free trapped passengers, though the exact circumstances leading to the collision remain under investigation by rail authorities and law enforcement.

Ethel Blunt met her future husband, Earle Page, at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital, where she was working as a theatre sister and he was a young surgeon. During their first encounter, Earle accidentally dropped an alcohol-soaked paper on her dress, which he quickly removed and dried, leaving a lasting impression. Ethel, born in 1875, was six years older than Earle. In 1904, Earle persuaded Ethel to become the matron of his newly established private hospital in Grafton. The couple married on 18 September 1906 in Sydney and went on to have five children: Earle Jr. (1907), Donald (1908), Mary (1910), Douglas (1912), and Iven (1914). Their partnership was marked by shared professional and personal endeavors, with Ethel playing a significant role in supporting Earle’s medical and political career.

Ibbott, who wanted to be a disc jockey as a child, began his DJ career at weddings, though was bored with the music selection such venues permitted. A TechTV story he saw in August 2004 got him interested in podcasting. The first Coverville podcast was launched on September 28, 2004. Coverville passed episode #300 on March 4, 2007  and episode #500 on August 26, 2008, which was celebrated with a "gala" in Las Vegas where fans could meet with artists featured on the show.

On 10 June 2012, the Local Coordination Committees reported 53 deaths nationwide, including 26 from shelling in Homs. The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights reported 12 deaths in Homs and 23 soldiers killed across Syria.

In 1974, Matej Pavšič explored a theory involving mirror particles, proposing that nature could be exactly symmetric under space inversion if mirror particles existed. The concept of mirror particles was first introduced by T.D. Lee and C.N. Yang in 1956 in their work on parity non-conservation. In 1966, Yu. Kobzarev, L.B. Okun, and I.Ya. Pomeranchuk elaborated on this idea, suggesting that mirror particles interact gravitationally but are decoupled from ordinary matter. Exact parity models, which replicate the Standard Model with a mirror counterpart, are significant in explaining dark matter.

Literature inspired by the Gulag includes memoirs, novels, and essays by former prisoners, often recounting harrowing experiences. Notable Russian writers Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, Varlam Shalamov, and Evgenia Ginzburg are central figures in this genre. Polish writer Gustaw Herling-Grudziński, liberated in 1942, left the Soviet Union with the Polish Anders Army and published *A World Apart* in 1951. Many such works, translated into multiple languages, provide invaluable insights into the brutal realities of the Soviet labor camp system.

Aleem Said, adhering to Sunni Islam, opposes abortion, describing it as a moral crisis, and condemns terrorism and violence, emphasizing their roots in societal failures while advocating adherence to Islamic principles and justice.

According to AGB Nielsen Philippines' Mega Manila household television ratings, the pilot episode of *Pepito Manaloto* earned a 28.2% rating, while the first episode of *Pepito Manaloto: Ang Tunay na Kuwento* scored 26.6%. The pilot episode of *Pepito Manaloto: Ang Unang Kuwento* received a 14.7% rating from Nationwide Urban Television Audience Measurement.

Washing Machine received widespread acclaim from music critics, with many praising its adventurous and exploratory nature. Stephen Thomas Erlewine of AllMusic described the album as Sonic Youth's most daring, challenging, and best work since *Daydream Nation*, noting that it marked a return to the fearless experimentation of their earlier SST records while incorporating tighter song structures. He highlighted the music's unpredictability and refusal to take easy paths, emphasizing its demand for attention. Peter Margasak of *CMJ New Music Monthly* also lauded the album, singling out the sprawling 19-minute closer, "The Diamond Sea," as its centerpiece. Margasak noted that the track exemplified the band's willingness to push boundaries, a sentiment echoed by Thurston Moore, who remarked that he would make every song 20 minutes long if possible. Together, these reviews positioned *Washing Machine* as a bold statement that revitalized Sonic Youth's creative trajectory following the more subdued *Experimental Jet Set, Trash & No Star*.

The Raptor engine is a family of methane-fueled rocket engines developed by SpaceX for use in the Starship spacecraft and its Super Heavy booster. It employs a full-flow staged combustion cycle, making it only the third engine in history to do so, following two earlier attempts that never advanced beyond testing. Methane was chosen as the primary fuel for its clean-burning properties, efficiency, and suitability for reuse. The Raptor is the most powerful methane-fueled engine ever flown and generates significantly more thrust than SpaceX's Merlin engines, benefiting from its advanced design to maximize power, efficiency, and reusability for deep-space missions.

Hibbertia bicarpellata is a shrub that typically grows to a height of 1.5 m (4 ft 11 in), its branches with ridges from below the leaf bases, and its branches and leaves covered with rosette-like bundles of hairs. The leaves are elliptic, 20–45 mm (0.79–1.77 in) long and 8–15 mm (0.31–0.59 in) wide on a petiole 0.5–2 mm (0.020–0.079 in) long. The flowers are arranged singly in leaf axils on a stiff peduncle 4.4–7.5 mm (0.17–0.30 in) long, with linear to lance-shaped bracts 3.5–5 mm (0.14–0.20 in) long. The five sepals are joined at the base, the three outer sepal lobes 7–10 mm (0.28–0.39 in) long and the inner lobes 5.6–6.6 mm (0.22–0.26 in) long. The five petals are egg-shaped with the narrower end towards the base, yellow, 6.8–9.6 mm (0.27–0.38 in) long and notched at the tip. There are twenty to twenty-six stamens arranged around the two carpels, each carpel with four ovules. Flowering occurs from February to September.

Archaeologist Emilio Estrada and co-workers wrote that pottery which was associated with the Valdivia culture of coastal Ecuador and dated to 3000–1500 BCE exhibited similarities to pottery which was produced during the Jōmon period in Japan, arguing that contact between the two cultures might explain the similarities. Chronological and other problems have led most archaeologists to dismiss this idea as implausible. The suggestion has been made that the resemblances (which are not complete) are simply due to the limited number of designs possible when incising clay.

The band started playing shows in early 2007. Towards the end of 2007, the band wrote and recorded a self-titled demo EP. The EP placed in the Top 5 Most Added Albums on US College Radio. It also gained rotation on US radio station KEXP, with single "4 To The Floor" played widely. The band were also played heavily on Australian network Triple J, and had two number one songs ("4 To The Floor" and "Nikee") on the alternative New Zealand b.net radio network. The songs "4 To The Floor" and "Time Mismanagement" charted on the NZ Industry Radioscope chart for 21 weeks in 2008, peaking at #3 and #2 respectively.

The arboviruses have expanded their geographic range and infected populations that had no recent community knowledge of the diseases carried by the Aedes aegypti mosquito. Education and community awareness campaigns are necessary for prevention to be effective. Communities are educated on how the disease is spread, how they can protect themselves from infection and the symptoms of infection. Community health education programs can identify and address the social/economic and cultural issues that can hinder preventative measures. Community outreach and education programs can identify which preventative measures a community is most likely to employ. Leading to a targeted prevention method that has a higher chance of success in that particular community. Community outreach and education includes engaging community health workers and local healthcare providers, local schools and community organizations to educate the public on mosquito vector control and disease prevention.

The terrestrial biosphere includes the organic carbon in all land-living organisms, both alive and dead, as well as carbon stored in soils. About 500 gigatons of carbon are stored above ground in plants and other living organisms, while soil holds approximately 1,500 gigatons of carbon. Most carbon in the terrestrial biosphere is organic carbon, while about a third of soil carbon is stored in inorganic forms, such as calcium carbonate. Organic carbon is a major component of all organisms living on earth. Autotrophs extract it from the air in the form of carbon dioxide, converting it into organic carbon, while heterotrophs receive carbon by consuming other organisms.

Martin Stääf was born 13 November 1978 in Varberg on the Swedish West Coast. He started playing the piano and did classical piano concerts between the ages of 6–8. Stääf started experimenting with synthesizers in the mid 80s and abandoned the classical music world to spend the next 10 years producing his own material. Stääf states: "I never thought about releasing any of my music until I was 17. Then, all of a sudden, it was time to get a job and I realized that there was nothing else I wanted to do at that time than making music."

The fourth season of *Lost* was produced by ABC Studios and Touchstone Television, with Damon Lindelof and Carlton Cuse serving as showrunners. Filmed entirely on location in Hawaii, post-production was completed in Los Angeles. Executive producers included Lindelof, Cuse, J.J. Abrams, Bryan Burk, Jack Bender, and Jeff Pinkner. The season's staff writers were Jeffrey Lieber, J.J. Abrams, and Lindelof, among others, with Stephen Williams as supervising producer. Regular directors for the season included Jack Bender and Stephen Williams, who directed multiple episodes, contributing to the series' continued critical and commercial success.

Dunin has written books and articles about cryptography, and been interviewed on radio and television about related subjects such as Kryptos, the Smithy Code, and Ricky McCormick's encrypted notes. In an interview with GIGnews.com, Dunin said that in the year 2000 she cracked the PhreakNIC v3.0 Code, an amateur cryptographic puzzle created by a hacker group, and that this launched her public interest in high-profile ciphers. Because of the location of Kryptos on CIA grounds, physical access to the sculpture is restricted. According to Wired News, in 2002, Dunin gave a presentation to CIA analysts about steganography and Al-Qaeda, and "[i]n 2002, Dunin was one of the lucky few who saw [Kryptos] in person", and "she also made rubbings of the text". Based on her visit, she launched the beginnings of what became a comprehensive website about the sculpture, and also became co-moderator of a Yahoo Group that is attempting to decipher the encrypted messages on the sculpture. In 2003, Dunin organized a team which solved the ciphers on Kryptos's sister sculpture, the Cyrillic Projector.

In the first year of the occupation, the German administration pursued a conciliatory policy toward the Belgian people in order to gain their support and co-operation. This policy was, in part, because there was little resistance activity and because the demands the Germans needed to place on Belgian civilians and businesses were relatively small on account of their military success. During the fighting in Belgium, however, there were incidents of massacres against Belgian civilians by German forces, notably the Vinkt Massacre in which 86 civilians were killed.
He was the son of Edmund Lechmere MP and his second wife Elizabeth, daughter of Rev. John Whitmore. He matriculated at Merton College, Oxford in 1785 aged 18.

In the wake of five democratic members of Cincinnati City Council, P.G. Sittenfeld, Chris Seelbach, Wendell Young, Tamaya Dennard, and Greg Landsman, being caught in an open meeting law(s) violation(s) scandal, Smitherman announced in May 2018, that he was launching a ballot initiative to place a charter amendment on the November Ballot to revert Cincinnati City Council terms to 2 years. This issue, which later became known as "Issue 10," was later placed on the ballot by a council vote. On November 6, 2018, Cincinnati voters approved "Issue 10" by a margin of 70.09% (71,461) in favor and 29.91% (30,502) opposed. The amendment will take effect in 2021, following the next council election.

Gittens and his girlfriend, Heather Chee, have a son, who was born in May 2021. Gittens' father told him when he was drafted in 2014 that he would not see any of his games in person until he was promoted to the major leagues. Gittens described it as "a lot of motivation, a little push for me to get where I am right now". Both of Gittens' parents attended his major league debut.

The current Barabanki district was established by the British in 1856 following their annexation of the Oudh State. Initially named Daryabad district, its headquarters were located in Daryabad. In 1859, the headquarters were relocated to Barabanki, a small revenue village outside Nawabganj, which was then the more common name of the town. The name "Barabanki" was chosen for the district to avoid confusion with other places called Nawabganj and because the civil station was technically outside Nawabganj. Under the Nawabs of Awadh, the area that would become Barabanki district was divided into five chaklas: Daryabad-Rudauli, Ramnagar, Dewa-Jahangirabad, Jagdispur, and Haidargarh.

Church of the Resurrection worship services have been described by news reports as "high octane" and "spirit-filled and Bible-based but centered on ancient liturgy," featuring a "combination of charismatic preaching and dramatic liturgy with priests wearing traditional robes and reading from the Book of Common Prayer." The blend of high church and evangelical elements is highlighted in the Rez service for the Easter vigil, a four-hour service featuring art, elaborate decorations, dance, dramatic scriptural readings, baptism and Holy Communion. The vigil began in the 1980s under Beasley's leadership; Beasley had a background in theater and incorporated actors, visual artists and musicians in the vigil. In 2011, prior to Rez's acquisition of its current building, a record 1,500 people attended the Easter vigil held in Edman Chapel at Wheaton College.

The arena opened with a resident basketball team in the Newcastle Comets, having been relocated from Sunderland where they had played as the Sunderland Scorpions. In 1996, the Comets were bought by John Hall as part of the Sporting Club project. Having spent just one season as the Comets in the arena, the team were renamed the Newcastle Eagles for the start of the 1996–97 season.

In Queensland, efforts to minimize losses and avoid a split in the Labor Party were led by senior figures within the party. State MP Jennifer Howard left Labor amid internal factional shifts. An initial attempt to create an alternate political vehicle at the state level failed. In February 2008, a party was formed for ex-Labor supporters, but it struggled to gain traction and ultimately dissolved without significant electoral success.

The *Lockhart Papers* are a significant authority on Jacobite history, offering detailed accounts of Scottish affairs from 1702 to 1715. George Lockhart authored *Memoirs of the Affairs of Scotland*, first published in 1714, covering events from Queen Anne's accession to the 1707 Union. A later 1817 edition included additional commentary and letters. The *Lockhart Letters*, edited by Anthony Aufrere, were also published in 1817 in two volumes, further enriching the historical record.
Medary, located in Brookings County, South Dakota, lies about 4.5 miles south of Brookings near the Big Sioux River.

Rainer Blasczyk's scientific work focuses on transplant engineering and immunotherapy to combat organ rejection. In collaboration with the Research Network on Invisible Organs, which he has led since 2018, Blasczyk aims to genetically modify allografts during their ex vivo period to make them immunologically invisible to the recipient's immune system. This innovation network, funded by the European Regional Development Fund and the state of Lower Saxony, seeks to reduce immunosuppression by permanently switching off tissue markers, preventing rejection and inducing tolerance toward transplanted organs.

The only known host plant for *Bissetia steniellus* is sugarcane. Female moths lay 90 to 300 scale-like eggs in clusters along the midrib of sugarcane leaves. The larvae hatch after 7 to 10 days and enter the sugarcane stem through a single hole above the node. Inside the stem, they create spiral galleries and feed in groups. After about ten days, the larvae begin attacking nearby canes as the initial feeding site becomes unsuitable. The larvae feed for approximately 25 to 30 days before pupating within the stem. Pupation occurs in a chamber constructed by the larvae, and adult moths emerge after 5 to 7 days. This life cycle, from egg to adult, contributes significantly to the damage caused to sugarcane crops, including spiral galleries and weakened stems, which can lead to substantial yield losses.
Abbie Norton married William H. Jamison, a music teacher, in 1892. They divorced in the 1910s. She died in 1955 in Los Angeles, aged 87.

Anas el-Fiqqi was the Minister of Youth and subsequently became the Minister of Information from February 2004 to February 2011. He was implicated in the harassment of journalists during the 2011 Egyptian revolution and for the closure of news channels such as Al Jazeera in Cairo. On February 12, the day after the president stepped down, he resigned and was reportedly placed under house arrest. El-Fiqqi was charged with financial irregularities and became subject to investigations. His detention was renewed for 15 days on March 9 for further investigation of reports that he misappropriated funds, used public funds to support the NDP and for personal purchases, and received a bribe to allocate the advertising rights from an Arab media festival to the nephew of Mubarak's office manager.

Carver Creative and Performing Arts Center (CCPAC) was the first magnet school in the Montgomery Public Schools (MPS) school district. CCPAC, originally located at George Washington Carver High School, was developed in 1982 to accommodate the growing need in Montgomery for specialized arts instruction for students. CCPAC began as a day program where students were bused from home schools to the Carver High School campus for magnet classes. As the program expanded and enrollment grew, the need to find a new location became more and more apparent.

President Trump announced in October 2017 he would end the smaller of the two types of subsidies under the ACA, the cost sharing reduction (CSR) subsidies. This controversial decision significantly raised premiums on the ACA exchanges (as much as 20 percentage points) along with the premium tax credit subsidies that rise with them, with the CBO estimating a $200 billion increase in the budget deficit over a decade. CBO also estimated that initially up to one million fewer would have health insurance coverage, although more might have it in the long-run as the subsidies expand. CBO expected the exchanges to remain stable (e.g., no "death spiral") as the premiums would increase and prices would stabilize at the higher (non-CSR) level.

The liver metabolizes alcohol at a constant rate of approximately 0.015% BAC per hour, or about one standard drink per hour. One British unit of alcohol contains 8 grams of pure ethanol, equivalent to 10 milliliters. This is approximately 0.35 US fluid ounces. Liver conditions or acetaminophen use can slow alcohol metabolism.

On 4 October 2017, Dutch Minister of Defense, Jeanine Hennis-Plasschaert, resigned over the deaths of 2 Dutch servicemen and gravely wounding of another in a training accident in Mali in July 2016. The Dutch Safety Board ruled that the military had been using old, defective grenades that had not been tested or stored correctly. General Middendorp also resigned his position due to these failures.

The MoD announced on 6 June 2007 that AirTanker had been given the approval it needed to continue with the project, allowing the company to seek the £2 billion private financing required to begin funding the aircraft. On 27 March 2008, a PFI deal was agreed with AirTanker, worth £10.5 billion over the course of the contract, and will involve 14 converted A330 aircraft being delivered from 2011 and being operated until at least 2035. The contract will be paid for at £390 million per annum. Of this running costs are £80 million and the remainder covers the consortium's financing and profit  and the capital cost of the project, including aircraft and infrastructure.

All seven constituent districts had sent their ninth through twelfth grade students to Bridgeton High School as part of sending/receiving relationships, until the school faced overcrowding issues in the 1970s. The initial school board was selected by the County Superintendent in December 1974, with the expectation that construction of a school could take as much as four years. By a margin of 11 votes, residents of the constituent communities approved a $10.2 referendum to cover the cost of the new high school building.

During World War II, over 1,000 underground newspapers were published in occupied Poland, with two of the most significant being *Biuletyn Informacyjny* of the Armia Krajowa and *Rzeczpospolita* of the Government Delegation for Poland. These publications covered news (often sourced from intercepted Western radio transmissions), politics, economics, education, and literature. The highest recorded publication volume for an issue of *Biuletyn Informacyjny* was 43,000 copies, while larger publications typically had print runs of 1,000–5,000 copies. The Polish underground also produced disinformation materials, such as fake German newspapers and leaflets from imaginary anti-Nazi German organizations, to lower German morale. Additionally, books and other printed items were distributed, often with slogans like "Do not destroy! Do not lose! Pass it on to others!" to encourage circulation and preserve the materials.

In his Allmusic review, Al Campbell awarded *Spring* four stars, noting the album's potential given the assembled talent. He praised Tony Williams' five compositions for providing space that allowed the musicians to explore freely, though he found the album less memorable overall. Campbell described the recording as cohesive yet lacking a strong thematic structure, ultimately concluding that while *Spring* showcased Williams' ambition and creativity, it fell short of fully realizing its promise.

In 1974 Pavšič considered a theory according to which nature is exactly symmetric with respect to space inversion, provided that one postulates the existence of mirror particles and mirror interactions among them. The idea of mirror particles was introduced in 1956 by Lee and Yang in their paper on parity non conservation, and was in 1966 further elaborated by Yu. Kobzarev, L.B. Okun and I.Ya. Pomeranchuk within the context of a CP invariant theory. Nowadays, the so-called exact parity models are considered in many works as an explanation of dark matter.

According to the 2020 U.S. Census, Ann Arbor, Michigan, had a population of 123,851 and 47,060 households. The city’s population density was 4,508 residents per square mile, making it less densely populated than Detroit proper and its inner-ring suburbs like Oak Park and Ferndale, but more densely populated than outer-ring suburbs like Livonia and Troy. The racial makeup of Ann Arbor was 65.9% White, 6.6% Black, 15.6% Asian, 5.5% Hispanic or Latino of any race, 0.8% Native American/Other (including Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander), and 5.6% Multiracial. Ann Arbor also has a small Arab American population, reflecting Michigan’s broader Arab American community. The city’s demographics are shaped by its status as a university town, with the University of Michigan playing a significant role in its cultural and economic landscape.

Gospatric, Earl of Northumbria, was the father of three sons—Dolfin, Waltheof, and Gospatric—and several daughters. The names of his sons are recorded in *De Obsessione Dunelmi* and by Symeon of Durham. A writ from about 1275, prepared by the monks of Holmcultram, claims Dolfin and Gospatric were illegitimate, with Waltheof being the only legitimate son. Ethelreda, Gospatric's daughter, was Waltheof's full sibling. Another document from the same period, the *Chronicon Cumbriae*, names Waltheof’s sisters as Gunhilda, Maud, and Ethelreda. Ethelreda’s son, William FitzDuncan, is also mentioned in this document, highlighting her marriage to Duncan II, King of Scots.

The United States Post Office and Courthouse was the first monumental structure in Oklahoma City and served as an anchor for future federal development. Plans for the construction of the building began in 1903 when Congress appropriated funds for a downtown facility. Due to the region's rapid growth, original funding was insufficient and additional money was allotted in 1906, 1908, and 1910. It was the first federal building constructed in Oklahoma, which became a state only five years prior to the building's 1912 completion.

Stepashin was born in Port-Arthur, Kvantun Oblast, USSR (now Lüshunkou, China) on 2 March 1952. He graduated from the Higher Political School of the USSR Ministry of the Interior (1973), in 1981 from the Lenin Military-Political Academy, and in 2002 from the Finance Academy. He is a Doctor of Law, Professor, and has a rank of the State Advisor on Justice of the Russian Federation. His military rank is colonel general.

On September 7, 2011, a Yak-42 aircraft carrying the Lokomotiv Yaroslavl hockey team crashed near Yaroslavl, Russia, killing 37 team members. The tragedy occurred as the team traveled to Minsk for their season opener. During the Opening Cup game in Ufa between Salavat Yulaev and Atlant Moscow Oblast, KHL president Alexander Medvedev announced the news, leading to the game’s suspension. The KHL postponed the season start to September 12 and canceled all celebratory events, instead organizing memorial ceremonies. Lokomotiv president Yuri Yakovlev announced the team’s withdrawal from the 2011–12 season. Nearly 100,000 fans attended a memorial at Arena 2000 in Yaroslavl.

In 2015, Radio Dabanga was taken down by Arabsat following a request from the Sudanese government. Arabsat labeled the station an enemy of Sudan’s government, citing issues beyond controversial news items, including its critical stance on the regime. After the takedown, Radio Dabanga began broadcasting with Eutelsat, continuing its mission to provide independent news amidst Sudan’s political challenges.

Watchman was ordered on 9 December 1916 and was laid down by John Brown & Company at Clydebank, Scotland, on 17 January 1917. Launched on 2 December 1917, she was completed on 26 January 1918 and commissioned the same day. She was assigned the pennant number G23 in January 1918; it was changed to G09 in April 1918, and to D26 during the interwar period.

The Bling Ring, a group of fame-obsessed teenagers from the Los Angeles area, targeted high-profile celebrities, including Paris Hilton, Lindsay Lohan, Orlando Bloom, Rachel Bilson, Ashley Tisdale, and Audrina Patridge, stealing over $3 million in luxury goods. According to Nick Prugo, the group initially chose female targets based on their admiration for their fashion, with Rachel Lee reportedly selecting victims whose clothing she liked. The group used celebrity websites and social media to track their targets' schedules, determining when they would be away from home, such as attending premieres or events. They located the celebrities' homes through online tools like Google Maps and real estate listings. Lee, described as focused and enthusiastic during the burglaries, often initiated the crimes, motivated by her fixation on the glamorous lifestyles of their victims. The Los Angeles Police Department noted that the group's activities evolved from opportunistic thefts to more calculated and organized crimes. Alexis Neiers, a member of the group, claimed that Lee was the leader and driving force behind the burglaries. Despite their planning, the group’s methods were far from sophisticated, with some members displaying erratic behavior during the crimes.

Early on Redding copied the rock and soul style of his role model Little Richard. He was also influenced by soul musicians such as Sam Cooke, whose live album Sam Cooke at the Copa was a strong influence, but later explored other popular genres. He studied the recordings of the Beatles and Bob Dylan. His song "Hard to Handle" has elements of rock and roll and influences of Eric Clapton and Jimi Hendrix. Most of his songs were categorized as Southern soul and Memphis soul.

Coal was first discovered in the United States in 1679 by French explorers along the Illinois River. In Kentucky, Dr. Thomas Walker discovered coal in 1750 in the Eastern Coalfield and used it to heat his campfire. Commercial coal production in the Eastern Coalfield began 150 years later. Kentucky’s first commercial coal mine opened in 1820 in Muhlenberg County in the Western Coalfield, producing 328 tons in its first year. By 1843, Kentucky produced 100,000 tons of coal, and by 1879, production reached one million tons, all sourced from the Western Coalfield.

Germaine Williams was born on December 9, 1974, in Kingston, Jamaica. He is of Jamaican descent. His father, Basil Williams, was a Jamaican and West Indian cricketer. The family moved frequently, living in The Bronx; Newark, New Jersey; Washington, D.C.; Atlanta; Miami; Buffalo; and London due to his mother's career requiring constant relocation. Canibus stated that he was an introverted child growing up. After completing high school in 1992, he spent a year working for AT&T Corporation and another year as a data analyst for the U.S. Department of Justice. His interest in computers and the Internet led him to study computer science at DeKalb College in Atlanta.

Hilton served two consecutive terms in the Legislative Assembly of Minas Gerais, between 1999 and 2007, for the PSL and PL parties. In 2006, he was elected federal deputy for Minas Gerais, being re-elected successively in 2010 and 2014. During the period he was a member of the Progressive Party, and from 2009 until 2016, he was affiliated to the Brazilian Republican Party.

A figure of the antinatalism and childfree movement, De Giraud is one of the co-creators of Non-Parents Day, celebrated between 2009 and 2011, alternately in Brussels and Paris. In 2008, he covered a statue of Leopold II in Brussels with red paint, to denounce the public valorisation of the king who established the colonial system of the Belgian Congo. In 2012, he organised a "denatalist" event in Paris to bring attention to the overpopulation taboo and to the value of refusing to give birth for ecological reasons.

As of the 2000 census, Volinia Township, Michigan, had a population of 51,104, with 19,676 households and 14,304 families residing in the area. The population density was 104 inhabitants per square mile. There were 23,884 housing units at an average density of 48 per square mile. The racial makeup of the township was 89.19% White, and 2.15% identified as two or more races. Additionally, 1.17% of the population identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

While still a pupil at Great Barr School, Winwood was a part of the Birmingham blues rock scene, playing the Hammond C-3 organ and guitar, backing blues and rock legends such as Muddy Waters, John Lee Hooker, Howlin' Wolf, B.B. King, Chuck Berry, and Bo Diddley on their United Kingdom tours, the custom at that time being for US singers to travel solo and be backed by pick-up bands. At this time, Winwood was living on Atlantic Road in Great Barr, close to the Birmingham music halls where he played. Winwood modelled his singing after Ray Charles.
The Beach Leisure Centre, not physically connected to other buildings, features pools, flumes, and a wave machine.

The town of Rodez is the seat of the General Council of Aveyron and the Council of the community of the Grand Rodez [fr]. The conurbation has a fire service barracks, a municipal police force, an SAMU and an SMUR (both medical emergency services), a poison control centre and a remand prison while the State administers the prefecture of the department, and a gendarmerie brigade. A new remand prison will be located in the commune of Druelle in July 2013 with a capacity of about 100 male inmates, including a capacity of ten for people eligible for day parole. Finally, the administrations of the State are present in Rodez (tax office, Bank of France, social security, family allowance, etc.).

Jean Bédard is a laundry employee and union advisor. He is a perennial candidate for the Marxist-Leninist Party, having run under its banner in seven federal and six provincial elections. He appears to have been a low-profile candidate; during the 2003 election, the newspaper Le Soleil indicated that it was unable to obtain an interview with him.

The downy birch aphid (*Euceraphis punctipennis*) is a slightly bluish-green aphid, lightly dusted with waxy particles. It has delicate, membranous wings held roof-like over its body and notably long legs. During spring and summer, all adults are female, reproducing asexually and giving birth to live young. It closely resembles the silver birch aphid (*Euceraphis betulae*), which hosts exclusively on silver birch (*Betula pendula*). The distinction between these species arose from their strict host specificity, with *E. punctipennis* found only on downy birch (*Betula pubescens*).

The Zika virus remains without approved vaccines or specific antiviral treatments, though clinical trials, such as the NIH's live attenuated vaccine, are ongoing. Current treatment is supportive, focusing on symptom management. While drug screening studies are limited, efforts to identify antiviral inhibitors and therapeutic options, especially for pregnant women, continue.

Unaegbu was born in Amankwo-Inyi village, Oji River LGA of Enugu State, Nigeria, the first child of five surviving children to Mr. John Ejike Unaegbu and Mrs. Cecilia Enonanma Unaegbu. He attended the Government Secondary School, Minna in 1990, studying sciences and graduated in 1996. He then attended Muhanatu Computer Institute, graduating in 2000 with distinctions in Certificate and Diploma in Computer Data Processing. He then attended the University of Nigeria, Nsukka from 2002 to 2006, graduating with second class in Mass Communication. As a student, Unaegbu served as the Editor of the university's Roar Magazine. He also won faculty and poetry prizes.

The SCM is a statistical ensemble of random graphs having vertices () labeled , producing a probability distribution on (the set of graphs of size ). Imposed on the ensemble are constraints, namely that the ensemble average of the degree of vertex is equal to a designated value , for all . The model is fully parameterized by its size and expected degree sequence . These constraints are both local (one constraint associated with each vertex) and soft (constraints on the ensemble average of certain observable quantities), and thus yields a canonical ensemble with an extensive number of constraints. The conditions are imposed on the ensemble by the method of Lagrange multipliers (see Maximum-entropy random graph model).

Chromolithographs are mainly used today as fine art instead of advertisements, and they are hard to find because of poor preservation and cheaper forms of printing replaced it. Many chromolithographs have deteriorated because of the acidic frames surrounding them. As stated earlier, production costs of chromolithographs were low, but efforts were still being made to find a cheaper and faster way to mass-produce colored prints. Although purchasing a chromolithograph may have been cheaper than purchasing a painting, it was still expensive in comparison to other colour printing methods which were later developed. Offset printing replaced chromolithography in the late 1930s.

"Deadwood: The Movie" received universal acclaim from critics. On Rotten Tomatoes, it holds a 98% approval rating based on 42 reviews, with an average rating of 8.3/10. The critical consensus describes it as "emotionally satisfying," "wholly welcome," and "lovely." On Metacritic, the film has a weighted average score of 86 out of 100, based on 15 reviews, indicating "universal acclaim." Critics praised its direction, imagery, and ability to provide a satisfying conclusion to the beloved series.

The white shell reaches a height of 1 mm. The solid shell has a depressed, turbinate shape. It is openly perforate to imperforate. The sculpture shows distant longitudinal lamellate ribs that cross the whorl from the suture to the umbilicus. Their interstices contain raised spiral threads, which grow coarser on approaching the umbilicus. The protoconch contains 11⁄2 whorl, concluding with a prominent varix. The three whorls are tabulate above, and rounded below. The body whorl descends rapidly. The aperture is roundly ovate. The sharp outer lip is convex. The inner lip spreads as a distinct callosity over the body, and sometimes seals the umbilicus partly or wholly up. The columella is concave. The narrow umbilicus is deep, and is bordered with a raised ridge, or is closed up. The foramen is large, and distant from the margin, to which a furrow joins it. The fasciole is extremity short, terminating half a whorl behind the aperture. it is bordered by keels and is traversed by lamellae, which correspond to the longitudinal ribs.

Sheets of cirrus clouds commonly fan out from the eye walls of tropical cyclones. (The eye wall is the ring of storm clouds surrounding the eye of a tropical cyclone.) A large shield of cirrus and cirrostratus typically accompanies the high altitude outflowing winds of tropical cyclones, and these can make the underlying bands of rain—and sometimes even the eye—difficult to detect in satellite photographs.

HMS *Watchman* was ordered during World War I and laid down by John Brown & Company on the Clyde on 17 January 1917. She was launched on 2 November 1917 and completed and commissioned on 26 January 1918. Initially assigned the pennant number G29, she joined the Royal Navy as one of twenty-one "W" class destroyers.

Artifacts recovered from underwater sites need stabilization to manage the process of removal of water and conservation. The artifact either needs to be dried carefully, or the water replaced with some inert medium (as in the case of The Mary Rose). Artifacts recovered from salt water, particularly metals and glass need be stabilized following absorption of salt or leaching of metals. In-situ conservation of underwater structures is possible, but consideration needs to be given to the dynamic nature of the site. Changes to the site during intrusive investigation or removal of artifacts may result in scouring which exposes the site to further deterioration.

Colbert appeared as a correspondent on The Daily Show between 1997 and 2005. During this time, the comedian developed the character that would later form the basis for his Colbert Report persona, one that its creator calls a "fool who has spent a lot of his life playing not the fool". Colbert frequently cites Stone Phillips — whom he describes as having "the greatest neck in journalism" — as a source of inspiration for the character, as well as Geraldo Rivera, "because he's got this great sense of mission... He just thinks he's gonna change the world with this report". As a correspondent, Colbert was regularly pitted against knowledgeable interview subjects, fellow correspondent Steve Carell, or host Jon Stewart in scripted exchanges which typically revealed the character's lack of knowledge of whatever subject he was discussing. Other Daily Show correspondents have since adopted a similar style; former correspondent Rob Corddry recalls that when he and Ed Helms first joined the show's cast in 2002, they "just imitated Stephen Colbert for a year or two".

The International Basketball Federation (FIBA) was founded on June 18, 1932, in Geneva, Switzerland, by representatives from eight national basketball federations: Argentina, Czechoslovakia, Greece, Italy, Latvia, Portugal, Romania, and Switzerland. The founding members included Attilio Ponisio (Argentina), Josef Kral (Czechoslovakia), Simeon Mavroskoufis (Greece), Mario Biasini (Italy), Janis Grinbergas (Latvia), Francisco de Oliveira (Portugal), Leon Popescu (Romania), and Henry Machoud (Switzerland). On the same day, FIBA approved its first by-laws and a unified set of playing rules, marking a significant step in the global organization of basketball. Four years later, basketball made its debut as an official medal event at the 1936 Berlin Olympics, where the United States won the first Olympic basketball title, defeating Canada in the final. The games were played outdoors on clay courts, which proved challenging due to weather conditions. The first FIBA World Championship, now known as the FIBA Basketball World Cup, was held in 1950 in Buenos Aires, Argentina, where the host nation claimed the gold medal.

Bang Kachao’s rural charm, abundant green space, and proximity to central Bangkok have made it a prime target for developers, leading to land use conflicts with conservation groups. In October 2016, following the passing of King Bhumibol Adulyadej, the military government declared its intention to preserve Bang Kachao as a "green lung" for Bangkok. A three-year project involving PTT, The Chaipattana Foundation, and other stakeholders was launched to restore public green spaces, aiming to keep 60% of the area free from development. However, environmental protections in Thailand face challenges, including enforcement issues and competing interests. Despite concerns, one resident sold her family’s landholding for 500 million baht (approximately $14.3 million), reflecting the pressure of development. These dynamics highlight the ongoing struggle to balance conservation and urban expansion in Bang Kachao.

The park was originally the estate of Seattle mayor and shipbuilder Robert Moran. Due to poor health, Moran moved to Orcas Island and between 1906 and 1909 built his estate, which included a large mansion named Rosario. Wood and stone material found on the island were used to construct the estate's houses and buildings. In 1921, Moran gave a large portion of his property to the state of Washington for the creation of Moran State Park. The mansion and its grounds remain in private hands, operated as Rosario Resort and Spa.

As of 2015, Essert-Pittet had a population of approximately 604 residents. In 2008, 10.3% of the population were resident foreign nationals. Between 1999 and 2009, the population grew by 40%, with migration accounting for 30.8% of the increase and natural growth (births and deaths) contributing 9.2%.

Holbeach Clough is also known as Saracen's Head, with late 18th- and early 19th-century maps including both names. Saracen's Head might be associated with two public houses of that name that existed at the settlement—and still exist in modern form—one for perhaps 400 years, the name possibly taken from the coat of arms of significant local Willoughby, Irby, or Littlebury families. The Saracen's Head public house might have served travellers and traders crossing the then wide estuary of the River Welland to Fosdyke. The Anglo-Saxon coastline of the area ran immediately north of Holbeach Clough until a dike, the Roman Bank, was constructed for land reclamation in the 14th century, this running from the west at Moulton Seas End. The Saracen's Head Ward of the local council covers Holbeach Clough.

Kaari Upson (b. 1972, San Bernardino, California) is an American artist known for her exploration of human relationships and the body. Often incorrectly listed as born in 1970, Upson studied drawing, painting, and sculpture at the New York Studio School before earning her BFA in 2004 and MFA in 2007 from the California Institute of the Arts. While a student, she began a project inspired by the discovery of personal belongings from a stranger, which became central to her practice.

On 13 May 2011, Coren attracted controversy after joking about a privacy injunction by posting on his Twitter account: "god, ANOTHER injunction tonight. another footballer. and SUCH a boring one. fucking shit midfielder... he's yet another very ugly married man who's been carrying on with a gold-digging flopsie he should have seen coming a MILE away". Then on 14 May he tweeted "Gareth Barry looks remarkably relaxed when you consider that... first touch for Gareth Barry... not according to what I've heard... time for a bet. what chance Barry to score? tiny fiver on barry to score at 22–1. wdv been nice to get a double with Giggs in the match before... Barry's been pulled off...". This was later deleted but was archived.

Lectionary 228 is a Greek manuscript containing 43 lessons from the Gospels, Acts of the Apostles, and Epistles, along with 50 lessons from the Old Testament lectionary. The text is written in minuscule letters on 150 paper leaves, with the New Testament section comprising 100 leaves. Each leaf measures approximately 15 inches in length, and the text is arranged in two columns per page, with 42 lines per column. The entire codex, including both Old and New Testament sections, totals 200 leaves.

The Riverside Area Council (#045) was founded in 1921, originally named the Riverside Council. In 1927, the Hemet-San Jacinto Valley Council (#028) merged into the Riverside Area Council. In 1944, the council changed its name to the California Inland Empire Council but reverted to the Riverside Area Council in 1945. The Grayback Council (#024) was established in 1921 as the Redlands Council. The Arrowhead Area Council (#048) was formed in 1927, originally named the San Bernardino Valley Council, which adopted the name Arrowhead Area Council in 1933. These councils played a significant role in the development of Scouting in the Inland Empire, reflecting the region's growth and commitment to youth programs.

After the war, Geulen became involved with the Jewish community in Belgium and maintained contact with the children with whom she had come into contact. She was involved in trying to reunite the hidden children with surviving family members and became actively involved in the relief organisation Aid for Israelite Victims of the War (Aide aux Israélites Victimes de la Guerre, AIVG) which supported Jewish survivors of Nazi concentration camps in Belgium. She became involved in activism for pacifist and anti-racist causes. She married Charles Herscovici, a Jewish concentration camp survivor of Roma origin, in 1948.

As per the BPL household survey conducted in 2005, the proportion of BPL households in Mohammad Bazar CD Block was 35.3%, compared to 42.3% in Birbhum district. Six CD Blocks—Murarai II, Nalhati II, Rampurhat II, Rampurhat I, Suri II, and Murarai I—had a proportion of BPL families exceeding 50%, while three CD Blocks—Rajnagar, Suri I, and Labhpur—had less than 30%. The remaining ten CD Blocks in the district, including Mohammad Bazar, had a proportion of BPL families between 30% and 50%. According to the District Human Development Report, Birbhum, there is no clear link between the share of BPL households and the proportion of agricultural labourers. However, the report highlights a pattern of higher BPL household shares in blocks with disadvantaged populations, particularly among Scheduled Castes (SCs), Scheduled Tribes (STs), and Muslims. This indicates a correlation between poverty levels and the presence of socially and economically marginalized groups in the district.

Foley took her first degree at Swarthmore College in 1964. She then received an MAT (Master of Arts in Teaching) in English (1966) and an MA in Classics (1967) from Yale University, and a PhD in Classical Philology from Harvard University (1975). Her doctoral thesis was entitled 'Ritual Irony in the Bacchae and Other Late Euripidean Plays'. She taught at Swarthmore until 1979, when she moved to Barnard College, Columbia University. In 1998 she was the President of the American Philological Association (now the Society for Classical Studies). In Spring 2008 she was the 94th Sather Professor of Classical Literature at the University of California, Berkeley; her Sather Lectures focused on the restaging of Greek tragedies in America, and the ways in which modern productions of these plays explored themes of contemporary concern including slavery, race, the status of women, immigration, and identity. These lectures were later published as Reimagining Greek Tragedy on the American Stage (2012). She has also been Visiting Professor at Dartmouth College and New York University.

Citizens' Gavel, a civic tech organization based in Nigeria, operates with offices in Lagos and other locations across the country. The organization is led by its founder and Team Lead, Nelson Olanipekun, and comprises a 7-member rapid response team, over 160 volunteer lawyers, and additional full-time staff. Citizens' Gavel operates in 24 states in Nigeria and has established two arms for diversification: JusticeLab and Podus. JusticeLab focuses on addressing systemic weaknesses in the judiciary to restore citizens' faith in the legal system, while Podus connects victims of security agency brutality to nearby lawyers. The Podus app, available on the Google Play Store, functions similarly to Uber, allowing users to log in, describe their case, and alert a lawyer in their vicinity for assistance. Through these initiatives, Citizens' Gavel aims to enhance access to justice and promote accountability in Nigeria's legal system.

The Tarnogród Confederation, formed on 26 November 1715, was a union of Polish nobles opposing King Augustus II and his Saxon forces. Stanisław Ledóchowski was elected its marshal. Supported by Russian mediation, the Confederation sought to end the king's reliance on Saxon troops, culminating in the Silent Sejm of 1717.

In 2004 Risen came upon information about the National Security Agency's surveillance of international communications originating or terminating in the United States, code-named "Stellar Wind". He and Eric Lichtblau, who had obtained similar information, co-wrote a story about "Stellar Wind" just before the 2004 United States presidential election. Risen said the New York Times spiked the story at the request of the White House. The two reporters rewrote and resubmitted the story a number of times after the election but were rejected each time. Risen decided to publish the information in a book along with information about the CIA's Operation Merlin. He warned the paper about the book and suggested it publish the information itself. Bill Keller, Executive editor of the New York Times, and Philip Taubman, Washington Bureau chief, were furious with Risen. Keller and Taubman negotiated with the Bush administration and, after a delay, the paper published much of Risen and Lichtblau's report about "Stellar Wind" in December 2005. Risen's book, titled State of War: The Secret History of the CIA and the Bush Administration, was published in January 2006.

In 2004, James Risen, a reporter for *The New York Times*, uncovered information about a secret National Security Agency (NSA) surveillance program code-named "Stellar Wind," which involved warrantless collection of Americans' phone and email data. Risen co-wrote the story with fellow journalist Eric Lichtblau, but the newspaper, under pressure from the White House, initially spiked the report. Executive Editor Bill Keller and Washington Bureau Chief Philip Taubman, after meetings with President George W. Bush and senior officials, decided not to publish the story before the 2004 presidential election. Frustrated by repeated rejections, Risen decided to include the revelations in his book, *State of War: The Secret History of the CIA and the Bush Administration*, published in January 2006. Facing the imminent release of Risen's book, *The New York Times* finally published much of the report in December 2005, citing additional reporting that confirmed legal disputes within the Bush administration over the program. The decision to delay publication sparked internal conflict, with Keller and Taubman reportedly furious at Risen for his persistence. The story, which exposed the NSA's warrantless surveillance, earned Risen and Lichtblau the Pulitzer Prize and remains one of the most significant investigative reports of the post-9/11 era.

On September 11, 1842, General Adrian Woll led a force of 1,400 men to invade Texas and capture San Antonio. Robert Neighbors, serving in John C. Hays's company of volunteers, was attending court in San Antonio on September 15 when he and approximately 52 others, including court officers, were captured. They were forcibly marched to Perote Prison in Mexico, where Neighbors was held until his release on March 24, 1844.

Grace Y. Yi earned her B.Sc. in 1986 and M.Sc. in 1989 in mathematics from Sichuan University in China. She obtained an M.A. in statistics from York University in 1996 and completed her Ph.D. in 2000 at the University of Toronto under the supervision of Donald A. S. Fraser. She became an Assistant Professor at the University of Waterloo in 2001.

The station consisted of a 11⁄2-story dwelling built on a platform supported by wooden piles, with a lantern at the center of its peaked roof. It was constructed in 1905, using the same design by U.S. Lighthouse Board Architect Carl Leick used at the Desdemona Sands Lighthouse at the mouth of the Columbia River. The light was deactivated and the entire structure torn down in 1944.

Retaj Al Rayyan Mecca is a 4-star hotel located in Mecca, Saudi Arabia. The hotel has 22 stories and 670 rooms and suites as well as a restaurant, fitness center, and meeting rooms. A report published by Trade Arabia stated that the Al Rayyan Mecca hotel constitutes the Retaj Group's first step towards its “expansion plan in the GCC, particularly in Saudi Arabia.”

Chromolithographs, once widely used for advertisements, book illustrations, and decorative art, are now primarily valued as collectibles and historical artifacts. Their rarity today stems from the labor-intensive production process, which required multiple heavy stones for layering colors, and the fragile nature of the materials, leading to deterioration over time. While chromolithographs were more affordable than paintings, they were costly compared to later photomechanical and offset lithography methods, which replaced chromolithography in the late 19th and early 20th centuries due to their efficiency. Efforts to preserve surviving chromolithographs highlight their significance in the history of printing and art reproduction.

In 2014, Dragan Bender signed a seven-year contract with Maccabi Tel Aviv but was loaned to Ironi Ramat Gan in the Israeli second division for the 2014–15 season. He made his debut for Ramat Gan on October 22, 2014, against Ramat HaSharon, recording 13 points, 13 rebounds, and 4 assists. On December 3, 2014, Bender achieved a double-double with 23 points and 12 rebounds, and on January 1, 2015, he scored a season-high 25 points against Maccabi Ra'anana. By the end of the season, he averaged 9.7 points, 7.4 rebounds, 2.6 assists, and 1.1 blocks per game across 28 appearances, showcasing his potential and prompting Maccabi Tel Aviv to recall him for the following season.

According to the 2000 census, Parma, Missouri, had a population of 852, with 352 households and 222 families residing in the city. The population density was 10.14 people per square mile, with 405 housing units at an average density of 4.82 units per square mile. The racial makeup was 48.4% White, 49.3% African American, 0.6% other races, and 1.7% two or more races, while 1.3% of the population identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

The Canadian Grain Commission (CGC), established in 1912, operates under the Canada Grain Act and reports to Parliament through the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food. It is led by three commissioners, including a Chief Commissioner, appointed by the federal cabinet. The Chief Operating Officer, overseeing daily operations, reports directly to the Chief Commissioner.

The 1991 elections were held as the previous Lok Sabha, with Chandra Sekhar at its helm had been dissolved just 16 months after government formation. Over 500 million eligible voters were once again given the chance to elect their government. The elections were held in a polarised environment and are also referred to as the 'Mandal-Mandir' elections after the two most important poll issues, the Mandal Commission fallout and the Ram Janmabhoomi-Babri Masjid issue.

WDSC-TV shut down its analog signal, over UHF channel 15, which due to major equipment failure had been operating at significantly reduced power since September 25, 2008, on December 15 of that year. The station's digital signal continued to broadcast on its pre-transition UHF channel 33. Through the use of PSIP, digital television receivers display the station's virtual channel as its former UHF analog channel 15.

Caroline Bowman began her career with international tours of *Fame* in China and *Grease* in Turkey, where she performed as Rizzo on an outdoor stage overlooking the Bosphorus Strait. After returning to the United States, she joined the national tour of *Spamalot* as a performer before moving to New York City. Bowman made her Broadway debut as an understudy in *Wicked*, eventually playing Elphaba. In 2013, she originated a role in *Kinky Boots* before starring as Eva Perón in the national tour of *Evita*. Her early career showcased her versatility and rapid rise in the theater world, blending international and national experiences.

Bottreaux Castle, a 12th-century motte-and-bailey fortress, was likely built between 1154 and 1189 during the reign of Henry II by Sir William des Bottreaux. The castle served as the principal residence of the Bottreaux family, who settled in Cornwall following the Norman Conquest and became one of the most prominent families in medieval Cornwall. By the late 14th century, William de Botreaux (1337–1391) had been elevated to the title of Baron Botreaux. The last male heir of the family, William Lord Bottreaux, died in 1461, and the castle was likely de-castellated soon after. By 1478, William Worcester described it as a manor house, and in 1538, John Leland noted its ruins, calling it "the strongest, though not the biggest" he had seen. By 1602, Richard Carew recorded its use as a prison. Today, only earthworks remain of this once significant fortress.

During the dictatorship of Miguel Primo de Rivera, it was proposed in early 1928 that Spanish aviation companies be combined and state-controlled as public utilities. This led to the creation of Compañía de Líneas Aéreas Subvencionadas S.A. (C.L.A.S.S.A.), into which Iberia, Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.), Compañía Española de Tráfico Aéreo (C.E.T.A.), the pilot training company Compañía Española de Aviación, and the airship line Transaérea Colón were merged. Iberia ceased independent operations on May 29, 1929, though its name remained registered.

In 2009, Flawless took part in the third series of Britain's Got Talent. They auditioned in London shortly before Diversity. Amanda Holden stated they were 'jaw droppingly outstanding', while Piers Morgan called the group 'electrifying'. They received three yeses from the judges.

Wildberg is a township classified as a Luftkurort (air spa) and Schaferlaufstadt (shepherds' run town) located in the German state of Baden-Württemberg. It lies in the eastern part of the Calw district, bordering the Böblingen district to the east. Situated in the Nagold Valley, Wildberg is positioned at the transition between the Northern Black Forest and the Black Forest foothills. To the west, the area features high plateaus with Lower Muschelkalk formations. The municipal area ranges in elevation from 355.59 meters to 637.54 meters above sea level.

The rodded medium (plasma medium) is also known as the wire mesh, and wire grid. It is a square lattice of thin parallel wires The initial research pertaining to this medium was conducted by J. Brown, K.E. Golden, and W. Rotman.

Construction at AFRRI's main headquarters/facility in Bethesda, Maryland began in November 1960, preceding the organization's official charter and establishment dates. It was occupied by January 1962 and fully operational by November 1963. Facilities include a Training, Research, Isotopes, General Atomics (TRIGA) nuclear reactor, laboratory space, an animal facility. a high-dose cobalt-60 facility, a 54-megaelectron volt linear accelerator (LINAC), and a low-level cobalt-60 irradiation facility.

In October 2017, President Donald Trump announced the end of the Affordable Care Act's (ACA) cost-sharing reduction (CSR) subsidies, a smaller subsidy designed to reduce out-of-pocket costs for low-income enrollees. The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) projected that this decision would increase premiums on ACA exchanges by approximately 20% in 2018 and 25% in subsequent years. Additionally, the CBO estimated that ending the subsidies would add $194 billion to the federal deficit over a decade due to higher premium tax credits. While the ACA exchanges were expected to remain stable, the CBO noted that some insurers might exit the marketplaces. Initially, around 1 million fewer people were expected to have health insurance coverage, though enrollment in ACA exchanges was projected to rise over time.

Conservation programmes in the United Kingdom aim to protect endangered species by addressing habitat loss, pollution, and illegal activities. One such species is the freshwater pearl mussel (*Margaritifera margaritifera*), which is raised in protected environments due to its declining populations and the challenges it faces in the wild. With a lifespan of over 100 years, this species has suffered from habitat degradation, water pollution, and the loss of host fish, such as salmon and trout, which are essential for its breeding cycle. The freshwater pearl mussel was granted legal protection under the Wildlife and Countryside Act in 1981, and since 1998, it has been illegal to hunt, sell, or buy these mussels in the UK. Despite these protections, illegal pearl fishing continues to threaten their survival. Conservation efforts focus on safeguarding their nutrient-poor, clean river habitats and addressing issues like water quality and the availability of host fish to support their complex lifecycle.

In 2018, parents at Watchung Hills Regional High School in Somerset County, New Jersey, challenged *Fun Home* in the 12th-grade curriculum, but the challenge was rejected, and the book remained. In May 2019, a lawsuit was filed against the school’s administrators, claiming that if *Fun Home* was not removed, minors would suffer irreparable harm and New Jersey statutes would be violated. Following the challenge, North Hunterdon High School removed the book from its libraries but restored it in February 2019. The controversy highlighted debates over intellectual freedom and the inclusion of the award-winning graphic memoir in educational settings.

During the Mamluk period, multi-story buildings occupied by rental apartments, known as a *rabʿ* (plural *ribaʿ* or *urbu*), became a common feature of urban housing and continued to be prominent during the later Ottoman period. This type of housing was relatively unique to Cairo at the time. These apartments were often laid out as multi-story duplexes or triplexes, providing efficient use of space in the densely populated city. When attached to caravanserais (*wikalas*), the two lower floors of these buildings were typically used for commercial and storage purposes, while the upper floors were rented out to tenants. Despite their integration with commercial complexes, the apartment buildings had their own separate street entrances, ensuring privacy and functional separation from the bustling activity below. The oldest partially-preserved example of this type of structure is the Wikala of Amir Qawsun, built before 1341. Residential buildings in Cairo were further organized into close-knit neighborhoods called *harat*, which often featured gates that could be closed at night or during times of unrest, providing an additional layer of security and community cohesion.

Julian Simon criticized the prevailing notion that natural resources are finite, arguing that this term is inappropriate and misleading when applied to such resources. He contended that the concept of "finiteness" is problematic from both practical and economic perspectives, describing the issue as "just semantic." Simon believed that framing resources as finite muddles public discussion and leads to misguided policy decisions. To illustrate his point, he used the example of copper, emphasizing that the quantity of services derived from copper should not be considered finite. He argued that human ingenuity and technological innovation continually find substitutes, improve efficiency, and discover new methods of extraction, effectively expanding the availability of resources. Furthermore, Simon highlighted that potential sources of copper extend beyond Earth, including extraterrestrial mining on asteroids or other celestial bodies. By focusing on human creativity and enterprise as the "ultimate resource," Simon maintained that resource scarcity could be overcome indefinitely, provided that social, economic, and political systems foster innovation and freedom.

The immunohistochemical (IHC) assessment of estrogen receptor testing (ERT) in breast carcinoma is used to determine the presence of estrogen receptors (ER) on cancer cells, guiding endocrine therapy decisions. Breast carcinomas that are ER-positive or hormone receptor (HR)-positive are more likely to respond to endocrine treatments, such as tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors. Monitoring ER activity is essential as it helps predict treatment efficacy and disease progression. In 2025, six cases of breast carcinoma were analyzed using ERT IHC assessment. Samples were prepared by fixation in paraformaldehyde and glutaraldehyde, followed by paraffin embedding. The staining process utilized a standardized immunohistochemical system, employing specific monoclonal antibodies to detect ER activity. The level of ER activity was classified based on the intensity and percentage of stained tumor cells, aiding in treatment planning. This precise assessment ensures tailored therapeutic strategies for patients with ER-positive breast cancer.

The second accident, in 1958, involved a fuel rupture and fire in the National Research Universal reactor (NRU) reactor building. Some fuel rods were overheated. With a robotic crane, one of the rods with metallic uranium was pulled out of the reactor vessel. When the arm of the crane moved away from the vessel, the uranium caught fire and the rod broke. The largest part of the rod fell down into the containment vessel, still burning. The whole building was contaminated. The valves of the ventilation system were opened, and a large area outside the building was contaminated. The fire was extinguished by scientists and maintenance men in protective clothing running along the hole in the containment vessel with buckets of wet sand, throwing the sand down at the moment they passed the smoking entrance.

The setup of a hot air balloon begins with unpacking the balloon from its carrying bag, laying it out on the ground, and connecting it to the basket and burner. A gasoline-powered fan is commonly used to blow cold air into the envelope, partially inflating it to establish its basic shape. Once the balloon is partially inflated, the burner is activated to heat the air inside, causing the envelope to rise. A crew member, stationed opposite the mouth of the balloon, holds the crown line, a rope tied to the apex of the envelope. This "crown-man" prevents excessive sway and ensures the envelope does not rise prematurely before it is sufficiently buoyant. The crown line also helps keep the balloon stable during inflation. Once the balloon is upright, the pilot and passengers board the basket. When ready for launch, the pilot directs more heat into the envelope, making the balloon lighter and allowing it to lift off, marking the start of the flight.

Football in Turkey stems back to the late 19th century, when Englishmen brought the game with them while living in Salonica (then part of the Ottoman Empire). The first league competition was the Istanbul Football League, which took place in the 1904–05 season. The league went through several variations until the creation of the Millî Lig (Süper Lig) in 1959. Between the creation of the Istanbul League and Millî Lig, several other regional leagues took place: Adana (1924), Ankara (1922), Eskişehir (1924), İzmir (1924), Bursa (1924), and Trabzon (1922), to name a few. The first competition to bring forth a national champion was the former Turkish Football Championship, which began in 1924 and continued until 1951. The championship format was based on a knockout competition, contested between the winners of each of the country's top regional leagues. The National Division (Turkish: Millî Küme) was the first national league competition in Turkey. Started in 1937, the National Division consisted of the strongest clubs from the Ankara, Istanbul, and İzmir leagues. The championship lasted until 1950.

Many linguistic sources relating to the Slavic languages describe Silesian as a dialect of Polish. However, many Silesians consider themselves a separate ethnicity and have been advocating the recognition of Silesian as a distinct language. According to the last official census in Poland in 2011, over 500 thousand people declared Silesian as their native language. Many sociolinguistic sources (e.g. Tomasz Kamusella assert that the determination between a language or a dialect is ultimately a matter of extralinguistic criteria, such as national attachment or the political attitudes of its users, and this changes over time. See: Agnieszka Pianka, Alfred F. Majewicz, Tomasz Wicherkiewicz) Language organizations such as SIL International and various linguistic resources such as Ethnologue, and Poland's Ministry of Administration and Digitization, recognize Silesian as a distinct language. In 2007, Silesian was assigned its language code szl within the ISO 639-3 standard.

Traditionally, the Dongzhi Festival is a time for families to get together. One activity that occurs during these get-togethers (especially in the southern parts of China and in Chinese communities overseas) is the making and eating of tangyuan (湯圓) or balls of glutinous rice, which symbolize reunion. Tangyuan are made of glutinous rice flour and are sometimes coloured pink or green. Each family member receives at least one large tangyuan in addition to several small ones. The flour balls are cooked in a sweet soup or savory broth with both the ball and the soup/broth served in one bowl. It is also often served with jiuniang, a mildly alcoholic, unfiltered rice wine containing whole grains of glutinous rice (and often also sweet osmanthus flowers),.

The ERT immunohistochemical assessment is a semi-quantitative method used to predict the likelihood of success to anti-estrogen therapy in breast carcinoma. ER-positive breast carcinomas are likely to respond to various endocrine treatments, causing the monitoring of ER activity an essential variable in disease and treatment progression. In 2002, 6 cases of breast carcinoma were received, characterized, and analyzed through the ERT IHC assessment. The level of known ER activity was classified (negative, low, medium, and high) and selected for observation. After embedment in a paraffin block, the samples were stained using a hematoxylin and eosin staining (H & E staining) system. The IHC analysis was then performed the same day using anti-ER monoclonal antibodies, and resulted in a consistently strong correlation in the carcinoma samples with ER activity, where as the level of ER activity increased, the IHC response increased respectively.

The Eagle Point site was selected by Charles Mulford Robinson, who wrote a report, "Report on the Improvement of the City of Dubuque, Iowa". Architect Alfred Caldwell directed the building of many of the structures at the park, which made use of the limestone found in the area.
