Several prominent members were repressed, and in 1929, the Society was forcibly closed, marking the end of its significant contributions to Russian natural sciences.

In 2003, Edel released Flowers: The Jennifer Paige Hits Collection as a result of her parting ways with her record label. In October of the same year, Paige admitted she was no longer with either Hollywood Records or Edel Records. Paige continued writing for other artists, and compiling songs for a new studio effort. In April 2006, "The Calling", was chosen by Lifetime Television for their made-for-TV movie, Augusta, Gone, based on the book of the same name. The song was written by Paige and co-writer/producer Chris Landon of Loudnoize Entertainment.

The name Chernobyl has become synonymous with catastrophic nuclear disaster and systemic failure, often invoked as a metaphor for analogous crises. Pundits highlight it as a cautionary tale of ignoring science, with comparisons drawn to events like the global climate crisis. Ukraine has preserved the disaster area as a site of reflection, while numerous cultural works have explored its legacy, symbolizing both human error and nature's reclamation of industrial ruin.

ViviSat proposed the Mission Extension Vehicle (MEV) concept in 2011. At that time, the project was planned to be a 50/50 joint venture of aerospace firms US Space and Alliant Techsystems (ATK), to operate as a small-scale in-space satellite-refueling spacecraft. In the joint venture, ATK was to be responsible for the technical design, production and operation of the satellite, and US Space would be responsible for the financing and business-side of operations.

The management goal is to restore extension of the joint. Treatment is generally with a splint that holds the first joint of the finger straight continuously for 8 weeks. This should begin within a week of the injury. The splint may be worn just at night for a few additional weeks after this. The splint acts to immobilize flexing of the joint.

In November 1992, during the First Nagorno-Karabakh War, Polad Hashimov, while training with the 702nd Motorised Rifle Brigade, fought in the successful defense of the villages of Marzili and Novruzlu in the Aghdam District of Azerbaijan. In February 1994, he participated in Operation Murovdagh and was involved in clashes with Armenian forces at the Guzgu, Koroghlu, and Omar military posts.

The constituency was renamed to NA-61 (Rawalpindi-V) from NA-54, and was delimited to include all of Rawalpindi Cantonment and 3 census charges of Chaklala Cantonment. In the earlier delimitation, much of Chaklala was included in this constituency. Now what area has been shifted to the new NA-60 (old NA-56).

Carpenter attended Helena High School in Helena, Montana, where he was a member of the National Honor Society and had a 3.7 grade point average. As a football player at Helena, Carpenter was a two-time all-state and two-time all-league selection as a kicker and punter. During his senior season, he converted 26 of 29 point after attempts and made five field goals with a long of 53 yards. Additionally, he was a first all-conference and second-team all-state selection as a wide receiver, setting school records with 931 receiving yards and 13 touchdowns.

The alliance among Octavian, Mark Antony and Marcus Lepidus, commonly known as the Second Triumvirate, was renewed for a five-year term at Tarentum in 37 BC. However, the triumvirate broke down when Octavian saw Caesarion, the professed son of Julius Caesar and Queen Cleopatra VII of Egypt, as a major threat to his power. This occurred when Mark Antony, the other most influential member of the triumvirate, abandoned his wife, Octavian's sister Octavia Minor. Afterward he moved to Egypt to start a long-term romance with Cleopatra, becoming Caesarion's de facto stepfather. Octavian and the majority of the Roman Senate saw Antony as leading a separatist movement that threatened to break the Roman Republic's unity.

The video for "War Paint" was released via the singer's YouTube account on July 8, 2015. The video was produced in a studio in Brooklyn, New York. It was filmed in one large room and took twelve hours to film. Garen Barsegian directed the video and Oleksii Babenko edited the video in post-production. Fletcher was directly involved with the creative process. Fletcher's friend Stephanie Troyak choreographed the video and other friends of the artist joined her on-set to perform dance routines. The choreography incorporated wild and in other parts, gentle dances. The video has a juxtaposition of both dark and colorful themes and Fletcher has described it as having a "cool vibe". The video includes neon tube lights as props and features multiple costume changes. Babenko impressed Fletcher with his editing of her backing dancers and managing to recreate her initial ideas for the visual.

In the 2019 Idanre/Ifedore Federal Constituency election, Tajudeen Adefisoye of the Social Democratic Party (SDP) defeated Kayode Akinmade of the PDP, securing 16,186 votes (41.7%) against Akinmade's 14,191 votes (36.5%).

Curtis applied to the BBC and was hired to make a film for one of its training courses, comparing designer clothes in music videos to the design of weapons. He was subsequently given a post on That's Life!, a magazine series that juxtaposed hard-hitting investigations and light-hearted content. He was a film director on Out of Court, a BBC Two legal series, from 1980 until 1982.

By October 2018, Marvel Studios was developing a limited series starring Anthony Mackie's Sam Wilson / Falcon and Sebastian Stan's Bucky Barnes / Winter Soldier from the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) films. Malcolm Spellman was hired as head writer of the series, which was announced as The Falcon and the Winter Soldier in April 2019. Spellman modeled the series after buddy films that deal with race, such as 48 Hrs. (1982), The Defiant Ones (1958), Lethal Weapon (1987), and Rush Hour (1998). Kari Skogland was hired to direct the miniseries a month later, and executive produced alongside Spellman and Marvel Studios' Kevin Feige, Louis D'Esposito, Victoria Alonso, and Nate Moore.: 15 Written by Spellman, the first episode is titled "New World Order", and was released on the streaming service Disney+ on March 19, 2021.

The Orlando Branch Campus of Everglades University is located in a 28,000 square-foot building on Trafalgar Court, in Maitland, Florida. The campus relocated from Altamonte Springs to Maitland on July 7, 2015, and held a ribbon cutting ceremony which Maitland Mayor A. Dale McDonald attended. The facility houses offices, classrooms, a computer lab, a library, faculty lounge and a student lounge. The university expects that due to its proximity to the metropolitan Orlando area that the campus will quickly expand to 1,000 students, making it the largest Everglades University campus. The staff, faculty, and students at the Boca Raton Campus have participated in the Walk to Cure Arthritis, Making Strides Against Breast Cancer Walk, collecting toys for Toys for Tots, and hosted several blood drives with OneBlood.

During the 2012 offseason, the Vancouver Canucks signed free-agent defenceman Jason Garrison to a six-year, $27.6 million contract. Garrison, a native of White Rock, B.C., joined his hometown team after four seasons with the Florida Panthers and was expected to replace longtime Canucks defenceman Sami Salo, who departed in free agency. Salo, a fan favorite and key contributor during his decade in Vancouver, signed a two-year, $7.5 million contract with the Tampa Bay Lightning on July 1, 2012. Garrison was brought in to help stabilize the Canucks' blueline following Salo's departure and quickly became a reliable presence, particularly after injuries to Kevin Bieksa forced him to play out of position as a left-shot defenceman on the right side. Despite the challenges, Garrison formed a strong defensive pairing with Dan Hamhuis and logged significant ice time, solidifying his role as a key contributor to the team.

MGM Home Entertainment released I Want to Live! on DVD on May 7, 2002. Kino Lorber reissued the film on DVD featuring a restored print in October 2015. In November 2016, Twilight Time released the film on Blu-ray disc in a limited edition of 3,000 units.

Publicists are known for their strong written and oral communication skills, sound judgment, and ability to work under pressure. Motivated by the goal of promoting clients and maintaining positive public images, they must handle high-stress situations, such as managing crisis communication when rumors or negative news go viral. Common college majors for publicists include public relations, journalism, communications, and marketing, with internships at PR firms or talent agencies providing valuable early experience. Starting at entry-level roles, publicists often work their way up through hands-on experience. Universities like New York University and Southern New Hampshire University offer degrees in communication and public relations, equipping students with skills in relationship-building, social media management, and brand strategy. These abilities are crucial for navigating the fast-paced, deadline-driven environment of the profession.

The National Roller Hockey League (NRHL) was founded in 2014 and began its inaugural season in 2015–2016 with four teams: Alkali Revive, Alkali Surge, Canfield Alkali Chiefs, and Detroit Bordercats. The Bordercats had an undefeated regular season, eliminated Alkali Revive in three playoff games, and swept the Alkali Surge in the Commissioners Cup Finals. Detroit’s Max Halverson was named playoffs MVP for his outstanding performance.

Aleutian terns breed in colonies, often in mixed-species groups with Arctic terns, across coastal and tundra habitats near freshwater or saltwater. They exhibit strong site fidelity, returning to stable breeding sites annually. Pairs form shortly after arriving at breeding grounds in May, with nest-building occurring from late May to early June. Their nests are shallow depressions typically built on low vegetation such as mosses, lichens, and grasses. The typical clutch size is two eggs, though occasionally one or three are laid. Both parents share incubation duties and feeding of the chicks, though the female incubates more and fishes less. Aleutian terns spend less time brooding their chicks compared to Arctic terns, contributing to higher chick mortality rates in this species.

A new version, the F4F-4, entered service in 1941 with six machine guns and the Grumman-patented Sto-Wing folding wing system, which allowed more aircraft to be stored on an aircraft carrier, increasing the number of fighters that could be parked on a surface by more than a factor of 2. The F4F-4 was the definitive version that saw the most combat service in the early war years, including the Battle of Midway. Navy F4F-3s were replaced by F4F-4s in June 1942. During the Battle of Midway, only VMF-221 still used F4F-3s. VF-42 of the Yorktown was the last carrier group converted to the F4F-4, and that was done as it left Pearl Harbor on the way to the Battle of Midway as VF-3 flew in new F4F-4s with Com. Thach.

Signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), commonly quoted for electrical signals, can be applied to various forms of signals, such as isotope levels in ice cores, biochemical signaling, or financial trading signals. Metaphorically, SNR refers to distinguishing meaningful information (signal) from irrelevant or distracting elements (noise). In online forums and communities, off-topic posts and spam are regarded as noise, while appropriate discussions and relevant contributions are considered the signal, emphasizing the importance of maintaining focus and clarity in communication.

The treasure consists of silver and other metalwork, of varying type, quality and dates in the 1st to late 2nd centuries of the Common Era. The hoard was hidden in the late 2nd or early 3rd century, but contained heirloom pieces like the repoussé silver jug that was made in Italy in the 1st century CE. The find totalled 93 items, some of which were dissociated handles and silver appliqués, with a total weight of 25 kg. Most of the items are bowls, cups and jugs, but there is also a phiale for libations.

In 2018, parents challenged Fun Home in the Watchung Hills Regional High School curriculum. The challenge was rejected, and the book remained in the school. One year later, a lawsuit was filed in May 2019 against the administrators of the school asking for removal of the book. The lawsuit claims that if the book is not removed, "minors will suffer irreparable harm and that New Jersey statutes will be violated." After the Watchung Hills High School challenge, administrators at nearby North Hunterdon High School removed Fun Home from their libraries as well, but the book was later restored in February 2019.

According to the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops (USCCB), the bill "would force the Church to alter its structure in violation of its own religious principles." One of the sponsors of the Bill, Andrew J. McDonald, stated that the effort was an "attempt to create a forum for a group of concerned Catholic constituents to discuss their legislative proposals regarding parish corporate finances." Bishop William E. Lori, at that time Bishop of Bridgeport, described it as "unconstitutional," as a violation of the First Amendment to the United States Constitution. Connecticut Attorney General Richard Blumenthal opened an investigation to "determine its intent and its possible violation of the constitution."

As of the 2000 census, Alburgh village had a population of 488, with 206 households and 132 families residing in the area. The population density was 1,220.0 people per square mile, with 241 housing units. The racial makeup was 94.67% White, 2.46% Native American, 0.41% Asian, and 2.46% from two or more races.

This event marked the 10th anniversary of the UFC and to celebrate the anniversary the UFC inaugurated its Hall of Fame, with Royce Gracie and Ken Shamrock as the first inductees. UFC President Dana White said; "We feel that no two individuals are more deserving than Royce and Ken to be the charter members. Their contributions to our sport, both inside and outside the Octagon, may never be equaled.”

Around 30—35 opposition youth activists organised a protest in front of Baku's City Hall on 15 November chanting "Freedom!" but were soon violently confronted by police forces. The arrested activists were released within the next hours.

Tanerko, a bay horse with no distinctive markings, was bred in France by his owner François Dupre. He was sired by Tantieme, a two-time Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe winner (1950, 1951) and champion sire in France, out of the mare La Divine, a daughter of Chanteur, who won the Ascot Gold Cup in 1947. Tanerko was trained at Chantilly by François Mathet, who also trained Tantieme, establishing a successful partnership with Dupre during the post-war era.

The Women's United Soccer Association (WUSA), the first official professional women's soccer league in the United States, began in 2001. Jacqui Little, a sixth-round draft pick (48th overall) by the Bay Area CyberRays, made 19 regular season appearances that year, starting 15 games and contributing 2 goals and 1 assist. She also made a brief appearance in the 2001 Founders Cup, where the CyberRays won the championship by defeating the Atlanta Beat on penalties.

Dr. Lee Iu-cheung, born in Hong Kong to a family originating from Xinhui, Guangdong, spent his childhood in the Central and Western District. After excelling in his Cambridge University local examinations, he was awarded the prestigious Lee Iu Cheung Engineering Bursary. In 1913, he joined the Faculty of Engineering at the University of Hong Kong. In 1919, he pursued a special study on River Conservancy and Sanitary Engineering at Cornell University but had to abandon his studies due to his father’s passing. Upon returning to Hong Kong, he became a lecturer at the University of Hong Kong, contributing significantly to the institution’s development.

The diagnosis of DFSP-FS depends on finding a dermal tumor with characteristic areas consisting of spindle-shaped cells with atypical, vesicular nuclei aligned in smooth muscle-like bundled or herringbone patterns. The spindle-shaped cells are often rapidly growing as indicated by their relatively high mitotic index. These sites form from 5% to 100% of the tumor with any remaining sites consisting of DFSP lesions (i.e. bland, uniform, relatively slowly proliferating spindle-shaped tumor cells arranged in a characteristic cartwheel or whorled pattern). The diagnosis of DFSP-FS is strongly supported by finding that the tumors' cells express the COL1A1-PDGFB fusion gene; a finding that these tumor cells do not express or only weakly express CD34 protein strongly indicates that it is a DFSP-FS rather than a DFSP tumor.

Picquet returned to France in 1772. He took up a ministry at Verjon and then as a chaplain to the nuns of the Visitation. A private audience with Pope Pius VI occurred in 1777 and he retired in 1779. He died at Verjon, Ain, France, in 1781.

The 89th Brigade went into winter quarters at Phalempin in late 1918, shortly before the Armistice. The 68th Siege Battery was disbanded in 1920, and a new 68th Battery, Royal Garrison Artillery, was formed from a wartime battery soon after.

The University of San Andrés, formally established on September 1, 1988, is a daughter institution of St. Andrew's Scots School, founded in 1838 by Scottish immigrants. Named after Saint Andrew, the university's crest features a saltire, thistle, and open book, symbolizing its Scottish heritage and academic mission. It was founded by the Scottish Civil Educational Association of San Andrés, which was succeeded in 2010 by the Fundación Universidad de San Andrés. The university continues the legacy of its founders, emphasizing education and cultural exchange.

Zharman is a shallow saline steppe lake. It lies at the bottom of a vast depression of the Turgay Basin, between lakes Sarymoin and Zharkol to the north, and Aksuat to the south. The coastline is very indented, with multitude of bays and peninsulas. Shores are sandy and the bottom is muddy. Meadows near the shore are a seasonal grazing ground for local cattle.

After graduating from high school, he attended Ohio State University in Columbus, Ohio and then the University of Florida in Gainesville, Florida, where he played for the Ohio State Buckeyes football team and the Florida Gators football team, respectively. He played for the Gators in 1926, 1927, and 1928, and Brumbaugh, Rainey Cawthon, Clyde Crabtree and Royce Goodbread were members of the 1928 Gators' "Phantom Four" backfield that helped the team lead the country with 336 points scored. The Gators finished the 1928 season 8–1, losing only to the Tennessee Volunteers by a single point, 13–12. Brumbaugh was later inducted into the University of Florida Athletic Hall of Fame as a "Gator Great."

Legends of Eisenwald is a medieval RPG/strategy game set in a fictionalized version of medieval Germany, where the superstitions of the time come to life. The main campaign follows the youngest child of Baron Lahnstein, who returns to reclaim their lands amidst political and supernatural challenges. The game also features two standalone scenarios, *The Masquerade* and *Cursed Castle*, set in the Lahnstein Castle and a mysterious cursed fortress, respectively, offering unique stories and quests.

He was the son of William Tempest, Prothonotary of the Court of Common Pleas, and his wife Elizabeth Cooke, born 16 April 1682. He was educated at Eton College and matriculated at King's College, Cambridge in 1700. He in turn became Prothonotary of the Court of Common Pleas.

Kerry Coombs joined the University of Cincinnati in 2007 as the defensive backs coach under Brian Kelly. That year, the Bearcats led the nation with 26 interceptions. In 2009, Coombs was promoted to associate head coach, adding responsibilities for special teams while continuing to coach defensive backs.

In 1995 the machine went through a major overhaul and is now working in Wolsztyn as a tourist attraction, called Beautiful Helen (pl. Piękna Helena). It is occasionally used in regular service, pulling trains to Poznań or Leszno. Currently Beautiful Helen waits for repair in Wolsztyn.

HMS *Ardent* (1894) was commissioned into the Royal Navy on 16 October 1894 at Chiswick, with Lieutenant Charles Campbell as her commanding officer. She was commissioned for service on the Mediterranean Station, based in Malta. Departing Portsmouth shortly after commissioning, *Ardent* stopped at Gibraltar to join the third-class cruiser HMS *Pique*, and the two departed for Malta in November 1894. Upon arrival, *Ardent* became tender to the fleet flagship, HMS *Ramillies*, and was tasked with training stokers in the management of water-tube boilers.

Walter G. Alexander was born in 1922 in Petersburg, Virginia, into a family with a legacy of medical professionals. He attended public schools in Orange, New Jersey, before earning a scholarship to Rutgers University in 1943, where he studied mechanical engineering, participated in ROTC, and competed on the track team. After graduating, he worked as a draftsman for Douglas Aircraft in California. Later, he pursued dentistry at Howard University, earning his doctorate in dental surgery in 1952. Alexander practiced dentistry in New Jersey, where he lived and worked until his retirement in 2007.

In 1973 and 1974, the 28th Surgical Hospital (Mobile) (Army) (MASH) helped phase in new designs for operating rooms and patient facilities, replacing the previous canvas tents. Following this, all other configurations of army deployable hospitals were inactivated or converted to the Combat Support Hospital (CSH) configuration, with the 212th MASH being the last to convert.

The Royal Swedish Academy of Letters, History and Antiquities, currently located in Stockholm, Sweden, was founded by Queen Lovisa Ulrika in 1753 as Kongl. Swenska Witterhets Academien, dedicated to advancing humanities, social sciences, and cultural heritage. Its origins trace back to early 18th-century Stockholm. Reconstituted by Gustav III in 1786, it adopted its current name, Kungl. Vitterhets Historie och Antikvitets Akademien, and expanded its objectives to include promoting research and preserving cultural heritage. The Academy collaborates internationally, inspired by institutions like the Royal Society of London, with its director playing a key role in its operations.

Fokou was selected in the seventh round by the Philadelphia Eagles as the 230th overall pick in the 2009 NFL Draft. He played all 16 games and won the starting strong-side linebacker job for the final four games. He ended the season with 39 tackles.

SAMOS 3, a classified United States Air Force reconnaissance satellite, launched on 9 September 1961 from Space Launch Complex 3W at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, aboard an Atlas LV-3 Agena B rocket. The mission ended in failure when the Atlas booster lost electrical power shortly after liftoff due to a late umbilical disconnection, which severed critical systems. The resulting power loss caused the Agena's Range Safety Officer (RSO) system to activate, destroying the vehicle. SAMOS 3 carried seven instruments, including a micrometeorite detector and a magnetometer, all of which were lost. Post-accident investigations led to procedural and design changes, including improved umbilical release mechanisms and modifications to the RSO system to prevent premature activation during similar scenarios.

The LM5 Tour received a four-star rating from *The Times* and was reviewed by Minnie Wright of the *Daily Express*. Wright described the tour as a "fiery showcase" of empowerment messaging, highlighting its consistent themes of unity and acceptance. She praised the band's authentic connection and identified their "divine four-part harmonies" as a standout feature, executed flawlessly in both soft and powerful numbers. Wright emphasized that these harmonies, alongside the tour's messaging, left no doubt about the exceptional talent underpinning Little Mix's success.

At the 1999 New South Wales election, Oldfield was elected to the New South Wales Legislative Council, where One Nation achieved 6.34% of the vote and narrowly missing out winning a second seat (contested by Brian Burston). This was the only seat in the New South Wales Parliament that the party secured. It was their first seat ever held in New South Wales.

Architect Albert O. Clark came from St. Louis, Missouri to Rogers, Arkansas in 1904. He utilized the Classic Revival style when designing the Applegate Drugstore and Bank of Rogers Building elsewhere in the county. Clark was hired to build many buildings in Bentonville, including the Benton County Jail and the county courthouse. His building replaced an Italianate style structure that had served the county administration needs since 1874.: 6, 18 The very first courthouse at Benton was a log building erected in 1837.

On 5 July 2012, at least 37 civilians were killed across Syria. In Idlib, 28 civilians died, including 11 in Khan Sheikhoun. Six civilians were killed in Damascus Countryside, while two died from an IED in As-Suwayda. Rebel casualties remain unreported.

By the mid-1950s, the Gibson Guitar Company had lost significant market share to Fender, whose Telecaster and Stratocaster models were dominating the electric guitar market with their modern designs and lighter builds. In response, Gibson sought to reclaim its position by creating three radical solid-body guitar concepts, collectively known as the "modernistic" series. On June 27, 1957, Gibson President Ted McCarty filed three designs with the United States Patent Office: the Flying V, the Explorer (initially called the Futura), and the Moderne. The design patents for these guitars were issued on January 7, 1958. However, the Moderne received an exceedingly poor reception at the 1957 NAMM show, leading Gibson to abandon its production plans. While the Flying V and Explorer eventually entered production, the Moderne remained shrouded in mystery, with no confirmed examples from the 1950s ever surfacing. In the early 1960s, Gibson engineer Rendall Wall reportedly played a Moderne prototype during a local performance of *Bye Bye Birdie*, but he returned it to the factory afterward, describing it as unappealing. The Moderne’s elusive nature and its association with Gibson’s bold attempt to innovate during this period have since elevated it to near-mythical status among collectors and enthusiasts.

After playing for the Trojans, Seto joined the coaching staff in 1999 as a volunteer assistant under then-head coach Paul Hackett, working with the defense and special teams. In 2000, he served as an administrative graduate assistant, and with the arrival of head coach Pete Carroll in 2001, he became a graduate assistant involved in the defense, working with the general defense in 2001 and safeties in 2002. In 2003, he became a full coach, in charge of safeties, and from 2004 to 2005 he coached linebackers. From 2006 to 2010, he coached the USC secondary. In 2008, former college teammate Kris Richard joined the staff as a graduate assistant.

On 23 March 2019, following the Battle of Baghuz Fawqani, Islamic State lost its final significant territory in Syria to the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) backed by the US and its coalition partners, including the UK. This was widely announced as the "defeat of ISIL" by the SDF and its allies. British Prime Minister Theresa May praised the courage of the British Armed Forces and its allies and stated: "The liberation of the last Daesh-held territory wouldn't have been possible without the immense courage of UK military and our allies". Major General Chris Ghika, Deputy Commander Strategy and Information of CJTF-OIR, stated that "Operation Shader would remain" as ISIL was not "leaderless or rudderless" despite its losses. He added that he could not predict how the losses would affect the terror threat posed by ISIL to the UK.

Dern utilized her actual surname and nearly a dozen pseudonyms during her career. The majority of her books were published under the names Peggy Gaddis and Peggy Dern. Frequently used pseudonyms included Gail Jordan, Perry Lindsay, and Joan Sherman. Other pen names included Carolina Lee, Georgia Craig, James Clayford, as well as Roberta Courtland, Joan Tucker, Sylvia Erskine, and Luther Gordon.

The regular branching patterns of xylans may facilitate their co-crystallization with cellulose in the plant cell wall. Xylan also tends to crystallize from aqueous solution. Additional polymorphs of (1→4)-β-D-xylan have been obtained by crystallization from non-aqueous environments.

"Got What It Takes?" is a singing talent competition featuring eight young singers aged 11-14. In each episode, three contestants are chosen to compete in a sing-off, with the winner decided by votes from their mums, who also act as judges. Each week, contestants learn about the music industry and life as a pop star, guided by celebrity mentors. The performer with the most sing-off wins secures a place in the final, joined by two additional performers voted for by the public, where they compete for the chance to perform at a live event.

Chris Gittens, drafted by the Yankees in 2014, credits his father, Gary, for motivating him with the promise, "I'm not coming to see you play until you make it to the big leagues." This inspired Gittens throughout his journey. His girlfriend, whose name is not mentioned, gave birth to their son in May 2021. Both of Gittens' parents attended his MLB debut on June 5, 2021, marking the first time his father saw him play in person.

Elizabeth Hely Walshe was born in Limerick in 1835 into a Protestant evangelical family; her father, a clergyman and member of the Royal Irish Academy, greatly influenced her religious upbringing. She had three sisters and spent part of her life in Canada before returning to Ireland by 1865. A skilled artist and writer, she contributed to evangelical magazines like *Leisure Hour* and *Sunday at Home*, collaborating with George Etell Sargent on children’s stories. Deeply committed to religious education, she believed in its transformative power. Her works often reflected themes of Irish emigration, influenced by historical events such as the Great Famine.

In October 2008, ten members of the Gooch gang were put on trial for crimes including murder, attempted murder, and firearms offences. Colin Joyce and Lee Amos, arrested in 2000 at their Moss Side stronghold during a gangland meeting, were found with firearms and sentenced to nine years in 2001. Released early on licence in 2007, their release triggered a surge in violence, including fatal shootings. Their subsequent re-arrest and conviction marked a significant reduction in gang-related shootings in Manchester.

Gospatric was the father of three sons, and several daughters. The sons Dolfin, Waltheof and Gospatric are named in De obsessione Dunelmi and by Symeon of Durham, while a document from about 1275 apparently prepared to instruct advocates in a land dispute reports that of these three sons, only Waltheof was born to a legitimate marriage and that he was full sibling of Gospatric's daughter Ethelreda. A second document with some "nearly identical" content prepared about the same time names three sisters of Waltheof of Allerdale, Gunnilda, Matilla and Ethreda, their husbands, as well as Ethreda's son William Fitz Duncan. Gospatric's children were:

The Security Branch (SB) of the South African Police was responsible for suppressing anti-apartheid activism through surveillance, torture, abductions, and assassinations. The Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) found that SB officers often interpreted directives from the State Security Council (SSC) as authorizations to kill activists, with methods including poisoning, roadblock abductions, and hit squads. Prominent victims included the Cradock Four, murdered on SSC orders, and Reverend Frank Chikane, who survived a 1989 assassination attempt involving poisoning. The attempt on Chikane’s life was orchestrated by SB operatives under the instruction of the SSC. Targets were frequently abducted or killed at roadblocks or in detention, with the SB operating with impunity during its most violent period under Johannes Van der Merwe’s leadership from 1986 to 1988. The TRC linked the SB to widespread atrocities, leaving hundreds of families bereaved.

Hummel attended Lakeridge High School in Lake Oswego, Oregon. He was a member of the Lake Oswego Little League team that advanced to the U.S. semifinals of the 2007 Little League World Series. He played three seasons of college baseball at the University of Portland.

Arjun Babuta won one bronze in ISSF Junior World Cup 2018(Sydney). He won one gold medal and one bronze medal in 10m Air Rifle Event at ISSF Junior World Cup 2016, Gabala. He won the silver in the at 10th Asian Airgun Championship, Wako City in Japan.

The New York Times Building is at 620 Eighth Avenue, occupying the eastern side of the avenue between 40th Street and 41st Street, one block west of Times Square in the Midtown Manhattan neighborhood of New York City. The land lot is rectangular and covers 79,000 sq ft (7,300 m2). It has a frontage of 197.5 ft (60.2 m) on Eighth Avenue to the west and 400 feet (120 m) on both 40th Street to the south and 41st Street to the north. The site takes up the western half of its city block, which is bounded by Seventh Avenue to the east. The topography of the site generally slopes down from east to west.

Friedmann took up gymnastics at age seven, she stated that: "personally, I have always loved gymnastics. Not everyone can reach a high level, it's very special". Adar's main ambition is to compete at the 2024 Olympic Games. Her idol is Israeli rhythmic gymnast Linoy Ashram.

In the 12th century, Henry St Clair received a grant of the lands of Herdmanston in East Lothian from Richard de Morville, the High Constable of Scotland. This marked the establishment of the Sinclair Lords of Herdmanston. However, it remains unclear whether the Sinclair Lords of Herdmanston shared a common origin with the Sinclair Barons of Roslin. According to *sinclairgenealogy.info*, the first proven Baron of Roslin was William St Clair, who died in 1297. William St Clair was appointed Sheriff of Haddington in East Lothian in 1264, a position that may have been tied to his home area, as suggested by the frequent use of the name William in the St Clair of Herdmanston family. Despite this, *clansinclairusa.org* asserts that William St Clair of Roslin was likely unrelated to the St Clairs of Herdmanston. Writing in 1900, Archibald Allan proposed that Henry St Clair of Herdmanston might have been a son of the first William de St Clair of Roslin, though this William’s existence cannot be proven by contemporary evidence. Roland Saint-Clair, however, emphasized that William St Clair, who died in 1297, was the first verifiable Baron of Roslin. The grant of Herdmanston to Henry St Clair in 1162, shortly after Hugh de Morville’s death, underscores the family’s early prominence in Scotland.

In 2018, Wells filed with the Federal Election Commission to run for President in the 2020 Democratic Party primary. He filed in person at the office of Secretary of State Bill Gardner to enter the New Hampshire Democratic primary on November 13, 2019. He then went on to get on the ballot in the Texas and Louisiana Democratic primaries.
Vijay Ghate was born in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, on October 18, 1964. He began learning tabla at the age of three in Jabalpur and later trained under Suresh Talwalkar in Mumbai.

The new Flying Squirrels logo was unveiled on December 1, 2009. It is a black, red, and grey flying squirrel with a patch in the shape of an "R" (for Richmond) on top of an acorn over its heart. The logo was designed by San Diego-based sports branding firm Brandiose. It was named the logo of the year by Ballpark Digest in 2010 and the best minor-league logo by Baseball America in 2015.

As a research fellow, Stephanie Zimmermann coordinated the integration of components for the ATLAS Muon Detector Control System, ensuring their seamless operation during data collection. Described as "a leading member of the ATLAS muon group" by her colleagues, she played a major role in the system's activities and later served as Muon Run Coordinator, overseeing critical detector operations.

Jared Cunningham attended San Leandro High School, where he averaged 20 points, 6 rebounds, and 3 assists per game in his senior year. He earned All-State honors for his outstanding performance during his final season.

On March 10, 2019, the Pittsburgh Steelers agreed to trade Antonio Brown to the Oakland Raiders in exchange for third- and fifth-round picks in the 2019 NFL Draft. The trade became official on March 13, 2019. Before this, the Buffalo Bills were reportedly close to a deal for Brown, but the trade fell through after Brown and other sources refuted the report, leading to its cancellation.

While Yugoslavia remained a monarchy, Communist elements in Yugoslavia retained some influence in the National Assembly (in December 1920, the government prohibited all Communist activities). Relations between Yugoslavian Communists and the officials of the Soviet Union were developed. Initial relations, however, remained tense. In 1937, for example, Stalin had the Secretary-General of the League of Communists of Yugoslavia, Milan Gorkić, murdered in Moscow during the Great Purge.

Greeny is renowned for its distinctive tone, characterized by a haunting, out-of-phase sound in the middle pickup position, which delivers a nasal, almost vocal-like quality. The guitar is equipped with custom "Greenybucker" pickups, with the neck pickup being a key factor in its unique sound. Early in his career, Peter Green sought to emulate Eric Clapton's tone but inadvertently created something entirely his own when he reinstalled the neck pickup backwards after it was rewound with heavy formvar wire, typically used in Stratocaster pickups. This, combined with a flipped alnico magnet, resulted in the iconic out-of-phase tone. Kirk Hammett, the guitar's current owner, describes Greeny as "one-of-a-kind," with a sound that is both bright and warm, capable of cutting through clean or overdriven amps. Despite its storied history, including a catastrophic accident that left its neck broken multiple times, Greeny remains a singular instrument celebrated for its tonal versatility and historical significance.

Fyter Fest (2020) was a professional wrestling event produced by All Elite Wrestling (AEW), featuring scripted matches where wrestlers portrayed heroes, villains, or nuanced characters in storylines. These narratives, designed to build tension and culminate in matches, were developed on AEW's weekly television programs, *Dynamite* and *Dark*. The event continued AEW's tradition of advancing storylines toward future events like Fight for the Fallen and All Out.

In September 2017, the president launched the Free Senior High School (SHS) policy, which will make secondary high school free for students in Ghana. The president states it is a "necessary investment in the nation's future workforce" and will help parents who are unable to pay for their children's education due to financial hardships. The program met with positive reaction from the nation, parents and students were excited and fervent, but private schools opposed to the program state it will decrease the number of students enrolling in their system.

On January 24, 2014, Jersey Jack Pinball announced that Pat Lawlor would be designing their third release and that the machine would feature an original theme. On October 13, 2016, "Dialed In!" was announced, which was the first pinball machine to feature Bluetooth connectivity and smartphone integration.

On November 15, 2011, opposition youth activists organized a protest in front of Baku's City Hall, chanting anti-government slogans. Police dispersed the protest, arresting several activists, who were later released.

Sky News and BBC News reported that the East London Mosque on Whitechapel Road in Whitechapel was surrounded by armed police, with residents instructed to stay indoors. The mosque was evacuated at around 3pm, searched by officers, and police later confirmed it was a bomb scare, giving the all-clear shortly before 5pm.

The club's origin has been traced to a cricket team that was founded c.1863 in Preston and was linked to the Wellfield Road School. At first, this team played on a strip of land called "The Marsh", on the Ribble Estuary at Ashton-on-Ribble, which is now a suburb in the western part of the town. In 1863, some team members proposed a move to Moor Park, where Preston Corporation had opened new playing fields. This caused a split but those in favour of the move went anyway and formed Preston North End Cricket Club, so called because Moor Park is on the north side of town. The first club president was George Howarth. On 3 August 1867, William Sudell, then aged sixteen, became a member.

Prior to 2007, Pennsylvania Route 830 began at the village of Allens Mills in Jefferson County, running through Washington and Pine Creek Townships. The route interchanged with Interstate 80 at Exit 86 and traveled southeast, making a right turn onto what is now SR 1830, passing the old entrance to the DuBois Regional Airport. PA 830 then made a sharp right curve near the present-day alignment, continuing to US 322. The state-maintained section between US 322 and Red Barn Road is designated SR 1830, while the portion of Red Barn Road between SR 1830 and modern PA 830 is SR 3011. The old PA 830 alignment between the PA 830/SR 1830 intersection and Allens Mills Road remains intact but is no longer state-maintained. This section was realigned in 1984 and later reactivated as part of PA 830 until its decommissioning in 2007.

The WindSingers, a prominent Hungarian a cappella group, aim to showcase their unique musical vision through diverse jazz and pop arrangements, including original compositions. Regular performers at the Budapest Jazz Club, a key venue for world-class jazz in Hungary, they have also toured extensively in countries like Serbia, Slovakia, Romania, and Austria. Notably, they earned silver diplomas in both pop and jazz categories at the vokal.total a cappella competition in Graz in 2014. That same year, supported by the Cseh Tamás Program, they released their debut album, featuring original songs. Their performances are celebrated for their humor, creativity, and vibrant energy, captivating audiences both locally and internationally.

The neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses (NCLs) are a group of inherited neurodegenerative disorders with pathological phenotypes that auto fluorescent lipopigments present in neurons and other cell types. Over the past two decades, accumulating evidences indicates that NCLs are caused by mutations in eight different genes, including genes encoding several soluble proteins (cathepsin D, PPT1, and TPP1). Mutations of gene TPP1 result in late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis which is associated with the failure to degrade specific neuropeptides and a subunit of ATP synthase in the lysosome. Mutations in the TPP1 gene lead to late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis, a fatal neurodegenerative disease of childhood. It has been demonstrated that a single injection of intravitreal implantation of autologous bone marrow derived stem cells transduced with a TPP1 expression construct at an early stage in the disease progression could substantially inhibit the development of disease-related retinal function deficits and structural changes. This result implies that ex vivo gene therapy using autologous stem cells may be an effective means of achieving sustained delivery of therapeutic compounds to tissues such as the retina for which systemic administration would be ineffective.

On April 22, 2007, Chuck Taylor entered the inaugural *Rey de Voladores* tournament, defeating PAC, Ricochet, and Retail Dragon in the semi-finals before overcoming Lince Dorado in the finals to win the tournament. Around this time, tensions arose within the Kings of Wrestling stable, leading to a split. Chris Hero, Larry Sweeney, and Claudio Castagnoli formed one faction of the Kings of Wrestling, while Team F.I.S.T. (Friends in Similar Tights), consisting of Chuck Taylor, Icarus, and Gran Akuma, became a separate team. At the *Aniversario!* event in May, the Kings of Wrestling and Team F.I.S.T. clashed in a six-man tag team match, which Team F.I.S.T. won. Despite this, the Kings of Wrestling reunited with Team F.I.S.T., solidifying their dominance as the top stable in Chikara and later recruiting Mitch Ryder, Max Boyer, and Shayne Hawke to their ranks. Riding the momentum of this success, Taylor went on to win the *Young Lions Cup V* in June 2007. He defeated Cabana Man Dan, Lince Dorado, MosCOW the Communist Bovine, Player Uno, Ice Cream, Jr., and Amigo Suzuki in the early rounds before defeating Arik Cannon in the semi-finals and his longtime rival Ricochet in the finals to claim the prestigious championship.

During his years at Los Alamos, Ulam was a visiting professor at Harvard from 1951 to 1952, MIT from 1956 to 1957, the University of California, San Diego, in 1963, and the University of Colorado at Boulder from 1961 to 1962 and 1965 to 1967. In 1967, the last of these positions became permanent, when Ulam was appointed Professor and Chairman of the Department of Mathematics at the University of Colorado. He kept a residence in Santa Fe, which made it convenient to spend summers at Los Alamos as a consultant. He was an elected member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, the United States National Academy of Sciences, and the American Philosophical Society.

The ear's exposed position and projection from the side of the head make it particularly vulnerable to avulsion injuries, with dog bites being the most common cause. Human bites can also lead to avulsions due to high-energy trauma. Partially avulsed ears may be reattached through meticulous multi-layer repair or staged techniques, while completely avulsed ears often require microvascular surgery, though its success rate is lower due to vessel damage. If reattachment is unfeasible, reconstruction using costal cartilage, implants, or external silicone prostheses is possible. Prostheses, crafted by specialists, offer a customizable solution but require replacement every two years due to wear.

On January 28, 1905, the Japanese Cabinet decided to incorporate Liancourt Rocks (Takeshima) into Japanese territory, asserting the islets were *terra nullius*—land belonging to no one. The decision was based on the claim that no evidence existed of prior occupation by another country. The government cited records of Nakai Yozaburo, a Japanese fisherman, who reportedly moved to the islets in 1903 and engaged in seal hunting there. The Cabinet stated this constituted occupation under international law. However, critics argue the incorporation process lacked transparency, as the announcement was limited to a small local newspaper notice, contrasting Japan's more formal claims to other territories.

After Trump was elected, Sanders was named to the position of deputy White House press secretary in his new administration. On May 5, 2017, she held her first White House press briefing, standing in for Press Secretary Sean Spicer, who was serving on Naval Reserve duty. She continued to cover for Spicer until his return to the podium on May 12. She stood in for Spicer during the dismissal of James Comey and the controversy following it. Her defense of the Trump administration's actions led to some speculation that Trump was considering promoting her to replace Spicer. This was denied at the time by her father. But on May 26, The Wall Street Journal again suggested that Sanders was being considered as a possible replacement for Spicer, in the context of wider staff changes and the investigation into alleged communications with Russia. She continued to fill in for Spicer occasionally.

Aleutian terns breed in colonies, and are site-faithful if their habitat is sufficiently stable. Pairs form on the breeding area, shortly after arrival. The pairs build the nest during the last half of May and first half of June, shortly before egg-laying. The typical clutch size is 2 eggs (occasionally 1 or 3). The nest is a shallow depression usually built on low vegetation such as mosses, lichens, field horsetail, cottongrass, hairgrass or coastal bluegrass. Both parents incubate the eggs and feed the chicks, although the female does more incubating and less fishing than her partner. Aleutian terns are reported to spend less time brooding chicks than do Arctic terns; consequently, Aleutian tern mortality rate is higher during the chick stage.

After visiting Moscow (where he produced no fewer than eight poetic pieces of invective aimed at Benckendorff), on 9 May 1841, Lermontov arrived to Stavropol, introduced himself to general Grabbe and asked for permission to stay in the town. Then, on a whim, he changed his course, found himself in Pyatigorsk and sent his seniors a letter informing them of his having fallen ill. The regiment's special commission recommended him treatment at Mineralnye Vody. What he did instead was embark upon the several weeks' spree. "In the mornings he was writing, but the more he worked, the more need he felt to unwind in the evenings," Skabichevsky wrote. "I feel I'm left with very little of my life," the poet confessed to his friend A. Merinsky on 8 July, a week before his death.

The calendar is a lunisolar calendar which adopted Teiki-hō, where solar terms are equally divided by solar longitude, in place of Heiki-hō, in which they are equally divided by time. The lunar month starts on a day with a new moon. A leap month is added when there are three lunar months between a lunar month which includes a solstice/equinox and the following lunar month which includes a solstice/equinox. In that case the leap month is the lunar month which does not include any chūki 中気 (one of the twelve solar terms that are used to determine the months of the year). The months which include a solstice/equinox are fixed as the second, fifth, eighth and eleventh months respectively. The time of the day used in the calendar to determine the dates of solar terms and lunar phases is that observed at Kyoto.

As of the census of 2010, there were 134 people, 65 households, and 35 families living in the village. The population density was 6,700.0 inhabitants per square mile (2,586.9/km2). There were 69 housing units at an average density of 3,450.0 per square mile (1,332.1/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 99.3% White and 0.7% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.7% of the population.

The final step in the Olinalá painting process involves intricate decoration using soft brushes, often handmade from turkey feathers with a fine hair inserted at the tip for precision. These brushes allow artisans to paint elaborate motifs, often inspired by nature, religion, or history, covering the entire surface of the box or furniture. Natural powder pigments, mixed with a binding medium, are used to create vibrant colors. Common motifs include floral patterns and scenes from local traditions, while geometric and abstract shapes are also prevalent. The bold, directional brushwork and clean contrasts between light and dark or warm and cool tones give the pieces their distinctive visual clarity and artistic appeal.

As of the census of 2000, there were 2,605 people, 1,001 households, and 783 families residing in the CDP. The population density was 1,373.2 inhabitants per square mile (530.2/km2). There were 1,038 housing units at an average density of 547.2 per square mile (211.3/km2). The racial makeup of the CDP was 98.27% White, 0.84% African American, 0.35% Asian, 0.27% from other races, and 0.27% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 1.07% of the population.

Clayton, Missouri, is served by major arterial routes including Forsyth Boulevard, which transitions to Olympian Way between North Clayton and University City. Originally a trolley route, Forsyth Boulevard was part of the 1904 Olympic marathon course. Other primary routes include South Brentwood, Manchester, and Ballas Roads. Wydown Boulevard, recognized as a Great Street by the American Planning Association in 2010, features historic neighborhoods, bike lanes, and remnants of its streetcar past, exemplifying Clayton's blend of functionality and historic charm.

Hildegard was one of the first persons for whom the Roman canonization process was officially applied, but the process took so long that four attempts at canonization were not completed and she remained at the level of her beatification. Her name was nonetheless taken up in the Roman Martyrology at the end of the 16th century. Her feast is 17 September. Numerous popes have referred to Hildegard as a saint, including Pope John Paul II and Pope Benedict XVI. Hildegard's parish and pilgrimage church in Eibingen near Rüdesheim houses her relics.

Correa lawrenceana var. genoensis is a shrub that typically grows to a height of 2 m (6 ft 7 in) and has egg-shaped leaves 24–65 mm (0.94–2.56 in) long, 7–34 mm (0.28–1.34 in) wide and more or less glabrous on the lower surface. The flowers are usually borne singly, sometimes in groups of up to seven, in leaf axils on stalks 7–25 mm (0.28–0.98 in) long with thread-like bracteoles. The calyx is urn-shaped, 9–12 mm (0.35–0.47 in) long and glabrous, and the corolla is narrow cylindrical, 17–25 mm (0.67–0.98 in) long and yellowish green. Flowering mostly occurs in spring.

Lambert signed his first professional cycling contract with Falcon cycling team in 1972 under Albert Hitchen the Falcon team manager. His first win as a pro was on Stage Two of the Tour of the West, into Penzance in Cornwall. He rode the 1972 UCI Road World Championships which took place on the 6 August 1972 in Gap, France but punctured twice and did not finish. Lambert signed with the Holdsworth cycling team for 1974 alongside Les West and Colin Lewis and won the British National Road Race Championships for the first time that year. He raced and finished Paris-Roubaix in 1977 before signing for Viking in 1978. At the 1979 World Championship at Valkenburg aan de Geul Lambert finished in 26th place of just 44 riders finishing including no other British finishers. In 1980 Lambert signed with Weinmann and finished third in the Druivenkoers Overijse behind Fons De Wolf and Rudy Pevenage and Lambert pipped Bill Nickson to the 1980 National Road Championship by an inch for his second British champion title in Redditch. Lambert joined Falcon in 1981 and then spent six seasons there. He rode the 1982 World Championship at Goodwood and in 1983 Lambert won the National Criterium Championships. In total Lambert won 64 professional races between 1972 and 1987.

AllMusic reviewer Alex Henderson stated: "Lightnin' is among the rewarding acoustic dates Lightnin' Hopkins delivered in the early '60s. The session has an informal, relaxed quality, and this approach serves a 48-year-old Hopkins impressively well ... Lightnin' is a lot like being in a small club with Hopkins as he shares his experiences, insights and humor with you". The Penguin Guide to Blues Recordings awarded the album 3 stars, noting that "Lightnin's performances are unfailingly fluent, perhaps because he doesn't challenge himself: almost all the songs on Lightnin' are well-tried pieces from his core repertoire".

Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again was announced on May 19, 2017, with a release date of July 20, 2018. It was written and directed by Ol Parker. On September 27, 2017, Benny Andersson confirmed 3 ABBA songs that would be featured in the film: "When I Kissed the Teacher," "I Wonder (Departure)," and "Angeleyes." "I Wonder (Departure)" was cut from the film, but is included on the soundtrack album.

Homophyllia bowerbanki is a large polyp stony (LPS) coral with thick-walled, circular corallites that can reach a maximum diameter of over 15 mm. The corallites are irregularly shaped, often arranged individually or in short valleys with continuous walls containing multiple corallites. Commonly found in vibrant colors like neon green, its fleshy tissue covers the skeleton, and when polyps retract, the coral's intricate skeletal structure becomes visible.

In 2007, Coombs accepted the offer from Brian Kelly to join his staff at the University of Cincinnati as the team's defensive backs coach. The Bearcats led the nation with 26 interceptions in 2007. In 2009, Coombs was promoted to associate head coach in addition to his responsibilities as the team's defensive backs coach and special teams coordinator.

The Allmusic review by Al Campbell awarded the album 3 stars and stated "Considering the extraordinary talent assembled for Tony Williams' second Blue Note date as a leader, this could have been a landmark session. Unfortunately, it's not. Spring isn't totally forgettable; on the contrary... However, the five Tony Williams compositions... often failed to provoke the musicians into reaching crucial unity, making Spring haphazard, falling short of the expected goal".

Turkish Airlines pulled its Airbus A340 aircraft from scheduled flights in May 2016. From July 2016 onwards, TC-JDM and other A340 aircraft in the Turkish Airlines fleet were used on charter flights to carry passengers to Hajj. The aircraft was reconfigured to have an all-economy class layout, without any business class seats. During the winter period of 2016, a heavy downfall in traffic caused the airline to temporarily ground dozens of aircraft, including Deli Mike. In August 2017, Hürriyet reported that Turkish Airlines expected to maintain its Airbus A340 aircraft and use them for the Hajj flights until 2021. Deli Mike was the oldest aircraft of the Turkish Airlines fleet as of September 2017.

Willie Wood went undrafted in 1960 after shoulder injuries at USC and wrote letters to NFL teams requesting a tryout. Vince Lombardi of the Green Bay Packers responded, signing him as a rookie free agent. Initially trying out as a defensive back, Wood became a premier safety, starting from 1960 until his retirement in 1971, earning eight Pro Bowl selections and five first-team All-Pro honors.
Courtney Wetzel attended Oregon State University from 2007 to 2010, earning 81 caps and scoring 7 goals for the Beavers' soccer team; she did not play basketball.

Green imperialism is sometimes described as a combination of ecological and neoliberal imperialism, with eco-imperialism perceived to result in the commodification of Earth's resources. This tendency, known as "forgetting nature," reflects neoliberal policies extending market logic to environmental goals.

The packaged terminal air conditioner (PTAC), through-the-wall, and window air conditioners are similar. PTAC systems may be adapted to provide heating in cold weather, either directly by using an electric strip, gas, or other heaters, or by reversing the refrigerant flow to heat the interior and draw heat from the exterior air, converting the air conditioner into a heat pump. They may be installed in a wall opening with the help of a special sleeve on the wall and a custom grill that is flush with the wall and window air conditioners can also be installed in a window, but without a custom grill.

Building officials, often serving as chief building officials in some jurisdictions, are formally recognized as those responsible for enforcing building codes. They typically possess at least five years of experience in construction-related fields, often holding degrees in architecture or engineering. Many are licensed as inspectors or subcode officials in areas like building, plumbing, or fire protection. Certification is commonly obtained through organizations like the International Code Council (ICC) or the National Fire Protection Association (NFPA).

Histamine, a compound found in many foods and drinks, is particularly abundant in fermented products like red wine, which contains 20-200% more histamine than white wine. Individuals deficient in the enzyme diamine oxidase (DAO) may struggle to break down histamine, leading to symptoms. Experts suggest alcohol can elevate plasma histamine levels, exacerbating reactions. A study of 16 individuals with wine intolerance found no difference in reactions to low- and high-histamine wines. Additionally, substances like tannins in red wine may mimic histamine's effects, contributing to symptoms. Both red and white wines were compared in histamine content studies.

El Salvador counts nearly 360 rivers that connect to form ten hydrographic regions. There are four primary lakes in El Salvador including the Llopango (70 km2), Guija (44 km2), Coatepeque (24.8 km2), , Olomega ( 24.2 km2) and four reservoirs created by hydroelectric dams discussed in more detail below. El Salvador also obtains about 7.5 km3 of surface water per year from neighboring Honduras and Guatemala. The Cerrón Grande Reservoir, known locally as Lake Suchitlán, is the largest body of fresh water in El Salvador.

Advice has traditionally been defined as a recommendation to take action in fields like finance, law, and healthcare. For instance, a financial planner might advise a client on investing in ethical funds or ISAs based on their personal circumstances. However, Dalal and Bonaccio (2010) argued this definition is incomplete, as it often excludes emotional and cognitive guidance. Drawing on social science research, they proposed a broader definition encompassing suggestions for behavior, feelings, and thought processes. They highlighted that advice is not limited to action-oriented recommendations but also includes support that influences decision-making and coping, which is often overlooked in traditional definitions.

Dr. Karel Freja retired on 1 January 1935 but passed away at the age of 59 on 30 April 1937 in the General Hospital in Prague due to cirrhosis of the liver. Since the early 1980s, his remains have been located in the scattered meadow at the Malvazinky Cemetery in Smíchov.

In 1968, Governor Walter J. Hickel opened Alaska’s North Slope to oil extraction and initiated the construction of a 400-mile winter road from Livengood to Prudhoe Bay to transport equipment. Construction began in November 1968, and the road, later named the Walter J. Hickel Highway, was completed by March 1969. However, poor engineering exposed the permafrost, turning the road into a muddy trench by spring. Abandoned by April 1969, it was never maintained due to its environmental damage and economic inefficiency.

By the mid-1950s Gibson Guitar Company had lost considerable market share to rival Fender's Telecaster and Stratocaster models and created three modernistic solid-body guitar concepts in an attempt to strike back. On June 27, 1957, Gibson President Ted McCarty filed three designs with the United States Patent Office which would come to be called "Flying V", "Explorer", and (unofficially, by later historians) "Moderne". The design patents were issued on January 7, 1958; however, after getting an exceedingly poor reception at NAMM 1957, Gibson decided not to produce the Moderne. There is only a vague mention of "Mod. Guitars" on a shipping list, which some argue applies to Explorers. Gibson engineer Rendall Wall (and later co-founder of Heritage Guitars) played it in the early 1960s for a local performance of Bye Bye Birdie. Wall recounted that he got it out of Gibson's "morgue", and that he could have bought it for $50: it was so ugly that nobody wanted it. He returned it after the show, saying he was more a country player than a rock and roll player.

In 2009, D-Black began his career as an independent artist in the group D-Black & Kwaku T releasing a group album ‘Target Practice’. In 2010 D-Black and his rap partner both went solo. He went on to release three albums (Music, Love and Life, The Revelation, Lightwork’ 1 E.P (Smoke and Mirrors) and 1 mix-tape (Hunger & Thirst) over the years. He has been nominated for numerous awards and won several, including the 2011 BET Awards.

Nordhoff High School athletic teams are nicknamed the Rangers. The school is a charter member of the Citrus Coast League, a conference within the CIF Southern Section that was established in 2018. Prior to that, Nordhoff was part of the Tri-County Athletic Association.

The qualification period for Athletics was 1 January 2003 to 9 August 2004. For the men's triple jump, each National Olympic Committee was permitted to enter up to three athletes that had jumped 16.95 metres or further during the qualification period. The maximum number of athletes per nation had been set at 3 since the 1930 Olympic Congress. If an NOC had no athletes that qualified under that standard, one athlete that had jumped 16.55 metres or further could be entered.

At the age of 28, Canibus enlisted in the U.S. Army, seeking a new direction beyond his achievements in his teens and twenties. In 2004, he was caught smoking cannabis, a substance prohibited under military regulations. As a result, he was discharged from service, marking a significant turning point in his life and career.

In 1816, the citizens of the village of San Cristóbal de Alvarado sought the designation of Town from Ferdinand VII, King of Spain. The King granted the village the title of Town on 8 September 1816 by Royal Decree, which had a cost of 92,800 maravedís. Almost two years later, on 5 September 1818 the same Ferdinand VII authorized the first Honorable Ayuntamiento or city council. On 3 October 1818, the first City Council was established with Tomás Hondal serving as first Mayor.

Duck Game was developed by Vancouver-based developer Landon Podbielski. Podbielski originally planned to make a 2D platformer inspired by James Pond 3. A friend suggested adding a local multiplayer mode with guns and other weapons. The game was released for the Ouya on May 13, 2014. At launch, the game only featured a multiplayer mode, however in November 2014, single-player modes were added.

The Polish American Scholarship Committee was established in 1923 by Dr. Stephen Mizwa to bring students to universities in the United States. Mizwa worked with the president of Vassar College, Henry Noble MacCracken, who had visited Poland. The two expanded the Scholarship Committee's mission to promote cultural and educational exchanges between the United States and Poland. In December 1925, the Scholarship Committee changed into the new Kosciuszko Foundation.

The Cayuga’s Waiters performed their first annual "Spring Fever" concert in 1991, continuing the tradition annually until 2017. Described by *The Cornell Daily Sun* as "taking [...] A Cappella to another level," the concert became a hallmark of their performances. In the 1990s, they introduced a December Holiday Show, performed annually in December throughout the 2000s, further solidifying their presence in the collegiate a cappella scene.

Yi earned bachelor's and master's degrees from Sichuan University, China, in 1986 and 1989. She moved to Canada and continued her studies with another master's degree from York University in 1996, and a Ph.D. from the University of Toronto in 2000 under the supervision of Donald A. S. Fraser. She came to Waterloo as a postdoctoral researcher, and then continued there as an assistant professor in 2001.

Broad is the author or co-author of eight books, including Germs: Biological Weapons and America's Secret War (Simon & Schuster, 2001) which was a number-one New York Times Best Seller. His other titles include Betrayers of the Truth: Fraud and Deceit in the Halls of Science (co-written with Nicholas Wade, Simon & Schuster, 1982), Teller's War: The Top-Secret Story Behind the Star Wars Deception (Simon & Schuster, 1992), The Universe Below: Discovering the Secrets of the Deep Sea (Simon & Schuster, 1997), and The Science of Yoga: The Risks and the Rewards (Simon & Schuster, 2012). His books have been translated into more than a dozen languages. His work focuses on the social repercussions of science.

The 5 Year MD Program at All Saints University School of Medicine is designed for recent high school graduates. It includes four semesters of premedical courses covering foundational sciences, followed by the Basic Medical Science phase of the 4-Year MD Program, allowing seamless progression into advanced medical education.

Two torch designs were shortlisted in September 2009 for the 2010 Asian Games. A design named "The Tide" was chosen over one named "Exploit" by the organizers as the torch of the Games. "The Tide" weighs 98 g and is 70 cm long, and is tall and straight in shape, while dynamic in terms of image.

Edward Thomas Ryan, M.D., a leading expert in infectious diseases, has significantly advanced the understanding of host-pathogen interactions and immune responses during cholera, collaborating with researchers such as Islam K., Hossain M., and Charles R.C. Cholera, an acute secretory diarrheal infection caused by toxigenic *Vibrio cholerae*, is transmitted via the fecal-oral route and remains a major global health challenge. Dr. Ryan’s work, supported by organizations like the World Health Organization (WHO), has focused on the immune response to cholera, particularly the role of antibodies targeting the O-specific polysaccharide (OSP) of *V. cholerae* lipopolysaccharide (LPS). His research demonstrated that OSP-specific antibodies correlate with protection against cholera, highlighting the serogroup-specific nature of immunity. Collaborating with experts such as Mayo Smith L.M. and Bhuiyan T.R., Dr. Ryan has analyzed immune responses to OSP and contributed to the development of innovative cholera vaccines, including synthetic neoglycoconjugates. His findings have shown that vibriocidal antibodies, which largely target OSP, play a critical role in cholera protection. For his groundbreaking contributions, Dr. Ryan has been recognized with prestigious awards, including the NIH MERIT Award, underscoring his pivotal role in advancing cholera research and vaccine development.

There was wide media coverage in response to Tang's work on using classical computing rather than quantum computing to tackle the recommendation problem, due to the perception that it eliminates one of the best examples of quantum speedup. Some researchers came to the defence of quantum computing, such as Robert Young (Director of the University of Lancaster's Quantum Technology Centre), who said in a BBC news article, "If we hadn't invested in quantum computing, the quantum algorithm that inspired [Ms] Tang wouldn't have existed". Tang herself notes the divisive nature of comparing classical to quantum algorithms, and the trepidation of proving her algorithm to her adviser, "I started believing there is a fast classical algorithm, but I couldn’t really prove it to myself because Scott [Aaronson] seemed to think there wasn’t one, and he was the authority".

In colonial times, the land that became Belmont was farmland, much like the rest of western Bronx. It was primarily owned by the Lorillard family, for whom a street is named. After moving its tobacco operations from Lower Manhattan to the central Bronx in the late 18th century, the family greatly expanded its property in the area, which became known as the Belmont Estate. When the Lorillards moved to New Jersey in 1870, the city acquired part of their land for Bronx Park (including what is now the Bronx Zoo and New York Botanical Garden). Another part was divided into the streets that form Belmont today.

In August 2012, Uganda and Israel commemorated the 1976 Entebbe raid with a solemn ceremony at the base of a tower at the Old Entebbe Airport, where Yonatan Netanyahu, the commander of the elite Israeli commando unit, was killed during the operation. The ceremony, attended by Ugandan State Minister for Animal Industry Bright Rwamirama and Israeli Deputy Foreign Affairs Minister Daniel Ayalon, featured the laying of wreaths, a moment of silence, speeches, and a poem. The flags of Uganda and Israel were flown side by side, symbolizing the strong bilateral relations between the two nations. During the event, both countries renewed their commitment to fighting terrorism and working towards humanity. Forty years after the raid, in 2016, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, Yonatan Netanyahu’s brother, visited Entebbe to mark the anniversary of the rescue operation. His visit aimed to honor the legacy of the raid and strengthen Israel’s relations with sub-Saharan African nations.

Feature search (also known as "disjunctive" or "efficient" search) is a visual search process that focuses on identifying a previously requested target amongst distractors that differ from the target by a unique visual feature such as color, shape, orientation, or size. An example of a feature search task is asking a participant to identify a white square (target) surrounded by black squares (distractors). In this type of visual search, the distractors are characterized by the same visual features. The efficiency of feature search in regards to reaction time(RT) and accuracy depends on the "pop out" effect, bottom-up processing, and parallel processing. However, the efficiency of feature search is unaffected by the number of distractors present.

In Week 5, Vanderbilt faced Michigan, led by head coach Fielding H. Yost, in Ann Arbor. Commodores coach Dan McGugin, Yost’s brother-in-law, saw his team fall 18-0. Between 1905 and 1923, Michigan and Vanderbilt met nine times, with Michigan winning eight and tying once.

On September 16, 1910, Nara Tramway Co., Ltd. (奈良軌道株式会社, Nara Kidō) was founded and renamed Osaka Electric Tramway Co., Ltd. (大阪電気軌道株式会社, Ōsaka Denki Kidō, Daiki (大軌)) a month after. Osaka Electric Tramway completed Ikoma Tunnel and started operating a line between Osaka and Nara (present-day Nara Line) on April 30, 1914. The modern Kashihara, Osaka, and Shigi lines were completed in the 1920s, followed by the Kyoto Line (a cooperative venture with Keihan Electric Railway). Daiki founded Sangu Electric Railway Co., Ltd. (参宮急行電鉄株式会社, Sangū Kyūkō Dentetsu, Sankyū (参急)) in 1927, which consolidated Ise Electric Railway Co., Ltd. (伊勢電気鉄道株式会社, Ise Denki Tetsudō, Iseden (伊勢電)) on September 15, 1936.

Molecular solids are composed of neutral atoms or molecules held together by weak intermolecular forces, such as London dispersion forces, dipole-dipole interactions, or hydrogen bonds. The crystal lattice structure depends on the efficient packing of these molecules, influenced by their symmetry. Intermolecular interactions, which are electrostatic in nature, dictate the unique physical properties of molecular solids, including melting points.

Bandy, in its informal form, faded in North America as elements of the game were absorbed into the development of ice hockey. While ice hockey was growing and organizing in Canada and the United States during the 19th century, bandy was formalizing in Europe, particularly in the fens of England. Bandy arrived in its organized format in the United States much later, gaining recognition in the late 20th century.

The University of Padua, one of the oldest in the world, was founded in 1222. Renowned for academic excellence, it had 65,000 students in 2003. OECD studies highlight Veneto's population as a key contributor to North-Eastern Italy's strong school education achievements.

The company was founded by Konstantin Othmer and is a spin-off from his Core Mobility company. Through its MSIP its software "aggregates, analyzes, and delivers data to wireless carriers and device manufacturers. This information proves a valuable resource for these businesses to understand the quality of service their customers experience."
Digambar Ani Akhada, also called Bairagi Akhadas, comprises three akhadas devoted to Vaishnavism and Lord Vishnu.

Just two years after the first coal was discovered in the United States in 1750 explorer Thomas Walker discovered coal in what would become Kentucky and used it to heat his camp fire. Although his discovery came in the Eastern Coalfield it would be another 150 years before commercial coal production occurred there. In 1820 the first commercial coal mine in Kentucky opened in the Western Coalfield in Muhlenberg County. In its first year the mine produced 328 tons of coal. By 1843 the state produced 100,000 tons of coal, and by 1879 the state produced one million tons of coal, all coming from the Western Coalfield.

The old slang's vocabulary and form of speech had some variation in between different parts of the town. As noted above, the language was born north of the Pitkäsilta bridge, but it later spread to the southern neighborhoods as well, including Punavuori (Rööperi in slang). The variation was most prominent in between the slang spoken on the two sides of the bridge.

On January 4, 2021, Neftalí Feliz signed a minor league contract with the Philadelphia Phillies and was assigned to the Triple-A Lehigh Valley IronPigs to begin the 2021 season. In 15 games with Lehigh Valley, he recorded a 1-2 record and a 4.74 ERA. Feliz was selected to the Phillies' active roster on June 25, 2021, but after posting a 27.00 ERA in 2 appearances, he was designated for assignment on July 1 and elected free agency on July 3, 2021.

Hughes seems to have come across the phenomenon of radio waves nine years before they were proven to exist by Heinrich Hertz in 1888. In 1879 while working in London Hughes discovered that a bad contact in a Bell telephone he was using in his experiments seemed to be sparking when he worked on a nearby induction balance. He developed an improved detector to pick up this unknown "extra current" based on his new microphone design and developed a way to interrupt his induction balance via a clockwork mechanism to produce a series of sparks. By trial and error experiments he eventually found he could pick up these "aerial waves" as he carried his telephone device down the street out to a range of 500 yards (460 m).

As a result of the State of Rheinland-Pfalz administrative reform, Ramstein-Miesenbach, which has a population of approx. 9,200, was created on 7 June 1969 from the independent villages of Ramstein and Miesenbach. City designation was awarded in 1991. Ramstein-Miesenbach is the administrative center of the Verbandsgemeinde ("collective municipality") of Ramstein-Miesenbach which, with its approx. 19,100 inhabitants, is the largest Verbandsgemeinde in Kaiserslautern district.

Eureka Township, Sac County, Iowa, is represented on the Sac County Board of Supervisors by local officials. In the Iowa Senate, Republican Craig Williams represents District 6. In the Iowa House, Republican Gary Worthan represented District 11 until 2023. At the federal level, Republican Randy Feenstra represents Iowa's 4th Congressional District.

The *New York Times* review of *House of Usher*, published on September 15, 1960, was written by Eugene Archer. Archer noted that while American-International intended to faithfully adapt Edgar Allan Poe’s macabre tale, they ignored Poe’s distinctive prose style, resulting in a flawed execution.
