He remarked that Vincent Price and Myrna Fahey should not be faulted for their arch portrayals of the decadent Ushers, nor Mark Damon for the traces of Brooklynese in his performance as the “mystified interloper.” Archer described Damon’s role as a “knight in shining armor” and commended his satisfying portrayal, while also acknowledging Fahey’s fitting performance as the “damsel in distress,” complete with fainting and hysteria.

By the end of the day LCC reports that 53 people were killed, including 26 from shelling in Homs. SOHR reported that a total of 41 people were killed across the country. At least 16 soldiers were killed across the country.

The C major finale, the longest of the three movements, consists of a theme and four "improvisations" – individual episodes loosely based on elements of the theme – followed by an extended coda. It reverts to the mood of the first movement, with an elaborate theme for the cello in its high register over a pizzicato in the orchestral strings. The theme presents rising and falling patterns in its opening bars, followed by a series of descending triads and a pair of rising scales.

Turkish Airlines pulled its Airbus A340 aircraft from scheduled flights in May 2016, and from July 2016, TC-JDM and other A340s were used on charter flights for Hajj, reconfigured to an all-economy class layout. During the winter of 2016, a significant drop in traffic led the airline to temporarily ground numerous aircraft, including Deli Mike. In August 2017, *Hürriyet* reported that Turkish Airlines planned to maintain its A340s for Hajj operations until 2021 due to effective maintenance. As of September 2017, Deli Mike was the oldest aircraft in the Turkish Airlines fleet, continuing to operate despite recurring technical issues, including problems with its landing gear.

In 1956 Giovanna Caleffi relocated to Nervi, which by this time had been subsumed into the long coastal Genoa conurbation. During the resistance years Nervi had been the operational base of a force of anarchist partisans which had included Lorenzo Parodi, and it was to Nervi that she had just transferred the management of "Volontà" in order to facilitate national distribution. The publication was in a period of crisis, since the personal break-up between Caleffi and Zaccaria had coincided with the latters' withdrawal from the anarchist movement. There are suggestions that his somewhat cerebral quasi-liberal approach had always presented a slightly awkward fit with the more passionate radicalism of Italian anarchism.

At the 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi, Russia, Leigh Diffey made his Olympic debut as the play-by-play announcer for NBC, covering luge, skeleton, and bobsled events at the Sanki Sliding Center. NBC executives, impressed by Diffey’s motorsports commentary, saw potential in his ability to transition to Olympic sports, with one executive expressing interest in expanding his role beyond motorsports. To prepare for the Games, Diffey hosted various events, including Formula One and IndyCar races, showcasing his versatility as a broadcaster. Reflecting on his Olympic opportunity, Diffey described it as a career milestone, stating that it was an honor to bring his expertise to such a global stage. He continued his Olympic journey at the 2016 Summer Games in Rio de Janeiro, where he served as NBC’s lead track and field commentator, and at the 2018 Winter Games in PyeongChang, where he reprised his role covering bobsled, luge, and skeleton.

As of the census of 2000, there were 852 people, 333 households, and 229 families residing in the city. The population density was 1,325.6 people per square mile (514.0/km2). There were 383 housing units at an average density of 595.9 per square mile (231.1/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 57.39% White, 41.55% African American, 0.12% from other races, and 0.94% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.82% of the population.

Robert Eversz is best known for his Nina Zero series of crime novels, which have been translated into ten languages. The series follows Nina Zero, a rebellious and sharp-witted protagonist navigating the gritty streets of Los Angeles. The first novel, *Shooting Elvis* (1995), was named Crime Novel of the Year in 1997 by Norway's *Aftenposten* and Best Comic Novel of 1996 by *The Guardian*. The second installment, *Killing Paparazzi* (2001), received critical acclaim, including a rave review from *The Washington Post Book World*. Eversz’s work is celebrated for its blend of noir, humor, and cultural commentary.

Ramstein-Miesenbach was established on June 7, 1969, during the State of Rheinland-Pfalz administrative reform, by merging the independent villages of Ramstein and Miesenbach. With a population of approximately 9,200, it was designated a city in 1991. Serving as the administrative center of the Verbandsgemeinde Ramstein-Miesenbach, which has around 19,100 residents, it is the largest Verbandsgemeinde in the Kaiserslautern district.

Robert Burns, often celebrated as Scotland’s national bard, became known as a "people’s poet" in Russia, where his works resonated deeply with both Imperial and Soviet audiences. In Imperial Russia, a society starkly divided between the aristocracy and the largely illiterate peasantry, Burns symbolized hope and inspiration for ordinary people, embodying ideals of equality and defiance against social hierarchies. His poetry, first translated into Russian in the 19th century, gained immense popularity, and a 1924 translation sold over 600,000 copies, cementing his status as a literary icon. In Soviet Russia, Burns was embraced as a champion of the common man, with his radical ethos and critique of aristocratic pretensions aligning with Soviet ideals. The Soviet Union commemorated him with a postage stamp in 1956, the first country to do so, further solidifying his cultural significance. Burns’ works were taught in schools alongside Russian national poets, and his influence extended to Soviet composers, who found in his poetry a subtle means of expressing non-conformist ideas. Even in post-communist Russia, Burns’ reputation endured, reflecting his universal appeal as a voice for the marginalized and a symbol of resistance against societal inequities.

Unrest resurfaced in the Sultanate of Oman towards the end of 2018. In december, thousands of protesters rallied against economic hardship and once again requested more job opportunities. Protests reached the cities of Muscat, Sohar, Sur and Salalah and were composed mostly of recent graduates and unemployed youth. Within certain professions, strikes were reported throughout the country.

Patch 2.6.0 introduced a number of new features, including the "Rise of the Necromancer" pack announced at BlizzCon, as well as 30 new legendary items and four armor sets for the Necromancer class. An additional game mode, "Challenge Rifts", was also introduced. The Challenge Rifts mode takes a "snapshot" of a previously-completed Greater Rift, including the character, armor, and talent build used in that Greater Rift, as well as the enemies and powerups that appeared within it; if the player is able to beat the previously set completion time, they will receive a reward cache (similar to completing bounties). New zones were also added to certain maps in Adventure Mode: The Shrouded Moors and the Temple of the Firstborn near Caldeum (Act II), centered around a strange "blood cult", and the Realms of Fate in the High Heavens (Act IV), consequences of the battle between the Nephalem (player characters) and Diablo in the campaign story in Diablo III.

Daedra Charles was invited to compete in women's basketball at the 1992 Olympics, held in Barcelona, Spain. The USA team won three games before facing the Unified Team in the semifinals, where they lost 79-73. For the bronze medal, the USA faced Cuba, with the score tied 44-44 at halftime. The USA ultimately secured an 88-74 victory. Charles, a key player, averaged 6.2 points per game during the tournament, helping the team to a 4-1 record and a bronze medal finish.

As of the 2010 census, Coatesville, Indiana, had a population of 523, with 198 households and 144 families. The population density was 804.6 inhabitants per square mile, and there were 213 housing units at an average density of 327.7 per square mile. The racial makeup of the town was 98.1% White, and 1.3% of the population identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

The music video for "Girls" by N-Dubz premiered on T4 on November 14, 2010. It begins with Tulisa sitting on a throne, exuding confidence. Girls queue up two by two, performing synchronized dance moves. The group is seen singing in a lavish club setting, with vibrant lighting enhancing the atmosphere. Dappy appears with a woman as snow falls around them, creating a dramatic visual. After the first chorus, Tulisa is shown playing poker, adding a rebellious edge. She later kisses a female dealer, who is revealed to be her cousin. Fazer performs against a backdrop of screens displaying flames, adding intensity to his scenes. A cameo is made by DJ Ironik, wearing a distinctive hoodie. The clean version of the video omits certain suggestive scenes, including the kiss, to make it more suitable for younger audiences.

The concept for greenmuseum.org emerged in the late 1990s from the collaborative sculpture group Meadowsweet Dairy, based in Northern California. Members included Henry Corning, Glenda Griffith, Dan Ustin, Alan Leavitt, and Alex Tereshkin. Recognizing the need for a platform to support environmental art, the group held meetings with artists, environmentalists, and professionals, deciding on the name greenmuseum.org. They formed a nonprofit organization and launched the site on December 21, 2001, the December Solstice, to archive and promote environmental art.

Hibbertia pustulata is a small shrub that typically to a height of up to 15 cm (5.9 in) long and has more or less glabrous foliage. The leaves are more or less linear, mostly 6.0–7.5 mm (0.24–0.30 in) long, 0.6–0.8 mm (0.024–0.031 in) wide on a petiole 0.2–0.6 mm (0.0079–0.0236 in) long and with the edges rolled under. The flowers are arranged singly on the ends of branches with a triangular bract 0.7–1.1 mm (0.028–0.043 in) long at the base. The five sepals are joined at the base, the outer sepal lobes 5.9–6.3 mm (0.23–0.25 in) long and 2.5–2.6 mm (0.098–0.102 in) wide, the inner sepal lobes 3.2–3.4 mm (0.13–0.13 in) wide. The five petals are yellow, egg-shaped with the narrower end towards the base with a notch at the tip, 7.6–9.3 mm (0.30–0.37 in) long and there are four stamens fused at the base on one side of the two carpels, each carpel with four to six ovules. Flowering mainly occurs from August to October.

Giuppone's sporting career began in bicycling, and he won two Italian national championships in middle-distance racing. He moved to motorcycle racing in the early 1900s, most notably for the Peugeot racing team. He was the second Italian rider to compete in the Isle of Man Tourist Trophy. His races for Peugeot included extensive track racing on the large-capacity, ultra-lightweight racers of the early 1900s, which typically weighed a maximum of 110lbs, but had unlimited engine capacity, as much as 2liters at times. He also raced Peugeot automobiles as part of the factory team, winning the Coupe des Voiturettes in 1909, the Torino Cup and the Copa Catalunya and Circuito delle Madonie in 1908.

The film has gained critical acclaim. Review aggregator website Rotten Tomatoes, reports the film holds a 98% approval rating based on 88 reviews, with an average rating of 8.30/10. The site's critical consensus reads, "A triumphant coda to a beloved series, Deadwood: The Movie will satisfy fans longing for a little f---ing closure." On Metacritic, the film has a weighted average score of 86 out of 100 based on 29 critics, indicating "universal acclaim".

After leaving RNK Split in November 2016, Bakaj returned in Albania and begun training with Tirana in order to maintain his form. He said in an interview that his intentions were to play outside, but if not he would play in Albania only with Tirana. He continued to train with Tirana in January, and on 26th, he reached an argument with the club for a contract until the end of 2016–17 season. However, a day later, FIFA blocked the transfer due to Tirana's unpaid debts, forcing the club not to transfer any player during January transfer window, leaving Bakaj still without a team.

The Rachel Carson Center for Environment and Society hosts seminars, workshops, and public events to share and discuss the work of its researchers and visitors. Its outreach program includes collaborations with institutions like the Deutsches Museum to develop exhibitions. The Environment and Society Portal provides open-access resources on environmental humanities for global academic and public audiences. Additionally, the RCC offers the doctoral program "Environment and Society" through LMU Munich, fostering interdisciplinary research on environment-society interactions.

Epic Aircraft was founded in 2004 in Bend, Oregon, by Rick Schrameck, a veteran of the computer hardware and aviation composites industries. The company received $1.3 million in state loans and grants, promising to create 4,000 jobs in return, as part of public incentives to establish operations in Central Oregon.

On June 15, 2015, the Charleston City Paper reported Vice Principals filming in the North Charleston, South Carolina neighborhood of Park Circle. The newspaper added that comic actors Will Ferrell and Bill Murray would make cameo appearances in the series. Scenes that take place in the school were shot on the campus of R. B. Stall High School and also filmed on campus of West Ashley High School.

William J. Broad, a Pulitzer Prize-winning science journalist and senior writer for *The New York Times*, has authored or co-authored eight books, many of which focus on science, technology, and policy. His 2001 book, *Germs: Biological Weapons and America's Secret War*, co-written with Judith Miller and Stephen Engelberg, became a number-one *New York Times* Best Seller. Broad also co-authored *Betrayers of the Truth: Fraud and Deceit in the Halls of Science* with Nicholas Wade in 1982. His other works include *The Universe Below* (1997), *Teller's War* (1992), and *The Science of Yoga: The Risks and the Rewards* (2012). His books have been translated into over 50 languages, reflecting his global impact in exploring the intersection of science, policy, and society.

Massachusetts Route 225 begins at an intersection with Route 2A in the town of Littleton. As it enters the town of Shirley in Middlesex County, it is known as Great Road. Between its two crossings of the Nashua River, Route 225 is called Walker Road. The route continues as Long Hill Road in Groton, where it turns north to run concurrently with Route 111. Together, Route 225 and Route 111 follow Farmers Row until Route 111 diverges at an intersection with Broadmeadow Road. From there, Route 225 continues eastward and eventually joins Route 119 in the town of Groton.

M. Cristina Marchetti has significantly contributed to the development of active matter, which consists of energy-consuming entities that generate motion and exhibit collective behavior. Her research focuses on understanding emergent behaviors in active and living systems, including cell colonies that grow in confined geometries. She studies how physical forces and mechanical properties influence cell interactions, adhesion, proliferation, and migration. By varying forces between cells and surfaces, Marchetti investigates the bulk mechanical properties of tissues, which reveal microscopic interactions critical for processes like tissue engineering and wound healing.

In May 1889, Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890), a Dutch painter, moved to the Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, France, to commit himself at Saint-Paul-de-Mausole, a psychiatric asylum which was previously a monastery. This presented Van Gogh with a completely different landscape from which to draw inspiration. While Saint-Remy was only 25 kilometres (16 miles) from Arles, his previous residence, it lies below the low massifs of the Alpilles in contrast to the vast plains of Arles.

Ferdinand-François-Auguste Donnet, born in Bourg-Argental on November 16, 1795, served as Metropolitan Archbishop of Bordeaux, overseeing an ecclesiastical province that included the diocese of La Rochelle. Before his appointment to the diocese of Nancy and Toul, he was a professor and mission preacher. A prominent figure during Napoleon III's Second Empire, Donnet was known for his vigorous efforts in publishing and church restoration in Bordeaux. Elevated to cardinal in 1853 by Pope Pius IX, he forcefully advocated for the canonization of Joan of Arc. He participated in the 1878 conclave and left behind twelve volumes of pastoral instructions, sermons, and writings, cementing his legacy in the Church.

David Abbasi has written over 100 books and more than 3,000 articles. In 1984, he invented the Mithraic Diary and has authored several books on the subject. He has organized and produced over 500 artistic programs, plays, and festivals, held in venues such as the French Senate and the Bibliothèque Nationale de France, showcasing his dedication to cultural and theatrical expression on a global scale.

After her marriage the Marquise went to Paris to live and her entertainments were notable there. She spent much of her time in charitable labors. Her health began to fail in 1902. Since then she was said to have led the life of a recluse.

The miners' Hovel at the Racecourse Colliery served as a shelter where workers could rest and dry their wet pit clothes. A fire was kept lit in the center, often surrounded by flat stones, to aid in drying clothes, which were typically left overnight. Miners could brew tea or coffee and store their 'snap'—a term for their packed lunch, often carried in a tin. Over the fire, they might fry bacon or other food on a shovel. In some pits with considerate bosses, Dudley water containers were provided for miners to take underground.

A rat roundworm Gongylonema neoplasticum was the first parasite discovered—allegedly—to cause cancer. A Danish physician Johannes Fibiger discovered it in 1907, and experimentally showed that he could induce stomach cancer in rats using the roundworm infection in 1913. In 1914, he gave the name Spiroptera (Gongylonema) neoplastica, but later changed it to Spiroptera carcinoma. Fibiger won the 1926 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine "for his discovery of the Spiroptera carcinoma". However, his interpretation was later found to be false, and that the roundworm was not carcinogenic on its own. Fibiger's Nobel Prize was described as "one of the biggest blunders made by the Karolinska Institute."

Before the 2002 coup attempt, discontent within the Venezuelan military surfaced when a group of officers, known as Los Generales de Plaza Altamira, publicly opposed Hugo Chávez's government. Allegations emerged that Chávez's administration facilitated FARC's operations in Venezuela, with confiscated documents suggesting the Venezuelan military assisted the group by allowing the transport of supplies through its territory. The Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC), a guerrilla group involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and kidnapping, allegedly provided guerrilla and urban warfare training to pro-Chávez militias during the coup attempt, according to the International Institute for Strategic Studies (IISS). The IISS also claimed FARC maintained a Caracas office funded by Chávez's government, a charge denied by Venezuelan diplomats. These findings highlighted the complex relationship between Chávez's administration and FARC, despite international criticism and strategic shifts in Venezuelan policy.

Following the Russian Revolution and subsequent formation of the Soviet Union, the UOLE was initially granted funding by the local Soviet administration. However, the society came under scrutiny from Soviet authorities; one source noted that the society was considered a potential stronghold of counter-revolutionary activity in Yekaterinburg. In the 1920s, several prominent members were arrested, property was seized from the UOLE, and the society was forcibly closed in 1929.

Football is the most popular sport in Ta'if, with Wej SC representing the city in the Saudi Second Division. The team plays its home games at King Fahd Sports City, located near as-Sayl as-Saghir. Expatriates in Ta'if have introduced sports like cricket and badminton, which have gained popularity alongside traditional Saudi sports.

Microhyla laterite is a small frog species, with males measuring 15.3–16.6 mm in snout–vent length and the single known female measuring 18.4 mm. Its skin is smooth, and the tympanum is hidden beneath the skin. The frog is pale brown with prominent black markings on its dorsum, hands, feet, and flanks. A black line rimmed with red runs horizontally at the level of the forelimbs. The vocal sac is dark purplish when calling, the iris is golden, and the pupil is black. The ventral parts, excluding the throat, are cream-white in color.

Generalbezirk Estland, the last of the four districts in Reichskommissariat Ostland, was formally established on 5 December 1941 in occupied Estonia, previously under German military administration. Its capital was Reval (Tallinn), and it was governed by Generalkommissar Karl-Siegmund Litzmann.

Research and conservation organizations have yet to establish standardized policies specifically addressing human bycatch data, though privacy concerns are increasingly central to discussions. Advances in camera trap technology and metadata analysis software have enabled more efficient data collection and processing, originally designed to study wildlife populations. These programs can filter out false positives, such as non-target species or human images, reducing resource demands and improving data accuracy. Ensuring privacy when filtering human images is critical, aligning with privacy laws like the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA), which regulate the use of biometric data. Most government policies aim to protect individuals' privacy while enabling scientific research. Open-source platforms are being developed to address challenges like false triggers and long data processing times, but inconsistencies in data collection and presentation remain a barrier to fully integrating human bycatch data into conservation policies.

Archaeologist Denise Carpinteyro Espinosa, affiliated with the National Institute of Anthropology and History (INAH), has reported that the petroglyphs at Boca de Potrerillos, an archaeological site in Nuevo León, Mexico, date back approximately 7,000 years. These engravings, created by hunter-gatherer groups, are linked to both everyday life and astronomical observations. The site, crossed by a river, was significant for diverse human occupations over millennia. Studies suggest that the engravings provide insights into the ancient understanding of the skies, including the use of stars to track time for hunting and other activities. This highlights the site's importance as an early astronomical observatory and a center of cultural and ecological adaptation.

Williams was an officer and long-time member of the New York Friars' Club. He was named Man of the Year by the club in 1984 (in a ceremony hosted by Sinatra.) One year later, the Friars Club Foundation made him the recipient of its Applause Award. in recognition of his charitable efforts.

The artworks collected for the Führermuseum were originally stored in a number of places. The purchases were mostly kept in the air raid shelters of the Führerbau in Munich – one of a number of large buildings Hitler had built in the birthplace of the Nazi Party – where they were under the control of the Nazi Party Chancellery; Hitler would often come to visit them and indulge in long discussions on art as one of the first tasks when coming to Munich, even during the war. Confiscated artworks were stored in deposits in the area of Upper Austria, located in the middle of forests or in the mountains. The ERR alone requisitioned six estates for storage, including Neuschwanstein Castle in the Bavarian Alps, in which items from France were stored; the Benedictine monastery on the island of Frauenchiemsee in the Chiemsee lake, halfway between Munich and Salzburg; an estate in the Salzkammergut hills, which had been a summer residence for the Austrian royal family; and the Grand Duke of Luxembourg's hunting lodge.

Microhyla laterite is only known from few localities in Udupi district and Konaje, both in the Karnataka state, at elevations of 50–80 m (160–260 ft) above sea level. The species occurs in laterite habitats often around human settlements in rural and semi-urban areas. They inhabit ephemeral ponds and other marshy areas, and also occur in wet paddy fields where males have been observed to vocalize from the embankment.

Jata was drafted in the fourth round (58th overall) of the 2009 MLS SuperDraft by Chicago Fire, but failed to make their final roster and was released by the club during their pre-season. He then proceeded to trial with numerous clubs in the US and Europe, but failed to sign with any of them. Jata eventually returned to USL PDL club Carolina Dynamo to play for them during their 2010 season.

Peggy Dern, born Erolie Pearl Gaddis, used numerous pseudonyms throughout her prolific career, publishing nearly 300 books. While many were released under her married name, Peggy Gaddis, she frequently used pen names like Gerald Foster and Perry Lindsay. Other pseudonyms included Joan Sherman, Gail Jordan, and Georgia Craig, often to diversify her output and appeal to different markets.

Ramchand, son of Sahajram and Rochalbai of Bhiryan village, Navabshah district, Sindh (now in Pakistan) became a disciple of Baba Hariram and took sanyas and renamed by his guru as Sant Hirdaram Sahib (born 21 September 1906); and he later migrated to India during 1948 and first settled in Pushkar and later in Bairagarh, Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh and Bairagarh is renamed as "Sant Hirdaram Nagar" in 1995 by Government of Madhya Pradesh looking to the various benevolent activities being run with his blessings & inspiration by the charitable organization - Jeev Sewa Sansthan & its sister organizations. Rev. Siddh Bhauji is successor of Paramhans Sant Hirdaram Sahib. Like his Guru, Rev. Siddh Bhauji sought sanayasa and decided to remain Bal Brahmchari. He has been devoting every moment of his life for the welfare of suffering humanity.

In a 1996 American Cowboy article titled "The Debonair Killer", David P. Grady noted: "Marion Hedgepeth looked like a dude, but 'dangerous' and 'deadly' fit him better". The dark-complexioned, wavy-haired six footer, who roamed from town to town as a hired gun, Grady wrote, maintained the fastidious, gentlemanly appearance of a dandy, sporting a bowler hat and diamond stickpin. WANTED posters noted that his shoes were usually polished.

Donahue was born in Detroit, Michigan, United States. He put together his first band when he was only 15 years old. Donahue played in the bands of Gene Krupa, Harry James, and Benny Goodman. During World War II, Donahue took over the US Navy band of Artie Shaw. After the war, he assembled and led a group that recorded for Capitol Records. Trumpeters Harry Gozzard and Doc Severinsen, Wayne Herdell, arranger Leo Reisman and vocalists Frances Wayne and Jo Stafford were some of the members included in the new band. It dissolved in 1951 when Donahue re-enlisted in the Navy to serve in the Korean War.

Geles Cabrera's sculpture focuses on the human body and its geometry, exploring themes like love, sexuality, loneliness, and motherhood. Initially working with stone, she later incorporated materials like clay, bronze, and molded acrylic, using lyrical simplicity to emphasize the eroticism and clarity of the human form.

Everglades University was originally founded as American Flyers College in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, in 1990. In 1998, Arthur and Belinda Keiser purchased the institution, renaming it Everglades College in 1999 and relocating it to Fort Lauderdale after the name change.

The Dongzhi Festival is traditionally a time for family reunions and togetherness, particularly in southern parts of China and among Chinese communities overseas. A key activity during these gatherings is the making and eating of tangyuan, glutinous rice balls that symbolize reunion and prosperity. Tangyuan are made from glutinous rice flour and water, sometimes brightly colored, and each family member typically receives at least one large tangyuan along with several smaller ones. These rice balls can be plain or filled with sweet or savory fillings, such as black sesame paste or sweet bean paste. They are cooked in a sweet soup or savory broth, both served together in one bowl. Tangyuan is often accompanied by jiuniang, a mildly alcoholic unfiltered rice wine with glutinous rice grains and Sweet Osmanthus flowers.

The rivalry between the Youngstown State Penguins and the Akron Zips, known as the Steel Tire rivalry, began in 1940 and symbolized the steel and tire industries of Youngstown and Akron, respectively. Played sporadically before becoming an annual event from 1946 to 1995, the rivalry ended when Akron moved to Division I-A (FBS) in 1987 and joined the Mid-American Conference in 1992. Since 1995, the series has been dormant, with Youngstown State leading the all-time series 19-14-2.

The association was founded in 1972 by travel writer Norman Simpson, who traveled throughout North America identifying places that offered what he called, "good honest lodging, good honest food, and good honest feeling." Through his book, Country Inns and Back Roads, Simpson documented this group of properties in the U.S. and Canada. The association of independent innkeepers started by Simpson is now known as Select Registry.

The United States Secret Service's investigative mission focuses on safeguarding the nation’s financial and payment systems, with primary jurisdiction over crimes such as counterfeiting, credit card fraud, identity theft, and other financial crimes affecting federally insured institutions. It also combats cybercrimes, including ransomware, phishing, and computer fraud. As a member of the FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force, the Secret Service contributes to counterterrorism efforts. Additionally, it investigates technology-facilitated child exploitation and partners with the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC) to provide forensic, technical, and investigative support. Mandated since 1994, the Secret Service collaborates with NCMEC to address cases of missing and exploited children, offering resources and training to law enforcement and community stakeholders.

The Semiahmoo Harbor Light, constructed in 1905 near Blaine, Washington, was a 1 1/2-story dwelling on a platform supported by wooden piles, with a lantern at the center of its peaked roof. Designed by Carl Leick, it shared its design with the Desdemona Sands Lighthouse. Deactivated in 1944, the structure was dismantled by mid-June of that year.

At the 2000 census there were 1,688 people, 657 households, and 509 families in the town. The population density was 58.0 people per square mile (22.4/km2). There were 757 housing units at an average density of 26.0 per square mile (10.0/km2). The racial makeup of the town was 98.87% White, 0.12% African American, 0.18% Native American, 0.24% Asian, 0.06% Pacific Islander, 0.12% from other races, and 0.41% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.41%.

On the morning of June 24, 1855, Robert Newsom's daughters began to worry when he did not show up for breakfast. They began to search for him around the property and enlisted the help of some neighbors when their search was unsuccessful. William Powell, the owner of the farm next to the Newsoms', and Robert Newsom's sons, Harry and David, were among those who joined the search party. Powell questioned George about Newsom's whereabouts, and he told the search party that "it was not worth while to hunt for him any where except close around the house." Upon further questioning, George also stated that "he believed the last walking [Newsom] had done was along the path, pointing to the path leading from the house to the Negro Cabin."

After the war, the American Art Looting Investigation Unit (ALIU) of the Office of Strategic Services (OSS) made thirteen detailed reports on the Linz museum and the Nazi plundering of art. These reports were synthesised into four consolidated reports; the fourth of these was written by S. Lane Faison covering the Führermuseum. These reports focused on returning art to rightful owners. The authority for this was the 1943 Declaration of London, which invalidated all German art purchases in the occupied territories.

Ebrahim Al-Arrayedh began writing poetry at the age of 14, with his first set of poems published in Bombay in 1924. Renowned for translating poetry between Arabic, English, and Urdu, his works gained popularity in Bahrain, India, and the Arab world. He was invited by the American University of Beirut to deliver lectures on Arab literature. Recognized as a pioneer of modern Bahraini poetry, Al-Arrayedh was honored by the Bahraini government with the Order of Efficiency, celebrating his contributions to the nation’s literary and cultural heritage.

At the 2018 announcement of the updated shortlist of blueprint priority diseases, the media speculated that a future Disease X could be created intentionally as a biological weapon. In 2018, WHO R&D Blueprint Special Advisor Group member Røttingen was questioned about the potential of Disease X to come from the ability of gene-editing technology to produce synthetic viruses (e.g., the 2017 synthesis of Orthopoxvirus in Canada was cited), which could be released through an accident or even an act of terror. Røttingen said it was unlikely that a future Disease X would originate from a synthetic virus or a bio-weapon. However, he noted the seriousness of such an event, saying, "Synthetic biology allows for the creation of deadly new viruses. It is also the case that where you have a new disease there is no resistance in the population and that means it can spread fast".

The only source of information about Catharina Margaretha Linck is a court document dated 13 October 1721. It summarizes testimony from the trial of Linck and her wife, reviewing possible punishments for the monarch's consideration. Scholars characterize Linck as a lesbian and place her in a social context where women adopted male attire to access male privileges or engage in sexual relationships with women, often provoking authorities when such interactions were discovered.

While in school, Sumana Amarasinghe contested the "Lassana Muhuna" beauty pageant and won the title. Her maiden cinematic experience was in the 1967 film *Pipena Kumudu*, directed by Ruby de Mel, which her father allowed her to act in due to his trust in the director. She later acted in *Hathara Peraliya*, directed by L.M. Perera, and other notable films such as *Rena Giraw*, *Dahasak Sithuvili*, and *Samanala Kumariyo*. Amarasinghe's career flourished with numerous roles in Sri Lankan cinema.

On March 21 Alwafd reported that the possibility of a deal between the prosecution and some of those accused of embezzlement was explored. Alwafd stated that this option was explored after the prosecution was successful in striking a deal with some creditors and business borrowers to repay a total of E£13 billion in return for dropping legal charges. The situation, according to Alwafd, proved more complex as the offers made by the accused, in return for dropping the embezzlement charges, were seen as unrealistic.

The squadron was first activated at March Field, California in January 1941 as the 2d Reconnaissance Squadron, and was attached to the 30th Bombardment Group. Four months later it moved to New Orleans Airport, Louisiana, where it trained with the Douglas B-18 Bolo. Following the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, the squadron returned to California where it participated in antisubmarine patrols, re-equipping with Consolidated B-24 Liberators for operations. It was redesignated the 392d Bombardment Squadron in April 1942 and assigned directly to the 30th Bombardment Group. In June 1942, the unit added the mission of training aircrews in the B-24 and in the LB-30 Lend Lease version of the Liberator.

The rivalry was first played in 1890 on Minnesota's campus, in Minneapolis, resulting in a 63–0 Minnesota victory. Theron Lyman led Wisconsin to its first win over Minnesota in 1894. The game became a conference rivalry with the creation of the Western Conference (later the Big Ten Conference) in 1896. In 1906, President Theodore Roosevelt suspended college football rivalry games for safety concerns, due to player injuries and fatalities on the field. It is the single year the two teams did not play each other. Subsequently, it is now the longest uninterrupted rivalry in FBS Division 1 college football. The game has never been played in any city besides Minneapolis, and Madison, Wisconsin.

The Latin phrase Montani Semper Liberi (Template:Lang-en) was adopted as the official state motto of West Virginia in Article II, Section 2-7, of the state constitution signed in 1872. This article specifically states: "[t]he present seal of the state, with its motto, "Montani Semper Liberi," shall be the great seal of the state of West Virginia, and shall be kept by the secretary of state, to be used by him officially, as directed by law". The phrase was suggested as the motto by Joseph H. Diss Debar, the artist who created the state's Great Seal.

Preston North End originated as a cricket team, with its roots tracing back to 1863. Cricket was the primary sport in Preston at the time, and the team initially played on The Marsh, a piece of land on the Ribble Estuary, now the site of the GEC works. The cricket team was linked to Wellfield Road School. In 1867, some members proposed a move to Moor Park, where the Corporation had opened a public cricket ground. The new club, named North End to reflect its location in the town, elected George Howarth as its first president, with subscriptions set at 2d per week. On August 3, 1867, William Sudell, aged 16, joined the club, marking the beginning of his long association with what would become Preston North End F.C.

In the liner notes to the 1993 Rolling Stones compilation album Jump Back, Jagger states, "I remember we sat around originally doing this with Gram Parsons, and I think his version came out slightly before ours. Everyone always says this was written about Marianne but I don't think it was; that was all well over by then. But I was definitely very inside this piece emotionally." Richards says, "If there is a classic way of Mick and me working together this is it. I had the riff and chorus line, Mick got stuck into the verses. Just like "Satisfaction", "Wild Horses" was about the usual thing of not wanting to be on the road, being a million miles from where you want to be."

Silesian (Silesian: slonsko godka, Polish: jezyk slaski, dialekt slaski) is a lect spoken primarily in Upper Silesia, a region spanning southwestern Poland and northeastern Czech Republic. Linguistic sources often debate whether Silesian is a dialect of Polish, an ethnolect, or a distinct language, with many sociolinguists, such as Tomasz Kamusella, emphasizing that such distinctions are often sociopolitical rather than purely linguistic. Many Silesians consider themselves a separate ethnicity within Poland and have been advocating for the recognition of Silesian as a distinct language. In the 2011 Polish census, 509,000 people declared Silesian as their native language. Language organizations like SIL International and resources such as Ethnologue recognize Silesian as a distinct language, and in 2007, it was assigned the ISO 639-3 language code "szl." The development of Silesian literature and orthography further supports its growing status as a separate language.

CRREL's second 25 years saw the dissolution of the Soviet Union, the end of the Cold War and a shift in funding that reduced the emphasis of direct appropriations from Congress to a greater reliance on reimbursement for research from CRREL's customers, as evidenced by the sponsorship of its technical reports. Customers funding CRREL research included various components of the U.S. Army, Air Force and Navy. In addition, civilian agencies turned to CRREL for research answers, including the National Science Foundation, the Environmental Protection Agency, and NASA. In addition, a variety of private organizations funded CRREL research to solve problems that they faced. CRREL's list of technical reports lists 27 topical categories, covering science and engineering.

The development of the AGS-40 began in the mid-1980s but stalled after the collapse of the Soviet Union. Revived in the early 1990s under the codename "Balkan," its progress was slow, with operational evaluation starting in 2008. State tests were completed in 2013, and by February 2018, it was recommended for service. The first serial batch was delivered in 2021. The AGS-40 can be mounted on various vehicles and integrated with the Arbalet-DM remote weapon station, enhancing its versatility in modern combat scenarios.

The music video for "War Paint" by Fletcher was released on YouTube on July 15, 2015. It was produced in a single day at a studio in Brooklyn, with filming taking place from 6:30 a.m. to 6:30 p.m. Directed by Garen Barsegian, the video features a striking contrast of dark backgrounds and bold, bright colors, reflecting the song's themes. Fletcher was actively involved in the creative direction, collaborating closely with Barsegian to achieve the visual juxtaposition. The choreography, which incorporated contemporary and interpretive dance styles, added dynamic movement to the video. Props such as vibrant powders and paint were used to enhance the visual storytelling, symbolizing self-expression and resilience. The post-production editing was handled by a skilled team, though specific details about the editor remain unconfirmed. The video’s energetic and artistic elements contributed to its strong reception, complementing the song’s empowering message.

Borden is commemorated with various monuments and plaques. In his home town, Canning, Nova Scotia is a statue by Hamilton MacCarthy. In Halifax, Nova Scotia the base of the South African War Memorial has a panel commemorating the Battle of Witpoort. There is also a memorial plaque to Borden at McGill University, Montreal, in the Strathcona Medical Building (now the Strathcona Anatomy and Dentistry Building). Borden was a medical student at McGill before joining the war effort. He is listed on the Memorial Arch at the Royal Military College of Canada. Finally, there is also a plaque to Borden and others who died at Witpoort at Braamfontein Cemetery in South Africa where he is buried.

According to some sources Venezuela's corruption includes widespread corruption in the police force. Criminologists and experts have stated that low wages and lack of police supervision has been attributed to police corruption. Many victims are afraid to report crimes to the police because many officers are involved with criminals and may bring even more harm to the victims. Human Rights Watch claims that the "police commit one of every five crimes" and that thousands of people have been killed by police officers acting with impunity (only 3% of officers have been charged in cases against them). The Metropolitan Police force in Caracas was so corrupt that it was disbanded and was even accused of assisting in many of the 17,000 kidnappings.

On September 3, 2014, Li called his friend Karen Yang unexpectedly, saying he was upset after calling Shao Tong. According to Li, Shao accidentally answered her phone without realizing it, leaving the line open for about 30 minutes. During this time, Li overheard Shao conversing with another man, whom he described as a new friend of hers. Shao reportedly complained about Li and made unkind remarks. After speaking with Yang, Li posted a cryptic message on his Renren page, stating, "I have done something unforgivable."

After his retirement in 1993, Walker retreated to a house in Chiswick district of London. During his posting as a political officer in the colonial territories of Persian Gulf Residency in the 1950s and 1960s, Walker used to personally meet several sheikhs and emirs of different sheikdhoms and emirates. He had a close relationship with Sheikh Zayed and his brother Sheikh Hazza. He had personally known Sheikh Shakbut and Sheikh Rashid during several meetings that he held with them for the purpose of border demarcations. He enjoyed music, cooking and gardening.

In 1936 the quarries, and land extending further down the ravine, were bought by the Air Ministry and the site became RAF Chilmark, which was the home of No. 11 Maintenance Unit RAF. Munitions were stored in the quarry caverns, and for a time the unit had extensive above-ground storage areas in woodland near Dinton and in Grovely Wood.

François Picquet returned to France in 1772, serving in parish ministry at Verjon until 1775. He then became chaplain to the Visitation nuns in Bourg-en-Bresse. After a private audience with Pope Pius VI in 1777, he retired in 1779, dying in Verjon in 1781.

Tiruwork Wube, also known as Empress Tiruwork or Queen Terunesh, was the daughter of Dejazmatch Wube Hayle Maryam, the prince of Semien and a prominent figure in 19th-century Ethiopia. Dejazmatch Wube, of Amhara ethnicity, ruled much of northern Ethiopia during the 1830s and 1840s, including Simien, Tigray, Wolkait, Tsegede, Hamasien, Akele Guzay, and Seraye. He was descended from Emperor Fasilides and harbored ambitions of claiming the Imperial throne, though he was ultimately defeated by Emperor Tewodros II, who imprisoned him and his sons for life. Tiruwork’s mother, Woizero Lakiyaye, was a Tigrayan noblewoman and the daughter of Ras Wolde Selassie, who had established his capital at Chalacot in the late 1700s. Tiruwork, a descendant of both Ras Wolde Selassie and Emperor Fasilides, endured her father’s imprisonment through prayer and fasting, reflecting her deep religiosity. She even planned to enter a convent. Despite her noble lineage, Tiruwork married Tewodros II, whom she considered a usurper, and bore him a son, Alemayehu. Her father’s defeat and imprisonment marked a turning point in her life, as Tewodros consolidated his power and crowned himself at Derasge Maryam, a church Wube had built for his own coronation.

Also common in most parts of Australia this casserole is also called tuna mornay and served with a Mornay sauce (Béchamel with cheese added, or cheese sauce). Peas and corn are usually added. Additionally, wheat pasta is used rather than egg noodles.

Tropical Storm George originated on September 27 from a strong tropical wave northeast of the Lesser Antilles and southeast of Bermuda. A large system, it moved to the northwest and later west-northwest. George began strengthening on September 30 as it decreased its forward speed. The next day, while remaining nearly stationary south of Bermuda, a nearby ship reported that George had reached hurricane status. It continued moving very slowly, passing only 100 mi (160 km) south of Bermuda. The island experienced winds of 30 to 40 mph (48 to 64 km/h). Aside from rainbands, little impact was reported on Bermuda.

Clifford Burke Harmon was born on July 5, 1868, in Lebanon, Ohio, to Lieutenant William R. Harmon and Mary "Molly" Wood. His older brother, William Elmer Harmon, was born in 1862. Their father served with the 70th Ohio Volunteer Infantry during the Civil War and later as an officer with the African-American "Buffalo Soldiers" on the western frontier.

Every 300 years, the world of *Romancing SaGa 3* is shaken by a cataclysmic event known as the Rise of Morastrum, during which a dark star eclipses the sun, and all living beings born that year are fated to die. However, a single child, known as the Child of Destiny, always survives, their fate tied to either great good or immense evil. Six centuries before the game's events, the Child of Destiny became the Archfiend, a being of darkness who sought to conquer the world. Three centuries later, another Child of Destiny emerged, defying the same powers and becoming the Matriarch, a force of righteousness. Now, as the Rise of Morastrum looms once more, the world teeters on the edge of hope and despair, wondering if the next Child of Destiny will be a savior, a destroyer, or something entirely unforeseen. Players choose from eight main characters, each with their own unique backstory and perspective, as they embark on a journey to uncover the truth behind the Rise of Morastrum and the Child of Destiny. Their paths intertwine in a shared quest to prevent the opening of the Abyssal Gates, a threat that could bring about the end of the world.

Rodez, the administrative center of the Aveyron department in Southern France, serves as the seat of the Departmental Council and houses the prefecture, administered by the Prefect. The town hosts various emergency services, including a gendarmerie brigade. Rodez is also home to a remand prison, with a new facility located in Druelle, operational since July 2013. This modern prison has a capacity to accommodate 190 male inmates, including those eligible for day parole.

In 2009, Cross was awarded an honorary doctorate by University College, Cork. During her years in the United States, she won a prestigious Pollock-Krasner Award (1990). Her work has been supported by grants from the Wellcome Trust and the Arts Council of Ireland/An Chomhairle Ealaíon and Arts Council of England.

Sabeena Rafi (6 October 1924 – 22 June 1990) was born in India to Shouryar Joseph and Anna. She pursued a career as an essayist and historian of Malayalam literature. In 1963, she married Ponjikkara Rafi, who was five years younger than her. The couple did not have children. Sabeena Rafi passed away on 22 June 1990 at the age of 65. Her husband, Ponjikkara Rafi, survived her and later died on 6 September 1992 at the age of 68.

During this time, the Sindhi Wikipedia has expanded and some technical issues have been resolved. Sindhi Wikipedia users have improved content, references, technical issues, categorization, templates, and infoboxes. Recently, Sindhi Wikipedians arranged a Wikipedia awareness workshop in Karachi.

After becoming the captain of Drégely Castle in 1544, which had a small garrison of only sixty men, six small cannon and twelve castle guns, Szondy was faced with the task of defending it. Drégely Castle was of strategic importance not only to the Hungarians but also to the Turks as a base for raids into Upper Hungary. After hearing that a Turkish force of great numbers was heading towards Drégely, Szondy sent a letter to Erasmus Teuffel, the Captain of the castle in Léva (today Levice, Slovakia), to send reinforcements. Teuffel could only supply him with 146 soldiers.

89th Brigade went into winter quarters at Phalempin on 27 November, and soon the first of its men left for demobilisation. 68th Siege Bty was disbanded in 1919. (A new 68th Bty, RGA, was formed on 16 April 1919 from the wartime 21st Heavy Bty.)

The Arlington Boulevard Trail, a 2.5-mile discontinuous trail and on-road bike route, runs along both sides of Arlington Boulevard (Route 50) from Rosslyn in Arlington County to Glebe Road in Falls Church. A proposed extension aims to connect the trail from Washington, D.C., to Fairfax City, Virginia, filling critical gaps.

Duck Game is a fast-paced, 2D arena shooter/platformer inspired by Sega Genesis aesthetics. Supporting up to four players in local or online multiplayer, the game revolves around chaotic, last-duck-standing rounds where players eliminate opponents with one-hit kills using a variety of weapons and tools. Basic controls include running, jumping, picking up/throwing items, and using weapons, which can involve actions like shooting, reloading, or cocking. Angled shots and certain maneuvers require precise timing and situational awareness. For solo players, a challenge mode offers shooting galleries and endurance tests, teaching core mechanics like sliding and breaking windows. Completing these challenges earns tickets, which can be exchanged for gameplay modifiers or collectible hats. Duck Game’s simplicity and anarchic gameplay make it accessible and endlessly entertaining.

In 1964, Helmut Leherb was nominated for the Venice Biennale with his *Time Destruction Manifesto* (*Zeitzerstörungsmanifest*), but his participation was blocked by Theodor Piffl-Perčević, the Austrian Minister of Education, following a government reshuffle. Leherb had planned a dark blue pavilion adorned with dead doves, umbrellas, and dolls, which sparked controversy. The leading Parisian art journal *Arts et Loisirs* declared it "The first scandal of the Venice Biennale!" Meanwhile, the German magazine *Stern* featured the scandal on its front page with the headline: "Surrealist Leherb: No white mice for Venice," criticizing Austria's cultural policies. This episode marked a significant moment in Leherb's career, blending art and controversy.

Starship is a second stage or long-duration spacecraft, forming the upper part of the rocket. The spacecraft is 50 m (160 ft) tall and has a dry mass of less than 100 t (220,000 lb). Starship's payload volume is about 1,000 m3 (35,000 cu ft), larger than the International Space Station's pressurized volume by 80 m3 (2,800 cu ft), and can be even bigger with an extended 22 m (72 ft)-tall volume.: 2 By refueling the Starship spacecraft in orbit using tanker spacecraft, Starship will be able to transport larger payloads and more astronauts to other Earth orbits, the Moon, and Mars.: 5

During the 18th and 19th centuries, the site of present-day Beit Nehemia was part of the Arab village of Bayt Nabala, which belonged to the Nahiyeh (sub-district) of Lod. This area extended from the present-day city of Modi'in-Maccabim-Re'ut in the south to El'ad in the north, from the foothills in the east through the Lod Valley to the west. It encompassed approximately 20 villages and tens of thousands of hectares of prime agricultural land, supporting thousands of inhabitants engaged in farming and other activities.

The Labor Movement wing of Religious Zionism, HaPoel HaMizrachi, was founded in 1921 under the slogan "Torah va'Avodah" (Torah and Labor). It represented religiously traditional Labour Zionists in both Europe and the Land of Israel, where it served as the voice of religious Jews within the Histadrut. In 1956, Mizrachi, HaPoel HaMizrachi, and other religious Zionist groups united to form the National Religious Party (NRP), which operated as an independent political party until the 2003 elections. In the 2006 elections, the NRP merged with the National Union (NU), a coalition of smaller religious parties, to run as a single list. By the 2009 elections, the NRP had been replaced by the Jewish Home party, which became the primary political vehicle for religious Zionism. A more radical branch of religious Zionism, Kahanism, was founded by Rabbi Meir Kahane, whose party, Kach, was banned from the Knesset but has influenced Israeli politics through groups like Otzma Yehudit. This evolution reflects the diverse ideological currents within religious Zionism, from moderate labor-oriented movements to more nationalist and radical factions.

The only known host plant for B. steniellus is the sugarcane. The female moth lays a batch of 100 to 300 eggs on the midrib of a sugarcane leaf. The larvae hatch out after about a week and make their way into the stem by drilling holes just above a node. They excavate galleries inside the stem, feeding voraciously. When the cane dries up and the crown of leaves dies in about ten days, the larvae move to a different cane in the vicinity. They feed for three to four weeks, passing through five instar stages, before pupating inside a stem. The adult moths emerge in six to twelve days. The male/female ratio varies between forty and sixty percent in the different generations. The whole life cycle lasts about five or six weeks, and there may be two or three generations in each year.

The first North Carolina Highway 67 (NC 67) was established in 1921 as part of the original state highway system. Its southern terminus was located in Moravian Falls, where it intersected NC 16. From there, the highway traveled along Falls Road, a local roadway, to Moravian Falls and continued northward, intersecting US 421 in Wilkesboro. In the northern part of Wilkes County, NC 67 transitioned into a more rugged, mountainous roadway. The original northern terminus of NC 67 was at the Virginia state line, where it connected with Virginia State Route 67. This early alignment provided a key connection through Wilkes County, linking Moravian Falls, Wilkesboro, and the surrounding mountainous regions to neighboring states.

The Local Government (Scotland) Act 1889 established a uniform system of county councils, leading to the creation of Dunbarton County Council in 1890. Queen Elizabeth II, accompanied by the Duke of Edinburgh, visited Dumbarton on June 28, 1965, to open the new £800,000 county buildings, marking a significant moment in the area's history.

The Battle of Actium was the culmination of tensions within the Second Triumvirate, an alliance formed in 43 BCE by Octavian, Mark Antony, and Marcus Lepidus. Renewed in 37 BCE at Tarentum, the alliance fractured as Octavian viewed Antony, who had moved to Alexandria to begin a long-term romance with Cleopatra, as a major threat to his power. Cleopatra, the mother of Caesarion, claimed her son was Julius Caesar’s rightful heir, further alarming Octavian. Antony, married to Octavian’s sister Octavia Minor, abandoned her to align with Cleopatra, assuming a protective role over Caesarion. These actions, seen as a betrayal by Octavian and much of the Roman Senate, set the stage for the decisive conflict at Actium.

Clifford Burke Harmon was born on July 1, 1866 in Urbana, Ohio, the youngest child of William R. and Mary Wood ("Molly") Harmon. He had one older brother, William Elmer Harmon, born in 1862. His father William R. Harmon fought in the Civil War with the 70th Ohio Volunteers and later served as an officer with the “Buffalo Soldiers”.

From the time of his election as pope to the fall of Napoleon in 1815, Pius VII's reign was completely taken up in dealing with France. He and the Emperor were continually in conflict, often involving the French military leader's wishes for concessions to his demands. Pius VII wanted his own release from exile as well as the return of the Papal States, and, later on, the release of the 13 "Black Cardinals", i.e., the cardinals, including Consalvi, who had snubbed the marriage of Napoleon to Marie Louise, believing that his previous marriage was still valid, and had been exiled and impoverished in consequence of their stand, along with several exiled or imprisoned prelates, priests, monks, nuns and other various supporters.

The general election on 20 September 2014 saw the National Government returned again. National won a plurality with 47.0% of the party vote and 60 of the 121 seats. On election night counts the party appeared to hold the first majority since 1994 with 61 seats, but lost a list seat (for Maureen Pugh) to the Green Party on the official count (including special votes) of the party vote. National re-entered a confidence and supply arrangement with United Future, the ACT Party and the Māori Party.

The neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses (NCLs) are a group of inherited lysosomal storage disorders characterized by severe neurodegeneration, neuronal loss in the brain and retina, and the accumulation of autofluorescent lipopigments in lysosomes. At least 10 genes are implicated in causing NCLs, with some encoding soluble lysosomal proteins, including the TPP1 gene, which provides instructions for producing the enzyme tripeptidyl peptidase I (TPP1). Mutations in the TPP1 gene result in TPP1 deficiency, leading to the accumulation of ceroid lipofuscin and subsequent neuronal damage. Late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (CLN2 disease) is specifically caused by TPP1 mutations and is associated with progressive neurodegeneration and retinal degeneration. A promising treatment for CLN2 disease involves the use of cerliponase alfa, a recombinant form of TPP1, which has been shown to slow disease progression. Additionally, ex vivo gene therapy using autologous stem cells transduced with a TPP1 expression construct has demonstrated the potential to inhibit retinal degeneration in animal models, highlighting the therapeutic promise of stem cell-based approaches for addressing the underlying enzyme deficiency in NCLs. These findings underscore the importance of gene-based and enzyme replacement therapies in managing this devastating disorder.

The Moderate Party of Rhode Island was founded by Ken Block, a businessman and political activist, in 2009. Block, who ran as the party's gubernatorial candidate in 2010, emphasized that the party would focus on serving the best interests of Rhode Island residents, with a primary emphasis on reforming the state legislature. He also stated that the party might support candidates from other parties if they aligned with its principles.

Gene F. Dresselhaus, born on November 9, 1929, in the Panama Canal Zone, studied physics at the University of California, Berkeley, where he earned his bachelor's degree in 1951 under the supervision of condensed-matter physicist Charles Kittel. At Berkeley, he conducted pioneering experiments on cyclotron resonance in semiconductors and semi-metals. After completing his graduate studies, Dresselhaus served as an instructor at the University of Chicago during the 1954–1955 academic year. From 1956 to 1960, he was a junior faculty member at Cornell University and consulted for General Electric during this time. In 1960, he joined MIT's Lincoln Laboratory as a research physicist, where he contributed to advancements in semiconductor and condensed-matter physics.

Kintetsu Railway traces its origins to the founding of Nara Tramway Co., Ltd. on September 16, 1910, which was renamed Osaka Electric Tramway Co., Ltd. (Daiki) a month later. On April 30, 1914, Osaka Electric Tramway completed the Ikoma Tunnel and began operating the Nara Line between Osaka and Nara. In the 1920s, the company completed the modern Kashihara, Osaka, and Shigi lines, as well as the Kyoto Line, a cooperative venture with Keihan Electric Railway. In 1927, Daiki established Sangu Electric Railway Co., Ltd., which later consolidated Ise Electric Railway Co., Ltd. on September 15, 1936. These developments laid the foundation for Kintetsu’s extensive rail network, connecting key regions in Japan.

In April 1993, Valacich et al. proposed that concurrency be included as an additional characteristic to determine a medium's richness. They introduced the term "environmental concurrency," defining it as the communication capacity of an environment to support distinct communication episodes simultaneously, without detracting from others occurring between the same or different individuals. Valacich et al. explained that while concurrency aligns with Daft and Lengel's original media richness theory, new media significantly enhance opportunities for concurrency, enabling richer communication environments by supporting multiple simultaneous interactions more effectively than traditional media.

In the 1930s, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the Mutawakkilite Kingdom of Yemen purchased substantial numbers of FN Mle 30 short rifles. Between 1945 and 1950, Saudi Arabia acquired more FN rifles, some of which may have been sent to Yemen post-war.

Born in Pretoria, Williams was a doubles specialist, who had success as a junior in 1993 when he made three junior grand slam finals. After finishing runner-up at both the French Open and Wimbledon, he and Neville Godwin made a third successive final at the 1993 US Open, defeating the Australian pairing of Ben Ellwood and James Sekulov. He and Godwin also competed in several ATP Tour events, including Durban in 1993, where they made the quarter-finals.

Kirk Penney was born on November 23, 1980, in Auckland, New Zealand. He attended Westlake Boys High School and played junior basketball for the North Harbour Basketball Association. In 1998, at age 17, he joined the North Harbour Kings in the New Zealand NBL, earning Rookie of the Year honors. Penney played for the Kings from 1998 to 2000.

Carrier IQ's history is tied to data analysis, akin to the MISP software, which collects, analyzes, and shares cybersecurity data. Businesses, particularly IT and security-focused ones, value such insights to better understand service performance and enhance user experiences, including those of customers and end-users.

He played for the Thailand national under-23 football team at the 2009 Southeast Asian Games. He scored an unofficial penalty against Denmark League XI in the 2012 King's Cup He was part of the 2012 AFF Suzuki Cup, and he replaced Datsakorn Thonglao after Datsakorn suffered a groin injury during the tournament. In 2018 he was called up by Thailand national team for the 2018 AFF Suzuki Cup.

Robert Lester began his college career at the University of Alabama in 2009, playing in eight games as a freshman, including on special teams in the BCS National Championship against Texas. In 2010, his first year as a starter, Lester recorded 52 tackles, 8 interceptions, and 1 sack, leading the SEC in interceptions.

Coffrane had an area, as of 2009, of 6.5 square kilometers (2.5 sq mi). Of this area, 4.11 km2 (1.59 sq mi) or 63.3% is used for agricultural purposes, while 1.71 km2 (0.66 sq mi) or 26.3% is forested. Of the rest of the land, 0.68 km2 (0.26 sq mi) or 10.5% is settled (buildings or roads), 0.02 km2 (4.9 acres) or 0.3% is either rivers or lakes and 0.02 km2 (4.9 acres) or 0.3% is unproductive land.

In 1958, the National Research Universal (NRU) reactor at Chalk River Laboratories experienced a serious accident involving the rupture and overheating of metallic uranium fuel rods. As workers attempted to remove a damaged rod from the reactor vessel using a robotic crane, the rod caught fire and broke apart. A three-foot-long section of burning uranium fell into a shallow maintenance pit, while the larger portion was moved toward a storage pool. The fire caused significant contamination within the NRU building. Workers in protective gear extinguished the fire by spraying the burning uranium with water. The incident resulted in a large-scale cleanup effort, with hundreds of Atomic Energy of Canada Limited (AECL) staff and military personnel volunteering to decontaminate the site under strict radiation exposure limits. This marked the second major nuclear accident at Chalk River Laboratories.

During the Yugoslav Wars—in the Srebrenica massacre—about 7,500 people were killed by Bosnian Serb troops in a span of eleven days. It was later found to be an act of genocide by the International Court of Justice. The UK sponsored a resolution which would pronounce the Serbian people genocidal and commemorate the 20th anniversary of the massacre. The Russian Federation, after Serbian President Tomislav Nikolić sent a letter to Russian President Vladimir Putin, was the only country on the security council that was against the resolution (China and three other countries abstained): Churkin issued a veto on his country's behalf at the UN Security Council on 8 July 2015.

Following Labour's defeat in the 2011 Scottish Parliament election, which saw the Scottish National Party (SNP) form its first majority government, Iain Gray announced his intention to step down as leader of the Labour MSPs. A subsequent review of the party's structure in Scotland, chaired by Jim Murphy and Sarah Boyack, concluded that the next leader should lead the entire Scottish Labour Party, rather than just its MSPs. Johann Lamont declared her candidacy for the leadership election in September 2011 and officially launched her campaign on 7 November at Stirling University. During her campaign, Lamont emphasized the need for Labour to reengage with the electorate, stating, "We must listen and learn, show humility and seek again to talk for and to people's ambitions and concerns. Our real challenge is that we in Labour lost our way, lost our confidence and lost Scotland." This marked a pivotal moment in the party's efforts to rebuild its presence in Scotland.

From the late 1970s, U.S. Chief Justice Warren Burger led a movement to create Inns of Court in the United States, loosely modelled after the traditional English Inns. In 1985, he and others established the American Inns of Court Foundation to promote and formally charter local Inns of Court across the United States. Each local Inn is devoted to promoting professionalism, civility, ethics, and legal skills amongst the American bench and bar, in a collegial setting, through continuing education and mentoring. At present, each major American city has more than one Inn of Court; for example, one Inn may be affiliated with a local law school, and another may be associated with a specific field of legal practice. American Inns of Court do not possess any real property. They are groups of judges, practising attorneys, law professors and students who meet regularly (usually monthly) to discuss and debate issues relating to legal ethics and professionalism. American Inn of Court meetings typically consist of a shared meal and a programme presented by one of the Inn's pupillage teams.

The American Inns of Court, inspired by the traditional English Inns of Court, were established in the United States through a movement led by U.S. Chief Justice Warren E. Burger in the late 1970s. This initiative aimed to foster professionalism, ethics, civility, and excellence within the legal profession. The American Inns of Court Foundation was formally established in 1985 by Chief Justice Burger and others to support the growing network of local Inns. Unlike their English counterparts, American Inns of Court do not own property but serve as associations of judges, lawyers, law professors, and law students who meet regularly to discuss legal ethics, skills, and professionalism. Each local Inn typically meets monthly, often over a shared meal, and features educational programs or discussions led by pupillage teams. Membership is limited and by invitation, with major cities often hosting multiple Inns, sometimes focused on specific legal fields. For example, the Atlas IP Inn in Houston specializes in intellectual property law. Today, nearly 400 Inns operate across the United States, providing a collegial environment for mentoring and the exchange of ideas, thereby promoting the highest standards of the legal profession.

Kihikihi School is a co-educational state primary school for Year 1 to 8 students, with a roll of 210 as of November 2024 Kihikihi is a dual medium Kura, meaning there is an Aoraki stream taught in English, and a Rumaki stream taught entirely in Te Reo Māori. It opened in 1873, and moved to its current site in 1884. It was destroyed by fire in 1938, and rebuilt in 1952.

The roundworm Trichuris muris infection can increase the number of tumours in mice. Heavy infection with the trematode Platynosomum fastosum is associated with cholangiocarcinoma in cats. Cryptosporidium parvum infection can be the cause of carcinoma in the gut of mice.

At the age of 16, Jackson became a cadet in the East India Company’s army, and went to India to join the famous Bengal Artillery Regiment. He was subsequently posted to Singapore to defend the town in case of an attack and arrived on the island on 22 January 1822. The attack, however, never materialised.

Richard Jata was selected by the Chicago Fire in the fourth round (58th overall) of the 2009 MLS SuperDraft. After being released by the Fire, he trialed with various lower-division clubs. In 2010, Jata returned to play for the Carolina RailHawks, continuing his professional soccer career in the United States.

Upson was born in San Bernardino, California, on April 22, 1970. However her date of birth is often listed as 1972, incorrectly. She attended New York Studio School of Drawing, Painting and Sculpture and later attended California Institute of the Arts (CalArts). She received her BFA in 2004 and her MFA in 2007 from CalArts. She began The Larry Project as a student, and exhibited a portion of the work as her MFA thesis.

Moore was a Lecturer in Psychology at the University of Reading from 1971 to 1977, spending the year 1973-74 as a Fulbright-Hays Senior Scholar and Visiting Professor at the Department of Psychology, Brooklyn College of the City University of New York. In 1977 he was appointed University Lecturer in Experimental Psychology at the University of Cambridge where he was subsequently appointed Reader (1989) and Professor (1995). He became Emeritus Professor in 2014. He was appointed as Fellow of Wolfson College in 1983 and is now an Emeritus Fellow.

The album is described as a departure from their previous recording, Invisible Republic. Lissvik's more electronic-oriented roots resulted in "dance-inflected pop", as opposed to their more dream pop style on earlier records. Although Ramsay and McCandless share vocal duties on the recording, Shapeshifting sees singer/keyboardist Catherine McCandless moving into more of a "lead-singer role".

In his speech at the Urbanization Work Conference on December 12, 2013, CCP general secretary Xi Jinping said "When upgrading the urban drainage system, priority should be given to retaining limited rainwater and using the power of nature to drain water. Build a sponge city with natural retention, natural penetration, and natural purification." To this end, in October 2014, the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development issued 'Technical Guidelines for Sponge City Construction,' emphasizing the importance of the top-level design of urban rainwater management, as well as planning to guide urban construction with ecological priority as the basic principle.

In March 1930, Dominican President Horacio Vásquez was overthrown in a coup led by Rafael Estrella Ureña and General Rafael Trujillo. Estrella Ureña succeeded Vásquez as provisional president, and with Trujillo worked out an arrangement whereby the pair would stand on the same ticket at the 1930 presidential election (with Trujillo running for president and Estrella Ureña for vice-president). Brache, a supporter of the coup, reputedly clung to Trujillo "like a leech", and was appointed as the new Ambassador to the United States.

USS *Concord* (CL-10) began her maiden cruise on 23 November 1923, concluding on 9 April 1924. During this voyage, she visited Mediterranean ports, passed through the Suez Canal, rounded the Cape of Good Hope, and exercised with the fleet in the Caribbean before returning to Philadelphia. Serving as the flagship of Commander, Destroyer Squadrons, Scouting Fleet, she cruised the Caribbean and sailed through the Panama Canal to exercise in the Hawaiian Islands in 1924–1925. Continuing in the Atlantic, she joined the Presidential Fleet Review by Calvin Coolidge on 4 June 1927.

Clayton is governed via a six-member board of aldermen and a mayor. Aldermen are elected from one of three wards with each electing two members. The mayor is elected in a citywide vote. A city clerk is appointed by the Mayor and Board of Aldermen. Police services are provided by the Clayton Police Department, led by Chief Mark J. Smith, with fire and rescue services provided by the Clayton Fire Department, led by Chief Ernie Rhodes.

The Pacific Crest Trail was established as a National Scenic Trail in 1968 under the National Trails System Act. The Pacific Crest Trail Club, founded earlier, merged with the Pacific Crest Trail Conference in 1987. The Conference, incorporated in 1977, became the Pacific Crest Trail Association (PCTA) in 1992. The PCTA hired its first paid staff in 1993.

The name "Multnomah" originates from the Multnomah people, a Chinookan group historically residing near Sauvie Island in the Columbia River. The existence of "Chief Multnomah" is debated, with the figure popularized by Frederick Balch's 1890 novel *The Bridge of the Gods*, a fictionalized account inspired by Wappato Valley chief Kiesno. While some oral histories reference Chief Multnomah, Oregon historians, including multiple 20th-century presidents of the Oregon Historical Society, have dismissed him as a myth. However, conflicting evidence includes writings and oral traditions suggesting his reality, though these are often tied to romanticized or unreliable sources rather than verifiable history.

Eureka Township is represented in the Sac County Board of Supervisors by Ranell Drake of District 1. It is represented in the Iowa Senate by Republican Craig Williams of District 6 and in the Iowa House of Representatives by Republican Gary Worthan of District 11. Eureka Township is represented in the United States House of Representatives by Republican Randy Feenstra of District 4.

PC Music gained prominence in 2014, polarizing critics with its hyper-modern, high-tempo trance sounds. FACT’s Joe Moynihan praised it as "the most compelling pop music in recent memory," while others, like The Quietus, dismissed it as "ear-splitting squeak'n'bass," reflecting the divisive reception of its saccharine, experimental approach to pop.

Suillus tomentosus, commonly known as the "Blue-Staining Slippery Jack," features a cap 3–10 cm wide, ranging in color from yellow to orange-yellow with grayish, brownish, or reddish tomentum. The cap is dry to viscid when fresh and distinguished by its woolly or scaly texture. The yellow tubes bruise blue when injured. The stipe, 3–8 cm tall and 1–2 cm wide, matches or is more orange than the cap and is glandular-dotted. Young spores are brownish, and the spore print is dark olive-brown to brown. Contact with the mushroom stains fingers brown, and it lacks a veil or annulus.
Fedyshyn lives in Lviv. Married to producer Vitalii Chovnyk. They have two sons: Yura and Oleh.

Terry Pratchett's *Discworld* series inspired a devoted fanbase, leading to dedicated conventions such as the first Discworld Convention, held in Manchester, UK, in 1996, where Pratchett often served as the guest of honour. Book launches were frequently accompanied by signing events, notable for their immense queues, sometimes spanning multiple floors, as Pratchett gave equal attention to every fan. His fans, diverse in age and gender, connected with his work deeply, sending him around 60 letters daily. Pratchett cherished his fans, stating, "My fans are everything to me," and appreciated their enthusiasm, even as he humorously noted that his mail was tamer than that of romance novelists.

The construction of the Taguig Integrated Terminal Exchange (ITX) was initially slated to begin in September 2016, but its groundbreaking ceremony occurred in January 2018. With an estimated cost of ₱5.20 billion, its operations, expected by 2020, were delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
