The Multnomah people received their name from their chief. Yet, the existence of their great chief named Multnomah has been up for debate. Other Native American tribes in the Columbia River Valley area spoke of him in their oral history, while Oregon historians dismissed him as just a myth. Therefore, there is conflicting evidence of whether or not he was real. The Oregon Historical Society had multiple presidents throughout the 1900s who dismissed him as only an imagined chief. However, on top of the oral descriptions of him there were writings including newspapers and journals, which indicate he was indeed real.

Elizabeth Hely Walshe was born in 1835 in Limerick to an evangelical Protestant family. Her father was a clergyman and she had at least one sister. She lived in Canada for some years but was back in Ireland by 1865. She was an accomplished artist and musician. She worked as a Sunday-school teacher. She also believed in the education of the poor. Walshe was a regular contributor to Leisure Hour Magazine and Sunday at Home. She also worked with George Etell Sargent on stories for children. Like many writers of that era the Irish Famine has an impact on the themes of her stories.

Pinus radiata is widely used in temperate California and similar climates worldwide for landscaping and forestry due to its fast growth and adaptability. It is particularly commonly grown as a landscape tree in coastal areas of California outside its native range, where the climate closely resembles its natural habitat. These areas feature temperate conditions, though the species does not tolerate temperatures below -15°C (5°F). P. radiata thrives in a broad range of soil types and climates, making it highly versatile. In optimal conditions, it can reach its full height within 40 years, making it ideal for forestry and landscaping. While its natural distribution is influenced by a combination of biotic and abiotic factors, human activity has significantly expanded its range along the California coast.

Helene P. Foley earned her B.A. from Swarthmore College in 1964 and went on to receive an M.A.T. in 1967 and an M.A. in 1968 from Yale University. She completed her Ph.D. in Classical Philology at Harvard University in 1975, with a doctoral thesis titled *Ritual Irony in the Bacchae and Other Late Euripidean Plays*. Foley began her teaching career at Stanford University before joining the faculty of Barnard College, Columbia University, in 1979, where she has served as department chair since 1981 and has been a member of Columbia’s graduate faculty in Classics since 1984. In 1998, she served as President of the American Philological Association, now known as the Society for Classical Studies. In Spring 2008, Foley was the Sather Professor of Classics at the University of California, Berkeley, where her lectures focused on "Reimagining Greek Tragedy on the U.S. Stage." These lectures culminated in her 2012 publication, *Reimagining Greek Tragedy on the American Stage*. Foley’s academic work has centered on Greek literature, women and gender in antiquity, and the performance and reception of classical drama, establishing her as a leading scholar in the field of Classics.

Zoya Semenduyeva immigrated to Israel in 1990, where her literary work continued to flourish. Her poems were published in the collection *At the Obelisk* in 1999, featuring works in Juhuri, Russian, and Hebrew. In 2007, she released the book *Daughter of Two Fathers*, which included a play and fairy tales. Semenduyeva's writings also appeared in the Israeli literary and journalistic almanac *Mabat*, and she became a member of the Union of Writers - Natives of the Caucasus in Israel in 1996. Her contributions to literature reflect her deep connection to her Mountain Jewish heritage and her ability to bridge cultures through language.

In 2003, *Flowers: The Jennifer Paige Hits Collection* was released by Edel Records following Jennifer Paige's departure from her record label. In October of that year, Paige confirmed she was no longer with Hollywood Records or Edel Records. After parting ways, she continued writing for other artists and compiling songs for a new studio project. In April 2006, her song "The Calling," co-written and produced with Chris Landon of Loudnoize Entertainment, was selected by Lifetime Television for the made-for-TV movie *Augusta, Gone*, based on the book of the same name.

During an interview with People in 2002, Martin expressed that he believes in "love", "the power of healing", and "God", thanks to his parents. The name he chose for his son Matteo means "gift from God". According to his statements in a 2021 interview, he still believes in God. He was raised Catholic but he said is not "the person who would ever look down upon one religion". He expressed that he also admires and likes Buddhist philosophy, but does not subscribe to the religion, since if he does, he cannot "be of anything else"; he does not want to be limited in certain aspects and follow a religion's specific rules. He tries to remain "open to everything" and makes "a concerted effort to always find new teachings and new paths" everywhere he goes and in every situation he finds himself in. Martin believes that everyone can "decide what makes them happy" and although "everyone needs to accept the life they were given", it does not mean they "should not live it as fully as possible".

Released in 1999, *Mezzamorphis* marked Delirious?'s first major push into the mainstream, reaching No. 25 on the UK Albums Chart and topping the UK Independent Albums Chart. Certified Silver in the UK, the album aimed to appeal to both Christian and secular audiences, but faced resistance from mainstream outlets like BBC Radio One. While the music press praised its intricate soundscapes, many core Christian fans criticized its lack of overt worship themes, sparking backlash and raising questions about the band's evolving identity.

The industrial sector of the state includes some of the key sectors like pharmaceutical, automobile, textiles etc. Sricity located in Chittoor district is an integrated business city which is home to firms including PepsiCo, Isuzu Motors, Cadbury India, Kellogg's, Colgate-Palmolive, Kobelco etc. The PepsiCo firm has its largest plant in India at Sri City. The state is also emerging as destination for the automobile industry which already hosts companies including Ashok Leyland in Krishna district, Hero Motors in Chittoor district, Kia Motors in Anantapur district.

Homer Bailey signed a one-year, $7 million contract with the Minnesota Twins on December 31, 2019. He made his debut on July 28, 2020, earning his first victory against the St. Louis Cardinals. Bailey was designated for assignment on September 25, 2020, and subsequently released by the Twins on September 28, 2020.

John Ponsonby was commissioned into the Royal Air Force as a pilot in April 1983, following nine years of service as an infantry officer in the British Army. On 10 April 1983, he was appointed to a permanent commission and promoted to flight lieutenant with seniority from 10 March 1980. He was subsequently promoted to squadron leader on 1 July 1988 and to wing commander on 1 January 1994. Ponsonby was appointed an Officer of the Order of the British Empire (OBE) in the 1998 New Year Honours List and served as an aide-de-camp to Queen Elizabeth II in 2001, relinquishing the role on 1 August 2002. He was promoted to air commodore on 1 January 2002 and awarded the Queen's Commendation for Valuable Service in the 2002 Operational Honours List for his gallant and distinguished services in Northern Ireland between 1 October 2001 and 31 March 2002. Ponsonby achieved the rank of air vice marshal on 4 January 2005, marking the culmination of his distinguished RAF career.

Luca Pacioli was born between 1446 and 1448 in the Tuscan town of Sansepolcro where he received an abbaco education. This was education in the vernacular (i.e., the local tongue) rather than Latin and focused on the knowledge required of merchants. His father was Bartolomeo Pacioli; however, Luca Pacioli was said to have lived with the Befolci family as a child in his birth town Sansepolcro. He moved to Venice around 1464, where he continued his own education while working as a tutor to the three sons of a merchant. It was during this period that he wrote his first book, a treatise on arithmetic for the boys he was tutoring. Between 1472 and 1475, he became a Franciscan friar. Thus, he could be referred to as Fra ('Friar') Luca.

"The Blackpool Letters is a scary calling card of monstrous production, talent and performance...Scott Liss and the Sixty-Six are the area’s rising secret weapon and there is no way in hell this band won’t go on to bigger and better things. My advice would be to immediately buy this disc and see these guys locally before they wind up in New York or LA." - John Pfeiffer, The Aquarian Weekly

Krensky was born in Chicago, Illinois in 1951 and attended New Trier High School (East). He received a B.A. in biology, Summa Cum Laude, and M.D. from the University of Pennsylvania. He was a Resident (medicine) and Fellow in Pediatrics at Children’s Hospital Boston and a research fellow at Dana Farber Cancer Institute, Boston. After one year on the faculty at Harvard and Boston Children’s Hospital, he moved to Stanford in 1984. He was on the faculty at Stanford for 23 years and developed the Children’s Health Initiative, a $500 million investment in research, education and clinical care, at Stanford and Lucile Packard Children's Hospital.

Francis Stephen Ruddy attended high school in New York, earned a Bachelor of Arts degree from the College of New Rochelle in 1959, served in the U.S. Army, received a Ph.D. in International Law from Cambridge University in 1969, and obtained degrees from Loyola University (1965) and NYU (1967).

In September 2019, it was announced that a sequel anime film of Given anime television series was in the works by adapting the second story arc of the manga. The key staff and cast members reprised their respective roles for the film. Fuji TV's new boys' love (yaoi) anime label, Blue Lynx handled the development of the film. The key staff, including director Hikaru Yamaguchi expressed their gratitude for the film.

In September 2016, it was discovered that iOS 10 weakened the encryption of local backups made with iTunes compared to iOS 9. The security firm ElcomSoft identified that iOS 10 introduced an alternative password verification mechanism that bypassed certain security checks, allowing passwords to be brute-forced approximately 2,500 times faster. ElcomSoft reported that up to six million password attempts per second could be made on iOS 10 backups, compared to 2,400 per second on iOS 9. Apple acknowledged the issue, attributing it to a password hashing weakness, and addressed it by removing the weak hash in the iOS 10.1 update. The flaw was tracked as CVE-2016-4685.

John Pfeiffer reviewed *The Blackpool Letters* by Scott Liss and the Sixty-Six, praising its "monstrous production, talent, and performance." He described the band as the area's "rising secret weapon" and confidently predicted their ascent to "bigger and better things." Pfeiffer advised readers not to worry about its appeal to pop aficionados, emphasizing the album's bold creativity and unique artistry as its defining strengths.

Plevitskaya was born Nadezhda Vasilievna Vinnikova to a peasant family in the village of Vinnikovo, near Kursk. She loved to sing, and after two years in a religious chorus, she became a professional singer in Kiev, where she married Edmund Plewicki, a Polish dancer. Soon, they moved to Moscow, where she began singing in the well-known Yar restaurant, whose specialty was gypsy bands with beautiful female singers. While on tour, at a concert in 1909, at the Nizhny Novgorod fair, she was heard by the great tenor Leonid Sobinov. He brought her to the attention of a wider public, which soon included the tsar's family as well as the opera singer Feodor Chaliapin.

The first race of the season was the Argentine Grand Prix, where Hill qualified 17th, although Edwards was on the back row of the grid. In the race, Hill was running tenth when his engine failed with a few laps remaining while Edwards finished 11th, two laps down on the winner Denny Hulme. In Brazil, Edwards qualified 25th and last, over two seconds slower than his nearest rival Richard Robarts, and Hill qualified 21st. The race was rain shortened and Hill finished 11th after Edwards retired on lap three with chassis problems. At the South African Grand Prix, Embassy Hill entered only one car, for Hill, who qualified 18th and finished 12th.

Parent was elected to the National Assembly of Quebec in a 1984 by-election in the Montreal division of Sauvé, winning a landslide victory in what had previously been a Parti Québécois (PQ) seat. The PQ were in government during this time, and Parent served for the next year in opposition. He was re-elected in the 1985 provincial election, in which the Liberals won a majority government under Robert Bourassa's leadership, and served as a government backbencher. He was appointed as chair of the province's education committee on February 11, 1986, and held the position until August 9, 1989.

John Cleveley the Younger (1747–1786), son of shipwright and artist John Cleveley, was the twin brother of Robert Cleveley. Both were raised and trained in Deptford's Royal Dockyard, following their father’s profession. Unlike their father, they reached wider audiences by exhibiting at the Free Society of Artists and the Royal Academy.

However, as China opened up and reformed post-1979, many Han acquired enough money to begin to travel. One of the favorite travel experiences of the wealthy was visits to minority areas, to see the exotic rituals of the minority peoples. Responding to this interest, many minority entrepreneurs, despite themselves perhaps never having grown up practicing the dances, rituals, or songs themselves, began to cater to these tourists by performing acts similar to what the older generation or the local residents told. In this way, the groups of people named Zhuang or other named minorities have begun to have more in common with their fellow co-ethnics, as they have adopted similar self-conceptions in response to the economic demand of consumers for their performances.
Raymond married Wilma Rose Tochluk on August 10, 1947. The couple had three children, and divorced in 1956. He married Bernetta Della Curti in September 1957, and they had one child.

When alcohol is consumed rectally, it bypasses the stomach and liver, entering the bloodstream directly through the blood vessels in the rectum and colon. Unlike oral consumption, the lower gastrointestinal tract lacks alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH), an enzyme that breaks ethanol into the toxic compound acetaldehyde. This bypass neutralizes the body's ability to metabolize alcohol before it reaches the bloodstream, leading to rapid intoxication. The liver, eventually exposed to high alcohol concentrations, can become overwhelmed, increasing the risk of severe damage. Acetaldehyde, more toxic than ethanol, contributes to many chronic effects of alcohol consumption.

Germano Almeida's first work, *O Dia das Calças Roladas*, was written in 1982 and published in 1983. It recounts a strike on the island of Santo Antão, drawing from real events. His most famous novel, *The Last Will and Testament of Senhor da Silva Araújo* (1989), tells the story of a businessman turned philanthropist who leaves his fortune to his illegitimate daughter, highlighting themes of colonialism and independence. A motion picture adaptation of this novel, directed by Portuguese filmmaker Francisco Manso, was released in 1997 and won an award at Brazil's Festival de Cinema de Gramado. The miners' Hovel is the workmen’s mess room and the place where miners could dry their wet pit clothes at the end of the day. A fire was kept lit in the hovel and wet clothes left hanging in there overnight were usually dry again by morning. The men could also brew a hot drink in the hovel, eat their 'snap'(lunch usually in a tin or cloth covered bowl) or fry bacon and egg on a shovel over the fire. In some pits with a good boss, a barrel of beer may be provided to take underground.

There are currently two products approved as maintenance therapy following treatment of inoperable locoregional Stage III NSCLC with induction chemotherapy: Tarceva (erlotinib), a targeted small molecule from Genentech, a member of the Roche Group, and Alimta (pemetrexed), a chemotherapeutic from Eli Lilly and Company. According to Robin McGrath, there are Inuit stories that show there was a history of conflict between the Inuit and the Dene, as well as others which may have involved Europeans. This conflict seems to have been instigated by both the Dene and the Inuit and possibly was caused by trade disputes but sometimes due to raids for women. One of the better known of these battles was recorded by European explorer, Samuel Hearne. In 1771, Samuel Hearne was the first European to explore the Coppermine River region. It was here that Matonabbee, leader of Hearne's Chipewyan Dene guides, and his companions massacred a Copper Inuit group at Bloody Falls. Further exploration did not take place until the period of 1820–1853, which included the Sir John Franklin expeditions of 1821 and 1825. John Rae encountered Copper Inuit at Rae River in 1847, and at Cape Flinders and Stromness Bay in 1851. During the McClure Arctic expedition, Irish explorer, Robert McClure abandoned his ship, HMS Investigator, at Mercy Bay on Banks Island in 1853 during his search for Franklin's lost expedition. It provided extensive amounts of wood, copper, and iron which the Copper Inuit used for years. Richard Collinson explored the area in 1850–1855.

The neural pathway of the jamming avoidance response (JAR) in *Gymnarchus* is nearly identical to that in *Eigenmannia* and *Apteronotus*. S-units encode amplitude modulations, while O-units, distinct from P-units in Gymnotiformes, code for phase differences within a narrow intensity range. Phase differences between the electric organ discharge (EOD) and stimulus are calculated in the electrosensory lateral line lobe, with O-units sharply tuned to the EOD frequency.

Development also occurred at the University of New South Wales (UNSW), where developers implemented L4 on several 64-bit platforms. Their work resulted in L4/MIPS and L4/Alpha, resulting in Liedtke's original version being retrospectively named L4/x86. Like Liedtke's original kernels, the UNSW kernels (written in a mix of assembly and C) were unportable and each implemented from scratch. With the release of the highly portable L4Ka::Pistachio, the UNSW group abandoned their own kernels in favor of producing highly tuned ports of L4Ka::Pistachio, including the fastest-ever reported implementation of message passing (36 cycles on the Itanium architecture). The group has also demonstrated that device drivers can perform equally well at user-level as in-kernel, and developed Wombat, a highly portable version of Linux on L4 that runs on x86, ARM, and MIPS processors. On XScale processors, Wombat context-switching costs are up to 50 times lower than in native Linux.

Seismic-induced ore deposits are often associated with regions of active faulting, where fractures and crushed zones act as conduits for hydrothermal solutions, leading to mineralization. A 2015 case study by Christine Howald in Nevada focused on the relationship between seismic activity and mineral deposits, particularly gold and silver. The study identified and dated two separate seismic events using fault zone analysis and geochronological methods. These events triggered the upwelling of hydrothermal fluids, initiating mineralization within the fault zone. Mineral deposition was facilitated by the presence of fractures, which provided pathways for fluid flow, and by changes in pressure and temperature conditions. The study highlights the importance of structural features, such as fault zones, and the role of seismic activity in creating favorable conditions for ore deposit formation.

The fluorinase catalyses an SN2-type nucleophilic substitution at the C-5' position of SAM, while L-methionine acts as a neutral leaving group. The fluorinase-catalysed reaction is estimated to be between 106 to 1015 times faster than the uncatalysed reaction, a significant rate enhancement. Despite this, the fluorinase is still regarded as a slow enzyme, with a turnover number (kcat) of 0.06 min−1. The high kinetic barrier to reaction is attributed to the strong solvation of fluoride ion in water, resulting in a high activation energy associated with stripping solvating water molecules from aqueous fluoride ion, converting fluoride into a potent nucleophile within the active site.

The DNA damage response (DDR) is a highly coordinated cellular mechanism that detects and responds to DNA double-strand breaks (DSBs), one of the most lethal forms of DNA damage. Detection of DSBs begins with the recruitment of the MRN complex (Mre11-Rad50-Nbs1) to the break site, which facilitates the activation of the ataxia-telangiectasia mutated (ATM) kinase, a central mediator of DDR signaling. ATM, along with ATR (ataxia-telangiectasia and Rad3-related) and DNA-PK (DNA-dependent protein kinase), is recruited to damaged DNA and phosphorylates key downstream targets, including H2AX, MDC1, and Chk2, to initiate signaling cascades. These cascades regulate critical cellular responses such as cell-cycle arrest, apoptosis, and the activation of DNA repair pathways. DDR not only ensures the detection and signaling of DSBs but also orchestrates their repair through pathways like homologous recombination and non-homologous end joining. ATM, in particular, plays a global role in mediating cellular responses to DSBs, including responses to oxidative stress. By integrating damage detection with repair processes, DDR maintains genomic stability and prevents chromosomal translocations and deletions, which are hallmarks of cancer and other genomic disorders.

There are no forms of complementary or alternative medicine that are evidence-based for allergic rhinitis. Therapeutic efficacy of alternative treatments such as acupuncture and homeopathy is not supported by available evidence. Overall, the quality of evidence for complementary-alternative medicine is not strong enough to be recommended by the American Academy of Allergy, Asthma and Immunology.

The cost of CO2 emissions avoided through calcium looping (Ca-looping) is generally lower than that of the oxyfuel combustion process, which ranges from EUR20-30 per tonne for post-combustion capture. This cost advantage arises from the reduced oxygen demand in Ca-looping calciners, requiring only a fraction of the oxygen needed in oxyfuel processes. Consequently, air separation unit (ASU) costs are significantly reduced, as ITM technology or other innovations can further lower oxygen supply expenses. In Ca-looping, CO2 is captured from both the oxy-fired combustion and the main combustor, enhancing overall efficiency. Sensitivity analysis (Figure 3) highlights that the cost of CO2 capture in Ca-looping is primarily influenced by variables such as sorbent performance, calciner efficiency, and energy integration. These factors, combined with the system's ability to minimize energy use in the calciner, make Ca-looping a cost-effective alternative for CO2 capture compared to conventional methods.

Simon criticized the notion that natural resources are finite: 'Incredible as it may seem at first, the term "finite" is not only inappropriate but is downright misleading when applied to natural resources, both from the practical and philosophical points of view. As with many important arguments, the finiteness issue is "just semantic." Yes the semantics of resources scarcity muddle public discussion and bring about wrongheaded policy decisions. [...] The quantity of the services we obtain from copper that will ever be available to use should not be considered finite because there is no method (even in principle) of making an appropriate count of it, given the problem of the economic definition of "copper," the possibility of using copper more efficiently, the possibility of creating copper or its economic equivalent from other materials, the possibility of recycling copper, or even obtaining copper from sources beyond planet Earth, and thus the lack of boundaries to the sources from which "copper" might be drawn.'

In the summer of 2019, at the initiative of the Association of the French aerospace industry, the Paris Air Show-2019 was held in Paris. Within the framework of the exhibition, an adviser to the Minister of Defense Industry of Azerbaijan Farrukh Vezirov met with the heads of French companies such as Nexter, Thales, CS Communication, Lacroix and others. Various projects were discussed: joint production of wheeled armored vehicles, etc.

On 21 September 2012, as part of a larger cabinet reshuffle, prime minister Jens Stoltenberg announced that Tajik would become the new Minister of Culture. She replaced Anniken Huitfeldt who became the new Minister of Labour and Social affairs. At the time, she was the youngest ever to become a minister in Norway (at 29), and her appointment made her the first Muslim to serve in the Norwegian cabinet.

Lucie Papineau, born in Quebec, Canada, earned a bachelor's degree in communications from the Université du Québec à Montréal. She wrote for *Petit Devoir*, a children's section of *Le Devoir*, from 1986 to 1988. Since 1998, she has been a regular contributor to *Magazine Enfants Québec*, writing a parenting column. Papineau is also an editor and author at the publisher Dominique et compagnie, where she has created numerous acclaimed children's books.

Basrur became the Medical Officer of Health for the East York Health Unit until East York was merged into the city of Toronto in 1998, when she became the first Medical Officer of Health for the new amalgamated city. She was widely hailed for her work during the 2003 Severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS) outbreak in Toronto, appearing in numerous television interviews on international networks, such as CNN. Basrur wrote a journal article with Barbara Yaffe and Bonnie Henry in the 2004 Canadian Journal of Public Health entitled "SARS: A Local Public Health Perspective". In August 2018, Major League Wrestling (MLW) announced a working agreement with Mexican-based Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA). On February 5, 2020, MLW revealed its first co-promoted event with AAA, scheduled for March 13, 2020, at Auditorio Fausto Gutierrez in Tijuana, Baja California, Mexico, in partnership with AAA's local affiliate, Promociones EMW. This marked MLW's second event in Mexico, following its October 5, 2019, collaboration with The Crash.

Popular alignment algorithms like BLAST and MegaBLAST use a non-spaced model with contiguous seeds, requiring exact matches along the seed length, which fails if a mismatching base pair occurs. In contrast, spaced models, as used by PatternHunter, allow non-consecutive matches, improving sensitivity by identifying similarities even with mismatches in predefined seed patterns.

The torpedo boat destroyer was commissioned into the Royal Navy on 30 April 1895 at Portsmouth under the command of Lieutenant & Commander Godfrey H B Mundy for service on the Mediterranean Station based at Malta. Ardent departed Portsmouth on 14 May 1895, stopping at Plymouth to join the third-class cruiser HMS Pique. They departed for Malta on 15 May 1895. Upon her arrival she became tender to the fleet flagship, the battleship Ramillies. Among Ardent′s duties was the training of stokers in the management of water-tube boilers.

Shortly after his professional career, he was coach of the Canadian Junior Football League (CJFL) team the Hamilton Hurricanes. He led them to an undefeated season in 1990. He later taught gym class at Vankleek Hill Collegiate Institute.

The Huffington Post journalist Mina Zaher criticized the depiction of the sexual awakening between Sam and Suzy, expressing discomfort with the scene where Sam touches Suzy's breasts, calling it "a step further or perhaps too far". Zaher questioned if the children's sexuality could have been portrayed in a more appropriate way. Reviewing the scene where the characters dance wearing only their underwear, the Catholic News Service stated "The interlude doesn't quite cross over into the full-blown exploitation of children, but it teeters on that edge". Professor Carol Siegel summed up the portrayal as "delicate and to a large extent inoffensive".

Dallas prosecutor Douglas D. Mulder charged Adams with the crime, despite the evidence against Harris, apparently because Harris was a juvenile at the time and Adams, as an adult, could be sentenced to death under Texas law. Adams testified that after leaving the drive-in movie, Harris dropped Adams off at his motel, where Adams and his brother watched TV and then went to sleep. He claimed he was not in the car when the shooting happened. Harris testified that Adams was not only in the car, but was the driver, as well as the shooter of Officer Wood.

On Rotten Tomatoes, *Paddleton* holds an approval rating of 94% based on an unspecified number of reviews, with an average rating not mentioned in the provided context. The critical consensus praises its tonal balance and strong lead performances. On Metacritic, the film has a weighted average score of 70, based on critics' reviews, indicating generally favorable feedback. Metacritic's system aggregates reviews using a nuanced approach, assigning scores from 0 to 100 and calculating a weighted average to reflect critical reception.

Giosue Giuppone began his sporting career in bicycling, winning three Italian national championships in middle-distance racing. In the early 1900s, he transitioned to motorcycle racing, notably competing for the Peugeot team and earning distinction as the first Italian to participate in the Isle of Man Tourist Trophy. Giuppone later raced large-capacity, ultra-lightweight cars for Peugeot, with racers weighing as little as 50 kg and featuring unlimited engine capacities of up to two liters. In 1908, he won the Torino Cup, Copa Catalunya, and Circuito delle Madonie, and in 1909, he triumphed in the Coupe des Voiturettes. His achievements solidified his reputation as a versatile and pioneering figure in early motorsport history.

The year 1965 marked the beginning of major military activity in what became known as the secret war in Laos. Although the full extent of the conflict was not revealed to the U.S. public until 1969-70, the war was not all that secret. News of the fighting frequently found its way into the pages of the Bangkok Post, The New York Times, and other newspapers. Congress was kept well informed. As former CIA Director Richard Helms has pointed out, the Appropriations subcommittees were regularly briefed on the war funds. Also, Senator Stuart Symington and other Congressmen visited Laos and gave every indication of approving what transpired. They believed, Helms noted, that "It was a much cheaper and better way to fight a war in Southeast Asia than to commit American troops."

The qualifiers went through to face members of the top 16. In this round, Davis came from 2–4 down to beat Joe Swail 5–4, in a match where both players missed chances. In the deciding frame, Davis won on the pink ball after Swail had missed the brown. After the match, Davis said it was a historic day as he had never won a match in the country. Stephen Lee received a walkover to the next round after his opponent Robin Hull withdrew due to medical reasons. World number 41 Stephen Maguire made a break of 89 in the final frame of his match against Peter Ebdon to win 5–4, and world number three O'Sullivan opened his match against Marco Fu with a century break of 110 and went on to win 5–1. Chris Small, who suffers from the spinal condition ankylosing spondylitis, whitewashed UK champion Matthew Stevens 5–0, in a match lasting almost three hours and which saw Stevens lose two frames on the black ball. Neil Robertson defeated Ken Doherty 5–3, and Joe Perry beat David Roe by the same scoreline. In the last match of the day, David Gray beat Fergal O'Brien 5–3.

Tim Bonner began his collegiate career at the University of Louisville, signing with the Cardinals as a three-star recruit in 2015. During his redshirt freshman season, he was accused of possessing a gun in his dorm, a claim he denied, leading to his dismissal in October 2015. Bonner transferred to East Mississippi Community College, where he was featured in season two of Netflix's *Last Chance U*. At EMCC, he recorded eight tackles and two sacks before committing to Florida Atlantic University to continue his football career.

Unlike thalattosauroids, the palate (roof of the mouth) was completely toothless. The vomer is long and slender, forming the lower midline of the snout. The succeeding pterygoids are larger, with a pair of sharp muscle scars at their lateral extent. A T-shaped ectopterygoid forms a strut between the pterygoid and the jugal, while the less complete palatine connects the pterygoid to the maxilla.

Nnamdi Moweta began managing the 'Warrior Family from Africa' in 1985, a stable that included Ghanaian fighter Prince Mama Mohammed, who challenged for the WBC Light Heavyweight World Title that December. Mohammed's career record stood at 12 wins, 2 losses, and 1 draw. The stable also featured Nigerian welterweight Young Dick Tiger, a relative of former world middleweight champion Dick Tiger, who was twice a world titleholder before his death in 1971.

Anna Page Scott (born October 13, 1863, in Dubuque, Iowa) was an American illustrator and painter. She studied at the Art Institute of Chicago, the Pennsylvania Academy of Fine Arts, and the Académie Colarossi in Paris. Around 1889, she settled in New York City, working as an illustrator. In 1897, she moved to Rochester, New York, where she began teaching at the Mechanics' Institute, continuing there until 1913.

If hot-rodding Fenders was the first breakthrough, the second was developing an extra gain stage for the guitar input. Smith was building a preamplifier for Lee Michaels, who needed a pre-amp to drive his new Crown DC-300 power amplifiers. Smith added an extra tube gain stage to the preamp, with three variable gain controls at different points in the circuit (what is now called a "cascaded" design), creating the first high-gain amplifier. He set about designing a guitar amplifier around the new principle, and in 1972 the Mark I was released.

The first COVID-19 lockdown in Melbourne began in March 2020, prompting Vika and Linda Bull to perform a gospel song every Sunday morning on social media. Dubbed the "Sunday Sing Song," the weekly performances uplifted spirits, with Vika noting they boosted morale and received positive feedback from their audience during challenging times.

In 2020, Hinchcliffe joined NBC Sports as analyst for IndyCar races and select IMSA races when he did not enter as a driver. In 2022, he rejoined NBC Sports as a full-time analyst joining play-by-play voice Leigh Diffey and analyst Townsend Bell, replacing fellow Canadian Paul Tracy, and will serve as an analyst for some IMSA races. In October of 2022, Hinchcliffe joined the crew of F1's "Weekend Warmup" for its 'grand-prix week' editions for the United States Grand Prix and the Mexico City Grand Prix.

*Star Odyssey* received a 1/5 rating from *Creature Feature*, which described it as "dreadful" with poor acting and bleak elements. *Popular Mechanics* called it a "dreadful trashpile," while *TechRadar* noted its reception depends on the viewer's mood, finding it either insufferable or hilariously bad.

Gerda Maria Ahlm was born in Västerås, Sweden. She studied at the Royal Swedish Academy of Art in Stockholm (1889-91) and later studied etching with the engraver Axel Tallberg (1896). She made several trips around Europe for further study in the years between 1892 and 1900. She studied at the Académie Colarossi in Paris and traveled in Italy, England, Norway, and Belgium.

Version 7.0 introduced a redesigned GUI. Components were renamed and reorganized; the Anti-hacker module was renamed to the Firewall, and the Anti-Spy module was integrated with the Privacy Control module. PC World described the new interface as "intuitive" and "great-looking".

Bob Randall first contested the seat of Henley Beach as a Liberal in 1977 and won it in the 1979 election, representing the Liberal Party. He held the seat with a margin of 0.8% after the 1980 Norwood by-election but lost it to Labor in the 1982 election. Randall contested the seat again as a Liberal in subsequent elections, including 1989, but was unsuccessful. Following the 1991 redistribution, Henley Beach was renamed Colton. In the 1993 election, Randall was defeated in Liberal preselection by former Adelaide Lord Mayor Steve Condous. He subsequently ran as an Independent for Colton but was easily defeated, later returning to the Liberal Party before joining Family First.

Even a considerable amount of time since the case was handed down, a few appellate decisions have ventured to expand or clarify Marcus. In a 1981 case, United States v. Espinoza, the Fourth Circuit rejected a defense claim that the holding required judicial review of all material alleged to be obscene. After the Eighth Circuit affirmed Marcus's First Amendment protections extended to searches intended to find indicia of membership in an organization in 1983, the Fifth Circuit held 12 years later that it did not apply to seizures of material with First Amendment implications when that material was sought not for its possible content but to corroborate a witness's testimony.

On 17 February 2021, he scored his first Champions League goal in a 3–2 away win over Sevilla in the 2020–21 UEFA Champions League round of 16. On 13 May 2021, Dahoud started in the 2021 DFB-Pokal final against RB Leipzig. He won his second trophy with Borussia Dortmund after winning the final, contributing with an assist to a Jadon Sancho goal in the 5th minute.

In February 1930, Dominican President Horacio Vásquez was overthrown in a coup led by General Rafael Leonidas Trujillo, with support from Rafael Estrella Ureña. Following the coup, Estrella Ureña became provisional president. Trujillo and Estrella Ureña arranged to run together in the May 1930 presidential election, with Trujillo as the presidential candidate and Estrella Ureña as his running mate for vice president. Rafael Brache, a supporter of the coup, was appointed to a government position under the new regime.

Zanthoxylum asiaticum is used medicinally by various African communities, including the Maasai, who treat malaria, cough, and influenza with it. The roots contain coumarins with antiplasmodial activity, and extracts have shown antiviral effects against H1N1 influenza in laboratory studies. Overharvesting from the wild may threaten its populations, necessitating sustainable management practices.

Zeineddin joined Platense at the age of fourteen, having previously spent three years with Vélez Sarsfield. He was promoted into Platense's first-team squad during 2020, with the centre-forward penning his first professional contract on 13 November. Zeineddin made the substitute's bench for a Primera Nacional match with Deportivo Morón on 5 December, with manager Juan Manuel Llop subsequently selecting him to come on with thirty-three minutes remaining of a 1–1 home draw; he replaced Daniel Vega. He made his first start on 11 December away to Estudiantes, which preceded one further appearance in a campaign that ended with promotion.

The qualification period for the men's triple jump at the 2004 Summer Olympics began on 1 January 2003 and ended on 9 August 2004. Athletes had to achieve a minimum distance of 16.95 meters to qualify, with each National Olympic Committee (NOC) allowed to enter up to three athletes meeting the "A" standard. Since the 1930 Olympic Congress, the maximum per nation has been three. An alternative "B" standard of 16.55 meters applied if no athletes met the "A" standard.

Next, the prosecution called Virginia Wainscott, Robert Newsom's oldest daughter, to the stand. When questioned about the investigation into her father's disappearance, Wainscott described how she had "hunted on all of the paths and walks and every place for him" before she "learned where the bones were put" on the evening of June 24, 1855. She described finding bones in the ashes that were found in and around the cabin where Celia lived, as well as a buckle, buttons, and a knife, which she identified as having belonged to her father.

Other assumptions can produce other results. Some of the authors of the 2004 UN report assumed that life expectancy would rise slowly and continuously. The projections in the report assume this with no upper limit, though at a slowing pace depending on circumstances in individual countries. By 2100, the report assumed life expectancy to be from 66 to 97 years, and by 2300 from 87 to 106 years, depending on the country. Based on that assumption, they expect that rising life expectancy will produce small but continuing population growth by the end of the projections, ranging from 0.03 to 0.07 percent annually. The hypothetical feasibility (and wide availability) of life extension by technological means would further contribute to long term (beyond 2100) population growth.

Aside from his membership in the Muiderkring (which was not as formerly supposed, an official club), at the start of the 1630s he was also in touch with René Descartes, with Rembrandt, and the painter Jan Lievens. He became friends with John Donne, and translated his poems into Dutch. He was unable to write poetry for months because of his anguish over his wife's death, but eventually he composed, inspired by Petrarch, the sonnet Op de dood van Sterre (On the death of Sterre), which was well received. He added the poem to his Dagh-werck, which he left unfinished: the day he has described has not ended yet, but his Sterre is already dead. After sending the unfinished work to different friends for approval, he eventually published it in 1658 as part of his Koren-bloemen.

In May 2012, France held a presidential election in which Socialist François Hollande defeated incumbent Nicolas Sarkozy. Hollande campaigned against austerity measures, vowing to shift Europe's economic approach and protect France's social benefits. He promised to eliminate the national deficit by 2017 and proposed a 75% tax on income exceeding one million euros. Hollande also pledged to restore the retirement age to 60 for those who had worked 41 years, reversing Sarkozy's reforms. In education, he aimed to hire 60,000 new teachers over five years and abolish homework to reduce inequality. Additionally, he proposed measures to regulate housing costs. Following his victory, Hollande's Socialist Party secured a majority in the National Assembly during the June legislative elections, solidifying his mandate to implement these reforms.

She contested the beauty pageant "Lassana Muhuna", while she was in school times and won the title. Then she acted in the film Hathara Peraliya directed by L.M. Perera. Her maiden cinematic experience came through 1967 film Pipena Kumudu, directed by Ruby de Mel. Her father allowed Sumana to go only for that movie due to Ruby de Mel. However, she lied to her father and later played a lot of other movies such as Rena Giraw, Dahasak Sithuvili, Samanala Kumariyo and Hathara Peraliya.

Accelerated soil burial tests are conducted to evaluate the biodegradability and durability of materials under controlled conditions that replicate biologically active soil environments. These tests simulate high humidity and temperatures, often around 29°C, to mimic natural degradation processes. Samples are typically buried for periods ranging from weeks to several months, depending on the study, and are occasionally exposed to air to assess degradation under alternating conditions. The experimental conditions must reflect the material's intended service life, with soil activity verified using a reference material like cotton, which should degrade within one to two weeks. Key environmental factors, such as soil humidity and temperature, are carefully controlled, and detailed records of the soil's microbiological activity are maintained.

The Antebellum, an 8,212-square-foot estate and gardens in Grogan's Point, was inspired by Houmas. Developed by the Westbrook Building Company in 1988, it was one of six historically inspired estates and inspired three additional plantation estates in East Shore.

The base of the building contains a steel superstructure with the Shops at Columbus Circle, Jazz at Lincoln Center, broadcast studios, and originally AOL Time Warner's headquarters. The towers' concrete superstructures rest above the base. Structurally, the building's base also includes the steel-framed lower sections of both towers. The steel frame extends 315 feet (96 m) high below the north tower and 349 feet (106 m) high below the south tower. Several column arrangements are used because of the differing needs of each tenant. Twenty-four entrances were originally provided at the base. To avoid interfering with the entrances and other open spaces, the building uses diagonal steel columns; concrete columns with stepped notches; and columns hanging from trusses.

The label has released music with a consistent sound that Clive Martin, writing for Vice, described as "A playful composite of disregarded sounds and genres". Lanre Bakare, writing for The Guardian, identified the music's elements as "the huge synth blasts favoured by Eurodance chart-botherers such as Cascada, grime's sub-bass, and happy hardcore's high-pitched vocal range". The styles and influences of music incorporated include bubblegum dance, Balearic trance, wonky and electro house. Cook cites Korean & Japanese pop music and gyaru culture, as well as the production work of Max Martin and Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis. His production involves layering discordant sounds on top of each other to produce chaotic mixes, similar to the techniques used in black MIDI music. Abrupt shifts in timbre and rhythm are used to create multiple perspectives of a personality. Cook also indirectly cites American musician Conlon Nancarrow as a source of inspiration in the PC Music Pop Cube Trailer 1.

The saying "nice guys finish last" is a condensation by journalists of a quotation by Durocher—he did not originally say this himself, though it has often been attributed to him, and he did appropriate it as his own. The original quotation was "The nice guys are all over there, in seventh place" (July 6, 1946) about the 1946 New York Giants—seventh place was next to last place in the National League. This was shortly afterwards rendered as "'Nice Guys' Wind Up in Last Place, Scoffs Lippy", thence its present form.

In 1992, director Barry Cockcroft created *Hannah Hauxwell: Innocent Abroad*, documenting Hannah Hauxwell's first trips outside the UK to France, Germany, Austria, Switzerland, and Italy, where she met the Pope. The series' popularity led to a follow-up documentary, *Hannah: USA*, filmed during her 1993 trip to the United States, showcasing her continued adventures and endearing her further to audiences worldwide.

Brandon was born in Hervey Bay, Queensland and moved to Brisbane in February, 2016 to pursue his music career and study at university. He graduated from Queensland University of Technology with a Bachelor of Music, then moved to Orlando, Florida to work with The Walt Disney Company in 2019. Having returned to Australia in February 2020, he is working on new music for release in late 2020.

Kenya is not included in the Cooperation Master Plan. Decentralized cooperation and NGDOs are the main actors. Currently, several Spanish NGDOs work in Kenya, including ANIDAN, the HIVDA Association, Más Por them and the Pablo Horstmann Foundation. Finally, Spain is an important contributor to both the United Nations Environment Program (UNEP) and the Program of the United Nations for Human Settlements (UN-HABITAT), both based in Nairobi.

Jesús Julio was born in Aspariegos, Spain. Jesús Julio was married to Rosa Urbón since 1995. Jesús Julio ‍acquired his law degree from University of Valladolid. Jesús Julio has worked in all administrations. Jesús Julio served as Secretary-General PP of Valladolid from 2012 to 2017. Jesús Julio ‍also served as the executive adviser to the secretary of state of the territorial agency of the ministry of public administration, government of Spain.

The journal *Infection and Immunity* was established in 1970 to provide a dedicated platform for publishing original research on infection and immunity, previously scattered across various journals. Erwin Neter, affiliated with the University at Buffalo, served as its first editor-in-chief, reflecting the growing need for specialized dissemination in microbiology and immunology.

The primary goal of treating a mallet finger injury is to restore function and alignment by allowing the tendon or bone to heal properly. Most cases are managed nonsurgically with a splint, worn continuously for 6–8 weeks. Treatment should ideally begin within a week, with nighttime splinting continuing for 1–2 weeks afterward.

The first article about *Incilius melanochlorus* was published by Edward Drinker Cope in 1875, where he described the toad as *Bufo melanochlorus*. In 1877, Cope formally described it as a novel species under the same name. The holotype, thought to be a preserved specimen, was collected before 1877 by an unknown individual and taken to the Academy of Natural Sciences in Philadelphia. The species name derives from the Ancient Greek words μελανω (melanō), meaning "blackened" or "dark," and χλωρός (khlōrós), meaning "green" or "pale green." The English name for the species, "Black-green Toad," was likely coined later by scientists updating common names to reflect its taxonomy and coloration.

In Binningen, as of 2000, 57,864 individuals (34.7% of the population) had completed non-mandatory upper secondary education, while 27,603 (16.6%) had completed higher education (university or Fachhochschule). Of those with tertiary education, 44.4% were Swiss men, 31.1% Swiss women, 13.9% non-Swiss men, and 10.6% non-Swiss women. Additionally, 5,820 students in Binningen came from another municipality, while 1,116 residents attended schools outside the municipality.

In 1955, Coltrane was freelancing in Philadelphia while studying with Sandole when he received a call from trumpeter Miles Davis. Davis had been successful in the 1940s, but his reputation and work had been damaged in part by heroin addiction; he was again active and about to form a quintet. Coltrane was with this edition of the Davis band (known as the "First Great Quintet"—along with Red Garland on piano, Paul Chambers on bass, and Philly Joe Jones on drums) from October 1955 to April 1957 (with a few absences). During this period Davis released several influential recordings that revealed the first signs of Coltrane's growing ability. This quintet, represented by two marathon recording sessions for Prestige in 1956, resulted in the albums Cookin', Relaxin', Workin', and Steamin'. The "First Great Quintet" disbanded due in part to Coltrane's heroin addiction.

WDSC-TV shut down its analog signal on February 17, 2009, operating on UHF channel 15 before the shutdown. Its analog signal had been operating at significantly reduced power since 2000 due to major equipment failure. After the analog shutdown, WDSC-TV's digital signal continued broadcasting on UHF channel 33, with digital television receivers displaying it as virtual channel 15 through the use of PSIP.

Since May 2009, the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) has utilized its fleet of American-supplied F-16 multirole fighter jets to conduct precision airstrikes against militant positions in North-West Pakistan. Equipped with high-resolution infrared sensor pods provided by the United States, 10 F-16s carried out detailed reconnaissance in the Swat Valley before launching attacks. The PAF has flown over 300 combat missions in the Swat Valley and more than 100 in South Waziristan, targeting mountain hideouts, training centers, and ammunition depots. Using precision-guided bombs, the F-16s have destroyed key militant infrastructure, including buildings containing ammunition caches. These operations, supported by reconnaissance data from drones, marked a significant enhancement in the PAF's ability to conduct targeted strikes in challenging terrain.

Brittney Sykes, in addition to her WNBA career, plays internationally during the fall and winter for Hapoel Petah Tikva, an Israeli team based in Petah Tikva and part of the Ligat ha'Al league. She is joined by her former Syracuse University teammate Alexis Peterson, who played for the Seattle Storm during her 2017 rookie WNBA season. Most recently, Peterson was waived by the Las Vegas Aces in May 2023.

Prior to the 2017 contest, Slovenia had participated in the Eurovision Song Contest twenty-two times since its first entry in 1993. Slovenia's highest placing in the contest, to this point, has been seventh place, which the nation achieved on two occasions: in 1995 with the song "Prisluhni mi" performed by Darja Švajger and in 2001 with the song "Energy" performed by Nuša Derenda. The country's only other top ten result was achieved in 1997 when Tanja Ribič performing "Zbudi se" placed tenth. Since the introduction of semi-finals to the format of the contest in 2004, Slovenia had thus far only managed to qualify to the final on four occasions. In 2016, Slovenia was represented by ManuElla and the song "Blue and Red", but the country didn't qualify for the final.

The *Love Hina* manga was adapted into a 24-episode anime television series by Xebec, a division of Production I.G. Directed by Yoshiaki Iwasaki and written by Sho Aikawa, the series aired on TV Tokyo from April 19 to September 27, 2000. The opening theme, "Sakura Saku," and the closing theme, "Kimi Sae Ireba," were both written by Ritsuko Okazaki and performed by Megumi Hayashibara. The CD single featuring these themes debuted at number 7 on the Oricon charts. A bonus 25th episode was later created and released as a DVD exclusive. Character designs for the series were handled by Makoto Uno.

Specialists note the oldest mentions for Eastoft (Lincolnshire and West Riding of Yorkshire) Eschetoft around 1170, Esketoft around 1200 and Esktoft 13th century and suggest an Old Scandinavian origin, with the name formed from eski "ash-tree" and toft (< Old Norse topt) "a homestead, the site of a house and its out-buildings", sometimes toft may also signify "a settlement site and its accompanying land". On this basis, it would mean "Homestead, house or curtilage where ash trees are growing". The difficulty to articulate the group /skt/ and the attraction of the common word East- explain the final evolution Eastoft. On this basis, it was finally understood as "Homestead with enclosure to the east of Crowle. Toft is sometimes associated with tree names. Similar place names in Normandy include Ectot-l'Auber and Ectot-lès-Baons (Eschetoth, Esketoth 1074). from eski "ash-tree" and toft. Eshetoftes, YWR, Eshetoftes 1158–67. < OE æsċ "ash-tree", is the Anglo-Scandinavian version of this place name.

A Little Bit TV, the fifth incarnation of the *A Little Bit...* series of television programmes, began in 2012, profiling some of the most well-known television presenters from fifty years of Irish television. The series followed its predecessors, including *A Little Bit Country* (2007), which profiled performers in the country and Irish music genre, *A Little Bit Showband* (2008), focused on Ireland’s showband era, *A Little Bit Funny* (2009), which highlighted popular comedians of the past, and *A Little Bit Eurovision* (2011), celebrating the history of Ireland in the Eurovision Song Contest. The launch of *A Little Bit TV* coincided with RTÉ Television’s celebration of 50 years of broadcasting, marking a continuation of the series’ exploration of Irish cultural history.

The Rolling Stones' compilation album *Jump Back* was released in 1993, featuring liner notes where Mick Jagger reflected on "Wild Horses." Jagger recalled originally working on the song with Gram Parsons, whose version with the Flying Burrito Brothers was released slightly before the Stones' own. While many speculate the song was written about Marianne Faithfull, Jagger dismissed this, stating that their relationship had ended by then. He described the song as deeply personal, evocative, and emotionally resonant. Keith Richards, who co-wrote the track, described its creation as a classic example of his collaboration with Jagger, with Richards crafting the riff and chorus while Jagger developed the verses. Richards compared the song’s theme to "Satisfaction," describing it as expressing the longing and frustration of being far from where one wants to be.

The university is a daughter institution of St. Andrew's Scots School, an institution founded by Scottish immigrants in 1838. It is named after Saint Andrew, the patron saint of Scotland, and its crest is composed of the Saltire (the flag of Scotland), and thistles (the national flower of Scotland). The current institution was formally established on September 1, 1988, by the Scottish Civil Educational Association of San Andrés (ACEESA). This entity was superseded in 2010 by the University of San Andrés Foundation(FUdeSA).

In April, Federico Macheda began his second loan spell with Birmingham City during their visit to Doncaster Rovers, where the Blues secured a 3–1 victory with two goals from Macheda and one from Lee Novak. The win, which ended Doncaster's nine-game unbeaten home run, lifted Birmingham to 20th in the Championship table. Macheda's second goal, a stunning 35-yard strike, was later awarded Birmingham City's Goal of the Season. In a subsequent match against Middlesbrough, Novak suffered a facial injury during first-half stoppage time but continued to play. Against Nottingham Forest, Birmingham conceded an early second-minute goal to Matt Derbyshire, and despite Paul Robinson's late header, described by skysports.com as "agonizingly close," they lost 1–0. These results highlighted Birmingham's struggle to avoid relegation during a tense end to the season.

Brian Moore began his academic career as a Lecturer in Psychology at the University of Reading from 1971 to 1977, spending 1973–74 as a Fulbright-Hays Senior Scholar and Visiting Professor at Brooklyn College, City University of New York. In 1977, he was appointed University Lecturer in Experimental Psychology at the University of Cambridge, becoming a Reader in 1989 and a Professor in 1995. He became Emeritus Professor in 2014. Appointed a Fellow of Wolfson College in 1983, he is now an Emeritus Fellow.

Peest is an esdorp which developed around the heath. It was first mentioned around 1300 as "apud Peest". The etymology of the name is unclear. The farms which were constructed prior to 1850 are parallel to the communal pasture while the later farms are at right angles. In 1840, the village had 80 inhabitants.

DCPA is released directly into the environment during its use as a herbicide. DCPA exists in both the vapor and particulate phases when exposed to the air. In the vapor phase, DCPA should react slowly with hydroxyl radicals with an estimated half-life of 36 days. Particulate-phase DCPA may be physically removed from air by wet and dry deposition. With a high Koc of 3900, DCPA is presumably immobile in soil, and thus may strongly attach to inorganic material in soil and other environments.

The album *Heliocentric* was primarily recorded in late 2012 at Studio Mecanique in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. The band worked with Swiss producer Julien Fehlmann, known for his work with The Ocean, Coilguns, and Unfold, due to his understanding of their vision and preference for using their live setup. The sound quality was described as polished and professional. Samples of the tracks were released on the band’s website in 2012, showcasing the album’s refined production.

The All-China Federation of Trade Unions (ACFTU), established in 1925, is the sole legal trade union organization in China and operates under the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). It was created to represent workers and serve as a "transmission belt" for Party control, aligning labor interests with the goals of a socialist command economy. By the end of 2002, the ACFTU reported a membership of 134 million, while the estimated number of urban workers in China was approximately 256 million. Chinese trade unions are organized hierarchically, with the ACFTU at the national level and branches extending to provincial, city, district, and workplace levels, where the workplace union serves as the lowest organizational unit. Membership is open to all manual and mental workers who rely on wages or salaries as their primary income and accept the Constitution of the Chinese Trade Unions. The ACFTU functions as a bridge between the CCP and workers, representing their interests while maintaining Party oversight.

According to the 2010 census, Climax, Michigan, had a population of 767, with 296 households and 211 families residing in the village. The population density was 712 inhabitants per square mile. There were 316 housing units, with an average density of 307 units per square mile. The racial makeup of the village was predominantly White, comprising 96.6% of the population, while 2.1% identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

On July 1, 2015, the Lightning signed free agent forward Erik Condra to a three-year contract with a $1.25 million average. Condra had played the previous five seasons with the Ottawa Senators, which is the team he was originally drafted by in the 2006 NHL Draft in the 7th round. Condra had 9 goals and 14 assists in 68 games. Condra is known for his strong work on the penalty kill, which could make Condra a key third/fourth-line player and penalty killer for the Lightning. Condra also has ties to Lightning head coach Jon Cooper, who played for him while Cooper was coach of Texarkana of the USHL. The signing of Condra likely means that Brenden Morrow's tenure with the team is coming to an end. Later in the day, the Lightning announced the signing of free agent defensemen Matt Taormina to a one-year, two-way contract. Taormina played 76 games with the Worcester Sharks of the AHL, notching 11 goals and 38 points. This will be Taromina's second stint with the organization, having previously played with both the Lightning and the Crunch over two seasons.

Omar Naber actively promoted his Eurovision 2017 entry "On My Way" across Europe. On April 13, he performed at the London Eurovision Party, held at Here at Outernet in London, hosted by Lucie Jones, Paddy O'Connell, Rylan, and Scott Mills. Earlier, on April 5, he participated in Eurovision in Concert in Amsterdam, hosted by Cornald Maas and Edsilia Rombley. Additionally, Naber took part in promotional activities in Tel Aviv, Israel, from April 3 to April 6, performing during the Israel Calling event. These appearances were part of his efforts to connect with fans and build momentum for his performance in Kyiv.

The site of Kemp Town station has been redeveloped as the Freshfield Industrial Estate. The portal of the railway tunnel leading through to the site of Hartington Road Halt (now a block of flats), may be viewed from the compound of a self-storage warehouse and van rental company. Hartington Road Viaduct was demolished in 1973, and Lewes Road Viaduct followed in 1976. The supermarket building located where Lewes Road Viaduct approached Lewes Road Station incorporates tall arches of dark brick in its outer façade, recalling the viaduct. A gap in the Victorian houses of D'Aubigny Road, uphill from the supermarket, indicates where the station once stood.

In 1969, Avalon Hill dominated the wargame market, producing an average of two games per year with high-quality mounted boards and components. That same year, Jim Dunnigan founded Simulations Publications, Inc. (SPI), which pursued a strategy of rapid game development based on subscriber feedback. SPI introduced "test games," experimental designs with minimal packaging to gauge interest and refine concepts. Among these, *Korea: The Mobile War*, designed by Jim Dunnigan, was the 13th test game and was published in 1971. The game reflected SPI's innovative approach to wargaming, emphasizing historical simulation and affordability to expand the hobby's reach.

Erikson met his first wife Eleanor (Jo) Erikson while working in a lab under Dr. Waclaw Szybalski. The two divorced after sometime. He later remarried to his second wife Donna. They were married for 32 years until his passing and had a daughter named Amanda.

The Berthouville Treasure, discovered in 1830 near Berthouville, France, consists of approximately 90 gilt-silver objects dating from the 1st to 3rd centuries AD. Weighing around 55 pounds (25 kilograms), the treasure includes bowls, cups, pitchers, plates, and fragments, many bearing votive inscriptions. It was intentionally buried in antiquity, likely to protect it. Among the items is a repoussé silver jug, crafted in Roman Syria, and heirloom pieces such as statuettes of the Gallo-Roman god Mercury. The collection is celebrated for its intricate craftsmanship and is one of the finest examples of ancient Roman silverwork.

Bang! Bang! Eche! began playing shows in 2007 and wrote and recorded their self-titled demo EP towards the end of that year. The EP gained significant traction, placing on US College Radio and receiving rotation on Seattle’s KEXP, where the single "4 To The Floor" was widely played. In Australia, the band was heavily featured on the alternative Triple J network. In their home country of New Zealand, "4 To The Floor" and "Nikee" both reached number one on the bNet alternative radio network, while "Time Mismanagement" peaked at number four. On the NZ Industry Radioscope chart, "4 To The Floor" and "Time Mismanagement" charted for several weeks in 2008, solidifying the band’s growing international and domestic reputation.

During Barack Obama's presidency, the United States experienced a significant transformation in energy production. Oil production surged, driven primarily by the fracking boom, which was fueled by private investment on private land. Simultaneously, the administration promoted renewable energy, with solar power generation increasing twenty-fold and wind energy production tripling. In response to the Deepwater Horizon oil spill in 2010—the largest marine oil spill in U.S. history—Obama established a national commission to investigate its causes and recommend reforms. To further his environmental legacy, Obama withdrew 3.8 million acres in the Atlantic and 115 million acres in the Arctic from offshore oil and gas exploration in December 2016. These efforts reflected his broader commitment to transitioning toward clean energy and reducing carbon pollution.

Sriya married Tamil actor-producer Vikram Krishna on 9 March 2008 at Park Sheraton hotel in Chennai. Vikram Krishna is the son of veteran film producer G. K. Reddy and brother of actor Vishal Krishna and had starred in a couple of Tamil films, before turning full-time film producer, primarily producing films featuring his brother Vishal in the lead role under his GK Films Corporation. Sriya since has been co-producing films with her husband. The couple have a daughter.

Born in Hong Kong to a migrant family from Guangdong Province, China, Lee Iu-cheung (1896–1976) spent his childhood in the Sheung Wan district. Lee was awarded the Lugard Scholarship upon passing all his Cambridge University local examinations held in Hong Kong. He then joined the Faculty of Engineering at the University of Hong Kong (HKU) in 1913. Lee left for Cornell University, where he enrolled in a special study on River Conservancy and Sanitary engineering in 1919. However, upon the death of his father, he abandoned his studies and returned to Hong Kong, where Lee became a part-time lecturer in Hydraulic Engineering at his alma mater.

The main convent and monastery are part of the Salesian order, founded by Don Bosco. The convent and school were opened in 1960 and have around 400 students in their primary school, kindergarten, as well as the school for children with learning disabilities. The school also hosts a number of community activities in the afternoons and summer camps.

Gland has a population (as of December 2020) of 13,258. As of 2008, 32.1% of the population are resident foreign nationals. During the 10-year period 1999–2009 the population increased at a rate of 21.4%: 9.5% due to migration and 12% due to births and deaths.

The band's debut album Lugburz was a traditional black metal album, with a raw, lo-fi production style. On their following album Minas Morgul the band's sound changed significantly, and has been characterised as "a departure from straight black metal in favor of bold atmospheric experimentation which retains a definite blackened feel." The band's sound has remained relatively consistent since then. It was frequently described as "epic", "hypnotic", and "atmospheric", and makes extensive use of keyboards, choir vocals, clean singing, synthesized instruments, and programmed drums. Though the drums are programmed, they are played using a keyboard rather than with a drum machine. Their sound has been described as "a particularly widescreen version of progressive black metal, a style that more recently has been infused with an almost medieval strain of twiddly folk music of the lutes 'n' flutes variety." The band's music is deeply influenced by the literature of J. R. R. Tolkien, particularly The Lord of the Rings. Most of the band's lyrics are derived from Tolkien's own works. Silenius claimed to be influenced by dark wave and ritualistic music as well as fantasy literature.

Allobates femoralis is distributed across the Amazon Basin and tropical eastern South America, including Guyana, Suriname, French Guiana, Brazil, and parts of Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Bolivia. It inhabits terra firme forests, typically on the forest floor below 300 m elevation, though in Colombia and Ecuador it can be found up to 1,000 m. A single reported sighting in Venezuela likely referred to Ameerega picta rather than Allobates femoralis.

Epipedobates machalilla has a snout–vent length of 14.4 to 16 millimetres (0.57 to 0.63 in) for males and 15.0 to 17.6 millimetres (0.59 to 0.69 in) for females. Their heads are longer than they are wide, and the tympanum is small. The forelimbs have a moderate length and the fingers are unwebbed. The toes do not have lateral fringes and the terminal discs are expanded. It is dark-coffee in colour with a gold iris and cream ventral surfaces.

Chukwuemeka Ike was born on April 28, 1931, in Ndikelionwu, a town in present-day Anambra State, Nigeria. He began his early education in his hometown before attending Government College Umuahia from 1945 to 1950 for his secondary education. At Umuahia, he started writing for the school magazine, *The Umuahian*, and was influenced by teachers such as Saburi Biobaku, who had an honors degree in English from Cambridge. The school was a nurturing ground for literary talent, producing notable Nigerian writers like Chinua Achebe, Christopher Okigbo, and Ken Saro-Wiwa. After completing his secondary education, Ike proceeded to the University College, Ibadan, where he earned a B.A. degree in 1955. While at Ibadan, he was invited by Chinua Achebe, who was two years ahead of him at Umuahia, to join the university’s magazine club, which further honed his writing skills. Ike married Adebimpe Olurinsola Abimbolu, a librarian and professor, in 1959. His first novel, *Toads for Supper*, was published in 1965, marking the beginning of his illustrious literary career. He later earned a master’s degree from Stanford University in 1966. In 1971, he became the chief executive of the West African Examinations Council (WAEC), and in 2008, he ascended as the Igwe of Ndikelionwu. His only son, Prince Osita Ike, tragically passed away in 2016.

Founded in late 2014, the NRHL began its inaugural season on February 20, 2015. Four teams (Alkali Revive, Alkali Surge, Canfield Chiefs and Detroit Bordercats) played a 12-game regular season followed by playoffs. Following an undefeated regular season, the Detroit Bordercats needed three games to eliminate Alkali Revive to advance to the Commissioners Cup Finals where they swept the best-of three series against Alkali Surge. Kyle Siciliano was named playoffs MVP.

On September 18, 2012, Mother Jones published excerpts from a secretly recorded video of Mitt Romney speaking at a private fundraiser. The event, held on May 17, 2012, at the Boca Raton, Florida, mansion of private equity manager Marc Leder, required attendees to pay $50,000 per plate. The video, provided to Mother Jones by James Earl Carter IV, grandson of former President Jimmy Carter, captured Romney's controversial "47 percent" remarks. Scott Prouty, the bartender who filmed the video, revealed himself as the videographer during an appearance on MSNBC's *The Ed Show* on March 13, 2013, explaining his motivations for releasing it.

Alongside the series order announcement, it was announced that Melissa Barrera and Veronica Osorio had been cast as Lyn and Emma, respectively. On November 14, 2017, Ser Anzoategui, Chelsea Rendon, Carlos Miranda, and Maria Elena Laas were announced as having joined the series' main cast. It was also announced that Mishel Prada had replaced Veronica Osorio in the role of Emma. On December 8, 2017, the final additions to the cast were announced. These consisted of the castings of Elena Campbell-Martínez, Ramsés Jiménez, Luis Bordonada, Elizabeth De Razzo, Renee Victor, Adelina Anthony, and Erika Soto in recurring roles.

Parents with intellectual disabilities face discrimination in their parenting role. They have difficulties managing housing and financial issues because of their socio-economic disadvantage, which may lead to debt. As their children grow up and their intellectual ability surpasses their parents', new challenges arise like providing children with assistance in education. A 2018 systematic review found low quality evidence that some parents, mainly mothers with intellectual disabilities, provided effective parenting when provided training and support for the required skills.

She was born in Longueuil, Quebec and received a bachelor's degree in communications from the Université du Québec à Montréal. From 1987 to 1989, she wrote for Petit Devoir, a weekly insert in Le Devoir for children 6 to 12 years old. Since 1990, she has been a regular contributor to Magazine Enfants Québec, writing a column about children's books. Papineau is director for baby books and picture books for the publisher Dominique et compagnie.

George Blanda played as a quarterback and kicker at the University of Kentucky from 1945 to 1948. Coach Paul "Bear" Bryant arrived during Blanda's sophomore year, following a 1-9 season in 1945. Under Bryant, the Wildcats lost only three games each season from 1946 to 1948. Bryant later gained fame at Alabama.

In 1919, the *Governor R. M. McLane* was taken out of service for engine repairs, including the installation of a new boiler. On December 3, 1919, while moored near the Baltimore Dry Dock and Shipbuilding Company, the vessel was nearly destroyed by a fire that also consumed six other vessels, including the 150-foot U.S. Army steamer *Major L'Enfant*. The fire claimed the life of a watchman aboard the *Major L'Enfant*. Using $13,000 in insurance funds, plans were made to rebuild the *Governor R. M. McLane*. During its absence, the Navy loaned the state the vessel *Governor P. F. Thomas* as a temporary replacement.

The Canal Street Schoolhouse stands southwest of downtown Brattleboro, on a rise above the south side of Canal Street (United States Route 5), a major thoroughfare. It is a two-story stone structure, topped by a hip roof and set on a stone foundation. Its front half is nearly bisected by a projecting square tower, which is capped by an open octagonal belfry and cupola. The main facade, facing Canal Street, is symmetrical, with the main entrance in the tower base, flanked by sidelight windows, and sheltered by a semicircular portico supported by Doric columns. Above the entrance are windows arranged in Palladian fashion, the three sections each set in separate openings. A circular clock face is set on the tower's third stage, which is surmounted by a low balustrade and the belfry. The tower is flanked by single sash windows on the first floor and oval windows on the second, with bands of three sash windows at the outermost bays.

Born in Pomfret, Vermont, Dana was the son of John Winchester and Hannah (Putnam) Dana. He graduated from Dartmouth College in 1795 and then studied law. In 1798, he was admitted to the bar and began the practice of law in Fryeburg, Maine, which was then a part of Massachusetts.

SSG (acronym for Social Service Group) is institute sponsored social service group which undertakes numerous social and environmental initiatives. Ranging from eye checkup and blood donation camps to conducting cleanliness drive in zoos and beaches, SSG conducts more than 15 large scale volunteered programs in and around the college campus. It's the largest volunteer organization in the institute.

Jack Varsalona, former President of Wilmington University, received numerous accolades during his career, including the Community Leadership Advocate of the Year Award from Goodwill of Delaware and Delaware County. This award recognized Wilmington University's scholarship programs benefiting graduates and employees of Goodwill. Additionally, Varsalona was honored with the Rev. Roberto Balducelli Appreciation Award, intended for Italian-American community leaders who demonstrate a strong commitment to giving back. This award celebrated his dedication to community service and his impactful leadership.

LeBlanc was born in Atlanta, Georgia, to a Dominica father (from Portsmouth) and a Jamaican mother, Vans LeBlanc and Winsome LeBlanc, who had temporarily relocated to Atlanta to avoid the dangers of Hurricane David. LeBlanc grew up in Dominica until age eight when her family moved to Maple Ridge, British Columbia. LeBlanc began playing soccer at age 12 and was named one of the top 20 Division I recruits by USA Today in 1997, even though Maple Ridge Secondary School did not have a girls soccer team. She was also an all-provincial basketball player and was voted British Columbia's Most Defensive Player in basketball in 1997.

After Donald Trump was elected, Sarah Huckabee Sanders was named deputy press secretary in his administration. She held her first White House press briefing on May 5, 2017, standing in for Press Secretary Sean Spicer, who was unavailable due to Naval Reserve duty. Sanders continued to cover for Spicer until May 12, during a tumultuous period following Trump’s abrupt firing of FBI Director James Comey.
