The squadron's assigned role was high-level strategic bombing with a variety of free fall nuclear bombs. Both the B1 and B1A types were equipped with various free-fall nuclear weapons. These may have included Blue Danube, Red Beard, Violet Club the Interim Megaton Weapon, Yellow Sun Mk.1 and certainly Yellow Sun Mk2. American bombs were also supplied to the RAF V-bombers for a short period under the Project E arrangements.

Beat Assailant, born in Miami, Florida, later moved to Atlanta before recording *Hard Twelve*, produced by Max (Dany Wild). After this, he began experimenting with live instrumentation, spending years on the road with a nine-piece band, culminating in the album *Rhyme Space Continuum*. His album *Imperial Pressure*, recorded in Paris, was praised by critics for its intelligent blend of hip-hop, jazz, rock, and electronic music. His third album, *Rhyme Space Continuum*, was lauded for its innovative approach, showcasing his evolution as an artist and his ability to merge diverse musical styles seamlessly.

The Charleston City Paper reported on the filming of *Vice Principals* in North Charleston, South Carolina, where much of the series was shot in the Park Circle neighborhood. School scenes were filmed at R.B. Stall High School and West Ashley High School, which stood in for the fictional North Jackson High. The Charleston City Paper also noted potential cameo appearances by Will Ferrell and Bill Murray.

The Starship spacecraft, serving as the second stage of the SpaceX Starship rocket system, is 50 meters tall with a dry mass of 85 tons. It features a payload volume significantly larger than traditional rockets, with an 8-meter diameter cargo bay and a height of up to 18 meters, surpassing the International Space Station's pressurized volume. Designed for deep-space missions, Starship can be refueled in orbit by docking with a tanker spacecraft, enabling it to reach destinations like the Moon and Mars. This capability allows Starship to carry up to 150 tons to low Earth orbit and return with 50 tons, supporting long-duration missions and interplanetary travel.

Constantijn Huygens was an active member of the Muiderkring, an informal circle of intellectuals and artists centered around Pieter Corneliszoon Hooft at Muiden Castle. In the early 1630s, Huygens corresponded with notable figures such as René Descartes, Rembrandt, and Jan Lievens. He also became friends with the English poet John Donne, whose works he translated into Dutch. The death of Huygens' wife, whom he affectionately called "Sterre," left him unable to write poetry for months. Inspired by Petrarch, he eventually composed the sonnet *Op de dood van Sterre* ("On the Death of Sterre"), a poignant elegy that he added to his unfinished work *Dagh-werck*. This sonnet, reflecting his grief and love, was later included in his 1657 poetry collection *Koren-bloemen*, which showcased his literary achievements and emotional depth.

In 1966, Governor Walter J. Hickel opened the North Slope to oil extraction. To improve access to the oil fields, a 400-mile winter road was planned between Livengood and Prudhoe Bay. Construction started in November 1968, and the "Walter J. Hickel Highway" was completed by March 1969. Due to poor engineering, the construction of the road exposed the underlying permafrost to thawing, and the road was abandoned by April of that year. Maintenance was not performed as the route was farther west than the planned Trans-Alaska Pipeline System.

The Republic of China established the Taiwan Provincial Government in 1945, appointing Chen Yi as its first Chief Executive and Governor-General. During his rule, the February 28 Incident of 1947 occurred. Chen was later recalled, and the government-general position was abolished.

In 2002, Canibus signed up for the United States Army. In a 2005 interview, Canibus stated "I enlisted because I wanted to get away from the music... I wanted to do something that gave me a separate definition from what I had done all through my teens and twenties. I was 28 when I enlisted." In 2004, he was caught smoking cannabis and was subsequently discharged.

Eileen Friel earned her B.S. in Physics from the College of William & Mary and completed the Part III Maths Tripos at Cambridge University. She pursued her Ph.D. in Astronomy and Astrophysics at the University of California, Santa Cruz, where she completed her dissertation, "Abundance Analysis of Old Open Clusters," in 1986 under the supervision of Robert Kraft.

The Vietnam War introduced profound tensions into General Joseph Carroll's family life, as his sons took paths that clashed with his military career and values. His son Dennis fled to India to avoid the military draft, a decision that deeply strained the family. After Dennis returned to the United States, General Carroll, despite his own high-ranking military position, donned his three-star general's uniform to represent Dennis before a Selective Service board, successfully securing his status as a conscientious objector. Meanwhile, James Carroll, ordained to the Roman Catholic priesthood in 1969, became an outspoken antiwar activist, participating in protests that embarrassed his father, who was then serving as the founding director of the Defense Intelligence Agency. This ideological divide over the war marked the first major clash between father and son, reflecting their conflicting views on faith, patriotism, and duty. James ultimately left the priesthood in 1974, influenced by his antiwar convictions, and pursued a career as a writer, exploring themes of family, Catholicism, and the military in his works. The rift between General Carroll and his sons, particularly James, symbolized the generational and political divides that defined many American families during the Vietnam era.

In Ireland, Digifone was created in 2000 as the demerged mobile business of Esat Digifone, which had been purchased by BT. Esat Digifone was originally a joint venture between Esat Telecom and Telenor, but after BT acquired Esat Telecom, it also acquired Telenor's share in Esat Digifone. Following the acquisition, Esat Telecom became BT Ireland, while the demerged mobile operations rebranded as Digifone. In 2001, Digifone was rebranded as O2 Ireland after the demerger from BT, becoming part of mmO2 plc. In 2006, O2 Ireland became a subsidiary of Telefónica after its parent company, O2, was acquired. In June 2013, Hutchison Whampoa announced its acquisition of the Irish arm of O2 for €780 million. The merger was completed in March 2015, with O2 Ireland being integrated into Hutchison Whampoa's subsidiary, Three Ireland.

In 1963 Lieutenant William Szili offered his account of the shooting. He was the executive officer of a Company of Marines guarding the western boundary of the base. According to Szili, he and his company commander, Captain Arthur J. Jackson, who was awarded a Medal of Honor during World War II, had consumed approximately six martini cocktails at the base officer's club, on September 30, 1961. He said he left Jackson at the officer's club, went home to sleep, and was woken by a call from one of the base's provost officers, that Jackson had found Lopez in a "restricted area", and that Jackson needed his help.

Veronica Osorio and Melissa Barrera were announced as Emma and Lyn, respectively, alongside the series order on May 22, 2017. However, Mishel Prada later replaced Osorio as Emma. On August 1, 2017, Ser Anzoategui, Chelsea Rendon, Carlos Miranda, and Maria Elena Laas were confirmed as main cast members. The final cast additions, announced on December 8, 2017, included Elena Campbell-Martínez, Ramsés Jiménez, Luis Bordonada, Elizabeth De Razzo, Renee Victor, Adelina Anthony, and Erika Soto in recurring roles. Campbell-Martínez, Jiménez, Bordonada, De Razzo, Victor, Anthony, and Soto portrayed various supporting characters integral to the series' exploration of family and community.

The film grossed ₹16.94 crore (US$1.9 million) on its opening day. Heroine had an average weekend of ₹25 crore (US$2.9 million) nett. The film had collected a fair ₹31 crore (US$3.5 million) in its first week. The film had a heavy fall in second week and managed only ₹60 crore (US$6.9 million) nett. It collected Rs. 144 million.

Dresselhaus studied physics at University of California, Berkeley, receiving his bachelor's degree in 1951 and his doctorate in 1955. At Berkeley he worked under the supervision of Charles Kittel and Arthur F. Kip on early cyclotron resonance experiments on semiconductors and semimetals. As a postdoc Dresselhaus was for the academic year 1955–1956 an instructor at the University of Chicago. From 1956 to 1960 he was an assistant professor at Cornell University. He was also a consultant to General Electric Research Laboratories from 1956 to 1960 and to the Oak Ridge National Laboratory from 1958 to 1960. From 1960 he worked at the Lincoln Laboratory of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and from 1977 at the Francis Bitter National Magnetic Laboratory of MIT. He was also a professor of physics at MIT.

Newsarama ranked the character, as Gilgamesh, as the second worst Avengers member commenting: "What's that you say? You aren't familiar with Gilgamesh, the ancient Sumerian hero? And why is this guy wearing a cow on his head? Let's just say there's a reason this guy is called "The Forgotten One". Maybe next time you can get Mithras, or at least Marduk".

As of 2004, the EU recognises the northern part of Cyprus as being outside the control of the Greek Cypriot–led government of the Republic of Cyprus. EU legislation is suspended in the north under Protocol 10 of the 2003 Accession Treaty, and the euro does not officially circulate there. While Cyprus is in the process of joining the Schengen Area, internal border controls remain due to the island's division, and the Schengen Agreement is not in effect. The EU considers Turkish Cypriots as EU citizens, even if they reside in the north, which is outside the EU's customs and fiscal territory. Fundamental EU freedoms are not fully applicable in the north.

Hitler's Table Talk reveals he continued to wish for a unified Protestant Reich Church of Germany for some time after 1937, which had largely proven unsuccessful. This was in line with his earlier policy of uniting all the Protestant churches so they would purvey the new racial and nationalist doctrines of the regime and act as a unifying rather than divisive force in Germany. By 1940, Hitler had abandoned even the syncretist idea of a positive Christianity. According to Thomas Childers, after 1938 Hitler began to publicly support a Nazified version of science, particularly social Darwinism, at the core of Nazi ideology in place of a religious one; a development that is reflected in his increasingly hostile remarks towards Christianity in the Table Talk. Historian Richard Weikart characterized Hitler's belief in "evolutionary ethics as the expression of the will of God" who routinely "equated the laws of nature and the will of Providence".

The base of the Deutsche Bank Center contains retail spaces, a theater, and CNN studios, with office spaces and residential towers resting above. Structurally, the base includes a steel frame that extends 248 feet below the north tower and 314 feet below the south tower, supported by concrete-clad steel trusses capable of bearing over 42,000 tons. To accommodate the diverse needs of the spaces, several column arrangements are used, including 60-foot spans for studios, 45-foot spans for offices, and 20-foot spans for residential areas. The columns are strategically positioned to avoid interference with the building's 24 original entrances and open spaces, utilizing angled and exterior columns to maintain clear spans and ensure functionality across the structure.

On December 31, 2019, Bailey signed a one-year contract with the Minnesota Twins. On July 28, 2020, Bailey made his Twins debut earning his first victory over the Cardinals. Days after being activated from an IL stint, Bailey was designated for assignment by the Twins on September 25, 2020. He was released on September 27.

In 1900, John Wallace Pringle achieved the rank of Major and was appointed an inspecting officer in the Board of Trade. At the start of World War I in 1914, he became deputy director of railway transport with the temporary rank of Colonel. In 1916, he was confirmed as Colonel and returned to the Board of Trade as Chief Inspecting Officer. When the Ministry of Transport was formed in 1919, he transferred to the new ministry, retaining his position as Chief Inspecting Officer until his retirement in 1929.

Serena Katherine "Violet" Dandridge (1878–1956) was born on March 15, 1878, at her family home, "Rosebrake," in Shepherdstown, West Virginia, to Danske Bedinger Dandridge and Adam Stephen Dandridge. She grew up with two younger siblings, Stephen Hawks and Dorothea Spotswood. Adam Stephen Dandridge, her father, served as a soldier under General Robert E. Lee during the Civil War. Violet was a descendant of Alexander Spotswood Dandridge, who married the daughter of General Adam Stephen, a prominent Revolutionary War figure. Her early life was shaped by her family's rich historical and cultural legacy.

Many holidays in the Hindu, Buddhist, and Jaina traditions are based on lunar cycles in the lunisolar timekeeping system, rooted in the Hindu calendar. Festivals like Vaisakhi, Pongal, and those associated with Sankranti are based on the solar cycle. Lunar cycle-based festivals vary significantly on the Gregorian calendar, sometimes by several weeks. In contrast, solar cycle-based ancient Hindu festivals almost always fall on the same Gregorian date annually, with variations of only one day in exceptional years. Pongal, for instance, is celebrated around January 14, marking the Sun's transition into Capricorn.

The Goliath stick insect has evolved passive camouflage through plant mimicry, resembling leaves or branches with its green coloration and shape. To enhance this mimicry, it employs a swaying movement that mimics the motion of leaves and branches in the wind. This adaptation helps the insect avoid detection by predators, increasing its chances of survival.

On the morning of Sunday, June 24, 1855, Robert Newsom's daughters became concerned when he did not appear for breakfast. They enlisted neighbors, including William Powell, a neighboring slave owner, to help search for him. Newsom's sons also joined the search effort. Powell questioned George, an enslaved man on the Newsom farm, about Newsom's whereabouts. Initially, George claimed he did not know anything but later revealed that the last place Newsom was seen walking was the path between his house and Celia's cabin. This information led the search party to confront Celia, another enslaved person on the farm, with Powell taking the lead in questioning her.

Scapuzzi signed for Italian Serie B side Varese on loan on 8 January 2013 until the end of the season. He made his debut as a 77th-minute sub against Brescia on 22 January 2013. In August 2013 he has signed for Italian Serie B side Siena on loan. He will wear the No. 14 shirt at Siena next season.

Especially during the 1970s and 1980s, the PAC and ANC were highly active in neighbouring active countries, where they were in exile from repression within South Africa and accommodated by sympathetic regimes or opposition parties. The Security Branch was thus involved in several operations outside South Africa. It had a close working relationship with regimes in Swaziland, Malawi, and (after 1986) Lesotho – along with SADF's Military Intelligence division, it set up a joint security task force with representatives of the Basotho police and defence force. In one notable example of Branch activity outside South Africa's borders, the Branch was involved in target identification for the SADF's 1985 raid on Gaborone. Several Branch officers applied for amnesty from the TRC for their roles in gathering intelligence for and planning the raid, and they testified that they had attended planning sessions with SADF as representatives of the Branch's national office and of several of its regional divisions.

In 2011 Dóra Vers (mezzo), Veronika Földi (alto) and Márton Nagy-György (tenor) founded the group in the jazz faculty of the Béla Bartók Conservarory of Music. They started to cooperate with the singer-composer Viktor Magyaróvári (Kayamar), whom they knew from the classical faculty os the Conservatory. Kayamar's bass-line (many articles count him as one of the lowest basses on earth) and his arrangements highly determine the image of the group.

Paula Cole's seventh studio album, *7*, was released via her website on March 23, 2015, and to other digital music outlets on April 10, 2015. Cole described the songs on *7* as arriving "suddenly and urgently," with her subconscious needing to "reach out" and process her experiences. Recorded live as an acoustic quartet, the album features a soft, soulful sound reminiscent of the 1960s. Cole worked with original band members Jay Bellerose and Kevin Barry, alongside upright bassist Dennis Crouch, to create a deeply personal collection of songs reflecting a seven-year life cycle.

Allobates femoralis is distributed in the Amazon basin and tropical eastern South America. Its geographical range includes Guyana, Suriname, French Guiana and Brazil, and the eastern parts of Colombia, Ecuador, Peru and Bolivia. A single reported sighting in Venezuela may have referred to Ameerega picta. It is found on the forest floor, usually at elevations below 300 m (1,000 ft), but in Colombia and Ecuador occurs at altitudes of up to 1,000 m (3,300 ft).

A Rodriguez well, or Rodwell, operates by using a heated probe to melt a shaft over a hundred feet deep into the Antarctic ice. At the shaft's end, a subsurface cavity forms, where melted ice collects as liquid water. Freshwater supply is renewed by continuously melting surrounding ice, inspired by observations of room-temperature sewage injected into Arctic ice, which formed a pocket approximately 100 feet deep. The lifespan of a Rodwell is about seven years, determined by water usage and the station's population. Once the well exceeds 500 feet in depth, the energy required to pump and recirculate water becomes excessive, marking the end of its operational life.

*Chthamalus stellatus* is a sessile barnacle that attaches to rocks and other firm surfaces in the intertidal zone, particularly on exposed rocky shores. It is typically cone-shaped but may become more tubular in crowded colonies. As an adult, it is a suspension feeder, using its feathery, rhythmically beating appendages—modified legs called cirri—to draw plankton and detritus into its shell for consumption.

Friel was a physics major at the College of William & Mary. After going to the University of Cambridge for Part III of the Mathematical Tripos, she returned to the US for doctoral study in astronomy and astrophysics at the University of California, Santa Cruz. Her dissertation, A study of the stellar populations in two high galactic latitude fields with comparison to galaxy models, was completed in 1986 under the supervision of Robert Kraft.

The Money Order Initiative, led by Bonnie S. Klapper, targeted the laundering of drug proceeds through structured money orders. As part of the initiative, the government froze numerous bank accounts linked to illicit activities. Between 1992 and 1996, over 100 defendants were successfully prosecuted. Klapper notably tried and convicted Juan Manuel Ortiz, who had a prior criminal background involving drug trafficking, before his conviction for laundering millions in drug proceeds.

Bride-wealth, also known as bride price or bride dowry, is a payment made by the groom and his family to the bride's family, common in regions such as Africa, parts of Asia, and pastoralist societies like the Maasai in Kenya and Tanzania. This payment, often in the form of money, livestock, or valuables, reflects the bride's value based on her family credentials, education, skills, beauty, and reputation. Typically kept by the bride's parents, bride-wealth can significantly support poor families, as seen in cases where it sustains them for years. The bride's father or eldest brother usually arranges her marriage, prioritizing family reputation and economic benefit over other considerations.

Game II of the 1991 State of Origin series is remembered for its fierce intensity, highlighted by a heated confrontation in driving rain at the Sydney Football Stadium. Approaching half-time, New South Wales forward Mark Geyer’s crude tackle on Queensland hooker Steve Walters sparked a brawl, with Andrew Gee running in to assist his teammate. Referee David Manson issued multiple cautions in an attempt to diffuse tensions. As players left the field for the break, Queensland captain Wally Lewis continued his altercation with Geyer, cementing the clash as one of Origin’s most iconic moments. The monsoon-like conditions only added to the drama of a game that remains a classic in State of Origin history.

Harold Lothrop Borden is commemorated with a bronze bust statue by Hamilton MacCarthy in his hometown of Canning, Nova Scotia. The statue honors his sacrifice during the South African War, with panels at its base depicting key events, including the Battle of Witpoort, where he was killed. A memorial plaque dedicated to Borden and fellow medical student Gunner O'Reilly is located at McGill University, where Borden studied medicine before enlisting. He is also listed on the Memorial Arch in South Africa, and a plaque commemorating his death at Witpoort is located there. Borden is buried in Braamfontein Cemetery, South Africa, under a headstone marking his service with the 1st Battalion, Canadian Mounted Rifles.

Microhyla laterite is known from Manipal in Udupi District and Konaje in Mangaluru District, Karnataka, India, at elevations of 50–80 m above sea level. It inhabits laterite habitats, including ephemeral ponds, marshy areas, and wet paddy fields near rural and peri-urban areas. Male frogs have been observed vocalizing from embankments, with activity peaking between 19:15 and 21:30 during the southwest monsoon season.

Women's United Soccer Association (WUSA), the first official professional women's soccer league in the United States, began in 2001. Little Rimando was a sixth-round draft pick (41st overall in the global draft) by the Bay Area CyberRays. In 2001 Little Rimando started three of her 15 regular season appearances, contributing a goal and two assists. She made a brief appearance in the 2001 WUSA Founders Cup as the CyberRays secured the inaugural championship.

His ecclesiastical province corresponded broadly with the Roman Aquitania Secunda (including Poitiers) but also included the French Antilles. Donnet argued forcefully for the canonisation of Christopher Columbus. Earlier he had been titular bishop of Rhosus appointed to the diocese of Nancy and Toul. A major figure in Napoleon III's Liberal Empire period he was renowned for his energy, e.g. in publishing and in the restoration of churches in his diocese of Bordeaux (including Bazas though without that title). Donnet was named cardinal by pope Pius IX in 1852 and participated in the conclave of 1878. Eleven volumes of his pastoral instructions, sermons and writings were posthumously published.

The Benton County Courthouse in Arkansas has a rich history dating back to 1837, when a log building was erected to serve as the first courthouse and host county court sessions. In 1904, architect Albert O. Clark moved from St. Louis to Rogers, Arkansas, designing notable structures like the Applegate Drugstore and Bank of Rogers in the Romanesque Revival style. Clark later contributed to Bentonville’s architectural landscape, replacing the Italianate-style courthouse, which had served county administration since 1872, with a Classical Revival design.

The Ural Mountains extend about 2,500 km (1,600 mi) from the Kara Sea to the Kazakh Steppe along the border of Kazakhstan. Vaygach Island and the island of Novaya Zemlya form a further continuation of the chain on the north. Geographically this range marks the northern part of the border between Europe and Asia. Its highest peak is Mount Narodnaya, approximately 1,895 m (6,217 ft) in elevation. Transverse faults divide the mountain chain into seven major units, each of which has its own typical pattern of mountain ridges. From north to south, these are the Pay-Khoy, Zapolyarnyy, Pripolyarnyy, Polyarnyy, Severnyy, Sredniy, Yuzhnny Ural and Mugodzhary. The average altitudes of the Urals are around 1000–1300 meters, the highest point being Narodnaya peak in the Pripolyarnyy Ural which reaches a height of 1894 meters.

The original production was directed by American director Molly Smith. The play premiered through Arena Stage at the Kreeger Theater in Washington D.C. on January 12th, 2018, running until February 18th, 2018. A print edition of the play will be published by Northwestern University Press in 2020.

Legends of Eisenwald is a fantasy role-playing game set in a fictitious duchy of Eisenwald in medieval Germany in times of Holy Roman Empire. The main focus of the story in the main campaign revolves around the life of the youngest child of Baron Lahnstein who on return to his lands finds that the family castle is impossible to enter. There are two separate single scenarios: The Masquerade and Cursed Castle that are separate stories happening in other locations of Eisenwald.

In late 1839, in partnership with the mercantile firm of Daniel Cooper and James Holt, he went back to New Zealand to acquire land for cattle runs and set up trading stations. He acquired deeds to nearly two million acres. For the trading stations he placed agents and stock-in-trade at carefully chosen landings. For his own base he chose Wellington and in late 1840 built his premises in Te Aro and completed his own wharf, Wellington's first, the following year. Rhodes lived by the eastern corner of the junction of Cuba Street and Manners Street. He bought out Cooper and Holt's share in 1850.

Germaine Williams, known professionally as Canibus, was born on December 9, 1974, in Kingston, Jamaica, and is of Jamaican nationality. His father, Basil Williams, was a Jamaican cricketer. Due to his mother’s career, the family frequently relocated, living in The Bronx, Washington, D.C., Atlanta, Miami, Buffalo, and London. Canibus described himself as an introverted child. He graduated from high school in 1992 and spent a year working for AT&T Corporation before working as a data analyst for the U.S. Department of Justice. His interest in technology led him to study computer science at DeKalb College in Atlanta.

At the 2000 census, Knowlton, Wisconsin had a population of 102,437, with 38,660 households and 27,487 families. The population density was 286 people per square mile, and there were 41,157 housing units at an average density of 115 per square mile. The racial makeup was 94.54% White, and 3.66% of the population identified as Hispanic or Latino of any race.

The verification regimes in arms control treaties contain many tools to enable them to hold parties accountable for their actions and violations of their treaty agreements. The START Treaty verification provisions were the most complicated and demanding of any agreement at the time by providing twelve different types of inspection. Data exchanges and declarations between parties became required and included exact quantities, technical characteristics, locations, movements, and the status of all offensive nuclear threats. The national technical means of verification (NTM) provision protected satellites and other information-gathering systems controlled by the verifying side, as they helped to verify adherence of international treaties. The international technical means of verification provision protected the multilateral technical systems specified in other treaties. Co-operative measures were established to facilitate verification by the NTM and included displaying items in plain sight and not hiding them from detection. The new on-site inspections (OSI) and Perimeter and Portal Continuous Monitoring (PPCM) provisions helped to maintain the treaty's integrity by providing a regulatory system manned by a representative from the verifying side at all times. In addition, access to telemetry from ballistic missile flight tests are now required, including exchanges of tapes and a ban on encryption and encapsulation from both parties.

Amanda DaCosta, a native of Katonah, New York, attended Somers High School, where she was a varsity soccer athlete for five years starting in 8th grade. She earned all-section and all-league honors four times, was named league MVP twice, and played for the ENY North and Region 1 ODP teams from 2000–2005.

The ꞌAreꞌare people, an ethnic group native to the Solomon Islands, primarily reside in the southern region of Malaita Island, with smaller populations in northwest Small Malaita and parts of Guadalcanal. Their language, ꞌAreꞌare, part of the Austronesian family, had approximately 17,800 native speakers in 1999, up from 8,000–9,000 recorded in the 1970s. The 1993 speaker count was documented by linguists studying the region. According to census data, the total population of the Solomon Islands was 285,176 in 1986 and grew to 515,870 by 2009. ꞌAreꞌare speakers traditionally engage in subsistence agriculture, cultivating taro, yams, and sweet potatoes, alongside fishing and pig farming. Predominantly Christian today, the ꞌAreꞌare people maintain a deep cultural connection to ancestral burial grounds, which hold spiritual and historical significance. Their oral traditions, including music and storytelling, are central to cultural transmission, though the lack of a standardized writing system poses challenges for preserving their language and beliefs. Efforts to address this include initiatives by local organizations to develop a written standard for ꞌAreꞌare, intended for use in education and cultural preservation. This aligns with broader trends in the Solomon Islands to integrate indigenous languages into early education, ensuring the survival and vitality of the ꞌAreꞌare language for future generations.

Charles was named to the national team invited to compete at the 1992 Olympics, held in Barcelona, Spain. The USA team won their first three games, but then played the Unified Team and fell, 79–73. The USA team then faced Cuba for the bronze medal. The game was tied at halftime, and Cuba had a small lead midway through the second half, but the USA went on a run to retake the lead, and finished with an 88–74 victory and the bronze medal. Charles averaged 6.2 points per game.

In his junior season at San Leandro High School, Cunningham averaged 17.8 points, 4.5 rebounds and 3.3 assists per game. As a senior, He averaged 20.4 points, 6.3 rebounds and 2.6 assists per game. He earned academic All-America honors his senior year.

Mirpur City lies at 459 metres or 1,506 feet above sea level and is linked with the main Peshawar-Lahore Grand Trunk Road at Dina Tehsil. It is the headquarters of Mirpur District, which has two subdivisions: Mirpur and Dadyal. Mirpur is now one of the largest city of Azad Kashmir. The building of the new city in late 1960s paved the way for New Mirpur, situated on the bank of Mangla Lake. The project of four-star hotel PC has been completed. The government is planning to make the second rotating hotel of Pakistan in Mirpur, Azad Kashmir.

Tariq Fancy, the former chief investment officer for sustainable investing at BlackRock, has gained significant attention for his critiques of the financial services industry's approach to ESG (environmental, social, and governance) investing. In a March 2021 op-ed for *USA Today*, Fancy claimed that Wall Street is misleading the public with "greenwashing" practices, presenting sustainable investing as impactful while it often lacks real social or environmental benefits. He described this as a "deadly distraction" from addressing critical global challenges like climate change. Fancy expanded on these points in a three-part essay series titled *The Secret Diary of a 'Sustainable Investor'*, published on Medium, where he detailed his experiences at BlackRock and argued for stronger regulations to prevent ESG from being used as a marketing tool rather than a force for meaningful change.

After one year at Louisiana, Joseph became the head coach at Mississippi College in Clinton, Mississippi beginning in the 2005 season, when the Choctaws went 2-8. The Choctaws finished 5-5 and 8-2 in 2006 and 2007, respectively. His 2008 squad began the season ranked #25 in the NCAA's Division III, but fell from the polls after an opening week, 42–6 defeat at the hands of rival Millsaps and finished the year 5-5. In 2009, the Choctaws finished 9-3 and reached the Division III playoffs. After 4 consecutive losing seasons, Joseph stepped down as the Choctaws head coach in 2013, prior to MC's move to Division II.

In 2017, a study published by the IEEE and ACM analyzed software products for teaching modeling, surveying an international sample of 500 academics. The study found that 25% of respondents used StarUML in their courses, highlighting its significance in education.

While working in Dr. Waclaw Szybalski's lab, Raymond L. Erikson met Eleanor (Jo), whom he married before later divorcing. He then married Donna, with whom he shared 48 years of marriage and a daughter, Amanda.

Wannier functions have recently found application in describing the polarization in crystals, for example, ferroelectrics. The modern theory of polarization is pioneered by Raffaele Resta and David Vanderbilt. See for example, Berghold, and Nakhmanson, and a power-point introduction by Vanderbilt. The polarization per unit cell in a solid can be defined as the dipole moment of the Wannier charge density:

During World War II the Clarksdale Victory was a cargo supply ship. She took supplies to support the troops at the Battle of Okinawa. She was at Okinawa from April 26 to May 8, 1945. On April 27, 1945 a shell landed only 15 yards from the Clarksdale Victory. The shell exploded and fragments landed on her deck. One of Clarksdale Victory's lifeboats was damaged by the fragments. On the same day near by, the SS Canada Victory was hit by a kamikaze plane; the explosion in a cargo hold blew out the side of the ship, and she sank in seven minutes.

On a national level, Senator Rezin is an active member of the National Conference of State Legislatures (NCSL), which is a bipartisan organization that brings legislators and staff from across the county together to collaborate and share information to help craft the best solutions to problems states face. Senator Rezin currently serves on NCSL’s 63-member Executive Committee and Task Force on Energy Supply. Rezin also serves on the Board of Directors for the National Foundation for Women Legislators.

On January 10, 2018, Aguayo signed a reserve/future contract with the Los Angeles Chargers. He went perfect in the preseason (3-of-3 on field goals and 6-of-6 on extra points) and kicked the game-winning field goal in the Chargers' final exhibition. He was waived on September 1, 2018, after losing the kicking job to Caleb Sturgis.

Grandville Public Schools embraces the "be nice" program, a positive anti-bullying approach that goes beyond telling kids not to bully. "Be nice" was developed by the Mental Health Foundation of West Michigan, where Grandville Public Schools Board of Education secretary Christy Buck serves as executive director. "Be nice" has been adopted by many other districts throughout West Michigan.

After the 2002 Crawley Borough Council election, Labour held 19 seats, losing three to the Conservatives, including Furnace Green, where Conservative Brenda Blackwell (849 votes) defeated former Labour mayor Ray Calcott (531 votes). The Conservatives gained 16 seats overall, including all three in Pound Hill North. The Liberal Democrats retained two seats, holding Northgate.

The LGBTQ community has been under scrutiny and controversy in the public eye of collegiate athletics, but the NCAA moves to support the inclusion of these groups. The NCAA provides many resources concerning the education of the college community on this topic and policies in order to foster diversity. Title IX protects the transgender community within intercollegiate athletics and on college campuses.

Wilhelm Exner, born in 1840, graduated from the Polytechnic Institute at age 19. In 1862, he attended the World Exposition in London, where he began establishing his reputation by reporting on technical innovations. Appointed Professor of Engineering in 1869, Exner later became Rector of the University of Natural Resources and Life Sciences, Vienna, serving from 1875 to 1879, significantly shaping Austrian industrial and educational advancements.

The UK Foreign Secretary William Hague called for an urgent session of the UNSC following Friday's "appalling crime" at Houla. He urged the regime to grant full and immediate access to Houla for UN monitors and stop all military options, as demanded by special envoy Kofi Annan. However General Mustafa Ahmed al-Sheikh, head of the Turkey-based Free Syria Army (FSA) military council, said regime opponents had lost all faith in the UN Security Council, on which Damascus has Russia as a powerful backer (see also Russia–Syria relations).

Catherine Eddowes was buried on the afternoon of Monday, 8 October 1888. She was laid to rest in an unmarked public grave at #49336, square 318 in the City of London Cemetery in a service conducted by a Reverend Dunscombe. Her coffin was of polished elm, and bore a plate inscribed with the words "Catherine Eddowes, died Sept. 30, 1888, aged 43 years."

Route 225 begins at an intersection with Route 2A (Massachusetts Avenue) on the east side of the town of Lunenburg in Worcester County. The two-lane highway's name changes from Old Massachusetts Avenue to Groton Road when it enters the town of Shirley in Middlesex County. Route 225's name is West Main Street between its two crossings of the Nashua River—at the Shirley–Groton town line and closer to the center of Groton—and continues from the latter crossing as Long Hill Road. The highway turns north onto Farmers Row to run concurrently with Route 111, then the two routes follow Pleasant Street, which crosses the Nashua River Rail Trail, until Route 111 diverges onto Elm Street. Route 225 continues on Pleasant Street until it joins Route 119 on Main Street. Near the south end of downtown, the routes meet the west end of Route 40 (Lowell Road).

Pivotal did not appear on the racecourse until the late autumn of 1995. At Newbury Racecourse on 19 October he started a 16/1 outsider for a six furlong maiden race and finished ninth of the twenty runners behind the Barry Hills-trained Fly Tip. Eleven days later, Pivotal recorded his first success in a six furlong maiden race at Newcastle Racecourse. Ridden for the first time by George Duffield he took the lead inside the final furlong and won by two and a half lengths from Domak Amaam. In November, Pivotal was brought back in distance for a five furlong race at Folkestone Racecourse. He took the lead approaching the final furlong and accelerated clear of the field to win by four lengths in "impressive" style.

Nordhoff High School's athletic teams, known as the Rangers, are charter members of the Citrus Coast League, established in 2018 as part of the CIF Southern Section. Previously, Nordhoff competed in the Tri-County Athletic Association.

As of 31 December 2020, Conters im Prättigau had a population of 4,416. In 2008, 19.7% of the population were foreign nationals. Over the last 10 years, the population has experienced a slight decline. As of 2000, the majority of the population spoke German (88.7%), followed by Portuguese (2.9%) and Serbo-Croatian (2.6%) as the second and third most common languages, respectively.

The WindSingers went through many changes during the years but the goal to perform pop and jazz songs on the highest level stayed. They became one of the most successful a cappella group in Hungary and in the last years they had many concerts in Austria, Slovakia, Serbia and Romania too. The WindSingers became a regular guest at the Budapest Jazz Club which is one of the most prestigious jazz stage in Hungary. In the summer of 2013 and 2014 the group won one-one silver diploma during the vokal.total a cappella competition in Pop and Jazz categories. In the summer of 2015 their first album was released called "The Morning Comes".

Tropical Storm George originated on September 15, 1998, as a tropical depression approximately 300 miles south-southwest of the Cape Verde Islands in the eastern Atlantic. Initially moving westward, George began strengthening on September 17 and reached hurricane status the following day. Passing about 1,000 km (approximately 620 miles) south of Bermuda, George brought peak wind gusts of up to 148 kt at Bermuda Harbour Radio before the mast failed. In addition to rainbands, the storm caused significant wind damage, with widespread impacts reported across the island, including structural damage and disruptions to communication infrastructure.

N,N-Dialkylhydrazones are versatile intermediates in organic synthesis. The C=N bond can undergo hydrolysis, oxidation, reduction, and react with organometallic nucleophiles, while the N–N bond can be reduced to free amines. The alpha-hydrogen is significantly more acidic than in ketones, enabling deprotonation with lithium diisopropylamide (LDA) to form azaenolates, which can be alkylated by alkyl halides. Hydrazines SAMP and RAMP serve as chiral auxiliaries, facilitating enantioselective transformations in asymmetric synthesis.

Masich is an adjunct history faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University teaching American History and Public History courses. His lively lectures on subjects ranging from American Indians to American innovation have entertained and educated audiences from coast to coast. A recognized authority on the preservation and creative interpretation of history, he has been a faculty member of the prestigious Seminar for Historical Administration, an American Association for State and Local History (AASLH) Council member, an American Alliance of Museums (AAM) Accreditation Commissioner (2004-2015), and serves on the National Council for Public History's (NCPH) Editorial Board for The Public Historian. His popular presentations include “Unknown Stories of the Civil War,” “History of Innovation,” “Young George Washington,” and, for professional museum audiences, “Lighten Up!” (humor therapy for directors and other humor impaired museum professionals) and “History Museum Vampires: How to Suck the Life Out of Your Museum.”

Compared to drinking, alcohol intoxication begins far more quickly upon rectal consumption since the alcohol is absorbed directly into the bloodstream. The lower gastrointestinal tract lacks the alcohol dehydrogenase enzyme present in the stomach and liver that breaks down ethanol into acetylaldehyde, which is actually more toxic than ethanol (drinking alcohol) and is responsible for most chronic effects of ethanol. When rectally absorbed, ethanol will still eventually arrive at the liver, but the high alcohol content could overwhelm the organ. Additionally, consuming the alcohol rectally neutralizes the body's ability to reject the toxin by vomiting.

The *Lost in Translation* soundtrack has been widely celebrated for its impact and legacy. Frank Mojica of *Consequence of Sound* described it as "renowned for its soundtrack, and the music serves as the third star of the picture," and speculated on the role the film and its music played in the mid-2000s revival of the shoegaze genre. This resurgence saw bands like My Bloody Valentine and The Jesus and Mary Chain reform, with the latter performing alongside Scarlett Johansson during their Coachella reunion. Nat Cramp, head of the Sonic Cathedral record label, credited the soundtrack as a key factor in rekindling interest in shoegaze, noting its mainstream success. The soundtrack has also earned critical acclaim, ranking 22nd on *Rolling Stone*'s "The 25 Greatest Soundtracks of All Time," 7th on *Pitchfork*'s "The 50 Best Movie Soundtracks of All Time," and being featured in *Empire*'s "The 20 Soundtracks That Defined the 2000s." Its blend of artists, including Kevin Shields and The Jesus and Mary Chain, continues to resonate with audiences.

Richard Mentor Johnson was born on October 17, 1780, near present-day Louisville, Kentucky, in an area then known as Bryant's Station, Virginia. He was the son of Robert Johnson, a Revolutionary War captain and pioneer who purchased land in Kentucky, and Jemima Suggett Johnson, noted for her patriotic service during the war. Robert and Jemima married in June 1771 and had 12 children, including eight sons, five of whom served in the War of 1812. Richard was one of the surviving brothers. Robert, a legislator and settler, established Johnson's Station in Scott County, Kentucky, while Jemima came from a prominent Virginia family.

The West Midlands, the second most populous city-region in the United Kingdom after Greater London, established the position of Mayor of the West Midlands in 2017 following a 2015 devolution agreement between the UK government and local council leaders. This agreement, underpinned by the Cities and Local Government Devolution Act 2016, required a directly elected metro mayor for combined authorities to gain additional powers. The inaugural mayoral term lasted three years, with subsequent elections held every four years. In the 2017 election, Conservative candidate Andy Street defeated Labour’s Sion Simon to become the first Mayor of the West Midlands, leading the newly created West Midlands Combined Authority.

Sabeena was born on 6 October 1918 at Gothuruth, a small village in the periphery of Ernakulam district of the south Indian state of Kerala to Joseph and Mariamma. She earned two master's degrees, in History and Economics, and served our her career as a teacher. She married Ponjikkara Rafi in 1963, following a period of romance, but the couple did not have children. She died on 22 June 1990, at the age of 65; survived by Rafi, who died two years later, on 6 September 1992.

Hans was the co-artist for Marvel's limited series Angela: Asgard's Assassin #1-6 (2014) with Phil Jimenez; the series was written by Kieron Gillen and Marguerite Bennett. Russ Burlingame, for ComicBook.com in 2014, commented of the series creative team it was Kieron Gillen, Marguerite Bennett and Phil Jiminez who "got a lot of the attention. Stephanie Hans, a fairly young artist who is billed as doing backup stories in the book, didn't get quite as much notice, but she's done some beautiful work on Asgard in the past and is a favorite of both of the writers". Jesse Schedeen, in IGN's review of the first issue, wrote that "Phil Jimenez handles art duties on the main story, while Stephanie Hans tackles the interlude segment. Of the two, Hans stands out more thanks to the ethereal watercolor approach that goes hand in hand with the storybook quality". Petra Halbur, in her review of the series for The Mary Sue, wrote "Phil Jimenez and Stephanie Hans' artwork is breathtaking. The character designs are interesting and varied, the action sequences are beautifully realized and the use of color is just, like... the best".

Input voucher programs rely heavily on complementary policies and practices in order to function effectively. For instance, some existing distribution networks (shops, or retailers) must be in place already in order to ensure wide penetration of the input. Some access to microcredit may be necessary to help defray even the initial subsidized costs. Furthermore, subsidized fertilizer programs are most effective when they encourage non-users (farmers who otherwise would not use fertilizer) to participate, thereby creating new sources of production.

In 1956, Giovanna Caleffi relocated to Nervi, a district that had become part of the Genoa conurbation. Nervi had previously served as an operational base for anarchist partisans, including figures like Cesare Zaccaria, during the resistance. By January 1959, Caleffi transferred the management of *Volontà* to Nervi, aiming to strengthen its role as a hub for anarchist thought and organization. This move coincided with her personal break-up with Zaccaria, whose diplomatic yet reformist approach to Italian anarchism had begun to diverge from traditional revolutionary ideals. Despite their separation, Caleffi remained committed to advancing anarchist principles through her editorial and organizational efforts.

Sara Rey Álvarez launched the Independent Democratic Feminist Party on 15 January 1933, after the passing of the woman's suffrage law in Uruguay. Sofía Álvarez Vignoli left the party in early 1933 over the party's failure to defend a woman's right to divorce. From June 1933 onwards the party published a journal, Ideas y Acción.

In its original American broadcast, "Cheddar" was seen by an estimated 1.85 million household viewers and gained a 0.8/2 ratings share among adults aged 18–49, according to Nielsen Media Research. This was a slight decrease in viewership from the previous episode, which was watched by 2.13 million viewers with a 0.9/3 in the 18-49 demographics. This means that 0.8 percent of all households with televisions watched the episode, while 2 percent of all households watching television at that time watched it. With these ratings, Brooklyn Nine-Nine was the third most watched show on FOX for the night, beating The Grinder, but behind Grandfathered, and New Girl, second on its timeslot and tenth for the night, behind Grandfathered, CBS News: Campaign 2016, The Muppets, New Girl, Super Tuesday, NCIS: New Orleans, NCIS, and The Voice.

The review aggregator Rotten Tomatoes gives the film a 93% approval rating, based on 71 reviews, with an average rating of 7.9/10. The website's consensus reads, "Final Account falls shy of the definitive statement suggested by its title, but the belated reckoning on display remains chillingly valuable viewing." According to Metacritic, which sampled 17 critics and calculated a weighted average score of 76 out of 100, the film received "generally favorable reviews".

A 2013 study found that infants born to Mexican-immigrant women in the United States had mortality rates approximately 10% lower than those of non-Hispanic white women, supporting the "epidemiologic paradox," where certain immigrant groups exhibit better health outcomes despite socioeconomic challenges. Refugee communities, including Palestine refugees, often face socioeconomic hardships but maintain low infant and childhood mortality rates compared to the broader Arab world. However, neonatal mortality among Palestine refugees, linked to inadequate neonatal care, mirrors causes seen in other low-resource regions, such as sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. Leading causes of infant and neonatal deaths include infections, preterm birth complications, and birth asphyxia.

David Edward Hughes is credited with discovering the phenomenon of radio waves in 1879, nine years before Heinrich Hertz proved their existence. While working in London, Hughes noticed sparking in a Bell telephone receiver during experiments with nearby electrical equipment. He developed an improved detector based on his microphone design to pick up the unknown "extra current" and used a clockwork interruptor to produce a series of sparks. Through trial and error, Hughes found he could detect these "aerial waves" with his telephone device at a range of up to 500 yards (460 meters). Despite his groundbreaking discovery, Hughes did not publish his findings, leaving the formal proof of radio waves to Hertz in 1888.

The Chinatown Handy Guides were published at the height of the American Civil Rights Movement. With their focus on Chinese Americans however, the booklets promoted integration of Chinese in America rather than fighting against segregation practices that African Americans faced in southern states. Fang was a Chinese American who published his work during a time when "San Francisco entrepreneurs recognized the retail potential of the model family concept” to serve as “both a political defense mechanism and a means to strengthen (the Chinese community’s) economic muscle.”

On 2 December 2017 the local iwi Te Kawerau ā Maki placed an unofficial rāhui (traditional prohibition) over the kauri forest that covers large areas of the Waitākere Ranges to try to slow kauri dieback's spread, but initially at least the rāhui was frequently breached by the general public. The Waitākere Ranges were consequently the only large area of forest close to West Auckland that was still open to the public. On 20 February 2018, Auckland Council announced that all forested areas of the Waitākere Ranges would be closed to the public, as the rāhui had not been effective. At-risk parts of the Hunua Ranges to the south-east of Auckland were also to be closed as a preventive measure, even though kauri dieback had not yet been recorded there.

Antonio Riva, an Italian merchant, was executed by firing squad in Beijing on August 17, 1951, alongside Japanese bookseller Ruichi Yamaguchi. They were convicted of involvement in an alleged American-backed plot to assassinate Mao Zedong and other Communist officials by firing mortar shells at a National Day procession near Tiananmen on October 1, 1950.

The ear is particularly vulnerable to avulsion injuries because of its position on the side of the head. The most common cause of ear avulsions is human bites, followed by falls, motor vehicle collisions, and dog bites. A partially avulsed ear can be reattached through suturing or microvascular surgery, depending on the severity of the injury. Microvascular surgery can also be used to reattach a completely avulsed ear, but its success rate is lower because of the need for venous drainage. The ear can also be reconstructed with cartilage and skin grafts or an external ear prosthesis can be made by an anaplastologist.

Chargois was claimed off waivers by the Los Angeles Dodgers on February 23, 2018. He split both 2018 and 2019 between the AAA Oklahoma City Dodgers and Los Angeles. With the Dodgers he was 3–4 with a 4.53 ERA in 60 games. Chargois was released by the Dodgers on December 28, 2019 to pursue an opportunity in Japan.

The Secret Service is tasked with safeguarding the payment and financial systems of the United States from a wide range of financial and cyber-based crimes. Financial investigations include counterfeit U.S. currency, bank and financial institution fraud, mail fraud, wire fraud, illicit financing operations, and major conspiracies. Cyber investigations include cybercrime, network intrusions, identity theft, access device fraud, credit card fraud, and intellectual property crimes. The Secret Service is also a member of the FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force (JTTF) which investigates and combats terrorism on a national and international scale. Also, the Secret Service investigates missing and exploited children and is a partner of the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC).

Milan Škriniar made his official Slovak Super Liga debut for MŠK Žilina on 27 March 2012, at the age of 17 years and 49 days, in a match against ViOn Zlaté Moravce. He scored his first goal in the league on 23 November 2012, also against ViOn Zlaté Moravce, during a 4-1 victory.

Baldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn was re-released in 2003 as part of *Baldur's Gate II: The Collection*, which included the expansion *Throne of Bhaal*. In 2004, it was bundled with *Baldur's Gate*, *Tales of the Sword Coast*, *Icewind Dale*, *Heart of Winter*, and *Icewind Dale II* in the *Black Isle Compilation Part Two*. The 2006 *Baldur's Gate: 4 in 1 Boxset* featured *Baldur's Gate*, *Tales of the Sword Coast*, *Baldur's Gate II: Shadows of Amn*, and *Throne of Bhaal*. Additionally, *Shadows of Amn* and *Throne of Bhaal* were included in *The Forgotten Realms Deluxe Edition*, *Ultimate Dungeons & Dragons*, and *Dungeons & Dragons Anthology: The Master Collection*. In 2010, *Baldur's Gate II Complete* was released digitally on GOG.com, bundling *Shadows of Amn*, *Throne of Bhaal*, bonus content, and digital manuals in PDF format.

O'Dwyer first played for Tipperary as a 16-year-old when he joined the minor team during the 2008 Munster Championship. He made his first appearance for the team on 30 April 2008 and scored two points in the 3-21 to 1-12 defeat of Limerick. On 13 July 2008, O'Dwyer came on as a substitute for Paddy Murphy when Tipperary suffered a 0-19 to 0-18 defeat by Cork in the Munster final.

On December 20, 1976, the dismembered body of a young white or Hispanic female was found in multiple suitcases beneath a highway bridge in White Haven, Pennsylvania. Investigators were unable to identify her and she was buried under the name "Beth Doe". After 44 years, investigators finally got a break in the case in 2020 when they compared her DNA to genealogy databases and identified Luis Colon Jr. as a relative of the deceased. Colon Jr. and his family told investigators that Colon Jr.'s aunt, Evelyn Colon, disappeared from Jersey City, New Jersey at the age of 15 in December 1976 and that she was pregnant and in an abusive relationship at the time she disappeared.

On 25 October 2014, President Raul Khajimba permanently appointed Raul Lolua as Minister for Internal Affairs of Abkhazia. Lolua was re-appointed in April 2015, replacing Beslan Khagba, who had served as acting minister. Following Lolua's re-appointment, protests erupted in Sukhumi. A month later, Prime Minister Beslan Butba was replaced by Artur Mikvabia. President Khajimba accepted Lolua's resignation in May 2016, appointing Beslan Khagba, previously Deputy Minister for Internal Affairs, as his successor.

Fakhri grew up in Tehran. She was an undergraduate student at the Sharif University of Technology. After completing her bachelor's degree, Fakhri moved to Rice University, where she studied carbon nanotube dynamics in complex media. Her doctoral research considered the dynamics of single walled carbon nanotubes in water. Specifically, Fakhri has proposed the use of near-infrared fluorescence of carbon nanotubes as independent reporters of cellular systems. Fakhri joined the Georg-August-Universität as a postdoctoral researcher.

A superdelegate is an unpledged delegate to the Democratic National Convention who is seated automatically and chooses for themselves for whom they vote. These superdelegates include elected officials, and party activists and officials. They make up slightly under 15 percent of all convention delegates.

The Dima Halam Daogah (DHD) is divided into two factions: DHD(J) and DHD(N). The DHD(J), also known as the Black Widows, has an estimated cadre strength of 300 militants, while DHD(N) has 1,152 cadres. Pranab Nunisa became chairman of DHD(N) on June 24, 2004, ousting Jewel Garlosa, who leads DHD(J). Dilip Nunisa serves as chairman of DHD(N), with other key figures including leaders of their respective armed wings and negotiation teams.

Loeb's offers a diverse range of products, including designer eyewear, men's clothing, shoes, and outdoor gear, catering to men, women, and children. The store features its own exclusive brands alongside renowned labels. For men's clothing, Loeb's provides a selection of casual and formal wear. Shoe offerings include popular designer brands, while sunglasses from world-famous labels are also available. Additionally, Loeb's carries outdoor and travel gear from leading brands, ensuring quality and style for various lifestyles. With a focus on premium products, Loeb's combines its own collections with a curated selection of global names to meet the needs of its diverse customer base.

The South African regulatory body for drugs, the Medicines Control Council (MCC), initially classified dagga as a Schedule 7 substance, which meant that it had no medicinal value and was "illegal to cultivate, analyse, possess, research, use, sell or supply without authorisation from the Department of Health." In 2016, it published regulations providing for the use of dagga for medical reasons, and expressed a desire to reclassify "cannabinoid medication" as a Schedule 6 substance, which would make it available for medicinal use. However, the Dagga Couple noted that partial decriminalisation in 2017 has reduced the significance of the proposed change in scheduling, and have called for a more drastic reclassification of the drug.

Natalie Angier, writing in The New York Times, called The Change: Women, Ageing, and the Menopause (1991) a "brilliant, gutsy, exhilarating, exasperating fury of a book ... tantalizingly close to being a potential feminist classic on a par with The Female Eunuch." In it, Greer writes of the myths about menopause—or as she prefers to call it the "climacteric", or critical period. "Frightening females is fun", she wrote in The Age in 2002. "Women were frightened into using hormone replacement therapy by dire predictions of crumbling bones, heart disease, loss of libido, depression, despair, disease and death if they let nature take its course." She argues that scaring women is "big business and hugely profitable". The book, including the medical information, was updated and reissued in 2018.

In 2010, Whitman was hired as the athletic director for NCAA Division III Wisconsin–La Crosse. While Whitman was at Wisconsin–La Crosse, Eagles teams won four national championships and 21 conference titles. Whitman was later named athletic director of Washington University in St. Louis in 2014.

The Minot Public Schools system operates ten elementary schools (K–5) in the city: Bel Air, Edison, John Hoeven, Lewis and Clark, Longfellow, McKinley Roosevelt, Perkett, Sunnyside, and Washington. The district also operates Bell Elementary, about five miles southeast of Minot. Jefferson Elementary closed in 2003. The old Washington Elementary building closed in 2007 and the students moved to a new building that was renovated from an old health care center. There are also two elementary schools (K–6) on the Minot Air Force Base: Dakota and North Plains. The 2011 flood resulted in the relocation of Erik Ramstad Middle School and the closure of Lincoln Elementary, as both buildings were damaged beyond economical repair. Longfellow Elementary was expanded after the flood and children who lived in the Lincoln neighborhood then attended Longfellow Elementary.

The Times selected Forest City Ratner Companies as the developer for its Eighth Avenue tower in February 2000. The following month, the Times began negotiating with the city and state. The Times wanted to pay $75 million and a two-thirds deduction in real estate taxes, but the state wanted $125 million for the site and the city wanted the Times to pay full taxes. Some commentators wrote about how the Times had opposed corporate tax relief despite seeking such relief for itself. The parties signed a nonbinding agreement in June 2000, wherein the Times agreed to pay $100 million. The Times was to occupy half of the planned tower, a single unit covering the second through 28th floors. The remainder of the space would be operated by Forest City and leased to office tenants. At the time, other media headquarters were being developed nearby, such as the Hearst Tower on 57th Street and the Condé Nast Building at 4 Times Square.

In 1936, the Air Ministry purchased the Chilmark quarries and surrounding land to establish a dedicated storage facility for explosives. The site became RAF Chilmark, home to No. 11 Maintenance Unit. Munitions were stored in underground quarries, with extensive above-ground storage areas located at Chilmark, Dinton, and other nearby sites.

The reception of *Housefull* (1999) was mixed. While Kalki praised Parthiban for attempting a "Speed"-style thriller in Tamil and noted its unique genre, another critic described the film as "awfully silly" and serving "no other purpose than to show Parthiban as being capable of political authority." The latter review appeared in *The Hindu*.

Brookfield is west of Milwaukee in Waukesha County in an area originally inhabited by Potawatomi Indians. The first white settler, William Howe, arrived in 1820 with a Presidential Land Grant giving him title to the area. Soon after, Robert Curren bought a claim in 1836 and established a tavern and inn.

In 1878, Lookout was wrecked in the Japan Sea. She was en route to Puget Sound from Shanghai, and was partially dismasted in a typhoon on September 12. She went up on a reef near the island of Kutsonoshima. Three crewmen drowned attempting to land the ship’s boats; the rest managed to land with the help of local fishermen.

The first church in Jerusalem, as described in Acts 2 and 4, shared all possessions, practicing common ownership inspired by the teachings of Jesus. This model influenced groups like the Hutterites, who have lived communally since 1528, and the Bruderhof, practicing common ownership for nearly 100 years. These Christian groups establish charities that own property and resources, supporting their members and advancing faith-based missions. Members are maintained by the charity, which reflects their commitment to shared living, mutual aid, and the integration of faith and community.

Many Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) models over-prioritize current high-value customers, often at the expense of potential customers. This stems from a mathematical issue where models focus heavily on aggregate metrics, masking inaccuracies at the individual level. While high-value customers are valuable due to their frequent purchases, brand loyalty, and lower service and communication costs, they can reach saturation, limiting growth opportunities. Effective marketing can elevate low-value customers by addressing their needs and increasing engagement, but many CLV models fail to account for this potential. Additionally, these models often neglect the importance of gathering insights from potential customers, which can be achieved through targeted surveys or feedback mechanisms. This approach helps businesses identify untapped opportunities and balance short-term gains with long-term customer value.

The Merchant Street Historic District, located in downtown Honolulu, is bounded by Bishop Street to the southeast and Nuuanu Avenue to the northwest. It features a mix of civic and commercial buildings surrounded by modern high-rises. Serving as a key example of Honolulu's architectural heritage, the district showcases styles such as Renaissance Revival and Classical Revival, reflecting commercial development from the mid-19th to early 20th centuries. Added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1973, it lies just south of the Chinatown Historic District.

Portable art found in prehistoric surroundings has its own digression ways. Some interpret objects markings (stones, bones, etc.) as counting systems. The possibility has been considered that these objects are ornamental or are "toys, hunting game, mortuary offerings, divination instruments, Mnemotechnics artifacts, menstruation fetishis, Puberty or fertility amulets or a healing Talisman”. It is difficult to establish the meaning and purposes of these portable pieces as are the first found in the Nuevo León State, but can be analogy compared with portable art found in the lower Pecos River (Texas) and the North American Great Basin.

The murders began in June 2004. As victims, Kopylov chose single women he spotted in deserted areas near forest parks, whom he then threatened at gunpoint and attacked with knives, stones, pieces of metal rods, pipes and animal bones. All of his victims were subjected to violent sexual acts, with one of them having a lit cigarette burned on her genitalia.

Diego Higa has performed drifting exhibitions in public spaces alongside notable figures, including Brazilian Red Bull ProSkater Letícia Bufoni. He also collaborated with YouTuber, influencer, and gamer Nyvi Estephan for a campaign promoting safe driving. A viral drifting video featuring Higa, filmed at his private racetrack in Registro, South Coast of São Paulo, was sponsored by his father’s NSC Garage. This racetrack, home to the Diego Higa Drift School, serves as a hub for teaching aspiring drifters, utilizing a 280 hp 350Z training car known as "Zetto" to refine their skills.

Near the end of May, Eliza sends Mattie to deliver a batch of cookies to the Bents, a family living nearby. On her way, while picking flowers, Mattie encounters Gardiner Bent, who is studying to become a teacher at a college in Philadelphia. Upon arriving at the Bents' home, Gardiner’s mother gratefully gives Mattie a small jar of homemade jam in return for the cookies. When Mattie returns home, Eliza notices her humming a tune and discerns that it reflects a mix of longing and uncertainty. Eliza gently advises Mattie to embrace the changes ahead, reminding her that growth often requires leaving familiar comforts behind. This advice causes Mattie to burst into tears, revealing her deep apprehension about leaving home. Eliza, however, realizes that Mattie’s hesitation stems not from fear but from a profound attachment to her family and surroundings. Jess, reflecting on Eliza’s own past, recalls how Eliza once faced similar emotions when she left home, offering a poignant parallel between their experiences.

Animation for the episode was provided by Squeeze,: 29:40 : 4 with Stephan Franck serving as head of animation. Andrews developed the series' cel-shaded animation style with Ryan Meinerding, the head of visual development at Marvel Studios. Though the series has a consistent art style, elements such as the camera and color palette differ between episodes.: 4 Concept art for the episode is included during the end credits, and was released online by Marvel following the episode's premiere.

The Royal Botanic Garden, Sydney, began its development along scientific lines under Charles Fraser, the first Colonial Botanist of New South Wales, who held the position from 1821 until his death in 1831. Appointed superintendent of the Botanic Garden in 1816, Fraser made significant contributions, including cataloguing hundreds of Australian native plants. Between 1827 and 1828, he brought back various plant specimens, including native red cedar, from his inland excursions to the Brisbane district and northern New South Wales. Initially thought to have come from the Illawarra region, the cedar was actually collected further north. Fraser often collaborated with explorers such as John Oxley on these expeditions. Following Fraser’s death in December 1831, Richard Cunningham succeeded him as superintendent, bringing expertise from his time at Kew Gardens. Richard’s brother, Allan Cunningham, also played a key role in the garden’s development, furthering its transformation into a scientific institution.

There are currently several Ferguson trademarks registered for class 09, audio visual equipment, in the UK: EU010787471 Registered by Ferguson Sp. z.o.o. 04/04/2012 but opposed and UKUK00000652009 EU003131927 Registered by Thomson Multimedia now Technicolor, dates back to 26/09/1946.

The papal conclave of August 1492, held at the Vatican Basilica, saw Cardinal Rodrigo Borgia elected as Pope Alexander VI. His election, notorious for allegations of bribery, was influenced by key figures like Cardinal Giambattista Orsini, whom ecclesiastical historian Infessura claimed was pivotal to Borgia's success. The Patriarch of Venice, Maffeo Gherardi, supported Orsini's candidacy, though Borgia ultimately prevailed. Cardinal Ascanio Sforza, a power-broker in the conclave, cast the decisive vote, securing Borgia’s controversial rise to the papacy.

David Lascelles, 8th Earl of Harewood, was born on 21 October 1950 at Harewood House, Yorkshire, to George Lascelles, 7th Earl of Harewood, and Marion Stein. He was the eldest of three siblings. Baptised at All Saints' Church, Harewood, his godparents included The Princess Elizabeth (later Queen Elizabeth II), then Duchess of Edinburgh. His father, a first cousin of Queen Elizabeth II, was the eldest grandchild of King George V and Queen Mary. At birth, David Lascelles was 13th in the line of succession to the British throne.

The name Chernobyl has become synonymous with the concept of a nuclear energy disaster. Referencing the political damage from the inept initial response to the disaster, pundits sometimes use the phrase "Chernobyl moment" to describe alleged analogous damaging failures, such as responses to the COVID-19 pandemic. Ukraine has announced an intention to open the disaster area to tourism. Numerous cultural works have referenced the disaster, some of which are listed in this article.

The village of Wissahickon was founded in the late nineteenth century by officials of the Pencoyd Iron Works. In the 1880s, mill owners and wealthy business owners from nearby Manayunk began seeking elegant homes on spacious lots in Wissahickon, drawn to land previously owned by prominent Philadelphia families such as the Camac, Dobson, Salaignac, and Wetherill families along the Wissahickon Creek. Grand single-detached and semi-detached homes were constructed in high Victorian style, featuring ample side and rear yards. The neighborhood became highly desirable due to its proximity to the Wissahickon Valley, tree-lined streets, modern sewer and water systems, and its reputation as a "parkside Victorian wonderland," offering a blend of natural beauty and refined living.

Barnes was born in Elkhart, Indiana, on July 4, 1935. His father, Sylvester, worked as a real estate investor; his mother, Lura, was a housewife. He attended Elkhart Central High School in his hometown. He then studied at Purdue University, where he played offensive and defensive halfback, left end, and cornerback for the Purdue Boilermakers. He registered 257 rushing yards on 62 carries, 319 yards on 20 receptions, 136 yards on seven kickoff returns, and 86 return yards off of his five interceptions during his time with the Boilermakers. He was one of the favorite receiving targets of Len Dawson, a future Pro Football Hall of Famer, but cornerback was his best position. He was drafted by the Chicago Bears in the fourth round (42nd overall selection) of the 1958 NFL Draft.

Cuba began sending medical aid to Haiti in 1998 following the devastation caused by Hurricane Georges, marking the start of an enduring collaboration. Since then, over 6,094 Cuban doctors have worked in Haiti, providing critical healthcare services. By 2010, Cuba had trained 550 Haitian doctors at the Latin American Medical School (ELAM) in Havana, with 567 more Haitians currently studying there. Between 1998 and 2010, Cuban medical teams conducted over 14.6 million consultations, performed 207,000 surgeries, and restored eyesight to 45,000 people. This aid contributed to a more than 50% reduction in infant, maternal, and child mortality rates and increased life expectancy in Haiti from 54 to 61 years. Cuba’s medical assistance has been a cornerstone of health improvements in one of the region’s most impoverished nations.

The first church in Jerusalem shared all their money and possessions (Acts of the Apostles 2 and 4). Inspired by the Early Christians, many Christians have since tried to follow their example of community of goods and common ownership. Common ownership is practiced by some Christian groups such as the Hutterites (for about 500 years), the Bruderhof (for some 100 years) and others. In those cases, property is generally owned by a charity set up for the purpose of maintaining the members of the religious groups.

Eisenhower, while accepting the doctrine of containment, sought to counter the Soviet Union through more active means as detailed in the State-Defense report NSC 68. The Eisenhower administration and the Central Intelligence Agency used covert action to interfere with suspected communist governments abroad. An early use of covert action was against the elected Prime Minister of Iran, Mohammed Mosaddeq, resulting in the 1953 Iranian coup d'état. The CIA also instigated the 1954 Guatemalan coup d'état by the local military that overthrew President Jacobo Arbenz Guzmán, whom U.S. officials viewed as too friendly toward the Soviet Union. Critics have produced conspiracy theories about the causal factors, but according to historian Stephen M. Streeter, CIA documents show the United Fruit Company (UFCO) played no major role in Eisenhower's decision, that the Eisenhower administration did not need to be forced into the action by any lobby groups, and that Soviet influence in Guatemala was minimal.

Alexander was born in Lynchburg, Virginia, in 1880, the son of former slaves. He attended public schools in Lynchburg and entered Lincoln University in Chester County, Pennsylvania in 1895 at the age of 14. He graduated in 1899 and then attended the Boston College of Physicians and Surgeons (now Tufts University School of Medicine), receiving his M.D. in 1903. After practicing medicine for one year in Kimball, West Virginia, he opened a practice in Orange, New Jersey, where he resided for the rest of his life.

In the late 1990s, the counties of Dublin, Kildare and Wicklow represented almost half of the population of the state and had only one health board, the Eastern Health Board. The rest of the state was served by seven health boards, each with a designated functional area. The 1999 Health (Eastern Regional Health Authority) Act was introduced and dissolved the Eastern Health Board and created four bodies in its place, thus bringing to eleven the number of regional health authorities and boards.

The RADA Library, established in 1904, houses over 40,000 items, including more than 15,000 plays, making it one of the largest collections of rare and out-of-print English-language plays. Alongside plays, the library contains books on various aspects of theatre. Early contributions to the collection came from notable actors and writers, including Sir Arthur Wing Pinero and Sir Johnston Forbes-Robertson, helping to create a vital resource for students and theatre enthusiasts.

The second installment in Supermassive Games' *The Dark Pictures Anthology* series, *Little Hope*, builds upon the narrative-driven horror foundation established by its predecessor, *Man of Medan*, while introducing a more intricate story structure. Directed by Will Doyle, the game explores themes of paranoia and persecution, centering on the fictional New England town of Little Hope and its dark history during the 17th-century witch trials. The development team was particularly intrigued by the historical period’s atmosphere of fear and its parallels to modern societal anxieties, which influenced the game’s concept of "Doubles"—characters in the present who bear striking resemblances to figures from the past. Inspired by Arthur Miller’s play *The Crucible* and films such as *The Witch* and *The Blair Witch Project*, *Little Hope* weaves a narrative that blurs the lines between reality and supernatural horror. English actor Will Poulter was hired to voice and provide motion capture for the lead character, Andrew, bringing his experience from projects like *Black Mirror: Bandersnatch* to the role.
