This installment’s layered storytelling and historical influences mark a departure from the more straightforward approach of *Man of Medan*.

In his May 2016 speech on energy policy, Donald Trump pledged to "cancel" the Paris climate agreement, a landmark accord adopted in December 2015 at the United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP21) in Paris, where nearly 200 countries committed to reducing carbon emissions. Trump criticized the agreement as unfair to the United States, claiming it imposed burdensome regulations while allowing countries like China and India to continue using fossil fuels. He inaccurately stated that the agreement gave "foreign bureaucrats control" over U.S. energy use, despite its reliance on voluntary national pledges. Within his first 100 days in office, Trump vowed to withdraw from the agreement, arguing it unfairly disadvantaged the U.S. economy and taxpayers. At a minimum, he said he would renegotiate the deal, though he ultimately pursued withdrawal. Trump tied his opposition to the Paris Agreement to his broader "America First Energy Plan," which prioritized fossil fuel production and reduced environmental regulations, framing the accord as a threat to U.S. sovereignty and economic growth.

Cliff Lubwa P’chong was born in Gulu, Uganda. He was educated at Koc Goma Primary School, then Gulu High School for his junior secondary school before joing Sir Samuel Baker School, Gulu, for his O'levels, then National Teachers' College Kyambogo where he qualified as a grade five teacher in 1969, before being posted to St Charles Lwanga SSS, Koboko. In 1976 he attained a bachelor's degree in literature and linguistics from the prestigious Makerere University, before proceeding to Durham and Exeter universities. He was a creative writing fellow at the University of Iowa (1987), and lectured in drama-in-education at the Institute of Teacher Education, Kyambogo. He had his early education there and in Kyambogo. He taught for several years, and then studied literature and linguistics at Makerere University.

"Year of the Gun" received mixed reviews, with some critics highlighting its overcomplicated plot. Janet Maslin of *The New York Times* criticized the film's transition from Michael Mewshaw's book, on which David Ambrose's screenplay is based, describing the plot as "dizzyingly overcomplicated." The story follows American journalist David Raybourne (Andrew McCarthy), whose novel, inspired by the Red Brigades, mirrors their real-life plan to kidnap former Italian Prime Minister Aldo Moro. Raybourne claims his work will resemble a "Day of the Jackal"-style blend of fact and fiction. The film explores his advance knowledge of Moro's kidnapping, intertwining political intrigue with personal stakes.

During the off-season, Vancouver signed defenceman Jason Garrison to a six-year contract worth $27.6 million. While the Canucks brought in Garrison, they lost longtime defenceman Sami Salo. The Canucks wanted to re-sign Salo, though they were unwilling to sign him to a two-year contract and offered only a one-year deal. Salo chose to leave and signed a two-year, $7.5 million contract with the Tampa Bay Lightning. It was believed that Garrison would step in and contribute to the power play and replace the departed Salo, who had performed a similar role. However, he struggled to adjust with his new team. He was taken off the power play unit and dropped to the third pairing within the first month. After the injuries to Bieksa, Garrison was moved from left defenceman to the right side pairing with Dan Hamhuis. The coaching staff came to consider this pairing to be the best and most trusted. Garrison finished the season with eight goals and eight assists, scoring three goals and three assists on the power play, and registering a +18 rating. His plus-minus was second on the team and led all defenceman.

Ocean embarked on a solo tour through North America and Europe to promote his mixtape Nostalgia Ultra and his other musical projects. The set lists to the various shows varied, though "Thinking Bout You" was performed at all shows. The track was included during Ocean's setlist at the April 2012 Coachella Musical Festival. Complex magazine stated the "his falsetto on “Thinking About You” made women in the audience swoon." Ocean later performed the song at the 2012 MTV Video Music Awards.

The first parasite allegedly discovered to cause cancer was the rat roundworm Gongylonema neoplasticum, identified by Danish pathologist Johannes Fibiger in 1913. Fibiger initially named the parasite Spiroptera carcinoma in 1914, later renamed Gongylonema neoplasticum. He experimentally induced stomach cancer in rats using the parasite, publishing his findings in 1913. For this work, Fibiger was awarded the 1926 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, credited for demonstrating the first controlled induction of cancer in laboratory animals. However, it was later revealed that the tumors were caused by vitamin A deficiency, not the parasite. Fibiger’s Nobel Prize remains controversial, reflecting the Karolinska Institute's misjudgment of his findings.

"Disco 2000" tells the story of a narrator falling for a childhood friend called Deborah, who is more popular than he is and wondering what it would be like to meet again when they are older. Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker based the lyrics on a girl he knew as a child and recalled, "the only bit that isn't true is the woodchip wallpaper." He elaborated:

At the 87th Geneva Motor Show held from 9 to 19 March 2017, exactly 30 years after the original CTR was launched, Ruf presented a new model paying tribute to the original CTR. The new CTR utilises an in-house developed carbon fibre body bearing resemblance to a Porsche 964 along with an aluminium chassis resulting in a dry weight of 2,640 lb (1,200 kg). The car is not based on the 911, unlike the original CTR. The new CTR is powered by a 3.6-litre water cooled twin-turbocharged Flat-6 engine producing 700 hp (710 PS; 522 kW) at 6,750 rpm and 880 N⋅m (649 lb⋅ft) of torque at 4,000 rpm. The car is capable of accelerating from 0–100 km/h (62 mph) in under 3.5 seconds and can reach a top speed of 360 km/h (224 mph). Power is sent to the rear wheels through a 6-speed manual gearbox. The car also sports retro components based on the original CTR such as the 'whale tail' rear wing five-spoke wheels, interior and the steering wheel. Only 50 examples of the 2017 CTR will be produced.

Many literary works have been published about the Gulag by former prisoners. Three of the most famous such Russian writers are Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, Eugenia Ginzburg, and Varlam Shalamov. A Polish writer Gustaw Herling-Grudziński was liberated from a camp in 1941, left Soviet Union with Polish Armed Forces in the East and published his A World Apart in 1951. A number of ex-prisoners wrote memoirs describing their experiences in the camps, many of which have been translated into English.

On 14 January 2022, Zhegrova signed a four-year contract with Ligue 1 club Lille and received squad number 23. Lille reportedly paid a €7 million transfer fee. Eight days later, he was named as a Lille substitute for the first time in a league match against Brest. His debut with Lille came on 6 February in a 1–5 home defeat against Paris Saint-Germain after coming on as a substitute at 85th minute in place of Jonathan Bamba. One month after debut, Zhegrova scored his first goal for Lille in his fourth appearance for the club in a 4–0 home win over Clermont in Ligue 1.

In 1991, Germaine Greer published *The Change: Women, Ageing, and the Menopause*, an influential feminist work exploring myths and societal attitudes surrounding menopause, which she preferred to call the "climacteric." Reviewed by Natalie Angier in *The New York Times*, the book was described as a "brilliant, gutsy, exhilarating, exasperating fury of a book." Greer challenged the medicalization of menopause, debunking fears of aging related to bone health, libido, and depression, and criticized the promotion of hormone replacement therapy as a profit-driven industry. In a 2002 article for *The Age*, she argued that frightening women into compliance with such treatments was "big business and hugely profitable." Greer also examined the broader invisibility of aging women in society. The book, which drew on diverse historical and cultural sources, was updated and reissued in 2018, reaffirming its relevance in discussions about women and aging.

Funnel cakes are made by pouring batter into hot oil in a circular pattern, traditionally using a funnel to control the flow. At concession stands, spouted containers or batter dispensers are often used for efficiency. Alton Brown recommends using choux pastry, which creates a lighter, airier texture. The expansion during cooking is achieved by trapping steam within the gluten and egg network of the choux paste.

Lee K. Abbott devoted himself almost exclusively to short stories, though he occasionally attempted novels. In 2008, when asked why he never completed a novel, he explained feeling "lost and afraid" when writing longer than his custom. He claimed to know "two or three things" about story writing, emphasizing that the genius of a story lies in its ability to evoke emotion. According to Abbott, a story succeeds when it makes readers "feel something deeply."

Franklin's main interest was the relationship between a patient's environment and mental illness. She developed planned environmental therapy to treat this relationship. She theorised that focusing on the well-functioning parts of a patient’s personality could help them to behave better in a social environment. Planned environmental therapy is seen as a forerunner of the therapeutic community movement.

The ROC government immediately established the Taiwan Provincial Government under first Chief Executive and government-general Chen Yi in September 1945. Chen was extremely unpopular and his rule led to an uprising – the February 28 Incident of 1947. Chen was recalled in May 1947 and the government-general position was abolished.

New Eskişehir Stadium will be a multi-use stadium in the Sazova Park district of (West) Eskişehir, Turkey. The all-seated stadium will be totally covered and have a capacity of nearly 32,500 people. It was also one of the 9 candidate host stadiums of the Turkish bid for EURO 2016. Despite losing the election, the Chairman of TFF said "we have to work on these stadiums like a winner of the election; I'm going to talk about it with the Prime Minister".

Bhadra Fort housed royal palaces and the beautiful Nagina Baugh and the royal Ahmed Shah's Mosque on the west side and an open area known as Maidan-Shah on the east side. It had a fortified city wall with 14 towers, eight gates and two large openings covering an area of 43 acres. The eastern wall on the river bank can still be seen. The fort complex was used as a royal court during his reign. On the eastern side of a fort, there is a triple gateway known as Teen Darwaza which was formerly an entrance to the royal square, Maidan-Shah. The road beyond Teen Darwaza leads Manek Chowk, a mercantile square. On the south side along the road, there is a congregational mosque known as Jami Masjid.

William W. Arnold married Mary Evelina Shaw on July 14, 1863, in Waimea. They had two sons: Clarence, born around 1869, who became a physician, and John, born circa 1871, who died by 1913. Their granddaughter was Urana Arnold.

In May 2007, it was revealed that Warner Bros. was in development on a Teen Titans film in which Robin was the only confirmed member. Akiva Goldsman and Mark Verheiden were writing it. The current status of the film remains unknown because on September 11, 2014, it was announced that Akiva Goldsman was developing a Teen Titans TV series called Titans. Heroic Hollywood's El Mayimbe was a guest on the October 13, 2015 episode of Collider Heroes and he mentioned that Warner Bros. is developing both a Teen Titans movie with Cyborg, among others, and an all-female group of heroes.

Elective spinal injections should be performed under imaging guidance, such as fluoroscopy, computed tomography (CT), or ultrasound (US), to ensure accuracy and safety. Imaging guidance allows precise needle placement, documentation of contrast distribution, and correction of inadvertent punctures, reducing risks like intravascular or intradiscal injection. Without imaging, incorrect needle placement rates can reach up to 50% in caudal and 53% in cervical interlaminar injections, significantly compromising therapeutic efficacy. Blind injections rely on anatomical landmarks, such as the spinous process and iliac crest, and techniques like loss-of-resistance, but studies show these methods are less reliable, with misplacement rates as high as 20% in cervical transforaminal injections. Image guidance improves outcomes and minimizes complications.

Olavo de Carvalho, a self-proclaimed philosopher and prominent far-right figure, became one of the most influential individuals in Jair Bolsonaro's administration. Bolsonaro, interested in Carvalho's ideas since 2014, began live video chats with him on conservative YouTube channels between 2014 and 2016. Depicted as Bolsonaro's "ideologue" in 2018, Carvalho refuted this title in 2017.

Ptitim is made by extruding dough through a round mold, before it is cut and toasted, giving it the uniform natural-grain-like shape and its unique nutty flavor. Unlike common types of pasta and couscous, ptitim was factory-made from the outset, and therefore is rarely seen home-made from scratch. The store-bought product is easy and quick to prepare.

Father Dubois landed at Norfolk, Virginia in August, 1791, bearing commendatory letters from the Marquis de Lafayette (whose wife was devout) to James Monroe, Patrick Henry, and members of other distinguished families including the Lees, Randolphs and Beverleys. All received him cordially, even ardent supporters of revolutionary principles like Mr. Monroe, who served as his host until Father Dubois rented a house in Richmond near a major bridge and opened a school to teach French, classics and arithmetic. Virginia had disestablished the Episcopal Church by statute in 1786, and that law also guaranteed freedom of religion, releasing the Commonwealth's small Catholic population from civil restrictions. Patrick Henry helped the priest learn English, and the two priests who alternated holding religious services in the capitol, the Episcopalian John Buchanan and the Presbyterian John Blair, became his friends. At the General Assembly's invitation, Rev. Dubois even once celebrated Mass in the courtroom of the new State House, but for two years mostly celebrated mass in rented rooms or at the homes of the town's few Catholic families.

Regions that are or were seismic active and with the presence of fault zones might indicate there are ore deposits. A case study in Nevada, US by Howald (2015) studied how seismic-induced fluids accumulate mineral deposits, namely sinter and gold, along spaces provided by a fault zone. Two separate seismic events were identified and dated by oxygen isotopic concentrations, followed by episodes of the upward hydrothermal fluid migrations through permeable normal fault zone. Mineralization started to take place when these hot silica-rich hydrothermal fluids met the cool meteoric water infiltrated along the fault zone until the convective flow system was shut down. In order to deposit minerals, seismic events that bring hydrothermal fluids are not the only dominant factor, the permeability of the fault zone also has to be sufficient for permitting fluid flows.

Carvalho claimed that global warming is a hoax produced by a global conspiracy. He based his claims on the Climategate episode in which hackers, on the eve of the Copenhagen Conference, disseminated thousands of e-mails from University of East Anglia climatologists in order to undermine the credibility of the conference. Carvalho claimed Climategate to be the work of a conspiracy led by the Rockefeller family, the Council of Foreign Relations, the Bilderberg Club, and the New World Order, indicating them also as leaders of the "global abortion and gay ... campaigns of the new bionic global religion, and of the Obama administration's proposal for universal control of the movement of capital."

"Creature Feature" found the movie to be an example of sci-fi so bad it has to be seen to be believed, giving the movie 2 out of 5 stars. "Popular Mechanics" noted the movie was a dreadful trashpile. Techradar found the movie either insufferable or hilariously bad, depending on your mood.

The palate of *Askeptosaurus* is distinguished from thalattosauroids by its plesiomorphic structure, including elongated ectopterygoids. The vomer is narrow and positioned medially, while the pterygoids are robust and contribute to the palate's strength. The ectopterygoid is long and supports jaw musculature, and the palatine plays a key role in connecting the palate to the maxilla, aiding in feeding mechanics.

The genus *Jacksonia* was first formally described in 1811 by James Edward Smith, based on an unpublished manuscript by Robert Brown. The description was published in *The Cyclopaedia; or, Universal Dictionary of Arts, Sciences and Literature*. The genus was named in memory of George Jackson, a botanist who contributed to plant studies. George Jackson died at the age of 29 in August 1811. The publication did not specify additional species described alongside the genus *Jacksonia*.

The music video for "Beautiful Liar" was directed by Jake Nava, who directed the most of Beyoncé's other videos. It was filmed over two days, during the two-week production of B'Day Anthology Video Album. Due to a busy schedule, the production team did not have enough time for the choreography. The dance sequences were choreographed spontaneously and the routines were rehearsed in 40 minutes. Beyoncé learned some of the choreography from Shakira, who created most of them and taught Beyoncé some belly dancing moves. Beyoncé originated the idea of playing a lookalike after an experience when she saw a boy dancing, and thought he was performing before a mirror, but realized he was dancing with another person. The music video premiered on MTV's Total Request Live on February 28, 2007.

In visual search experiments, reaction time (RT) slopes are used to assess attention by measuring how RT increases with display size. The slope is steeper in conjunction searches, requiring high attention, and shallower in feature searches, where targets pop out due to salience. Shallower slopes in feature searches reflect reduced attentional demands. However, the use of RT slopes is debated, as factors like arousal, distractor complexity, and associative knowledge also influence performance. Faster RTs with fewer distractors suggest attention modulates efficiency, but cumulative preparation and selection stages may complicate interpretations of RT slope as a pure attention measure.

"Killing All the Right People" was written by Linda Bloodworth-Thomason, the creator of Designing Women, whose mother died after contracting AIDS from a blood transfusion. Although there was a screening test available that could have been used to identify unsafe blood, not all blood banks utilized it, allowing infected blood to be unknowingly spread through transfusions. During her mother's illness, Bloodworth-Thomason saw the prejudice people with AIDS experience, particularly the prejudice against gay men with AIDS. The episode's title is derived from a comment she overheard in the hospital: "The good thing about AIDS is that it's killing all the right people." She incorporated a version of the remark into the script.

In 2002, the Fields Institute initiated the Fields Institute Fellows program to recognize outstanding contributions to activities at the Fields Institute and within the Canadian mathematical community. The fellowship is a lifetime appointment. Winners of the CRM-Fields-PIMS prize are automatically recommended for fellowship.

On February 9, *Al-Wafd* reported the possibility of a deal between the prosecution and some accused of embezzlement, involving offers to repay stolen funds in exchange for dropping charges. A previous deal with creditors and business borrowers had resulted in the recovery of $4 billion and the dismissal of legal charges. *Al-Wafd* described the situation as complex, noting that the accused made substantial offers to resolve the matter, aiming to recover funds and mitigate the economic impact of the cases.

Archaeologist Denise Carpinteyro Espinosa, from National Institute of anthropology and history, Center INAH, reports that petroglyphs (engraved stone) in the site, some are related to astronomy while others are more closely related to everyday life of these hunter-gatherers human groups who inhabited this site 7 thousand years ago. The site is crossed by a river and it is inferred that this site was of great importance for diverse human occupations during over 7,000 years. Boca de Potrerillos, we very ancient settlements. The level of engravings offers a significant knowledge level of the skies and studies that have been made on the handling time for hunting which was related to the stars or for many everyday things of life.

Bounded roughly by Fort Street at the southeast end and Nuʻuanu Avenue at the northwest, its older, low-rise, brick and stone buildings, surrounded by contemporary, concrete high rises, serves as an open-air, human-scale architectural museum of the city's commercial development between the 1850s and the 1930s. Its architectural styles range from nondescript 19th-century commercial through Richardsonian Romanesque, Italianate, and Mission Revival. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1973. Directly to the north is Chinatown, another historic district.

On 24 October 1995, an anonymous man telephoned police saying he could identify the killer, but the call ended prematurely. The recording of the call was broadcast on national television in January 1996 in an attempt to trace the caller. Later, the team of detectives was reduced to four. In February, a friend of the Van den Hurk family arrested for drug trafficking claimed to have been forced to smuggle heroin by men involved in the killing. The police said this testimony was flawed and was of no help. Meanwhile, Passie magazine offered a reward for details about the killer. Between May and June, the victim's stepbrother and stepfather were arrested in connection with the killing, and then cleared.

Blanda was a quarterback and kicker at Kentucky from 1945 to 1948. Coach Paul "Bear" Bryant, who later won fame and set countless records at Southeastern Conference rival Alabama, arrived in his sophomore year, following a 1–9 season in 1945. The Wildcats only lost three games in each of the next three years.

Dorstenia contrajerva is native to tropical America, including Mexico, Central America, and northern South America. It has been introduced to Florida in North America, where it is considered a weed in greenhouses and nurseries. Rarely found in the wild in North America, it is sometimes cultivated as a houseplant for its unique foliage.

On 2 December 2017, the local iwi Te Kawerau ā Maki placed an unofficial rāhui (traditional prohibition) over the kauri forest in the Waitākere Ranges to slow the spread of kauri dieback, a disease threatening the survival of the iconic trees. The rāhui covered the entire forest, aiming to protect the ecosystem by restricting human access. However, it was frequently breached by the public, and the Waitākere Ranges remained the only large forested area near West Auckland still accessible. On 20 February 2018, Auckland Council announced the closure of all forested areas in the Waitākere Ranges, citing the ineffectiveness of the rāhui and the doubling of kauri dieback cases. High-risk tracks in the Hunua Ranges were also closed as a preventive measure, despite no recorded infections there.

Nathan Bennett and Stephen A. Miles have researched the role of COOs. Their published works analyse the role and its effectiveness, classify the different types of COOs, and examine relationships between CEOs and COOs.

When a parent employs a child under age 18 (or under age 21 for domestic service), payments to the child are exempt from FICA tax. The exemption also applies when a child is employed by a partnership in which each partner is a parent of the child. The exemption does not apply when the child is employed by a corporation or a partnership with partners who are not the child's parent.

Tom Thorpe started his second loan spell with the visit to fellow strugglers Doncaster Rovers, where two second-half goals by Macheda and one from Novak gave Birmingham a 3–1 win which took them into 18th place, above Doncaster on goal difference. In an open game at Middlesbrough, both sides had chances before the home team scored twice in two minutes, shortly afterwards Emyr Huws "simply looked up and arched a dipping thunderbolt under the bar from 35 yards", which won the club's Goal of the Season award, and despite the dismissal of two players in the last ten minutes, Middlesbrough scored a third. Novak suffered a knee injury in first-half stoppage time that looked to have ended his season. Former Birmingham loanee Matt Derbyshire's second-minute goal separated the sides at Nottingham Forest, though an "outstanding" save from Robinson's powerful header denied Birmingham what skysports.com suggested would have been an undeserved equaliser.

Window air conditioners and packaged terminal air conditioners (PTACs) are compact cooling solutions ideal for small spaces. PTACs, often installed in exterior-facing walls using a wall sleeve and custom grille, are common in hotels, dormitories, and apartments. These systems can provide heating by circulating hot water from a boiler or reversing refrigerant flow. PTACs are ductless, energy-efficient, and vented through a wall opening. Window air conditioners, on the other hand, are installed in standard or storm windows and are easy to move between rooms. Both systems are cost-effective and versatile, with PTACs offering additional customization options for aesthetic integration.

The parish of Litlington covers 2,171 acres (879 ha) in a thin north–south rectangular shape. Its southern border runs along the county border with Hertfordshire on the Icknield Way that now follows the A505. Its northern border with Abington Pigotts follows a stream, and its western and eastern boundary with Steeple Morden and Bassingbourn follow field boundaries.

Jeff Unaegbu was born in Enugu State, Nigeria, as the first of five surviving children to Mr. John Ejike Unaegbu and Mrs. Cecilia Enonanma Unaegbu. In 1990, he attended the Government Secondary School, Minna, where he studied sciences and graduated in 1996. Following this, he enrolled at Muhanatu Computer Institute, earning distinctions in Certificate and Diploma in Computer Data Processing in 2000. From 2002 to 2006, Unaegbu attended the University of Nigeria, Nsukka, where he obtained a degree in Mass Communication. During his time at the university, he served as a student leader and won several academic and creative prizes.

Alexander Lindsay, 2nd Lord Spynie, was the eldest son of Alexander Lindsay, 1st Lord Spynie, and his wife, Jean Lyon, daughter of Lord Glamis. Following his father’s murder in 1607, Alexander was still a minor. In 1609, during the trial of his father’s murderer, Lindsay of Edzell, a protest was made on behalf of Alexander and his siblings to preserve their right to prosecute in the future. After coming of age, Alexander agreed to waive prosecution, accepting Lindsay of Edzell’s sworn affirmation that the killing was accidental. As compensation, Edzell undertook to pay 8,000 merks and transfer the lands of Garlobank in Perthshire to Alexander and his sister. On 7 March 1617, Lindsay of Edzell obtained a remission for the murder under the great seal.

At the age of eighteen, while a student at the University of Waterloo, she discovered Medievalism through the Society for Creative Anachronism, and began composing songs inspired by Arthurian legend and other fantasy books she had grown up enjoying. Soon after, she began going to science fiction conventions in Toronto, and there found a welcoming community. She made her very first recordings in 1992, and released her first album The Trial of Lancelot in 2000. Trial included her most popular song, "Mordred's Lullaby", which went viral after release and counted over twenty million views on YouTube as of August 2020.

When the first COVID-19 pandemic lockdown began in Melbourne in March 2020, Vika and Linda Bull decided to sing one gospel song each Sunday morning for their followers on social media. The weekly performance became known as the "Sunday Sing Song". Vika said "It's kept our spirits up and we've had great feedback from people".
The Tres Amigas SuperStation, proposed in 2010, awarded Alstom Grid a $200 million contract in 2011 to supply a 750 MW, 345 kV DC converter scheme. Construction was scheduled to begin in 2012.

In 1976, the New Jersey Superior Court, Appellate Division, in *M.T. v. J.T.*, 140 N.J. Super. 77, affirmed that a post-operative transsexual woman, M.T., was legally recognized as female for marital purposes. This landmark case validated her marriage to J.T. and established that successful sex reassignment surgery suffices for legal recognition of gender in marriage, marking a significant precedent for transsexual rights in the United States.
Holly Earl, from London, attended BYU Training Elementary School. Her older sister, Elizabeth Earl, was a child actress and later founded a PR agency.

During the 1960s to the 1980s, the Pan Africanist Congress (PAC) and African National Congress (ANC) were highly active in neighboring countries, operating in exile due to their ban in apartheid South Africa. Sympathetic regimes or opposition parties in countries like Tanzania, Zambia, Angola, and Botswana accommodated these liberation movements, providing bases for their activities. The South African Security Branch maintained close working relationships with neighboring governments, particularly with Lesotho, where a joint security task force was established with the Basotho police and defense force after 1982, in collaboration with the South African Defence Force (SADF). In 1985, the SADF, with Security Branch involvement, conducted a cross-border raid on Gaborone, Botswana, targeting ANC operatives and killing twelve people. Several Security Branch officers later applied for amnesty from the Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC), citing their roles in identifying targets and supplying intelligence for such operations, as part of the broader effort to uncover apartheid-era violations.

In 2014, Bender signed a seven-year contract with Maccabi Tel Aviv, a team that played in the Israeli Basketball Premier League and the EuroLeague. He was soon loaned to Ironi Ramat Gan of the Liga Leumit, the second-tier-level league in Israel, for the 2014–15 season. He made his debut on 21 October 2014, posting 13 points, 13 rebounds, and 4 assists vs Ramat HaSharon. He recorded another double-double on 12 December, with 23 points and 12 rebounds in a win over Maccabi Ra'anana. Bender scored a season-high 25 points as his team defeated Barak Netanya by one point on 1 January 2015. After 28 games, he finished the season averaging 9.7 points, 7.4 rebounds, 2.6 assists, and 1.1 blocks. As the season came to a close, Bender returned to Maccabi Tel Aviv.

Pawlenty was elected to the Minnesota House of Representatives in 1992, winning 49.1% of the vote in District 38B (suburban Dakota County). In the House, he authored bills instituting term limits for committee chairmen, funding for infant parenting classes, minimum sentences for repeat domestic violence offenders, and community notification for sex offenders. In response to a state budget surplus, he advocated reducing taxes rather than increasing education funding. He was reelected four times and was chosen House Majority Leader when Republicans gained the majority in the State Legislature in 1998.

The 2012–13 season marked the second and final year of the Vancouver Canucks' affiliation with the Chicago Wolves as their American Hockey League (AHL) farm team. The Wolves began the season strong, winning their first two games, a feat achieved only five times in franchise history. However, they faced challenges later in the season, including injuries to key players. Eddie Lack, the Wolves' standout goaltender, suffered a hip injury that required surgery, sidelining him and impacting the team's defensive performance. Despite this setback, Lack and fellow goaltender Matt Climie had earlier combined to set a franchise record for the lowest goals-against average (2.54) in Wolves history. The Wolves finished the regular season atop the Midwest Division with 91 points, securing their 15th playoff appearance in 18 seasons.

On 30 June the highest number of daily COVID cases since February was recorded with 375 new cases. The figures prompted senior health officials to urge younger people to get vaccinated. On 1 July it was announced that the delta variant accounted for two thirds of COVID cases in Northern Ireland. On the same day the Executive announced further easing of restrictions due to come into effect on 5 July. Relaxations due to come into effect on 5 July include:

PMS5 is an extension of the Planted Motif Search (PMS) algorithm, designed to identify motifs in a set of strings, S, where S is a collection of sequences over a defined alphabet. PMS5 computes the d-neighborhood, B<sub>d</sub>(x, y, z), within S. The set S' represents all l-mers in S, and L refers to the length of the l-mers analyzed during the computation.

Sally Kader, an advocate for global peace and interfaith dialogue, has been recognized for her efforts in promoting understanding and cooperation among diverse communities. As President of the US Federation for Peace in the Middle East (USFMEP) and co-founder of the International Federation for Peace and Sustainable Development (IFPSD), she has sought to increase awareness of issues such as human rights, youth leadership, women’s empowerment, and cultural heritage. Much of her work has been dedicated to fostering interfaith dialogue, hosting high-profile events with leaders from Muslim, Christian, Jewish, Hindu, and other communities. She has organized International Peace Conferences in New York and celebrated the International Day of Peace annually worldwide. In 2006, she led an International Peace Walk in Dubai, promoting unity and mutual respect.

In Bogotá, the average weekday public transit commute takes 97 minutes, with 32% of riders spending over two hours daily in transit. Commuters typically wait an average of 20 minutes at stops or stations, though 40% experience waits exceeding this time. The average distance traveled in a single trip is 8 km, while 12% of riders journey more than 12 km in one direction. These statistics highlight the challenges faced by Bogotá’s public transit users, reflecting the city’s extensive traffic congestion and the reliance on systems like TransMilenio and SITP for daily commutes.

On February 4, 2014, Steve Ballmer stepped down as CEO of Microsoft and was succeeded by Satya Nadella, who previously led Microsoft's Cloud and Enterprise division. On the same day, John W. Thompson took on the role of chairman, in place of Bill Gates, who continued to participate as a technology advisor. Thompson became the second chairman in Microsoft's history. On April 25, 2014, Microsoft acquired Nokia Devices and Services for $7.2 billion. This new subsidiary was renamed Microsoft Mobile Oy. On September 15, 2014, Microsoft acquired the video game development company Mojang, best known for Minecraft, for $2.5 billion. On June 8, 2017, Microsoft acquired Hexadite, an Israeli security firm, for $100 million.

Stephen Colbert appeared as a correspondent on *The Daily Show* from 1997 to 2005, first under host Craig Kilborn and later Jon Stewart. During his tenure, Colbert developed a satirical persona of a self-important, oblivious journalist, which he described as a blend of Stone Phillips’ severe gravitas and Geraldo Rivera’s sense of mission-driven reporting. Colbert often cited Phillips as an inspiration, specifically for his dramatic head tilt when delivering tragic news, and Rivera for his "busting this thing wide open" investigative style. Colbert’s character became a fan favorite for segments like "This Week in God" and "Even Stephven," the latter featuring mock debates with fellow correspondent Steve Carell. His absurd yet earnest delivery often pitted him against other correspondents in scripted exchanges, showcasing his comedic timing and deadpan humor. Notably, when correspondents like Ed Helms and Rob Corddry joined the show in 2002, they reportedly imitated Colbert’s style, further cementing his influence on *The Daily Show*. Colbert’s character laid the foundation for his later work on *The Colbert Report*, where he expanded his satirical take on conservative media personalities.

In late 2017, Germaine Greer publicly commented on the Me Too movement, expressing skepticism about its potential for lasting change. In November 2017, she called for women to respond to sexual harassment immediately by "taking names, talking loud, and drawing a crowd," rather than waiting to disclose incidents years later. Ahead of being named Australian of the Year in January 2018, Greer criticized the movement for portraying women as powerless victims and argued that cases involving men with economic power, such as Harvey Weinstein, were different. She controversially stated that if women "spread their legs" in exchange for career opportunities, it was "tantamount to consent" and dismissed subsequent complaints as "whingeing." Greer also expressed disdain for the dishonor of disclosure in high-profile cases, suggesting that women who spoke out after signing non-disclosure agreements were motivated by financial settlements. While acknowledging the abuses of power highlighted by Me Too, she lamented its failure to address the exploitation of minority and blue-collar workers, which she believed was its original aim.

Margaret Louise Cuninggim was born in Nashville, Tennessee, the daughter of Jesse Lee Cuninggim, who served as president of Scarritt College for Christian Workers. She spent her childhood on the Scarritt College campus with her brother, Merrimon Cuninggim, who later became a Vanderbilt trustee and held a leadership position at Salem College. Margaret graduated from Duke University in 1936, earned a master’s degree from Columbia University in 1947, and completed a Doctorate of Education at Northwestern University in 1958, establishing a strong academic foundation that would shape her career in student affairs and education.

The operation commenced on 8 April. In its first week Allied troops killed 709 VC/PAVN, in the second week 892 VC/PAVN were killed and in the last week of April 792 VC/PAVN were killed. Most of these losses resulted from squad and company-size firefights or helicopter gunship, tactical air strikes or artillery fire missions.: 465–6

Speculations and imaginings about purgatory have long reflected the diverse and sometimes conflicting ideas of Catholic saints and theologians, contributing to its rich theological and cultural tapestry. Popular images of purgatory often include notions of fire and suffering, yet recent Catholic thought, as noted by Paul J. Griffiths, preserves the essentials of the doctrine while offering a transformative vision of purification. Joseph Ratzinger, later Pope Benedict XVI, rejected the idea of purgatory as a "supra-worldly concentration camp" and instead described it as a necessary process of inner transformation. This process, he explained, enables a person to become "capable of Christ, capable of God," and united with the communion of saints. Such interpretations underscore purgatory's role as a journey toward holiness and ultimate unity with the divine, rather than mere punishment.

Scottish poet Robert Burns became a "people’s poet" in Russia. In Imperial times the Russian aristocracy were so out of touch with the peasantry that Burns, translated into Russian, became a symbol for the ordinary Russian people. In Soviet Russia, Burns was elevated as the archetypical poet of the people – not least since the Soviet regime slaughtered and silenced its own poets. A new translation of Burns, begun in 1924 by Samuil Marshak, proved enormously popular selling over 600,000 copies. In 1956, the Soviet Union became the first country in the world to honour Burns with a commemorative stamp. The poetry of Burns is taught in Russian schools alongside their own national poets. Burns was a great admirer of the egalitarian ethos behind the French Revolution. Whether Burns would have recognised the same principles at work in the Soviet State at its most repressive is moot. This didn’t stop the Communists from claiming Burns as one of their own and incorporating his work into their state propaganda. The post-communist years of rampant capitalism in Russia have not tarnished Burns' reputation.

The Michigan Wolverines defeated the Commodores 9 to 8. The game matched Michigan head coach Fielding H. Yost against his former player and brother-in-law, Dan McGugin. Because of the relationship between Yost and McGugin, the two teams played nine times between 1905 and 1923, with Michigan winning eight games and tying one.

In early 2010, Kevin Cramer announced his third candidacy for North Dakota's At-Large seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, opposing the Affordable Care Act during town hall meetings. He spoke on energy, taxes, jobs, and the U.S. Constitution at Republican Party rallies across the state. Meanwhile, Rick Berg, a former North Dakota state legislator, secured the Republican congressional nomination at the party's March 2010 convention and was later elected to Congress in November 2010, defeating incumbent Earl Pomeroy in the general election.

Everglades University was originally called the American Flyers College when it was founded in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, in 1990. In 1998, Arthur and Belinda Keiser purchased American Flyers College then changed the name to Everglades College in 1999 after relocating to Fort Lauderdale. They were already the owners of Keiser University.

After World War II, the American Art Looting Investigation Unit (ALIU) investigated Nazi art plundering and the planned Führermuseum in Linz, producing three detailed reports synthesized into four consolidated reports. The fourth, written by S. Lane Faison, focused on the Führermuseum. The 1943 Inter-Allied Declaration ("London Declaration") invalidated all German art purchases in occupied territories. The Roberts Commission, under U.S. Military Occupation Government (OMGUS), facilitated the restitution of looted art to rightful owners based on these findings.

On the day of the celebration of the Meitei Language Day, the Patriotic Writers' Forum bestow three awards to three prominent writers (either from inside or outside of Manipur) for composing short stories based on patriotic themes for Meitei literature (Manipuri literature) every year since 2008. The awards are (1) Pacha Meetei Literary Award, (2) R Kathing Tangkhul Literary Award and (3) Dr Saroj Nalini Parratt Literary Award. The awards consist of cash incentive, citation, memento and shawl.

Prior to its collection it had been assumed by Thomson that the species was close to the other "island hens" known in the Atlantic, possibly a gallinule, but on examination Lowe felt "compelled to refer it to a new genus". The generic name Atlantisia was named for the mythical island of Atlantis, destroyed by a volcano. The specific name rogersi honours the Rev. Rogers, who collected and sent the first specimens of the species to Lowe.

Males measure 15–17 mm (0.59–0.67 in) and females, based on a single specimen only, 18 mm (0.71 in) in snout–vent length. Skin is smooth. The tympanum is hidden by skin. The colouration is overall pale brown with prominent black markings on the dorsum, hands, feet and flanks. There is a distinct black horizontal band, with a red leading edge, at level with the forelimbs. The vocal sac is deep purplish black when calling. The iris is golden yellow with brown mottling. The pupil is black. Ventral parts are creamy white except for the throat.

Dorstenia contrajerva is native to southern Mexico, Central America, the Caribbean and northern South America including Peru. It is cultivated in Indonesia (Java) and Malaysia (Malacca), and locally in Africa and South America. In North America Dorstenia contrajerva has been introduced to Florida and is a weed in greenhouses and nurseries. It is sometimes cultivated as a house plant.

Carnovsky was born in St. Louis, Missouri on September 5, 1897 to Ike and Jennie Carnovsky, both Russian Jewish immigrants. His father, a grocer, took him to performances of the Yiddish theater. In 1975 he recalled: "There was such richness in their portrayals of Jewish life. I could savor it. Once I smelled greasepaint, I was committed." He graduated from Washington University in 1920 and then moved to Boston where he acted in his first professional stage production.

The Mauritanian Ansar al-Sharia was established by Islamists jailed in the countries Dar Naim central prison on 11 February 2013. Ahmed Salem Ould al-Hasan, one of the group's founders, described its purpose as combating secularists, implementing Sharia, and reinstating the position of Islamic scholars within the nation. The group was said to have been influenced by the founding and activities of Ansar al-Sharia (Tunisia)

The land that became Belmont was used for tobacco production in colonial times and was primarily owned by the Lorillard family, French Huguenot immigrants who established the Lorillard Tobacco Company in 1760. The family moved their tobacco operations to the central Bronx in 1792, expanding their property into an estate known as "Belle Mont." After relocating to New Jersey in 1870, the Lorillards sold portions of their land, and the city acquired part of it for Bronx Park. The neighborhood and Lorillard Place are named after the family, whose contributions shaped the area’s development into what is now Belmont.

Liam Byrne, MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill and former Minister for the West Midlands, was selected as Labour's candidate for the 2021 West Midlands mayoral election in February 2020. Initially, several politicians expressed interest, including former Dudley Council leader Pete Lowe, ex-MEP Neena Gill, and former MP Lynda Waltho. Waltho and Gill later withdrew, citing personal reasons. Byrne defeated Lowe and Salma Yaqoob in the selection process, with Lowe, an "anti-austerity" candidate, finishing second.

After the war, Andrée Geulen became actively involved with the Jewish community, maintaining lifelong connections with the children she had saved. She worked with the Aid for Israelite Victims of the War (AIVG), helping reclaim hidden Jewish children, securing reparations, and aiding in their naturalization. She also fought against inequality, racism, and anti-Semitism. In 1948, she married Charles Herscovici, a Jewish man who had survived the war in Switzerland with his brother, though his parents, grandparents, and another brother were killed at Auschwitz. Her postwar activism reflected her enduring commitment to justice and humanitarian efforts.

Justice Stephen Breyer wrote an opinion that was joined by Justices O'Connor and Souter. Based on precedent set by Buckley v. Valeo, Breyer believed that some restrictions on expenditures by political parties were constitutional. Even so, he found that the Party Expenditure Provision of the FECA did not apply to an independent expenditure made by a political party without coordination with a candidate. This meant that the independent advertisement paid for by the Colorado Republican Party was not in violation of the Party Expenditure Provision, and was protected by the First Amendment.

Davies made his first Bundesliga start with Bayern on matchday nine of the 2019–20 Bundesliga season, going the full 90 minutes in a 2–1 home victory over Union Berlin. He made his first UEFA Champions League start on November 6, 2019, in a 2–0 victory against Olympiacos. Throughout the 2019–20 season, coach Niko Kovač moved Davies to the left back position, following long-term injuries to Niklas Süle and Lucas Hernandez, to allow David Alaba to play at centre back. Davies earned high praise during his time in that role, gaining an early reputation as one of the best left backs in the world. On February 25, 2020, Davies set-up Robert Lewandowski's goal in a 3–0 away win over Chelsea in the first leg of the round of 16 of the UEFA Champions League; his performance during the match was widely praised in the media, with pundit Gary Lineker commenting that Davies played "beautifully," while former U.S. international Stuart Holden described him as a "world class left back."

Since Hurricane Georges, Cuba has sent medical aid to Haiti in the form of doctors, education and medical supplies. Over 3,000 doctors have been sent to Haiti since 1998 and have educated 550 Haitians at Latin American Medical School in Havana with 567 Haitians currently studying at ELAM as of 2010. From 1998 to 2010, Cuba performed over 207,000 Surgeries, restored eye sight to 45,000, 14.6 Million patient-doctor consultations, taught 100,000 how to read and helped in the birth of 100,000 children. In the aftermath of the 2010 Haiti Earthquake, Cuba was among the first responders sending medical teams seeing hundreds of thousands of patients, and performing over 70,000 surgeries. There has been documented change in infant mortality and life expectancy in Haiti due to Cuba's medical aid.

Washing Machine received generally positive reviews from music critics. Stephen Thomas Erlewine of AllMusic opined that the album is "easily [the band's] most adventurous, challenging and best record since Daydream Nation ... Not only are the songs more immediate than most of the material on their earlier records, the sound here is warm and open, making Washing Machine their most mature and welcoming record to date ... Washing Machine encompasses everything that made Sonic Youth innovators, and shows that they can continue to grow, finding new paths inside their signature sound". Similarly, Peter Margasak of CMJ New Music Monthly described the album as a "powerful consolidation of the band's accomplishments, but a distillation that looks forwards". He also highlighted the song "The Diamond Sea" as the album's centerpiece, stating that it was one of Moore's "most ambitious excursions into pure sonic colors, textures, and tension".

The Malankara Association, formerly known as the Malankara Syria Christian Association, manages the religious and social concerns of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church. It was established in its modern form in 1876 during the Mulanthuruthy Synod, held in Mulanthuruthy, with elected members comprising priests and lay representatives from all parish churches.

The village inn in Charlton St Peter is known as the Charlton Cat. Originally called the Red Lion, it was later renamed the Poores Arms after Edward Poore, a notable local figure. By the early 20th century, it was locally referred to as the Charlton Cat, a name formally adopted after the inn was rebuilt following a fire in 1821.

The township (Stadt) of Wildberg is situated on the eastern edge of the district of Calw, in the German state of Baden-Württemberg, and lies along the border with the district of Böblingen to the east. Wildberg is physically located in the valley of the Enz, between the Northern Black Forest to the west and the Upper Gäu [de] to the east. Elevation above sea level in the municipal area ranges from a high of 638 meters (2,093 ft) Normalnull (NN) to a low of 356 meters (1,168 ft) NN.

Arboviruses, transmitted by mosquitoes such as Aedes aegypti and Aedes albopictus, have expanded into temperate regions, infecting populations with limited recent knowledge of these diseases. Effective prevention against Aedes aegypti requires integrated vector surveillance, early detection of cases, and personal protective measures to prevent bites. Community health education programs focus on teaching residents how to reduce mosquito breeding sites, prevent bites, and recognize symptoms of mosquito-borne diseases. These programs also identify and address resource gaps, such as the need for mobile clinics or outreach initiatives, and promote preventative measures tailored to community needs. By identifying strategies most likely to be adopted, these efforts enhance disease prevention. Public health workers, schools, and local organizations collaborate to educate communities on vector control and disease prevention, fostering active participation in reducing transmission risks.

Pōkeno takes its name from the Ngāti Tamaoho settlement of Pokino, which was located north of the present town centre, near the corner of today's Fraser and Avon Roads. The village was abandoned at the start of the Waikato War in 1863. In December 1861, Pokino Camp was established west of today's Helenslee Road as a base for troops constructing the Great South Road, a project ordered by Governor George Grey to connect Drury to the Waikato River for military operations. Queen's Redoubt, built in 1862, replaced Pokino Camp as the primary military base on the northern frontier of the Waikato. It housed 450 soldiers and served as the launch point for the British invasion of the Waikato. By March 1867, military forces had left, and the redoubt's buildings were sold at auction. Despite its closure, the site remained in occasional use by local militia until at least 1869.

Cooper Hummel attended Lakeridge High School in Lake Oswego, Oregon. He was a member of the Lake Oswego Little League team, which advanced to the 2007 Little League World Series, finishing third in the U.S. He played three seasons at the University of Portland.

According to the 2010 census, Hillsboro, West Virginia, had a population of 260, with 120 households and 70 families. The population density was 487.1 people per square mile, with 140 housing units at an average density of 262.3 per square mile. The racial makeup was 97.3% White, 0.4% Native American, and 2.3% from two or more races.

Operation Toan Thang I commenced on 8 April 1968. Allied troops killed 1,100 VC/PAVN in the first week, 1,400 in the second week, and 1,600 in the last week of April. The primary causes of VC/PAVN losses were airstrikes, artillery, and ground assaults targeting their positions in Military Region III.

El Salvador's estimated hydroelectric potential is 2,258 MW, with 1,409 MW located on the Lempa River, of which only 21% is utilized. The Comisión Ejecutiva Hidroeléctrica del Río Lempa (CEL), a state-owned entity, oversees hydroelectricity, generating 41% of the country's hydroelectric output. Four hydroelectric plants on the Lempa River—Guajayo, Cerrón Grande, 5 de Noviembre, and 15 de Septiembre—contribute significantly, accounting for approximately 24% of El Salvador's total electricity production.

The Ladies of the House received positive reviews from horror outlets, including Bloody Disgusting, which praised its "perfection" and commended John Wildman and Justina Walford's direction, noting their "heads and hearts in the right place." Bloody Disgusting described the film as a "confetti explosion" of retro influences and a highly enjoyable effort. Twitch Film highlighted the movie's success in crafting a grindhouse-style experience, blending elements of Russ Meyer, Jess Franco, and Takashi Miike into a polished, modern horror aesthetic, resulting in an instant cult classic.

Lilliput Kidswear, a children's apparel company, was founded in 2003 by Sanjeev Narula. In April 2010, Bain Capital Partners and TPG invested $86 million for a 45% stake, with Bain contributing $60 million for 31%. Shortly after Lilliput's IPO, allegations of accounting fraud emerged, leading Bain to write off its investment. Bain later sued Ernst & Young in a U.S. court, claiming the auditing firm’s advice caused a $60 million loss. Bain exited Lilliput following these events, with the company eventually facing bankruptcy and liquidation.

In 2011, he won gold medal in world cup held in Changwon, Korea. He also made a New National Record in the same event by again breaking his own record 3rd time. By winning this gold he became the third Indian to qualify for the London Olympics. He finished 8th place at the ISSF World Cup finals in Poland in his Pet event. He also won five gold and a silver medal in the 34th National Games Ranchi, Bihar. At the 2012 Summer Olympics Rajput could not make it to the finals of the men's 50-metre rifle three positions at the Royal Artillery Barracks.

Stephanie Hans served as the co-artist alongside Phil Jimenez for Marvel's limited series *Angela: Asgard's Assassin* #1-6 (2014), written by Kieron Gillen and Marguerite Bennett. Russ Burlingame, writing for ComicBook.com, noted that Hans was responsible for the backup stories in the series and praised her previous work on Asgard, highlighting her as a favorite of both writers. Jesse Schedeen of IGN further elaborated on Hans' role, describing her contributions to the interlude segments of the series and emphasizing her ethereal watercolor approach, which complemented the storybook quality of the narrative. In contrast, Schedeen observed that while Jimenez handled the main story with solid action sequences, his work lacked the otherworldly atmosphere that Hans' art captured. Petra Halbur, in her review for *The Mary Sue*, commended the artwork of both Jimenez and Hans, describing it as "breathtaking" and praising the character designs, dynamic action sequences, and effective use of color. Together, their artistic collaboration brought a visually striking and varied aesthetic to the series, with Hans' distinctive style standing out for its dreamlike and atmospheric qualities.

MGM Home Entertainment released *I Want to Live!* on DVD on May 7, 2002. Kino Lorber reissued it with a restored print in October 2015, followed by a Blu-ray release in November 2016. Twilight Time's limited edition Blu-ray included 3,000 units.

High Rocks is a 200-foot (60 m) sheer rock face, composed of smooth red Lockatong Formation argillite interspersed with friable Brunswick Formation shale. Since slippery and crumbly rock makes climbing somewhat difficult here, High Rocks is notable mostly to local climbers. Over sixty routes have been put up here over seventeen walls, with difficulties up to 5.12c/d and V7. Most are used for trad climbing and top roping, with some bouldering and sport routes present as well.

The single was sold digitally on iTunes, Recochoku, A! MUSIC and other music streaming sites. The digital jacket for it was drawn by Yuki Suetsugu. It was also included on the Cosmic Explorer album and was re-arranged. For the album's first limited edition, the original single edit and the instrumental came as a separate CD and the music video was included on the bonus Blu-ray/DVD.

Sharon Jarvis began riding at the age of five when her parents purchased her a pony, sparking her lifelong passion for equestrian sports. She competed in her first para-equestrian event in 2006 and is classified as a Grade 3 rider. Her international debut came at the 2007 FEI World Para Dressage Championships in Gloucestershire, England. At the 2008 Beijing Paralympics, she competed in the Mixed Dressage events, finishing 7th in the Individual Championship Test and 8th in the Freestyle Test. In 2010, Jarvis achieved significant success at the FEI World Equestrian Games in Lexington, Kentucky, winning two bronze medals in the Individual Grade 3 Championship Test and the Grade 3 Freestyle Test, solidifying her status as one of Australia’s top para-equestrian athletes.

Lyerla went undrafted in the 2014 NFL Draft. He was signed by the Green Bay Packers on May 19, 2014 as an undrafted free agent. On August 2, 2014 at the Packers annual Family Night practice Lyerla tore the posterior cruciate ligament and medial collateral ligament in his right knee when trying to hurdle a defender during a non-contact practice. Initially placed on the Packers injured reserve list, Lyerla was waived with an injury settlement on August 26, 2014 that paid him through Week 8 of the season.

Consequence of Sound critic Frank Mojica stated that "Lost in Translation is renowned for its soundtrack, and the music serves as the third star of the picture." On the film's possible legacy, Mojica also wrote: "One has to wonder how much of a role the film and its soundtrack had in the rebirth of shoegaze in the mid '00s. After all, My Bloody Valentine eventually reformed, as did The Jesus and Mary Chain, with the latter performing with none other than Scarlett Johansson at their Coachella reunion." The head of one record label, Sonic Cathedral, cited the soundtrack as an important factor that led to a resurgence of the genre. The soundtrack has been placed on several "best of" lists, including Rolling Stone's "The 25 Greatest Soundtracks of All Time" at number 22, Pitchfork's "The 50 Best Movie Soundtracks of All Time" at number 7, and "The 20 Soundtracks That Defined the 2000s" by Empire.

Andrew E. Masich, a Carnegie Mellon University alumnus, serves as an adjunct professor at the university, where he teaches the course "Public History: Learning Outside the Classroom." Recognized as an authority on public history and museum leadership, Masich has been a faculty member of the prestigious Seminar for Historical Administration and a member of the American Association for State and Local History’s Council. From 2004 to 2015, he served as chair of the Accreditation Commission for the American Alliance of Museums, reflecting his leadership in the field. Additionally, Masich is a member of the editorial board for *The Public Historian*, a leading journal in the discipline. Known for his engaging presentations, he frequently speaks on topics such as museum innovation, historical storytelling, and the importance of preserving cultural heritage. Through his teaching and public engagements, Masich continues to inspire students and professionals alike to explore history beyond traditional classroom settings.

An uncommon finding in Pompeii revealed a graffito referencing Ovid's *Heroides 4*, a poem in which the Greek character Phaedra confesses her forbidden love for her stepson, Hippolytus. This graffito was inscribed near a painting depicting a Romanized version of Pompeian myth. Similar to findings in the House of Maius Castricius, this discovery highlights the interplay between text and image in Roman graffiti. Archaeologists have used the *Heroides 4* graffito to suggest that Roman citizens possessed a refined ability to interpret art and literature, appreciating both the literary allusion and the visual narrative presented in the painting.

By 1923, Vancouver became the primary cultural, social, and religious centre of Punjabi Canadians as it had the largest ethnic Indian population of any city in North America. The Punjabi population in Canada would remain relatively stable throughout the mid 20th century as the exclusionary immigration policies practiced by the Canadian government continued. However, a shift began to occur after World War Two. The Canadian government re-enfranchised the Indo-Canadian community with the right to vote in 1947.

The soundtrack album for the film, released on October 7, 2014, features original music primarily from the genre of country music, recorded by artists such as Lady Antebellum, Hunter Hayes, David Nail, Colbie Caillat, Kip Moore, Eli Young Band, Eric Paslay, Thompson Square, and Thomas Rhett. "I Did with You" by Lady Antebellum was released on September 8, 2014 as the first promotional single from the soundtrack. The band's other contribution, "Falling for You" is also available on the deluxe edition of their fifth studio album, 747.

Muriel Darton, one of the first British women allowed to vote following the 1918 Representation of the People Act, first appeared in the Electoral Register in 1919 while living in Crouch End, London. She co-ran a photographic materials business there. Darton died on 3 January 1945 in Sherborne, Dorset, aged 71. According to probate records, she left her effects, valued at £1,181, to her business partner, Frederick Hamilton Rose.

Luca Pacioli, also known as Friar Luca or Luca di Borgo, was born between 1445 and 1448 in Sansepolcro, a town in Tuscany, Italy. The son of Bartolomeo Pacioli, he was raised by the Befolci family in his hometown. Pacioli received an abbaco education, which focused on practical knowledge for merchants and was taught in the local Italian language rather than Latin. Around 1464, he moved to Venice, where he worked as a tutor in the household of the merchant Antonio Rompiasi while continuing to expand his knowledge of business and mathematics. During this time, he wrote his first arithmetic treatise. Pacioli became a Franciscan friar between 1472 and 1475, further shaping his intellectual and spiritual pursuits.

JoBeth Williams made her mark in motion pictures with a series of notable roles throughout the 1980s. In 1980, she starred alongside Gene Wilder and Richard Pryor in the hit comedy *Stir Crazy* and appeared with Christopher Walken and Tom Berenger in the action-drama *The Dogs of War*. Williams gained widespread acclaim for her portrayal of Diane Freeling, a mother battling supernatural forces, in Steven Spielberg’s *Poltergeist* (1982), a role she reprised in the 1986 sequel *Poltergeist II: The Other Side*. She joined the ensemble cast of the acclaimed comedy-drama *The Big Chill* (1983) and won the Kansas City Film Critics Circle Award for Best Actress for her performance in *American Dreamer* (1984). In *Teachers* (1984), she starred opposite Nick Nolte, and in 1986, she appeared alongside Jon Voight in *Desert Bloom*, further cementing her reputation as a versatile and talented actress.

In the first round of the 2004 European Open, Steve Davis faced Joe Swail in a closely contested match, ultimately winning 5–4. Davis clinched victory on the pink in the deciding frame after Swail missed the brown while attempting to clear the colors. Reflecting on the nerve-wracking encounter, Davis remarked, "That's what first-round matches in ranking tournaments are like." Meanwhile, Stephen Lee advanced to the next round via a walkover after his opponent, Sandro Eric Sosing, withdrew on medical grounds. In another notable match, Stephen Maguire caused an upset by defeating former world champion Peter Ebdon 5–4, sealing the win with a decisive 98 break in the final frame. Ronnie O'Sullivan also progressed with a commanding 5–1 victory over Marco Fu, opening with a break of 110. Chris Small, who suffers from a spinal disease, delivered an impressive 5–0 win against Matthew Stevens, despite admitting he had been in significant pain leading up to the tournament.

During the first year of the German occupation of Belgium in World War II, the administration pursued a relatively conciliatory policy aimed at maintaining order and minimizing resistance. Demands on civilians and businesses were initially limited to avoid unrest, and resistance activity remained minimal. However, violent incidents occurred, including the Vinkt Massacre, where 86 Belgian civilians were executed by German forces in May 1940. This atrocity highlighted the brutality of the occupation despite the Germans' initial efforts to pacify the population.

In the 1993 NCAA Division I women's basketball tournament, 31 conferences earned automatic bids, with 22 sending only their automatic qualifier. Two conferences sent a single at-large team, while 33 additional at-large teams came from 13 conferences.

In February 2016, Kanza Riaz of *The Express Tribune* wrote a blog heavily criticizing the Pakistani drama *Mann Mayal*, claiming that it was "teaching our society some horrendously wrong things." Riaz expressed disappointment with the plot, particularly the love story between the young Manahil (Mannu) and Salahuddin, which she argued had a negative influence on Pakistan's youth and perpetuated harmful norms for women. She criticized the portrayal of Mannu as a weak female character who, according to Riaz, resorted to "every cheap trick in the book" to attract Salahuddin, ultimately reinforcing regressive stereotypes. Riaz also discouraged the introduction episodes, stating that just three episodes in, she was "heavily disappointed." Furthermore, she commented on the drama's immense popularity, noting that its television ratings had "gone through the roof" and lamenting that young women were not only ardently watching the show but also being inspired by its problematic messages, such as normalizing inappropriate romantic dynamics. The controversy surrounding the drama's themes and portrayal of women was later addressed by director Haseeb Hassan in an interview with HIP, where he sought to clarify the intentions behind the series. Despite the criticism, *Mann Mayal* remained one of the highest-rated drama serials in Pakistan in 2016.

To share and discuss the work of its researchers and visitors, the RCC hosts a series of public colloquia, conferences, and workshops. The center’s outreach program also includes the development of exhibitions in collaboration with the Deutsches Museum. The Environment and Society Portal aims to make digitized environmental humanities sources and interpretive exhibitions more accessible to academics and the public at large. The Rachel Carson Center offers the doctoral program “Environment and Society” through LMU Munich.

During World War II, SS *Clarksdale Victory* served as a supply ship, supporting Allied forces at the Battle of Okinawa from April 26 to May 8, 1945. On April 27, a shell landed 45 feet (14 m) from the ship, scattering fragments across the deck and damaging a lifeboat. On the same day, the SS *Canada Victory* was struck by a kamikaze plane, causing an explosion in the number 5 hold that sank the ship in seven minutes, killing two Armed Guards and wounding twelve. The *Clarksdale Victory* continued its mission, delivering vital supplies despite the dangers of the Pacific theater.

Since much of the land was unfit for development, it did not attract many settlers. However, the land was very rich in timber, which meant that timber companies would pay much more than $2.50 an acre. To circumvent the requirements of the land grant, Edward Harriman, president of the Southern Pacific Railroad — which then owned the O&C — hired former surveyor Stephen A. Douglas Puter to round up people from saloons in Portland's waterfront district, escort them to the land office, have them register for an O&C parcel as a settler, and then transfer it to Puter's men. The accumulated parcels were then sold in large blocks to the highest bidder for timber harvest.

During the 1980s and 1990s, despite health issues, Margaret S. Collins increased her fieldwork, undertaking expeditions to the Caribbean. She passed away from congestive heart failure on April 27, 1996, at the age of 73, at the Southern Cross Club on Little Cayman, Cayman Islands, during one such expedition.

LTC William S. Key, newly discharged from the US Army following World War I was appointed Captain of Field Artillery in the Oklahoma Army National Guard. He was directed to organize a light artillery battery at Wewoka, Oklahoma. A 75mm battery was federally recognized as Battery A, 1st Oklahoma Field Artillery on July 28, 1920. Battery "A" became known as the "White Horse Battery" in recognition of the white horses used to tow the artillery pieces. On 18 July 1921, the 1st Oklahoma Field Artillery Regiment, consisting of a Regimental Headquarters and two firing battalions, incorporating the White Horse Battery and other subsequently established artillery units, was federally recognized.

The station began operations on January 15, 2008 as independent station KUQI; it assumed the Fox affiliation for the market on February 4, 2008, the day after Super Bowl XLII, replacing K47DF as the area's affiliate. KUQI began broadcasting Fox programming in HD by 2011. The original owner was High Maintenance Broadcasting, a company run by Deidre Gillis, Lori Hoffmann and Vanisha Mallory.

The modern theory of polarization, pioneered in the early 1990s and based on the Berry phase, defines polarization per unit cell in a solid as the dipole moment per unit volume. Wannier functions have recently been applied to describe polarization in crystals, offering a localized orbital perspective. This theory has been advanced by researchers like Resta and Vanderbilt.

Shadows of Amn was re-released, along with its expansion, Throne of Bhaal, as Baldur's Gate II: The Collection in 2003. In 2004, they were bundled with the original Baldur's Gate and Icewind Dale as Black Isle Compilation Part Two. In 2006, they were re-released with Baldur's Gate and Tales of the Sword Coast as Baldur's Gate: 4 in 1 Boxset. They were also included in The Forgotten Realms Deluxe Edition, Ultimate Dungeons & Dragons, and Dungeons & Dragons Anthology: The Master Collection. In 2010, Baldur's Gate II Complete was released in digital format on GOG.com. This version includes both Shadows of Amn and Throne of Bhaal. Bundled with it are the game manuals in PDF format, high-definition wallpapers, artwork, avatars, and the Shadows of Amn and Throne of Bhaal soundtracks.

Wetzel attended Oregon State University from 2007 to 2010. She ended her collegiate career with 81 caps for the Beavers and seven goals. She also appeared in two games for the women's basketball team.

On 30 June 2021, Northern Ireland recorded 375 new COVID-19 cases, the highest daily figure since February. Senior health officials urged younger people to get vaccinated amid rising cases. By 1 July, the Delta variant accounted for two-thirds of cases. The Northern Ireland Executive announced further easing of restrictions, including lifting outdoor gathering caps, effective from 2 July. Additional relaxations, such as increased household mixing, were scheduled to take effect on 5 July.

Little Hope is the second installment in the Dark Pictures Anthology series. Unlike Man of Medan, which was a modern-day story, Little Hope's story covers multiple timelines, with the narrative jumping back and forth between the present and the past. The game features more supernatural elements when compared to its predecessor and the team chose witchcraft as the game's main theme as they wanted to explore its root causes. Pete Samuels, the game's director, added that the team was intrigued by the "greed, paranoia, and fear of God" which motivated people to commit heinous acts during that period. The team took inspirations from both the Silent Hill series and The Crucible, which is a play about the Salem witch trials that took place in the Massachusetts Bay Colony during 1692–93. The game was also inspired by films including The Witch, The Blair Witch Project, Hellraiser, It Follows, The Omen, and Season of the Witch. Will Poulter was hired to voice and provide motion capture for Andrew, the game's lead character.

Iowa arrived in Lincoln fresh off of an upset 20–19 victory over Minnesota, the very team that scored more on Nebraska than any other team in Cornhusker history just seven weeks prior. It looked like it might be a long day for Nebraska when Iowa received the opening kickoff and returned it for a touchdown. On Nebraska's next possession, however, the game was tied up soon enough at 6–6. The squads held each other off until after the half when Nebraska was stalled and readied for a field goal attempt. The kick was no good, but a Hawkeye offside penalty on the attempt handed Nebraska a first down, which the Cornhuskers ultimately converted into a touchdown, and Iowa never recovered from the shift in fortunes. Iowa's win streak against Nebraska was ended at three, and the Cornhuskers improved to 21–10–3 in the series.

During the late 1990s Mankins led the NASA team that wrote "A Fresh Look at Space Solar Power". The report of that work was published by the IAF. He testified about Space Solar Power to the U.S. House of Representatives Science Committee in 2000.

Vânia Fernandes participated in technical rehearsals on 19 and 22 May 2008 and dress rehearsals on 23 May. For the performance, she wore a black dress, while her five backing vocalists—Zita, Paulo, Graciela, Gonçalo, and Susana—wore white. The dress was designed by a Portuguese designer, and the choreography for the backing vocalists was created by Jackie. The stage lighting featured blue waves projected on the screens, complementing the maritime theme of "Senhora do Mar (Negras Águas)."

Carvalho became one of the most influential individuals in the administration of Jair Bolsonaro. According to one account, Bolsonaro got interested in Carvalho's ideas in 2013. In 2014, Bolsonaro and Carvalho started transmitting their live video chats through politically conservative YouTube channels. In 2017, Carvalho was depicted as the "ideologue" of Bolsonaro, a title he has refuted.

The anime film *Given* was originally scheduled to release in Japanese theaters on May 16, 2020, but was postponed to August 22, 2020, due to COVID-19 concerns. Crunchyroll acquired international distribution rights and streamed the film on its website, while WeTV released it in Southeast Asia later in 2020.

The Mauritanian Ansar al-Sharia was established on February 11, 2013, by Islamists jailed in Dar Naim central prison. Ahmed Salem Ould al-Hassan, one of its founders, stated its purpose was to combat secularism, implement Sharia, and restore the role of Islamic scholars. The group was influenced by the founding and activities of Ansar al-Sharia in Tunisia.

UnHerd was founded in 2017 by Tim Montgomerie, who served as its initial editor. Montgomerie left the site in September 2018, and Sally Chatterton, previously a writer for The Daily Telegraph and The Independent, succeeded him as editor. Freddie Sayers joined UnHerd later, becoming Editor-in-Chief and CEO after serving as Editor-in-Chief of YouGov. As of October 2022, the UnHerd website listed seven staff members.

During the 2020–21 season, Mahmoud Dahoud scored his first Champions League goal with a long-range strike against Sevilla in a 3-2 victory, ending a 1,441-minute drought in the competition. He also started in the DFB-Pokal final, assisting Jadon Sancho's opening goal as Borussia Dortmund defeated RB Leipzig 4-1, securing Dahoud's first major career honor.

As college athletics grew, the scope of the nation's athletics programs diverged, forcing the NCAA to create a structure that recognized varying levels of emphasis. In 1973, the association's membership was divided into three legislative and competitive divisions – I, II, and III. Five years later in 1978, Division I members voted to create subdivisions I-A and I-AA (renamed the Football Bowl Subdivision and the Football Championship Subdivision in 2006) in football.

Goodman was born in April 1881 in Łowicz, Poland. He had a traditional Jewish education and even considered studying to become a Rabbi before turning from religion to secular politics and becoming a freethinker.
