His parents both died in the 1881–1896 cholera pandemic when he was eight years old, leaving him an orphan. He became a weaver in Łódź, an important industrial centre in Poland which had a large Jewish silk-weaving industry.

The golden parakeet is listed on the IUCN Red List as vulnerable. This is largely due to deforestation and the capture of wild birds for aviculture, where it is in high demand due to the attractiveness of its plumage. Locally, they are considered as pests for feeding on crops, and are hunted for food or sport. The current population is estimated to be in the range of 10,000 to 20,000.

Trichuris muris infection in mice disrupts epithelial homeostasis and induces inflammation, while heavy Platynosomum fastosum infection in cats causes biliary hyperplasia and fibrosis. Cryptosporidium parvum infection damages intestinal microvilli, impairing gut function.

Celtic's traditional rivals are Rangers; collectively, the two clubs are known as the Old Firm and seen by some as the world's biggest football derby. The two have dominated Scottish football's history; between them, they have won the Scottish league championship 107 times (as of May 2022) since its inception in 1890 – all other clubs combined have won 19 championships. The two clubs are also by far the most supported in Scotland, with Celtic having the sixth highest home attendance in the UK during season 2014–15. Celtic have a historic association with the people of Ireland and Scots of Irish descent, both of whom are mainly Roman Catholic. Traditionally fans of rivals Rangers came from Scottish or Northern Irish Protestant backgrounds and support Unionism in Ireland.

During the dictatorship of Miguel Primo de Rivera, it was proposed that the aviation companies in Spain should be combined and become state-controlled as a general interest public utility. This came into effect in early 1928. As a consequence, Iberia was merged into Compañía de Líneas Aéreas Subvencionadas S.A. (C.L.A.S.S.A.) together with the following other airlines Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.), Compañía Española de Tráfico Aéreo (C.E.T.A.) the pilot training company Compañía Española de Aviación (C.E.A.) and the airship line Transaérea Colón.

PMS5 is an extension of PMS0. If S = {s1, s2, ..., sn} is a set of strings (not necessarily of the same length), let denote the (l, d)-motifs present in S. Let S′ = {s2, s3, ..., sn}. PMS5 computes the (l, d)-motifs of S as . Here L refers to an l-mer.

Charles Fraser, Superintendent 1821–31, was the first botanist appointed to develop the gardens along scientific lines. Fraser made many inland excursions particularly with John Oxley and brought back plants and specimens. Fraser's plantings between 1827–8 from his Brisbane district and northern NSW travels survive, and include hoop pines (Araucaria cunninghamii), weeping lilly pillies (Waterhousia floribunda), a hoop (or Moreton Bay) pine (Araucaria cunninghamii), 2 swamp oaks (Casuarina cunninghamiana) on the eastern side of the palm grove. On his death in 1831 he was succeeded by Richard Cunningham. His brother, the explorer Allan Cunningham was also a director. A native red cedar Fraser collected in 1822 formerly thought to have been from the Parramatta region has been genetically tested and found to have been collected in the Dorrigo region. This tree grows still near the Palm House (beside Farm Cove Creek in Bed 9).

The Australian colony of Victoria received six 35 cwt guns in August 1865. They were used as mobile coast fortification guns with one gun being fitted to the colonial sloop Victoria during 1866 & 1867. Later four of the guns were used as field guns at Hastings. Three of these guns are known to survive.

Sikhism, a religion founded in the mid-fifteenth century by Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469–1539), emphasizes living in accordance with the teachings of the Sikh Gurus, meditating on God, and serving others. Snatam Kaur, deeply influenced by Sikhism, was taught by Yogi Bhajan (1929–2004), who played a pivotal role in promoting the Sikh tradition and Kundalini Yoga in the West. Her music reflects Sikh values of devotion, equality, and spirituality, drawing from the teachings of Guru Nanak and the essence of Sikh practice.

Prepare a container. Skin and wash the garlic. Put garlic and vinegar into the container and seal it. Sugar and salt may be added according to personal preference. Place it in a low-temperature environment. Storage time is not fixed, usually about 20 days, until the color is green.

Warspite joined the Eastern Fleet as flagship of Admiral Sir James Somerville, who had commanded her in 1927. Initially, Warspite was based in Ceylon, forming a fast group with the aircraft carriers Formidable and Indomitable, four cruisers and six destroyers. In March, Somerville received intelligence indicating the Japanese Fast Carrier Strike Force was heading towards the Indian Ocean and he relocated his base to Addu Atoll in the Maldives. Vice Admiral Chuichi Nagumo used five carriers and four battleships in the Indian Ocean raid a naval sortie into the Indian Ocean in April, attacking Allied shipping and bases in the Indian Ocean and the Bay of Bengal. Somerville's fleet was outnumbered and outclassed but he hoped to get close enough to launch a night-time torpedo bomber attack. Warspite's fast group set sail to intercept on 4 April, detecting the Japanese attack on the cruisers Cornwall and Dorsetshire and later, a scouting aircraft from the cruiser Tone. The fleets did not meet; Warspite withdrew to Addu Atoll and then to Kilindini on the East African coast to protect the convoy routes. The Japanese believed that she was still in Sydney and ordered the Attack on Sydney Harbour.

Witold Bełza's diary, donated to the University of Bydgoszcz by his son Władysław Bełza, was originally purchased by Witold's father in 1888. The diary contains poems, essays, and mementoes from renowned scholars, including lines by Adam Mickiewicz and a photo of Juliusz Słowacki taken in Oxford in 1909. It also features a sketch by Cyprian Norwid and fragments of music scores by composers Fryderyk Chopin and Stanisław Moniuszko. This unique collection reflects Bełza's deep connection to Polish culture and history, preserving a rich legacy for future generations.
Earl is from Ealing, West London. She attended Drayton Manor High School. Her older sister Elizabeth Earl is a former child actress and Disney PR expert and founded the PR agency Munch.

Born in Berlin, Clotilde was the daughter of Major Hans Edler von der Planitz (1863–1932) from Berlin and Margarete von Muschwitz (1868–1955). She was a member of the German lower nobility. On 25 January 1919, she married Alexander Sakharoff, a Russian dancer, teacher and choreographer.

Peter I of Alexandria was born and raised in Alexandria, Egypt. According to the Coptic Orthodox Church, he was given by his devout Christian parents to Theonas, the then Patriarch of Alexandria, to be brought up as a priest. Peter progressed through the ranks of holy orders and eventually succeeded Theonas, becoming the 17th Patriarch of Alexandria in 300 AD.

After a brief hiatus, the 1999 album Mezzamorphis attained number 25 in the UK mainstream album charts (number two in the independent section), and soon became certified silver in the UK. During this time, the band found resistance in the mainstream due to their "Christian" label, despite widespread critical acclaim in the music press. However, the work was aimed at a "secular" audience, causing many former fans to shun the album and claiming that Delirious? had "sold out" to mainstream markets.

The Ural Mountains extend approximately 2,500 km (1,550 mi) from the Kara Sea in the Arctic Ocean to the Kazakh Steppe, forming a natural boundary between Europe and Asia. To the north, the range continues with Vaygach Island and the islands of Novaya Zemlya. The highest peak is Mount Narodnaya, standing at 1,895 m (6,217 ft). Geographically, the Urals are divided into seven major units by transverse faults: Pay-Khoy, Zapolyarnyy, Pripolyarnyy, Polyarnyy, Severnyy, Sredniy, Yuzhnyy Ural, and Mugodzhary. The range has an average altitude of 1,000–1,300 m (3,300–4,300 ft), with elevations generally decreasing toward the south.

Swamp milkweed (Asclepias incarnata), commonly known as swamp milkweed, is an upright, clump-forming perennial that grows 100 to 150 cm tall. It emerges in late spring from knotty roots surrounded by rootlets and weakly creeping roots. The stems are slender and upright, with lance-shaped leaves arranged in pairs. The leaves are 3 to 6 inches (7.5 to 15 cm) long, narrow, and smooth-edged, with tapering, sharp tips. Occasionally, the leaf edges curve inward and upward, resembling the prow of a ship.

Maguire began a European concert tour in October 2010 supporting Plan B and Hurts. She has also written a song with fellow Birmingham rap artist Mike Skinner titled "Lock the Locks" which features on his last Streets album, Computers and Blues. Maguire also features on the track "Midnight Caller" which appears on Drum and Bass-duo Chase & Status' second studio album, No More Idols.

Buccleuch was Governor of the Royal Bank of Scotland from 1777 to 1812. He was joint founder of the Royal Society of Edinburgh and its first President, serving from 1783 until his death in 1812. He was Lord Lieutenant of Midlothian and of Haddington from 1794 to 1812. He was also appointed a deputy lieutenant of Northamptonshire on 9 May 1803.

Storm was founded in 1987 by Sarah Doukas in London. Doukas discovered Kate Moss, Cara Delevingne, Behati Prinsloo, and Anya Taylor-Joy. In 2009, Doukas sold a controlling stake in the company to 19 Entertainment. Doukas and her brother Simon Chambers continue to run the business. Their sister was the actress Emma Chambers.

"Can You Dig It? The Music and Politics of Black Action Films 1968-1975," a compilation album released by Soul Jazz Records on September 29, 2009, explores the vibrant soundtracks of Black action films from the 1970s. The album was issued as a double-CD collection and two separate double vinyl LPs. Jim Carroll, writing for *WIRE Magazine*, awarded the album four out of five stars, praising its meticulous curation and historical depth. Pitchfork included the album in its list of essential compilations, with Patrin describing it as a "wide-ranging collection" that tells a "thorough story" through its tracklist. Carroll highlighted the album's ability to capture the intersection of music and politics, celebrating its role in documenting a pivotal cultural moment.

PC Music's sound has been described by Clive Martin, writing for *Vice*, as "a playful composite of disregarded sounds and genres," blending influences from bubblegum dance, Balearic trance, wonky, and electro house. Lanre Bakare, writing for *The Guardian*, identified its elements as including "the huge synth blasts favoured by Eurodance chart-botherers such as Cascada, grime's sub-bass, and happy hardcore's high-pitched vocal range." A. G. Cook, the label's founder, draws inspiration from Korean and Japanese pop, gyaru culture, and the production techniques of Max Martin, Jimmy Jam, and Terry Lewis. His work incorporates techniques akin to black MIDI music, layering dense, intricate arrangements to create a maximalist sound. Cook also uses pitched-up, feminine vocals and bright synthetic textures to craft multiple perspectives of a personality, a method indirectly inspired by American musician Andy Kaufman, as referenced in the *PC Music Pop Cube Trailer 1*. This eclectic approach redefines pop music by embracing consumerist aesthetics and subverting traditional genre boundaries.

Parker represented Papua New Guinea in 2007 when they played matches against France and Wales in Europe. In 2008, he was part of PNG's squad for the 2008 World Cup. He scored a try for PNG while competing at the 2009 Pacific Cup. Parker played for PNG at the 2010 Four Nations. He was a member of PNG's 2013 World Cup squad and scored a try against Samoa.

Vânia Fernandes took part in technical rehearsals on 14 and 18 May, followed by dress rehearsals on 21 and 22 May. The Portuguese performance featured Vânia Fernandes wearing a black dress with embroidered elements, designed by Portuguese designer Cátia Castel-Branco. Five backing vocalists were lined up behind Fernandes, dressed in white and performing a routine choreographed by Paulo Jesus: Evelyne Filipe, Jéssica Pereira, Joana Dias, Jonas Lopes and Luís Sousa. The stage lighting displayed blue colours and the LED screens displayed blue waves.

K. A. Mathiazhagan was one of the founding leaders of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), which split from the Dravidar Kazhagam (DK) under the leadership of C. N. Annadurai in 1949. Along with Annadurai, Kudanthai K. K. Neelamegam, R. Nedunchezhiyan, and N. V. Natarajan, he was part of the "Aimberum Thalaivargal" (Great Five Leaders). The DMK gained prominence during the 1950s with the mummunai struggle. Mathiazhagan played a key role in the party's growth and was elected to the Tamil Nadu legislative assembly as a DMK candidate from the Coonoor constituency in 1967 and 1971, representing the party's commitment to social justice and reform.

In the Commonwealth realms, including Tuvalu, the sovereign is regarded as the symbolic source of justice, with criminal offences legally deemed violations against the Crown. Proceedings for indictable offences in Tuvalu are brought in the name of the King, referred to as *Rex* in Latin. Under common law, the sovereign is traditionally deemed incapable of wrongdoing ("the King can do no wrong") and is protected by sovereign immunity, preventing prosecution in their own courts. This reflects the monarch's role as a unifying figure and the embodiment of justice within the Commonwealth realms.

On July 30, 2011, Drew Pomeranz, Alex White, Joe Gardner, and Matt McBride were traded to the Colorado Rockies for Ubaldo Jimenez. Pomeranz made his MLB debut on September 11, 2011, pitching five scoreless innings against the Cincinnati Reds. In September 2011, he made four starts, finishing with a 2-1 record and a 5.40 ERA.

FitzRoy married Lady Dorothy Boyle, the daughter of Richard Boyle, 3rd Earl of Burlington and 4th Earl of Cork and Dorothy Savile, Countess of Burlington and Countess of Cork,: 91 in October 1741. In the biography written in the History of the Parliament, Lady Boyle was described as "a girl of the softest temper, vast beauty, birth, and fortune." After she died on 2 May 1742, her mother said that Dorothy was relieved of the "extremest misery", that Lady Boyle's husband exacted on her as what is described as "utmost brutality".

On 11 May, Huseyngulu Khan was captured and imprisoned by Russian forces. The following day, Russian troops entered Baku. After the assassination of Agha Muhammad, Huseyngulu fled to the Qajar state. Upon hearing this, Ibrahim Khalil Khan attempted to seize Baku. Following the new treaty, Huseyngulu took a position in Mashtaga and sent gifts to Fatali Shah, fearing another Russian invasion.

Davis was born in Sacramento, California and raised in Davis, California. The middle of four children, he has two twin sisters and an older brother. Davis was first exposed to athletics at the early age of seven. Davis's mother entered his brother into a nationwide track meet, the Hershey Invitational, which was recruiting the best athletes from each state to compete. Davis accompanied his mother to watch the race, when she told him moments before the competition that she had entered him as well. From that first race forward, he competed and won every local track event, even qualifying for the state nationals.

The romantic relationship between Mulan and Li Shang has received mixed critical reception, with some reviewers criticizing its implications. Critics accused *Mulan* of reinforcing heteronormative tropes in its ending, as Mulan's heroism ultimately aligns with societal expectations of finding an acceptable husband. Roger Ebert, writing for the *Chicago Sun-Times*, noted that while the film breaks from the tradition of retelling Western children's classics by drawing on a Chinese folk tale, it is still sold on the idea of romantic convention. Ebert observed that Mulan's feelings for Li Shang are more of a postscript to her journey, rather than a central focus. However, critics like Janet Maslin of *The New York Times* negatively opined on the film's perceived need to pair Mulan with "Mr. Right." Despite its feminist undertones, some reviewers argued that the film's message of empowerment is undermined by its adherence to conventional romantic resolutions.

Churchill saw action in both World Wars. He served overseas in World War I from 1916 to 1919 operating a Vickers Machine Gun. During World War II, he served with the Fort Garry Horse, the 12th Manitoba Dragoons, the Elgin Regiment and 1st Canadian Carrier Regiment in Northwestern Europe. In 1945, he was appointed Dean of Faculty at Khaki University in England.

The LM5 Tour received positive reviews from critics with The Times giving their tour a four out of five star rating. Minnie Wright from at Daily Express said "The tour is a fiery showcase of well-meaning (and often brilliantly pitched) empowerment messaging, and throughout which the band convey an authentic unity. The bonus delight, however, comes in the form of the shining punctuating moments in which they deliver divine four-part harmonies to perfection; soft and belting numbers alike, leaving no doubt as to the talent which underpins their success.

One-term incumbent Democrat Mark Begich had been first elected with 48% of the vote in 2008, defeating six-term Senator Ted Stevens by 3,953 votes (a margin of 1.25%). Begich was 52 years old in 2014 and was seeking re-election to a second term. Stevens, who would have been almost 91 years old at the time of the election, had already filed for a rematch back in 2009, but was killed in a plane crash the following year.

Ludwig Kietzmann, a veteran video game journalist formerly associated with GamesRadar+, praised Kat from *Gravity Rush* for her unique charm and relatability. He highlighted her red headband as a distinctive feature and described her as a refreshing contrast to most superheroes, emphasizing her optimism and ability to recover gracefully from clumsy, super-powered mishaps. Kietzmann noted that Kat’s character aligns seamlessly with the game’s gravity-defying controls, imagining her tumbling through the air with elegance and determination. He stated that her innate clumsiness, paired with her spirited recovery, makes her special and believable, adding depth to her role as a protagonist in the game’s origin story.

The Independent Democratic Feminist Party was launched by Paulina Luisi on March 4, 1933, shortly after Uruguay became the second Latin American country to enfranchise women in 1931. Sofía Álvarez Vignoli left the party in early 1933 due to ideological differences. That year, the party began publishing its journal *Ideas y Acción*.

Danon Scorpion began his racing career on July 3, 2021, in a 1,800-meter maiden race at Kokura Racecourse, ridden by Yuga Kawada. Starting as the 1.6 favorite, he defeated Ask Wild More by a neck. After a two-month break, he returned on September 4, 2021, to compete in a 1,800-meter allowance race at Chukyo Racecourse, again with Kawada as his jockey. Positioned as the 1.4 favorite, Danon Scorpion secured victory by a neck over Matenro Leo. These early performances showcased his potential and set the stage for his successful juvenile season.

The revival of the Ruf CTR was unveiled at the 87th Geneva International Motor Show, held from March 9 to March 19, 2017, marking 30 years since the debut of the original CTR, famously nicknamed "Yellowbird." The new model, officially named the CTR Anniversary, features a body constructed entirely from carbon fiber, with a carbon fiber monocoque chassis developed in-house by Ruf. Weighing just 1,250 kilograms (2,640 lbs) dry, the car is powered by a twin-turbocharged 3.6-liter flat-six Mezger engine, producing an impressive 700 horsepower and 649 lb-ft of torque. This powertrain enables the CTR to achieve a top speed of 225 mph (360 km/h) and accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h (62 mph) in just 3.5 seconds. Production of the 2017 CTR is limited to 30 units, excluding the prototype, making it a highly exclusive supercar.

The Gators in 2017 were led by third-year head coach Jim McElwain through the end of October until his firing. Randy Shannon was named the interim head coach on October 29. They finished the season 4–7, 3–5 in SEC play to finish in fifth place in the Eastern Division.

Silloth's largest church is Christ Church, situated in a complete rectangular plot which was planned into the original town design. It is on a commanding site at Criffel Street and the body of the church was completed in 1870. The porch tower and large broach spire were completed later in 1878, and house a ring of 8 bells which are chimed. It was designed by Carlisle architect Charles John Ferguson in the Gothic style, and is built with an interesting mix of local sandstone, and granite which was brought by the North British Railway from Newry in Northern Ireland. Much of the interior is faced with yellow brick trimmed with red.

Within the intestines, the majority of microbes can be found in the large intestine, where the pH is higher and more conducive to survival. These bacteria are often more efficient than our own digestive enzymes, and function to digest protein and carbohydrates. The results of over 690 human microbiomes have shown that the majority of bacteria of the gut microbiome belongs to four phyla: Bacillota, Bacteroidota, Actinomycetota, and Pseudomonadota.

Hibbertia pustulata is a small shrub typically growing to a height of up to 0.5 meters. Its foliage consists of linear to linear-lanceolate leaves, measuring 6.0–7.5 mm in length and 0.7–0.8 mm in width, with a short petiole approximately 0.5 mm long. The leaves are glabrous above, with scattered pale hair tubercles along the margins and towards the apex, and a central vein that bulges on the underside. The flowers are solitary, sessile, and terminal on branches, each subtended by a triangular bract measuring 0.7–1.1 mm in length and 0.6–0.75 mm in width. The sepals are narrowly elliptic-lanceolate, about 5.9–6.3 mm long, and glabrous or sparsely ciliate. The bright yellow petals are oblong and slightly emarginate, while the stamens are arranged in a cluster around the carpels. Hibbertia pustulata primarily flowers from mid to late spring, with its blooms often appearing in October and November.

Houmas was the inspiration for The Antebellum, an 8,212 sqft estate and gardens in Grogan's Point. It is one of six historically inspired estates developed by the Westbrook Building Company in 1988. In-kind, The Antebellum inspired three additional plantation estates in East Shore of The Woodlands.

Liam Byrne, MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill, was selected as Labour's candidate in February 2020. Five politicians showed interest in standing. Two of them: Neena Gill a Member of the European Parliament and Lynda Waltho, former MP for Stourbridge withdrew their candidacy. The candidates for selection were Byrne, Pete Lowe, a former leader of Dudley Council and vice chair of the West Midlands Combined Authority and Salma Yaqoob, the former leader of the Respect Party.

On 16 November 1917, the 71st (South African) Siege Battery handed over its guns to the 73rd (SA) Siege Battery as it prepared to move to reinforce the British artillery for the upcoming Battle of Cambrai. The movement of the battery and other artillery units was concealed under the cover story of routine training exercises. During its journey, the 71st (SA) Siege Battery collected ammunition to ensure it could keep its guns in action upon deployment. By 18 November, the battery had positioned its guns on the outskirts of Gouzeaucourt, coming under the command of the 63rd Heavy Artillery Group (HAG). The objective of III Corps during the Battle of Cambrai was to breach the German Masnières-Beaurevoir line, enabling cavalry to pass through and isolate Cambrai by cutting its railway connections. On 20 November, at 10:30 a.m., the rapid advance of tanks and infantry drove the enemy out of range of the 71st (SA) Siege Battery’s guns, marking a significant early success in the battle.

Veneto hosts one of the oldest universities in the world, the University of Padua, founded in 1222. OECD investigations show that school education achievements in North-Eastern Italy (whose population comes mainly from Veneto) are the highest in Italy. In 2003 the university had approximately 65,000 students.

Kenya is included in Spain's Cooperation Master Plan, with key actors including the Spanish Government, Spanish NGDOs like KUBUKA, and platforms such as Coordinadora. Spanish NGDOs, including KUBUKA and others, actively support development projects in Kenya. Spain also contributes significantly to United Nations programs based in Nairobi, such as UNEP and UN-Habitat, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable development and international cooperation in the region.

In 2013, Mark Carney, Governor of the Bank of England, announced that a newly designed £10 banknote, in polymer, rather than cotton paper, and featuring early 19th-century novelist Jane Austen, would be issued. The date of issue was subsequently confirmed as 14 September 2017. The decision to replace Darwin with Austen followed a campaign to have a woman on the back of a Bank of England banknote when it was announced that the only woman to feature on the back of a note — prison reformer Elizabeth Fry on the £5 note — was to be replaced by Winston Churchill.

Since the age of 18, Al-Arrayedh began writing poetry, with his first set of poems being published in Baghdad in 1931. Since he was a multi-linguist, he translated the works of poets between Persian, Urdu, English, and Arabic. His poems were popular in Iraq, Syria and Egypt; such that the American University of Beirut asked him to deliver lectures on Arab literature, which he had agreed to. He was awarded the Shaikh Isa bin Salman Al Khalifa Order - First Class, by the Bahraini government.

Piła traces its origins to an old fishing village, according to the website of the city. Following the German colonist movement of the 13th century, and particularly after the end of the First Mongol invasion of Poland of 1241, many German colonizers came to this densely wooded area of Poland. General immigration of German settlers diminished, however, when Poland, under King Casimir IV Jagiellon (1447–1492), finally defeated the Teutonic Order in 1466.

The preparation of Laba garlic begins by peeling and cleaning the garlic, ensuring it remains dry. The garlic is then placed in a container and covered with rice vinegar. Optional sugar can be added. Stored for about a week, the garlic turns green.

In 1544, György Szondy was appointed captain of Drégely Castle, which had a small garrison of 146 soldiers, two small cannons, and one castle gun. Strategically located in the Börzsöny Mountains, the castle was vital for defending Hungary against Ottoman advances. Upon learning of an approaching Turkish force, Szondy sent a letter to Erasmus Teuffel, requesting reinforcements. However, Teuffel could only send 50 soldiers, leaving Szondy and his men vastly outnumbered. Despite the castle's deteriorated state and limited resources, Szondy prepared for a heroic defense, understanding the critical role Drégely played in delaying the Ottoman advance toward central Hungary.

He married Mary Evelina (Eva) Shaw on July 14, 1863 in Hopewell, Indiana. They had a son, Clarence, born about 1869, who became a surgeon, and a son John born about 1872 and died by 1923. His granddaughter was Berthe Arnold.

The castle remains are located in a rural, isolated position, 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from the seat of the parish, almost at the top of Mount Balsamão, formerly Monte Carrascal, at 522 metres (1,713 ft) above sea level. It was constructed partly over a rocky surface, adapted to the flanks of the terrain and restricted by the roadway. The interior of the high fortification walls, is covered in pine and vegetation. At the top of the mountain is the Convent of Balsamão and along the flanks, the eight chapels of the Passos da Paixão de Cristo (Way of the Passion of Christ) that comprise the sanctuary and, immediately near the wall, the Capela dos Cajados (Chapel of Cajados) and cemetery. The hilltop is supported by the Azibo River, to the south (a tributary of Sabor), crossed by the Ponte de Paradinha, and in the north, the Ribeira de Veiga.

Preparations to found the Academy were begun in the spring of 1952, and the Academy was founded on 1 December 1952. When the Academy was founded, it had 10 full and 15 candidate members in nine research institutes and 43 smaller research laboratories. Its first president was Hong Myng-hi who, according to Andrei Lankov, was not a skilled administrator and chosen for his political loyalty. Hong was followed by Paek Nam-un, who was more adept.

The Kosciuszko Foundation traces its origins to the Polish American Scholarship Committee, established in 1923 by Dr. Stephen Mizwa at the request of the Polish Government to bring Polish students to American universities. Collaborating with Dr. Henry Noble McCracken of Vassar College, the Committee expanded its mission to foster U.S.-Poland cultural and educational exchange, becoming the Kosciuszko Foundation in December 1925.

Due to the proximity of Ike to Guam upon its formation, the island was placed under the "Condition of Readiness" level; this was the first time that such a high readiness level was issued since Typhoon Pamela in 1982. Although Ike passed somewhat near the island, the storm's compact size during its formative stages mitigated any damage. Despite being near typhoon intensity at the time, a station on Nimitz Hill only documented winds of 30 km/h (19 mph), with higher gusts.

Arthur and Stella Sanford acquired the Sanford House in 1936 from lumber baron George and Mary Avery. Arthur was born in Minneapolis to Austrian-Jewish immigrants James and Edith Sanford and was immersed in business from birth at his father's mercantile store. He dropped-out of the University of Minnesota and Wharton School of Finance and Commerce. He interned at Brown Brothers Harriman & Co and served in World War I as a sergeant major in a logistics casual company in Paris.

The industrial sector of Andhra Pradesh encompasses key industries such as manufacturing, IT, automobiles, and tourism. Sri City, an integrated business city located in SPSR Nellore District near Chennai, hosts over 220 companies, including global firms like PepsiCo, which also operates its largest Indian plant in Chittoor district. Andhra Pradesh is emerging as a hub for manufacturing and auto components, with Kia Motors in Anantapur and other industries in Krishna district. The state’s strategic infrastructure and policies attract significant domestic and international investments.

The International Women's Club Championship (IWCC) was the first international annual competition contested by women's champion clubs. The competition was founded and organised by the Japan Football Association and the L. League. The first International Women's Club Championship took place in Japan in November 2012 with participation from four teams: Olympique Lyonnais (Europe), Canberra United (Australia), INAC Kobe Leonessa (Japan) and NTV Beleza (cup winner, Japan).

Hill was said to be working on an original feature film in 2013, which was confirmed to feature late actress Karen Black in a leading role. However, she had died before the film could come into full fruition. A shortened excerpt of the footage was posted on the Death Grips YouTube account in October 2015 in a 15-minute video entitled "Bottomless Pit". It has been rumored that either Death Grips or Hill's side-project, the I.L.Y's, would be involved with the soundtrack of the film as well.

"On My Mind" by Ellie Goulding received mixed to positive reviews from music critics. Steven J. Horowitz of *Billboard* gave the song a 3.5 out of 5 rating, describing it as a "radio-ready anthem" with a "Message In A Bottle sound-alike riff" that recounts an illicit fling. Maeve McDermott of *USA Today* noted that the track was poised to be as ubiquitous as Martin's recent hits, highlighting its catchy and polished production. Matthew Horton of *NME* praised Goulding's vocal delivery, emphasizing its emotional depth and intensity. Carolyn Menyes of *Music Times* described the single as taking the band's "new poppy direction" and making it "dark, with yearning vocals and deep twists," which she noted elevated the song to a new level. These elements combined to create a track that resonated with listeners while showcasing Goulding's ability to balance pop sensibilities with emotional storytelling.

Ten pound notes were introduced by the Bank of England for the first time in 1759 as a consequence of gold shortages caused by the Seven Years' War. The earliest notes were handwritten, and were issued as needed to individuals. These notes were written on one side only and bore the name of the payee, the date, and the signature of the issuing cashier. With the exception of the Restriction period between 1797 and 1821, when the French Revolutionary Wars and the Napoleonic Wars caused a bullion shortage, these notes could be exchanged in full, or in part, for an equivalent amount of gold when presented at the bank. If redeemed in part, the banknote would be signed to indicate the amount that had been redeemed. From 1853 printed notes replaced handwritten notes, with the declaration "I promise to pay the bearer on demand the sum of ten pounds" replacing the name of the payee. This declaration remains on Bank of England banknotes to this day. A printed signature of one of three cashiers appeared on the printed notes, though this was replaced by the signature of the Chief Cashier from 1870 onwards.

According to the 2000 census, Isle of Hope, Georgia, had a population of 2,110, with 819 households and 606 families residing in the area. The population density was 1,266 people per square mile, with 859 housing units at an average density of 515.3 per square mile. The racial makeup was predominantly White (96.8%), with Hispanic or Latino residents of any race comprising 0.5% of the population. These figures reflect the community's demographic composition at the start of the 21st century.

Levine announced his candidacy for Manhattan borough president in January 2020. Levine was endorsed by Representatives Adriano Espaillat and Nydia Velázquez, among other elected officials, labor unions, and advocacy groups. He won the Democratic primary defeating New York State Senator Brad Hoylman by 7%, and defeated Republican Lou Puliafito in the November 2021 general election.

The South African regulatory body for drugs, the Medicines Control Council (MCC), initially classified dagga (cannabis) as a Schedule 7 substance, indicating it had a high potential for abuse and no accepted medicinal value, rendering it illegal. In 2017, the MCC published regulations permitting the use of dagga for medical purposes and expressed a desire to reclassify cannabinoid medication as a Schedule 6 substance. This reclassification would allow patients to access medicinal cannabis products by prescription at pharmacies. However, partial decriminalisation of personal cannabis use in 2017, following a High Court ruling, reduced the significance of this change. The Dagga Couple, prominent cannabis activists, called for broader reforms, advocating for full legalisation rather than limited reclassification.

The Sanford House in Sioux City, Iowa, was acquired in 1936 by Arthur and Stella Sanford. Arthur Sanford, born in Davison, Michigan, to Arthur and Margaret (Hamilton) Sanford, was the son of a Navy veteran. He attended but did not complete studies at the University of Michigan and Harvard University. Arthur interned at the Mayo Clinic and served as a medical officer during World War I, contributing significantly to his community and profession.

The modern nations of Greece and Turkey trace their roots to the Byzantine and Ottoman Empires, respectively, distinct regimes that shaped their histories. The Byzantine Empire, often seen as a continuation of Rome, holds significant historical importance, while Turkey's relationship to the Ottoman Empire is debated, with some academics viewing it as either a successor or a continuation, particularly in discussions of "neo-Ottomanism."

The details of *2PM Member's Selection* were announced on May 10, 2012, via JYP Entertainment's official website. The album includes a new version music video preview of "Only You," previously released on the single *Hottest Time of the Day*. Limited to 10,000 physical copies, the album features a 72-page photobook and six postcards of the group, offering fans exclusive content alongside the music.

Cetus lies far from the galactic plane, so that many distant galaxies are visible, unobscured by dust from the Milky Way. Of these, the brightest is Messier 77 (NGC 1068), a 9th magnitude spiral galaxy near Delta Ceti. It appears face-on and has a clearly visible nucleus of magnitude 10. About 50 million light-years from Earth, M77 is also a Seyfert galaxy and thus a bright object in the radio spectrum. Recently, the galactic cluster JKCS 041 was confirmed to be the most distant cluster of galaxies yet discovered.

Prior to 2017, Slovenia had participated in the Eurovision Song Contest 22 times since its debut in 1993. The country’s highest placing was seventh, achieved twice: in 1995 with Darja Švajger’s "Prisluhni mi" and in 2001 with Nuša Derenda’s "Energy." Slovenia’s only other top ten result was tenth place in 1997. Since the introduction of semi-finals in 2004, Slovenia had qualified for the final four times by 2017. In 2016, Slovenia was represented by ManuElla with the song "Blue and Red," but she did not qualify for the final.

Qi met Xi Zhongxun in 1943 whilst studying at the middle school in Suide County. At the time, Xi was still married to his first wife, Hao Mingzhu (Chinese: 郝明珠), with whom he had three children. Later, the head of education at the Counter-Japanese Military and Political University wrote to Xi, introducing Qi Xin, after having met her sister and father. After sending Xi her own autobiographical introduction, the two married at the prefectural party office in Suide County on 28 April 1944. The couple had their first child in 1949, a daughter named Qiaoqiao (Chinese: 桥桥), followed by another daughter in 1952 named An'an (Chinese: 安安), and two sons named Jinping and Yuanping (Chinese: 远平). They led a frugal and austere lifestyle, not having shoes for the younger sons.

The old Helsinki slang, born in working-class neighborhoods north of the Pitkäsilta bridge, spread to areas like Punavuori, nicknamed "Rööperi." Variation in vocabulary and speech was most prominent between neighborhoods, with the Pitkäsilta bridge serving as a dividing line. South of the bridge, Swedish influence dominated, while northward, Finnish elements became more pronounced.

El Salvador has approximately 360 rivers, forming 10 hydrographic regions. The country’s four primary lakes are Lake Ilopango (72 km²), Lake Guija (45 km²), Lake Coatepeque (24 km²), and Lake Olomega (24.2 km²). El Salvador receives 7.5 km³ of surface water annually from neighboring Honduras and Guatemala. The largest body of fresh water is Cerron Grande, locally known as Lake Suchitlan, covering 607 km². These water resources are vital for agriculture, hydroelectric power, and biodiversity, despite challenges like pollution and eutrophication in some areas.

The second 25 years of CRREL's history, beginning in 1986, were marked by the end of the Cold War, a major geopolitical shift, and the conclusion of the Gulf War. During this period, CRREL diversified its funding strategy, receiving support from various U.S. military branches, including the Army, Navy, and Air Force, as well as civilian agencies like NASA and NOAA, and private organizations. CRREL's research addressed challenges across eight topical categories, including signature physics, cryospheric sciences, biogeochemical processes, geochemistry, force projection, infrastructure, and water resources. These categories reflect CRREL's interdisciplinary focus on solving complex problems in extreme environments, advancing science and engineering for both military and civilian applications in all climates and seasons.

In 1993, Paula Cole was invited by Peter Gabriel to join his Secret World Tour, replacing Sinéad O'Connor. Recommended by Gabriel's studio engineer Kevin Killen, Gabriel left a message on Cole's answering machine, offering her the opportunity. With only one rehearsal in Germany, Cole performed in front of 16,000 fans shortly after. The tour, spanning 1993–1994, marked a pivotal moment in Cole's career, showcasing her talent on an international stage.

The Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) is Canada’s primary intelligence agency, tasked with investigating and collecting intelligence on threats to national security, including espionage, foreign interference, terrorism, and the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction. CSIS analyzes this intelligence to advise the Government of Canada and federal agencies on security risks. Additionally, CSIS manages security assessments and plays a critical role in countering foreign-influenced activities detrimental to Canada’s interests.

The primary purpose of *The Atheist Experience* is to engage in discussions or debates on the existence of gods and related topics, primarily between atheist hosts and theist callers. Geared towards a non-atheist audience, the show fosters confrontational debates to encourage theists to question their beliefs. Theists are intended to hear directly from atheists about their perspectives, aiming to clarify what atheists actually believe. Additionally, the show seeks to promote critical thinking and dialogue about theistic and atheistic worldviews.

Barrett would serve as a deputy district attorney for Washington County and later as district attorney for Oregon's fifth judicial district. In 1880, he served as a Republican in the Oregon House of Representatives serving Washington County. He returned to the House in 1891.

The club plays at Broadhall Way, previously home to Stevenage Town and Stevenage Athletic. Following the bankruptcy of the town's former club, the stadium was not used for three years. The newly formed Stevenage Borough had planned on playing Hitchin Town Youth at Broadhall Way in November 1976 as a "curtain-raiser" for the new venture. Volunteers prepared the pitch in anticipation, but their plans were scuppered when the ex-chairman of Stevenage Athletic – and stadium lease-holder – desecrated the playing surface at Broadhall Way by digging a trench across the full length of the pitch. The club eventually moved into Broadhall Way in 1980 as a result of the council re-purchasing the stadium, and allowed the football club to become its tenant.

Acute postoperative endophthalmitis caused by *Staphylococcus lugdunensis* was reported in a prospective, case-control study conducted at university-affiliated hospitals in Japan. Among 53 cases of post-cataract surgery endophthalmitis, severe ocular inflammation occurred at a mean delay of 6.5 days after surgery. Three cases experienced significant visual loss due to severe vitreous infiltration. Initial treatment included intravitreal antibiotics (vancomycin and ceftazidime), corticosteroid eye drops, and cycloplegic agents, with close monitoring every six hours. Pars plana vitrectomy was required in three cases due to the severity of the infection. Complications, including retinal detachment, adversely affected the final visual prognosis in these cases. Microbiological diagnosis was achieved through vitreous sampling, utilizing smear preparation, selective culture media, and antibiotic sensitivity testing. The study highlighted the importance of early diagnosis and aggressive management to mitigate the impact of this rare but severe postoperative complication.

John Thornton was born in 1949 in Raleigh, Mississippi, to Col. Robert L. Thornton, a former US Air Force officer who later became a high school teacher, and Mary E. (Kelly) Thornton, a professor at the University of Southern Mississippi. He has a sister, Nancy Thornton, and is married to Lillian Batte Thornton. His daughter, Amara Thornton, is a historian of archaeology. Thornton received his education at the University of Zambia in 1971 and completed his PhD in African history at the University of California, Los Angeles, in 1979.

On December 20, 1976, the dismembered body of a young white female was discovered along the Lehigh River in Carbon County, Pennsylvania, inside three suitcases. The unidentified victim, later buried as "Beth Doe" in an unmarked grave, remained nameless for over 40 years. In 2021, investigators identified a relative, Luis Colon Jr., through DNA and genealogical databases. Colon Jr. revealed that his aunt, Evelyn Colon, had gone missing in the 1970s while pregnant and living with her boyfriend. A letter from the boyfriend falsely claimed Evelyn had given birth and was fine, but she never contacted her family again. This breakthrough confirmed the victim’s identity as Evelyn Colon, solving the decades-old mystery.

On July 20, 2006, the newly formed Indian Motorcycle Company, largely owned by London-based private equity firm Stellican Limited, announced its new home in Kings Mountain, North Carolina. Picking up where the defunct Gilroy IMC operation left off in 2003, the company restarted production of the Indian Chief brand, manufacturing motorcycles in limited numbers with a focus on exclusivity rather than performance. The 'Kings Mountain' models were based on a series of motorcycles developed in 1999. In 2009, the Indian Chief incorporated a redesigned 105-cubic-inch Powerplus V-twin powertrain with electronic closed-loop sequential port fuel injection, supported by a new charging system that provided increased capacity for the EFI.

Following several collaborative screenwriting projects, Lambert and Varkle set out to produce a feature film. Beginning in 2016, they outlined several projects that would support a close character study of an isolated individual. Inspired by headlines of mass shooters brainwashed by online hate groups, they opted to tell an "angry young man" story. The filmmakers researched online communities of self-described "red pillers" and "incels" in order to make their character realistic and relatable.

The National ABU Robocon 2015 took place in the Badminton Hall of the Shree Shiv Chhatrapati Sports Complex, Balewadi, Pune on 7 March 2015. The theme for Robocon 2015 has been declared by Televisi Republik Indonesia (TVRI) as "Robomintion: Badminton Robo-Game". Institute of Technology, Nirma University, Ahmedabad won the National ABU Robocon 2015 Contest and were the first runners-up. Muffakham Jah College of Engineering and Technology (MJCET) stood third in the country and was also awarded the ‘Best Design and Aesthetics’ robot at the Asia Pacific Broadcasting Union Robotics Contest (ABU Robocon) 2015 held at Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Pune. U.V.Patel College of Engineering, Mehsana, Gujarat won the Best IDEA Award.

According to AGB Nielsen Philippines' Mega Manila household television ratings, the pilot episode of Pepito Manaloto earned a 28.2% rating. While the first episode of the show as Pepito Manaloto: Ang Tunay na Kuwento scored a 26.6% rating. The pilot episode of Pepito Manaloto: Ang Unang Kuwento a 14.7% rating from Nationwide Urban Television Audience Measurement.

The Space Infrastructure Servicing (SIS) vehicle, announced by MacDonald, Dettwiler and Associates (MDA) in 2010, competed with ViviSat's Mission Extension Vehicle (MEV). ViviSat's MEV attaches to satellites using a probe-in-the-kick-motor approach, providing propulsion and attitude control via its own thrusters. In contrast, MDA's SIS vehicle refuels satellites and employs a robotic arm and tools for inspection, repositioning, towing, and minor repairs, offering a more advanced satellite servicing solution.

Dan Drew first ran against incumbent Mayor Sebastian Giuliano in the 2009 Middletown mayoral race but was defeated. He successfully defeated Giuliano in 2011 to become mayor. Re-elected in 2013 with 67% of the vote, Drew served during a significant 2015 change that extended the mayoral term from two to four years.

Duck Game was developed by Vancouver-based Landon Podbielski, who initially envisioned it as a platformer. Inspired by multiplayer gaming, he spontaneously added local multiplayer with guns. The game launched on the Ouya on May 13, 2014, featuring four-player local multiplayer. A single-player mode was added shortly after release, with an update arriving about a week later.

Bhadra Fort, constructed by Ahmed Shah in 1411 AD, covered an area of 43 acres and was enclosed by a fortified city wall with 14 towers, eight gates, and two large openings. On the west side, it housed royal palaces, the Nagina Baugh, and Ahmed Shah’s Mosque, while the east side featured an open area known as Maidan-Shah. The eastern wall along the riverbank still endures. During Ahmed Shah’s reign, the fort complex served as a royal court. The triple-arched gateway, Teen Darwaza, located on the eastern side, was formerly the entrance to Maidan-Shah. Beyond Teen Darwaza, the road leads to Manek Chowk, a bustling mercantile square, with the southern side along the road hosting the vibrant Lal Darwaza market.

Walkabout Travel Gear was founded by Gia and Bradford Boyle. Their original inspiration came on March 3, 1995, on the island of Malta. While on a European backpacking trip, the couple realized no travel gear company was targeting independent, budget travellers. They cut their journey short, returning home to Salt Lake City, Utah. They used their remaining savings to research and purchase inventory and produce a print catalog. The catalog was designed to capture the excitement as well as the cost consciousness of budget, independent travel. Bradford Boyle had previous graphics experience.

Rachel Van Hollebeke began her international career with the U.S. youth national teams, playing for the U-19 and U-21 squads. She was a key player in the U.S. team that won the inaugural FIFA U-19 Women’s World Championship in 2002 in Canada, despite tearing her ACL in the final. At the 2004 FIFA U-19 Women’s World Championship in Thailand, her team finished third. Van Hollebeke joined the senior national team training camp in July 2006 and made her full debut under coach Pia Sundhage on March 5, 2008, at the Algarve Cup against China. She was named to the 2008 U.S. Olympic women’s soccer team on June 23, 2008, appearing in three matches and helping the team secure gold in Beijing. In 2011, she competed in the FIFA Women’s World Cup, scoring in the opening match against Korea DPR. At the 2012 London Olympics, she played in four matches, earning her second gold medal.

Qi Xin met Xi Zhongxun in 1943 while studying at a middle school in Suide County. At the time, Xi Zhongxun was married to his first wife, Hao Mingzhu, with whom he had three children. The head of education at the Counter-Japanese Military and Political University introduced Qi Xin to Xi Zhongxun after meeting her sister and father. Before their marriage, Qi Xin sent Xi Zhongxun a pair of cloth shoes. The couple married on 15 September 1944 in Suide County. Their first child, Xi Qiaoqiao, was born in 1949, followed by their second daughter, Xi An’an, in 1952. They also had two sons, Xi Jinping and Xi Yuanping. Qi Xin and Xi Zhongxun led a modest lifestyle and did not provide their younger sons with luxurious conditions, emphasizing simplicity and discipline in their upbringing.

ISG15 was originally identified in the late 1970s as a 15-kDa protein produced in response to type I interferon, a potent class of antiviral cytokines. Given the molecular weight, it was originally termed ‘a 15-kDa protein’, but later renamed interferon-stimulated-gene-15 when the cassette of interferon-stimulated genes were recognized. In 1987 it was identified that ISG15 cross-reacts with anti-ubiquitin antibodies, and subsequent experiments uncovered the ubiquitin-like conjugation of ISG15 to other cellular proteins, coined ‘ISGylation’. Given its inducibility by IFN-I, studies in the following decades focused on the antiviral activity of ISG15. These studies were carried out predominantly with in vitro systems and mouse models, and ascribed several antiviral functions to ISGylation. During this time, it was also discovered that ISG15 could be detected outside of cells. and in human serum samples. This free form of ISG15 could stimulate IFN-II production in lymphocytes. Finally, ISG15 could also be detected as an un-conjugated intracellular molecule with functions independent of ISGylation.

ProZorro, a Ukrainian public procurement platform, is built on the OpenProcurement toolkit, designed for transparent, competitive processes. Inspired by Georgia's e-procurement system, ProZorro adheres to global transparency standards, fostering public oversight and combating corruption in public spending.

Andrea went to North Mecklenburg High School in Huntersville, North Carolina. Andrea was selected as the female player of the year for the North Carolina High School Athletic Association in 1986–87. The Charlotte Observer named her North Carolina Miss Basketball in 1987. She played college basketball for North Carolina State University. She finished her NC State career third in scoring (2,136), third in field goals (917), third in steals (286), sixth in assists (402) and sixth in blocked shots (84).

In The Color of Water author James McBride writes both his autobiography and a tribute to the life of his mother, Ruth McBride. Ruth married Andrew Dennis McBride, a black man from North Carolina. James's childhood was spent in a chaotic household of twelve children who had neither the time nor the outlet to ponder questions of race and identity. Ruth did not want to discuss the painful details of her early family life when her abusive father, Tateh, lorded over her sweet-tempered and meek mother, Mameh ("tateh" and "mameh" are Yiddish terms of endearment for "father" and "mother," roughly equivalent to "daddy and "mommy"). Ruth had cut all ties with her Jewish family, as they had essentially disowned her when she married James's father.

The Williams Rugby Football Club (WRFC) was founded by David Coughlin '61 in the spring of 1959 following spending a year studying abroad at Oxford University in England. Starting with a handful of incoming freshmen with experience playing rugby while studying in England, the Club was formed and supported by Peter Pearson, the first Williams Rugby coach. The following fall, the WRFC entered the Eastern Rugby Union (ERU) and won the ERU Championship in 1960 with a 5-0-1 record. Following a 1961 tour in England, support and recognition from the College increased dramatically.

Major arterial routes in Clayton include Big Bend Boulevard, Brentwood Boulevard, Clayton Road, Forest Park Parkway, Hanley Road, and Interstate 170. Forsyth Boulevard (which changes to Old Bonhomme between North Clayton and University City) was at one time an ancient Native American trail and was part of the route of the 1904 Olympic marathon. Other primary routes in the city include Maryland Avenue and Wydown Boulevard, the latter of which was named a Great Street by the American Planning Association in 2010.

Prior to the NHL Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) expiring in September, teams reassigned players on two-way or entry-level contracts to minor league affiliates. The Canucks assigned 27 players, including Christopher Tanev, Eddie Lack, Zack Kassian and 2012 second-round draft pick Alexandre Mallet to the Chicago Wolves, while first-round pick Brendan Gaunce was sent to his junior team, the Belleville Bulls. When the CBA expired on September 15, the NHL enforced a lockout until a new agreement could be reached. Canucks management posted a message from the team to the fans stating that they would continue with community initiatives throughout the lockout, which they hoped would be resolved as soon as possible, and thanked fans for their loyalty and patience. They also reduced their employees work week to four days and instituted a 20% pay reduction.

The journal was established in 1970. Prior to that time, original research articles covering topics in infection and immunity were published in a section of the Journal of Bacteriology. As the size of this section grew, the need for a separate journal publishing peer-reviewed research in this area became apparent. The first editor-in-chief was Erwin Neter (SUNY Buffalo).

Wood was not selected in the 1960 NFL draft, and wrote a letter to head coach Vince Lombardi to request a tryout; the Packers signed him as a rookie free agent in 1960. After a few days with the quarterbacks, he requested a switch to defense and was recast as a free safety, and was a starter in the season. He started until his retirement in 1971.

Bikram Singh graduated from the Royal Military Academy, Sandhurst, in 1933 and was commissioned as a second lieutenant in the Indian Army. He was placed on the Unattached List for the Indian Army and attached to the 6th Royal Berkshire Regiment, a British unit, for his first year of service. Following this, he was posted to the 6th Royal Battalion (Scinde), 13th Frontier Force Rifles, stationed at Hangu in the North-West Frontier Province. The battalion later moved to Kohat. Bikram Singh spent six years in the North-West Frontier Province, serving there until 1939, gaining valuable experience in a challenging and strategic region.

The term "cement" originates from the Latin word "Caementum", which signifies chopped stone. Cement describes a binding substance, which will react chemically with water and develop into a material hard as stone. In fibre cement there is a fibre reinforcement, which contributes to making the fibre-cement material even stronger and to better withstand tension. Together with a carefully planned production process, fibre cement makes it possible to develop strong and long-lasting construction materials.

The Lancer Assault Rifle, an iconic weapon from the *Gears of War* series, has become a popular subject for merchandise, particularly in the form of detailed replica models. One of the earliest examples of Lancer merchandise was a life-sized replica offered by Amazon.com as part of a special *Gears of War 2* retail edition bundle, which launched alongside the game on November 7, 2008. This replica, measuring 37 inches long, 12 inches tall, and 5 inches wide, and weighing approximately 15 pounds, featured motorized trigger-activated sound and vibration, requiring three "C" batteries. Companies like TriForce Sales have also created 1:1 scale replicas using high-density foam, fiberglass, and polyurethane composites, ensuring exceptional detail and authenticity. These replicas, often crafted from in-game 3D models, cater to collectors and fans alike, with some models including additional features like removable clips and adjustable handles, further immersing fans in the *Gears of War* universe.

Niall Mason was born on January 10, 1997, in Brighton, England. With parents of English, American, and Indian heritage, Mason lived in England, Spain, and Qatar during his youth. Eligible to represent England, the USA, or India, he has expressed a preference for England.

Ewin Tang's groundbreaking work on classical algorithms, which demonstrated that certain quantum speedups could be matched by classical methods, garnered significant media attention for challenging the prevailing perception that quantum computing was inherently faster for problems like recommendation systems. Her research, completed as an 18-year-old undergraduate at the University of Texas, showed that the properties exploited by quantum algorithms could also be utilized by classical ones, reshaping the understanding of quantum advantage. Tang noted that while quantum algorithms inspired her work, she realized they were not necessarily faster in this case. In response to her findings, Robert Young, a Royal Society research fellow and director of the University of Lancaster's Quantum Technology Centre, defended quantum computing in a BBC article, emphasizing that such research would not exist without investments in quantum technologies. Tang described proving her algorithm to her Ph.D. adviser, James R. Lee, as a significant moment, with her work later undergoing formal peer review.

In 1982, the Feinberg Realty and Construction Company purchased 1,850 sq ft (172 m2) of land on 57th Street and bought out tenants' leases. The tenants included the Little Carnegie Theater, which closed abruptly in mid-1982 after the acquisition. Feinberg had hired Schuman Lichtenstein & Claman to design a 350,000 sq ft (33,000 m2) tower on the site.

Heroine grossed ₹7.5 crore on its opening day and ₹25 crore during its opening weekend. The film's first-week collection stood at approximately ₹35 crore, while its total collection by the end of the second week reached around ₹40 crore. Despite a strong start, the film's overall box office performance was average, with its lifetime earnings estimated at ₹45 crore.

The Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food is responsible for the Canadian Grain Commission. The Commission is governed by the Canada Grain Act (R.S.C. 1985, c. G-10), which provides for the appointment of three commissioners by the federal cabinet, one of whom is named chief commissioner. The CGC is headed by a Chief Operating Officer (CEO), who reports to the chief commissioner.

Stoudermire played for the Minnesota Golden Gophers from 2008 to 2012. He was medically redshirted in 2011. He finished his college career as FBS football's all-time leader in kickoff return yards with 3,615 yards on 144 returns.

Details of the album were announced on May 18, on the website Naver. They revealed the track list, concept photos, an interview of the group and a preview of the music video for a new version of the song "Only You", previously released on the single Hottest Time of the Day. It was later announced that the physical version of the album was limited to 20,000 copies. The album also includes a 72-page photobook and 8 postcards of the group.

Near the end of May, Eliza sends Mattie to deliver newly baked cookies to their eccentric neighbors, the Bents, as a sharing gesture. She stops to pick flowers and encounters the oldest Bent son, Gardiner, home from the Normal School in nearby Vernon, where he is studying to become a teacher. Gard walks her to his family's farm, where his mother returns the favor by filling her box with freshly cleaned catfish. Mattie misses supper but has no appetite when she returns home, and Eliza discerns from a tune she is humming that Mattie is taken with Gard and yearning to be a woman. Eliza innocently gives her daughter advice about judging men and Mattie bursts into tears, accusing her mother of trying to push her out. Eliza realizes that Mattie has glimpsed leaving home as a wife. While Jess recalls that Eliza herself once similarly shed tears, Eliza remembers leaving home as a happy time, humming the same song as Mattie, which she also fancied in her youth.

In 2012, Mustafa Barghouti attended a Land Day protest in Bethlehem, where he claimed he was struck by a tear-gas canister fired by Israeli forces. Speaking from his hospital bed, Barghouti reported injuries to his scalp and right ear and expressed concerns about potential spinal damage. He described being hit on the side of his head and back. An Israeli Defense spokesperson denied targeting him specifically, stating that tear gas was used to disperse rioters. Barghouti, a prominent Palestinian politician and activist, criticized the excessive use of force during the protest.

The group targeted media figures known for their fashionable tastes and stylish, status-driven lifestyles. Their primary targets were female, having been chosen, in the words of Prugo, "mainly because [of] women wanting women's stuff". If the members liked a celebrity's style, they became determined to steal the celebrity's clothes. Lee referred to their capers as "going shopping." They found the houses of their targets using Google Maps and website celebrityaddressaerial.com, and determined when the victims would be away by researching their schedules, such as appearances at celebrity events, through websites including Facebook and Twitter. When Lee wanted to add to her wardrobe, or more cash was desired, a burglary would be undertaken. The Los Angeles Police Department report later stated that what began as a "twisted adventure" fueled by celebrity worship "quickly mushroomed into an organized criminal enterprise." In the words of Neiers, Lee "was so manipulating, so conniving. Nick always did what she said. Rachel was in charge. She started it all."

In the first opinion poll after Francesco Storace announced The Right, the party received 3.2%. Surveys suggested it might draw votes from Forza Italia, National Alliance, and the Northern League. Alessandra Mussolini's party, Social Alternative, was also noted. Other polls placed The Right at 2-3%, rising to 3.5% after Romano Prodi's government fell.

Kader has been recognized for her distinguished contribution to global peace. She has sought to increase awareness of pressing global issues such as human trafficking and much of her work has been committed towards building peace in the world. In pursuit of these goals, Kader has hosted numerous events, panel discussions, peace conferences, vigils, prayer services and peace marches including International Peace Conferences at the Arab League Headquarters in Cairo, Egypt, Annual UN International Day of Peace celebrations throughout the world and an International Peace Walk in Sharm el-Sheikh, Egypt (December 19, 2005) among others. Through this work, Kader has strived to contribute towards peace-building initiatives by promoting mutual respect, camaraderie and religious tolerance and by condemning violence and hatred.

Eiríkur Önundarson came up through the junior programs of Íþróttafélag Reykjavíkur (IR) and played his first games with the senior team at the age of 17 during the 1991–1992 1. deild karla season. That season, he appeared in 14 games, averaging 2.9 points per game. In 1994, IR reached the 1. deild finals, where they faced Keflavík. In game two of the finals, Eiríkur scored 21 points, helping IR secure a victory and force a decisive game three.

In 1966, shortly after graduating high school, 16-year-old Jackson Browne began pitching his material to artists and publishing houses. By January 1967, he recorded demo tracks for Nina Music, the publishing arm of Elektra Records, at Jaycina Studios in New York City. These recordings, including an early version of "These Days" titled "I've Been Out Walking," were part of a double album created by Nina Music to showcase Browne's songwriting to potential artists. The demos, pressed in a limited run of 100 copies, were not intended for commercial release but served as a publishing tool. Browne’s youthful yet introspective songwriting, exemplified by "These Days," would later gain prominence through Nico’s 1967 rendition on her album *Chelsea Girl*.

Complementary and alternative medicine (CAM) for allergic rhinitis, such as acupuncture, has shown modest benefits in some studies, including reduced nasal symptoms and lower medication use. Acupuncture targeting acupoints like sphenopalatine (SPA) has been explored, but trials remain limited, and evidence quality is moderate. While the American Academy of Otolaryngology supports acupuncture, the American Academy of Allergy, Asthma, and Immunology does not recommend CAM due to insufficient evidence for consistent efficacy.

Although he made occasional attempts at novel writing, Abbott devoted himself almost exclusively to the craft of the short story. In 2012, when asked why he never went on to complete a novel, he replied, "...whenever I've gone longer than is my custom, I feel that I'm lying. I know only two things: the genius of story is its brevity; and, story succeeds by what it leaves out."

Supporters of austerity measures often compare government debt to household debt, using this metaphor to argue that overspending leads to unsustainable debt and potential bankruptcy. Critics, however, characterize this analogy as misleading and economically flawed.

In their Error Management Theory (see also Adaptive Bias), Martie Haselton and David Buss suggested that judgments about opportunities and threats, in cases of uncertainty, would consistently err toward minimizing potential costs to reproductive fitness. Smoke detectors have often been used as an analogy for how threat mitigating adaptations might function. Smoke detectors are designed to be overly-sensitive to the presence of smoke so that they don’t fail to respond in case of an actual fire. For this reason, they often give false alarms. However, if smoke detectors are too sensitive, we are likely to either turn them off or become desensitized to their warnings. The smoke detector principle holds that adaptations function similarly, erring on the side of caution. False alarms may be common, but overall costs are minimized.

The major shareholder of Fosun International was a Hong Kong incorporated company Fosun Holdings, which was a wholly owned subsidiary of British Virgin Islands incorporated company Fosun International Holdings. Three founders of Fosun International, Guo Guangchang, Liang Xinjun and Wang Qunbin, owned 64.45%, 24.44% and 11.11% respectively in Fosun International Holdings. Fan Wei, another founder, withdrew his stake in Fosun International Holdings in September 2015.

Litlington parish, covering 879 hectares (2,171 acres), is roughly rectangular, stretching from the Icknield Way along its southern border, now following Ashwell Street, to the Mill River in the north. It borders Abington Pigotts to the north, Steeple Morden to the west, and Bassingbourn to the east, with boundaries following ancient field lines.

Andrew Hughes joined Charlton Athletic from Scunthorpe United on July 1, 2011, signing a two-year deal. He made his debut for the club on August 6, 2011, in a 3–0 victory against Bournemouth. During the 2011–12 season, Hughes made 20 league appearances before his campaign was cut short in March 2012 due to an Achilles injury sustained in a match against Colchester United. The injury required surgery, sidelining him for nearly a year. Hughes returned to action in a development squad match against Millwall in February 2013 and rejoined Charlton’s first team on March 9, 2013, starting in a 1–0 win over Huddersfield Town. On June 26, 2013, Hughes signed a new one-year contract with Charlton, extending his stay until the summer of 2014. However, after making 28 appearances in the 2013–14 season, it was confirmed on May 7, 2014, that Hughes would leave Charlton Athletic after three seasons at The Valley.

On 21 September 2012, Prime Minister Jens Stoltenberg announced that Hadia Tajik would become the new Minister of Culture, replacing Anniken Huitfeldt, who assumed the role of Minister of Labour and Social Affairs. At 29, Tajik became the youngest minister in Norwegian history and the first Muslim to serve in the Norwegian cabinet, marking a significant milestone in the country's political landscape.

Error Management Theory, proposed by David Buss and Martie Haselton, explains how humans evolved cognitive biases to minimize the costliest errors in judgments under uncertainty, particularly regarding opportunities and threats. Judgments err toward minimizing high-cost errors, even if it increases the likelihood of less costly ones. This concept is often illustrated through the "smoke detector principle," which likens threat-mitigating adaptations to smoke detectors. Smoke detectors are intentionally designed to be overly sensitive, prioritizing early fire detection to prevent catastrophic outcomes, even if it means occasional false alarms. However, excessive sensitivity can lead to frequent, unnecessary activations, reducing their effectiveness. Similarly, the smoke detector principle suggests that evolutionary adaptations may favor heightened sensitivity to threats, accepting false positives to avoid missing critical dangers, thereby enhancing survival despite potential inefficiencies.

The artworks collected for the Führermuseum were originally stored in various locations, with many of the purchases kept in the air raid shelters of the Führerbau in Munich. The Führerbau, an administrative building for Adolf Hitler and the Nazi Party, served as a key storage site and way station for paintings acquired for the museum project. These artworks were under the control of the Nazi Party Chancellery, directly overseen by Hitler and his special commissioners. Hitler frequently visited the Führerbau during his trips to Munich, where he would inspect the collection and engage in extensive discussions about art. Beyond Munich, confiscated artworks were stored in remote deposits in Upper Austria, often hidden in forests or mountains, including the salt mines of Alt Aussee. The Einsatzstab Reichsleiter Rosenberg (ERR) requisitioned six estates for storage, including Neuschwanstein Castle in the Bavarian Alps, which housed items looted from France. Neuschwanstein’s secluded location made it an ideal repository for the ERR’s plundered treasures.

Charles Francis Shannon was born in Randalstown, County Antrim, he was the third child of Charles and Alice Shannon. As a child the family moved to his mother's hometown of Draperstown, County Londonderry where his father worked for the railway line. Growing up, both his family and locality influenced his interest in trade unionism, the Irish language, and Irish Republicanism. He was educated at Draperstown national school and St Columb's College, Derry. It was at Columb's that he studied history as a subject and developed an interest in journalism. Following the death of his father he moved to Belfast where he began to train for the civil service.

More than 78 percent of OCU faculty members hold terminal degrees in their fields. All classes are taught by professors, and not graduate assistants. Student to faculty ratio is 13:1 and the average class size is 16 for freshmen and 12 for upperclassmen. Notable faculty have included:

Raquel Torres Cerdán (born 19 November 1948 in Xalapa, Veracruz) is a Mexican anthropologist, chef, and restauranteur. Her father, Abel Torres García, worked as a waiter at Café La Parroquia in Xalapa before becoming its owner. Raquel’s interest in cuisine was sparked at 14 when she met Angel Obcejo, a chef at the restaurant who prepared dishes from around the world. Initially pursuing anthropology, she studied at the University of Veracruz, where she began exploring the cultural significance of food. This foundation led her to preserve and promote the indigenous and Afro-Mexican cuisines of Veracruz through her culinary work and research.

Geboers died on May 6, 2018 in a drowning accident on a lake in Mol, Belgium after jumping off a boat to save his pet dog. Geboers immediately had difficulty and failed to surface. Rescue crews recovered his body the next day. The dog, a recent gift from his wife, reportedly survived.

As of October, 2016, There are a 39 National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics (NAIA) schools that offer men’s and/or women’s varsity lacrosse, Lacrosse is an officially recognized sport by the NAIA. NAIA programs are currently in year two of NAIA invitational sport status. During this phase of recognition, teams compete to participate in a post season championship called the NAIA National Invitational. Each conference and A.I.I group receive one automatic qualifier with the remaining four bids going to the highest remaining teams from the NAIA national ranking. NAIA programs also regularly compete against NCAA DII and DIII teams. The Wolverine-Hoosier Athletic Conference (WHAC) announced on January 27, 2012, the addition of lacrosse for both men and women as conference sports effective the fall of 2012. The WHAC was the first conference in the NAIA to offer lacrosse as a conference championship sport. The Appalachian Athletics Conference and the Kansas Collegiate Athletic Conference recognized lacrosse as a conference sport in the Fall of 2015.

The Anglo-Saxon Chronicle records the town as Cippanhamme: this could refer to Cippa who had his Hamm, an enclosure in a river meadow. An alternative theory suggests that the name is derived from the Anglo-Saxon word ceap, meaning market. The name is recorded variously as Cippanhamm (878), Cepen (1042), Cheppeham (1155), Chippenham (1227), Shippenham (1319) and Chippyngham (1541). In John Speed's map of Wiltshire (1611), the name is spelt both "Chippenham" (for the hundred) and "Chipnam" (for the town). (There is another Chippenham, Cambridgeshire as well as Cippenham, Berkshire near Slough.) From Chapman's harbour. It might share toponomy with Copenhagen (København – "Market harbour". Older name: Køpmannæhafn, "Chapman's Harbour" ). In Swedish, Köpenhamn (pronounced "Shopenham"). In Norwegian, Kjøpenhavn (Pronounced "Shiopenhavn")

Research on sex differences in memory reveals that females generally outperform males in tasks involving episodic memory, particularly those requiring the recall of emotional, verbal, and face-related information. Women tend to access their memories more vividly and with greater emotional intensity, recalling richer details compared to men. This advantage is partly attributed to estradiol, a form of estrogen that plays a critical role in memory performance, brain circuitry reorganization, and protection against neurological damage. In contrast, men excel in visuospatial memory tasks, such as mentally rotating objects, tracking moving items, and recalling spatial layouts. During spatial navigation, men typically adopt an allocentric strategy, relying on Euclidean principles and global spatial cues, while women favor an egocentric, landmark-based approach. These differences in strategy and performance are influenced by both biological and cognitive factors, including hormonal variations and task demands. Additionally, men are more likely to recall information related to spatial and visuospatial contexts, whereas women demonstrate superior recognition memory, potentially due to their tendency to scan and process visual details more thoroughly. These findings highlight the complex interplay of biological, cognitive, and environmental factors in shaping sex differences in memory and navigation strategies.

During the cold season, extratropical cyclones known as nor’easters bring much of the wintry precipitation to the U.S. East Coast, particularly between December and March. These storms typically form along the coastline from Georgia to New Jersey before moving northward, often intensifying as they travel. While nor’easters can produce heavy snow in the Northeast, New York City and Philadelphia are largely shielded from lake-effect snow by their distance from the Great Lakes.
