The game features a simple control scheme: beyond basic movement controls, the player can pick up/throw a weapon, use their equipped weapon, strafe (walk backwards), quack, and ragdoll. Similar to fighting games, angled shots and some other maneuvers require precisely timed input combinations.

After the Spanish Civil War, the site of the Palacio del Marqués de Casa Riera was occupied by a building that housed the Secretaría General del Movimiento until 1977, with its facade on Calle de Alcalá prominently displaying a large yoke and arrows, the symbol of the regime. The garden was repurposed as a parking area. The current office building at Calle Alcalá No. 44, accessible also through the garden from Calle Marqués Casa Riera, features a simple, elegant, and modern structure made of stone, marble, and metals. Inside, it incorporates greenery and water elements as a continuation of the original gardens, including a contemporary fountain in the central courtyard.

The saying "nice guys finish last" is often attributed to baseball manager Leo Durocher, though he never originally said it in that form. On July 6, 1946, Durocher, referring to the 1946 New York Giants, remarked, "The nice guys are all over there, in seventh place." At the time, seventh place was next to last in the eight-team National League. Shortly afterward, the quote was altered to "nice guys finish last" in publications like *Baseball Digest*. This condensed version became widely popular and is now the commonly recognized form of the saying.

Takaji represented Lebanon internationally in futsal, playing at the 2007, 2008, 2010, and 2012 AFC Futsal Championships, captaining Lebanon in the last edition. With 13 goals, Takaji was the 2008 Arab Futsal Championship top scorer, and was awarded the Best Player award. He also helped Lebanon to a sixth-place finish at the 2010 Mediterranean Futsal Cup.

In 2014, Moose partnered with Pixel Zoo to create Shopkins animated content, including the webisodes. For the launch of Shopkins, a series of animated Webisodes were created for the ShopkinsWorld YouTube channel (now MooseTube Squad). There have been 85 released, along with 6 original music video clips, and has gained millions of views. WildBrain distributes Shopkins content on their channels (including the WildBrain - Cutie Cartoons channel) and, in 2016, has also been placed on Netflix amongst many other territorial stations globally. Moose also partnered with Universal to create 3 direct-to-video movies for global distribution. In 2019, Moose also released a spin-off line of Shopkins intended for preschoolers, Kindi Kids, which was supported by a CGI animated series. In 2021, to promote the toys for Season 5, the brand was supported by a new-look 2D animated series for the series' 4th season.

The only source of information about Linck is a court document dated 13 October 1721. It summarizes the testimony taken at the trial of Linck and her wife and reviews possible punishments for consideration by the monarch. Scholars of the period characterize her as a lesbian and place her in a social context in which women adopted male attire for a variety of reasons, including "to involve themselves sexually with another woman, either with or without the other woman's knowledge".

On 29 May 2012, the bodies of 13 unidentified men were discovered in the Assukar area, approximately 50 kilometers (31 miles) east of Deir ez-Zor in eastern Syria. According to opposition activists, the victims were electrical workers who had refused to end a strike in protest of ongoing massacres. A United Nations report described the men as having their hands tied behind their backs, with some appearing to have been shot in the head at close range. The head of the U.N. observer mission in Syria, Major General Robert Mood, condemned the killings as an "appalling and inexcusable act" and called for restraint from all sides to end the cycle of violence. On 5 June, a militant group claimed responsibility for the killings, which had been widely condemned by international observers. The incident highlighted the escalating brutality of the Syrian civil war and the targeting of civilians and workers in the conflict.

Acamas, a bay Thoroughbred with a distinctive white star, was bred in the United Kingdom by the renowned French entrepreneur and breeder Marcel Boussac. Sired by the legendary Mill Reef, who achieved notable victories in 1971 including the Derby Stakes, Eclipse Stakes, King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes, and Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe, Acamas was out of the mare Licata. Licata, a successful broodmare, produced both Acamas and Akiyda, the latter winning the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe. Acamas was sent into training in France under the guidance of François Mathet, continuing the legacy of Boussac’s celebrated breeding and racing operations.

In 1976 the legal case M.T. v J.T., 140 N.J. 77, 355 A.2d 204, 205 (NJ Super. Ct. 1976), which went to the New Jersey Superior Court, affirmed the validity of marriage of a trans woman to a man, and legally affirmed the plaintiffs gender as a woman. This is believed to be the first legal case which specifically addresses transsexual marriage.

Unlike the generally positive reviews received by Mulan, critical reception towards the character's romantic relationship with Li Shang has been largely negative, drawing much speculation from critics who accused Mulan of having "a typical girl-hooks-up-with-boy ending." Roger Ebert of the Chicago Sun-Times observed, "The message here is standard feminist empowerment: Defy the matchmaker, dress as a boy, and choose your own career. But Mulan has it both ways, since inevitably Mulan's heart goes pitty-pat over Shang, the handsome young captain she's assigned to serve under. The movie breaks with the tradition in which the male hero rescues the heroine, but is still totally sold on the Western idea of romantic love." The New York Times' Janet Maslin negatively opined, "For all of Mulan's courage and independence in rebelling against the matchmakers, this is still enough of a fairy tale to need Mr. Right."

The Mouassine Mosque (also known as the Al Ashraf Mosque) was built by the Saadian Sultan Moulay Abdallah al-Ghalib between 1562–63 and 1572-73. It is located in the Mouassine district and is part of a larger architectural complex which includes a library, hammam (public bathhouse), madrasa (school) and a triple arched fountain known as the Mouassine Fountain. The fountain, which provided locals with access to water, is one of the largest and most important in the city, decorated with geometric patterns and Arabic inscriptions. Along with Bab Doukkala Mosque further west, which was built around the same time and has a very similar format, the Mouassine Mosque appears to have been originally designed to anchor the development of new neighbourhoods after the relocation of the Jewish district from this area to the new mellah near the Kasbah.

As per the BPL household survey carried out in 2005, the proportion of BPL households in Mohammad Bazar CD Block was 35.3%, against 42.3% in Birbhum district. In six CD Blocks – Murarai II, Nalhati II, Rampurhat II, Rampurhat I, Suri II and Murarai I – the proportion of BPL families was more than 50%. In three CD Blocks – Rajnagar, Suri I and Labhpur – the proportion of BPL families was less than 30%. The other ten CD Blocks in Birbhum district were placed in between. According to the District Human Development Report, Birbhum, “Although there is no indication that the share of BPL households is more in blocks with higher share of agricultural labourer, there is a clear pattern that the share of BPL households is more in blocks with disadvantaged population in general and Muslim population in particular.” (The disadvantaged population includes SCs, STs and Muslims.)

The Tier System, used by U.S. military planners from approximately 2004 to 2011, classified unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) based on operational roles rather than specific models. It designated usage plans for reconnaissance, surveillance, and combat support. Each branch, including the U.S. Air Force, U.S. Marine Corps, and U.S. Army, developed its own tier system, which were not integrated. This decentralized approach reflected diverse operational needs rather than a unified framework across the military.

Jesse L. Lasky Jr. wrote eight novels, five plays, three books of poetry, and more than 50 screenplays, including eight for director Cecil B. DeMille. He won a Christopher Award and two Boxoffice Magazine Awards: in 1949 for *Samson and Delilah* and in 1956 for *The Ten Commandments*. Lasky co-wrote the script for *Hell and High Water* (1954) with Samuel Fuller. His prolific career spanned Hollywood and international locations, establishing him as a prominent figure in screenwriting and storytelling.

Horvat started playing handball at ORK Partizan from Bjelovar, where his first coach was Krunoslav "Kuna" Turković. Later he played for RK Zagreb and RK Dubrava, with short stints in Switzerland and Germany. He was a member of national teams of whole age categories under the Croatian Handball Federation, including several appearances for the senior team.

Some iOS 10.1.1 users reported that their devices were prone to unexpectedly shutting down at or around 30% battery charge (with one user describing the battery percentage as dropping unexpectedly from 30% to 1% before doing so, but still registering as 30% when plugging it in to charge it). Apple began the process of diagnosing this bug in iOS 10.2, and stated following the release of iOS 10.2.1 that it had reduced the occurrence of these shutdowns by "more than 80%" on iPhone 6S models and "over 70%" on iPhone 6 models. It also became possible to reboot the device after an unexpected shutdown without plugging it into power.

The initial four days of filming for *Time Teens* took place in 2013, but the resulting short film was deemed too brief to convey the full story. It was later expanded into a feature film, attracting a mix of professional and community actors who waived their fees. Filming occurred intermittently over 2013 and 2014, spanning 25 days across a year. Sponsors contributed £20,000 to the production budget. However, the release was delayed due to technical challenges and coordination issues, leaving the launch date indefinite.

The "Lockhart Papers" are a significant authority for the history of the Jacobites. Lockhart was the author of Memoirs of the Affairs of Scotland, dealing with the reign of Queen Anne up to the union with England, first published in 1714. These Memoirs, together with Lockhart's correspondence with the Pretender, and some other papers of minor importance, were published again in the two volumes of Lockhart Letters (1817), edited by Anthony Aufrere.

Jesús Julio Carnero, born in Aspariegos, Zamora, in 1964, spent much of his childhood in Corcos del Valle before moving to Valladolid. He earned his law degree from the University of Valladolid. Married since 1995, he served as President of the Provincial Council of Valladolid from 2012 to 2017 and has held various roles in Spanish politics as a member of the People's Party (PP).

On November 12, 2014, Crooked I announced his name change to Kxng Crooked during the release of the "Shady cXVpher." The name change coincided with the promotion of his upcoming mixtape, *Sex, Money and Hip-Hop*, described as his first studio album and a raw, unfiltered project. The mixtape, featuring the Slaughterhouse track "Total Slaughter," was set for release on December 16, 2014, through SMH Records, as reported by MTV News. The first single, "Freestylin' Under Oath," was released ahead of the album. While unrelated to the album, MTV News also highlighted H.E.R.'s "I Can't Breathe," a song inspired by the Black Lives Matter movement, emphasizing its social impact.

Following China's reform and opening up in 1979, increased wealth among the Han majority led to a surge in domestic travel, with affluent Han tourists favoring immersive experiences in ethnic minority regions. They visited areas to observe exotic rituals, often performed by groups like the Zhuang. Minority entrepreneurs began catering to this demand by organizing performances of traditional dances, songs, and rituals. Many learned these cultural practices from elders or local traditions, adapting them for tourist audiences. This economic demand for cultural displays provided new income opportunities but also commodified minority traditions, influencing how groups like the Zhuang preserved and presented their heritage. These performances became a blend of cultural preservation and adaptation to meet tourist expectations.

Khaled Takaji represented Lebanon in the AFC Futsal Championships in 2007 and 2008, captaining the team in the latter. He scored 13 goals in the 2008 Arab Futsal Championship, earning the Best Scorer award. Additionally, he helped Lebanon secure third place at the 2010 Mediterranean Futsal Cup.

The Labor Party avoided a split in Queensland due to the efforts of T. J. Ryan to minimise losses. Only one member of the state parliament, John Adamson, left the party and initially there was no attempt to create an alternate vehicle at the state level. However in October 1919, Adamson was part of the formation of a party for ex-Labor supporters that used the name. It had no electoral success and soon disappeared.

The Cape Ann Museum was founded in 1873 as the Cape Ann Scientific and Literary Association, with Dr. Sarah M. Davidson serving as its first president. In 1923, the Association moved to its permanent headquarters at the Captain Elias Davis House, located at 27 Pleasant Street in Gloucester. During this move, the organization acquired the Davis family collection and, in 1926, adopted the name Cape Ann Historical Association to better reflect its mission. Following the name change, the Association expanded its collections to include art, artifacts, and documents that illustrated the rich history and culture of Cape Ann. In 1937, Alfred Mansfield Brooks became curator and president of the Association, assembling the largest and finest collection of works by Fitz Henry Lane, a renowned 19th-century maritime painter. Under Brooks’ leadership, the museum’s focus on art and history deepened significantly. In 2007, the Board voted to rename the institution the Cape Ann Museum, with the tagline "Art | History | Culture," to more accurately represent its mission and commitment to preserving and presenting the traditions of Cape Ann. Today, the Museum continues to celebrate the region’s art, history, and cultural heritage.

Summoning's musical style has evolved significantly since their debut album, *Lugburz* (1995), which featured a raw, fast-paced black metal sound heavily influenced by the Austrian black metal scene, particularly Abigor. This album, notable for its use of live drums and complex melodic riffs, showcased a harsher, more traditional black metal approach. However, with the release of *Minas Morgul* later that same year, Summoning established the signature sound that would define their career. Moving away from the raw aggression of *Lugburz*, the band embraced a slower, more atmospheric style characterized by epic synthesizer layers, medieval-inspired melodies, and programmed drums drenched in reverb, creating a vast, otherworldly soundscape. Often described as atmospheric black metal, their music blends black metal elements with a cinematic, immersive quality. Summoning's lyrics and themes are deeply rooted in fantasy literature, drawing heavily from the works of J.R.R. Tolkien, with a focus on the darker aspects of his mythology, such as Mordor and Dol Guldur. While their early work featured live drumming, the band is now renowned for their use of programmed drums, which contribute to their unique, crawling, and expansive sound. This combination of literary inspiration and innovative musical elements has made Summoning a distinctive and influential force in the black metal genre.

In 1947, Bethel, Maine, became the site of pioneering work in organizational development led by Kurt Lewin, a visionary psychologist and social scientist. Lewin, along with colleagues from the National Training Laboratories (NTL), refined concepts such as group dynamics, T-groups, and behavioral science to enhance individual self-awareness and group functioning. Although Lewin passed away before the first summer session, his colleagues continued his legacy, conducting much of the research and training in Bethel’s remote and tranquil setting near the White Mountains. These summer sessions attracted scholars, researchers, and practitioners, fostering innovations in group training and development that significantly influenced the field of organizational development. The presence of NTL not only advanced behavioral science but also had a lasting economic and cultural impact on Bethel, contributing to its reputation as a hub for progressive community initiatives. In 2017, the National Training Laboratories moved its headquarters to Alexandria, Virginia, marking a new chapter in its history.
Anderson's wife is Vicky Anderson. They have 3 children. Anderson and his family live in Great Falls, Montana.

The project started in 1999 as a project for the development of learning content at the Bauhaus University in Weimar. After being used as the internal platform of the project the system was used for online courses at the university in 2002. Due to the interest of other educational organisations it was published under the GPL in 2004. The development of the open source software is managed in Weimar, Germany.

Keppel Sands State School is a government primary (Prep-6) school for boys and girls at 1325 Keppel Sands Road (23°19′39′′S 150°47′11′′E﻿ / ﻿23.3276°S 150.7863°E). In 2017, the school had an enrolment of 27 students with 3 teachers and 5 non-teaching staff (2 full-time equivalent). In 2018, the school had an enrolment of 28 students with 3 teachers and 5 non-teaching staff (2 full-time equivalent).

Andros Townsend was born on July 16, 1991, in Leytonstone, London, England. He is of Greek Cypriot descent through his maternal grandparents from Limassol and Jamaican descent through his father, Troy Townsend, a football coach. Raised in north London, Townsend attended the Capital City Academy, a sports college in Brent. At age 10, he tragically lost his half-brother Kurtis in a car accident.

In December 2018, it was reported that the airline had suffered heavy losses in recent years, especially during 2018 and that its owners were seeking potential buyers for the airline. On 31 January 2019, it was reported that payment of employee salaries would be delayed. Germania eventually ceased flight operations without previous notice in the night from 4 to 5 February 2019 and declared bankruptcy for itself and its technical and service divisions (excluding Germania Flug in Switzerland ). The final scheduled Germania flight to be in the air was ST 3711 from Fuerteventura to Nuremberg which landed on 5 February at 01:11 CET in the morning.

Rosen is the recipient of numerous awards in recognition of his contributions to the field of functional MRI, including the 2011 Outstanding Researcher award from the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA), and the Rigshospitalet's International KFJ Award 2011 from the University of Copenhagen/Rigshospitalet. He is a Fellow of the International Society for Magnetic Resonance in Medicine and an ISMRM Gold Medal winner for his contributions to the field of Functional MRI. In addition, he is a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering, a member of the Institute of Medicine of the National Academies and a member of the National Academy of Medicine. In 2017 Rosen was elected a Fellow of the National Academy of Inventors.

Her sculpture focuses on the female body in modular shapes. Common themes include love, loneliness and being a mother. First working in clay, metal and stone based on prehistoric art, she uses clear lines to emphasize the human form ́s eroticism. Later, she began using newspaper as an artistic material.

The game plays similarly to the Dynasty Warriors video game series. The premise of the game is to control characters from past Tales video games, and use them to fight large waves of enemies. In addition to using past game's protagonists, it is also possible to fight against past game's antagonists, and even unlock some to be played as in game.

"21 Grams" holds a 74% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes, based on 186 reviews, with an average rating of 7.1/10. The critical consensus praises Alejandro González Iñárritu's deft weaving of an uncommonly structured narrative with panache. On Metacritic, the film has a weighted average score of 70 out of 100, based on 41 critics' reviews, indicating generally favorable reviews.

Zentmyer was born in North Platte, Nebraska to Mary Elizabeth Strahorn and George Aubrey Zentmyer, Sr. While an undergraduate at University of California, Los Angeles Zentmyer was a sportswriter for the Bruin. He went on to graduate work at University of California, Berkeley. Both Zentmyer's master's and doctoral theses discussed the cytospora attacking the Italian cypress.

Gregory lived for a number of years in Deposit, New York. In 1832, he and his partners established the Deposit Bridge Company. He then moved to Cincinnati, Ohio and was in the railroad, lumber, and banking businesses. He helped former slaves in their flight to Canada. By 1844, he was the treasurer on the executive committee of the American Home Missionary Society. He was the president of the Cincinnati Young Men's Auxiliary of the American Bible Society in 1846. He moved to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, where he was a preacher. He established the Home Guards, local militia, in Philadelphia.

The 1948 and 1949 Kewanee A's played minor league home games at Northeast Park. The ballpark had a capacity of 2,500 in 1948 and 4,000 in 1949. Today, Northeast Park is still in use as a public park. The location is 1200 North Main Street, Kewanee, Illinois.

Traffic control for automated guided vehicles (AGVs) can be carried out through manual regulation, such as police officers or traffic signals, or automated systems. Local methods include dividing areas into sections (e.g., one-way or two-way zones), using sensors to detect obstacles, and integrating techniques like signage, lighting, and systematic traffic management for safe and efficient movement.

Trump pledged in his May 2016 speech on energy policy to "cancel the Paris climate agreement" adopted at the 2015 United Nations Climate Change Conference (in which 170 countries committed to reductions in carbon emissions). Trump pledged to cancel the agreement in his first hundred days in office. This pledge followed earlier comments by Trump, in which he said that as president, he would "at a minimum" seek to renegotiate the agreement and "at a maximum I may do something else." Trump characterizes the Paris Agreement "one-sided" and "bad for the United States," believing that the agreement is too favorable to China and other countries. In his May 2016 speech, Trump inaccurately said that the Paris Agreement "gives foreign bureaucrats control over how much energy we use on our land, in our country"; in fact, the Paris Agreement is based on voluntary government pledges, and no country controls the emissions-reduction plan of any other country.

Dorothy Cross received an honorary doctorate from Trinity College Dublin in December 2022. In 1990, she won a Pollock-Krasner Award in the United States. Her work has been supported by grants from the Arts Council of Ireland and the Arts Council of England.

Zika virus vaccine clinical trials are to be conducted and established. There are efforts being put toward advancing antiviral therapeutics against zika virus for swift control. Present day Zika virus treatment is symptomatic through antipyretics and analgesics. Currently there are no publications regarding viral drug screening. Nevertheless, therapeutics for this infection have been used.

Padri Jo Goth, home to over 70 families, was established on land purchased in 1825 by John and Elizabeth Whitehead. In 2004, the parish priest was Father Marc-Edy. The Sisters of Charity of Ottawa run a dispensary at St. Clare Convent, while the Franciscan Sisters of the Heart of Jesus manage St. Isidore Primary School, offering education up to grade 8. In 2011, 80 disadvantaged children were educated there, funded by the UK government’s pupil premium initiative.

The remains of the Castle of Balsamão are situated near the top of Mount Balsamão, approximately 4 kilometers from the seat of the parish. The mount, formerly known as Monte Carrascal, rises to an elevation of 522 meters and features a rugged terrain, with the castle partially built over rocky outcrops and adapting to the natural slope. The interior of the surviving high fortification walls is now covered with pine trees and vegetation. At the summit of Mount Balsamão stands the Convent of Balsamão, while along its flanks are the eight chapels of the Passion of Christ, forming a sanctuary. Near the castle walls lie the Chapel of Cajados and a cemetery. To the south, the hill is bordered by the Azibo River, a tributary of the Sabor, which is crossed by the Paradinha Bridge. To the north, the mount is supported by the Ribeira de Veiga, further enhancing its natural defensive position.

Moseley Braun was first elected to public office in 1978, when she was elected to the Illinois House of Representatives. She became the first African-American woman to serve as assistant majority leader in that body. As a state representative, she became recognized as a champion for liberal social causes. As early as 1984, she proposed a moratorium on the application in Illinois of the death penalty. In what became a landmark reapportionment case, Crosby v. State Board of Elections, she successfully sued her own party and the state of Illinois on behalf of African-American and Hispanic citizens. When she left the state legislature, her colleagues recognized her in a resolution as "the conscience of the House." In 1988, she was elected Cook County Recorder of Deeds, a post she held for four years.

The Penguins had an annual rivalry with the Akron Zips for possession of the Steel Tire, a trophy based on the prominent industries of each respective city: tires for Akron and steel for Youngstown. The rivalry began in 1940 and was played again in 1941, then was contested annually from 1959–1963 and 1967–1995. Akron moved to Division I-A (FBS) in 1987 and joined the Mid-American Conference in 1992. Since 1995, the series has been dormant. Youngstown State leads the overall series 19–14–2.

Carl Brumbaugh attended the University of Florida after high school and played for the Florida Gators football team from 1927 to 1929. As a member of the legendary 1928 Gators, Brumbaugh was part of the "Phantom Four" backfield alongside Clyde Crabtree, Royce Goodbread, and Rainey Cawthon. That season, the Gators led the nation in scoring with 336 points and finished with an 8-1 record. Their only loss came in a narrow 13-12 defeat to Tennessee. Brumbaugh alternated at halfback with Red Bethea and later became a "Gator Great" inductee in the University of Florida Athletic Hall of Fame, cementing his legacy as one of the program's early stars.

Melanie Susan Oxley is the daughter of Eric and Jan Oxley. Eric, an art teacher, was originally from Manchester and had migrated to Sydney before the Oxley family settled in the rural northern beach community of Kingscliff in 1965. Oxley's older brothers are Peter and Jeremy Oxley (born ca. 1961), who founded Australian pop-rock band The Sunnyboys, and her younger brothers are Damien and Tim (singer, guitarist, drummer in The Humdingers, The Verys, The Dearhunters, and Roger Loves Betty).

The adaptation of Vikram Seth's 1993 novel *A Suitable Boy* into a mini-television series was announced by BBC, with Charlotte Moore, then Director of Content at BBC, commissioning the project. The news was first disclosed by Aleph Book Company in an official press release on May 5, 2017. Renowned Welsh screenwriter Andrew Davies, known for his work on *War and Peace* (2016), was finalized to adapt the screenplay. The series, set in 1950s India, marked a significant milestone as BBC's first period drama with an entirely non-white cast. Piers Wenger, BBC's head of drama, described the adaptation as "a deliberate gamble" and praised its exploration of themes and settings deeply rooted in Indian culture, reflecting the novel's rich narrative and historical context.

Hop Wilson was born in Grapeland, Texas, on April 27, 1921 (though some sources list 1927). Influenced by Blind Lemon Jefferson, he learned guitar and harmonica as a child. At 18, he acquired his first steel guitar. After serving in the U.S. Army during World War II, he returned to Houston to pursue a career in music.

Manny Lagos joined the MetroStars midway through the inaugural MLS season in 1996 after MLS temporarily lifted regional restrictions on signing USISL players. He made an immediate impact, playing six games and recording two assists before suffering a devastating knee injury, tearing his ACL, MCL, and LCL in his left knee. Despite a determined comeback in 1997, Lagos played only 15 league matches and struggled to regain his previous form. Frustrated by his injuries, the MetroStars exposed him in the 1997 MLS Expansion Draft, where he was selected by the Chicago Fire. Lagos joined the Fire for their debut season in 1998, contributing to their success by playing 30 games and helping the team win the MLS Cup that year.

Barghouti claimed that at the annual Land Day protest in Bethlehem in 2012, he was struck in the head by a canister of tear gas shot by Israeli forces. "I was hit with a tear-gas bomb on the side of my head and my back," Dr. Barghouti told a reporter from his hospital bed. "My scalp is injured, my right ear has problems, and they are checking to see if I have any spinal injury." An Israeli Defense spokesperson, however, said that he had been attacked by fellow Palestinian protesters.

In 1955, John Coltrane was freelancing in Philadelphia while studying jazz theory with guitarist Dennis Sandole when he received a call from trumpeter Miles Davis. Davis, whose career had suffered in the late 1940s due to heroin addiction, was regaining momentum and assembling what would become the "First Great Quintet." This group included Coltrane on tenor saxophone, Red Garland on piano, Paul Chambers on bass, and Philly Joe Jones on drums. Coltrane played with the quintet from October 1955 to April 1957, a period during which Davis released several influential recordings that showcased Coltrane's developing talent. In 1956, the quintet recorded two marathon sessions for Prestige, resulting in the albums *Cookin'*, *Relaxin'*, *Workin'*, and *Steamin'*. However, the group disbanded in part due to Coltrane's struggles with heroin addiction. Despite this, the quintet's work during this period remains a landmark in jazz history, highlighting the early stages of Coltrane's transformative career.

The Tenpō calendar is a lunisolar calendar adopted in Japan in 1844, replacing earlier methods with the Teiki-hō system for dividing solar terms. It calculates solar terms and lunar phases based on the time in Kyoto. The lunar month begins on the day of the new moon, and a leap month is added when necessary to ensure alignment with solar terms. The leap month is designated as the previous month that would otherwise lack a solstice or equinox. The calendar fixes the Winter Solstice in the eleventh month, the Spring Equinox in the second month, the Summer Solstice in the fifth month, and the Autumn Equinox in the eighth month. This system ensures that the solstices and equinoxes occur in their designated months, though it becomes unworkable in certain years, such as 2033, due to the timing of lunar phases.

In a shared memory multiprocessor system with a separate cache memory for each processor, it is possible to have many copies of shared data: one copy in the main memory and one in the local cache of each processor that requested it. When one of the copies of data is changed, the other copies must reflect that change. Cache coherence is the discipline which ensures that the changes in the values of shared operands (data) are propagated throughout the system in a timely fashion.

The film received mixed reviews. New York Times film critic Janet Maslin, criticized the transition from book to film by saying, "But the plot, from a screenplay by David Ambrose based on Michael Mewshaw's book, turns out to be dizzyingly overcomplicated, and far too much of it hinges on the American journalist's supposed power to make trouble with his novel, which he says will be a "Day of the Jackal"-like mixture of real and fictitious characters. It is this journalist's advance knowledge of the plot to kidnap Aldo Moro, a former Italian Prime Minister, that makes so many waves."

On 6 August 2012, Syrian Prime Minister Riyad Farid Hijab defected to Jordan with his family, declaring his allegiance to the "freedom and dignity revolution" and denouncing the Assad regime as "killing and terrorist." Al Jazeera reported that three other ministers defected alongside him, though this was unconfirmed. Syrian state TV announced that Omar Ibrahim Ghalawanji, previously a deputy prime minister, was appointed to lead a temporary caretaker government. Hijab’s brothers, who worked in the Agriculture and Water Resources Ministries, also defected. Opposition sources revealed that the defections were planned for months in coordination with the Free Syrian Army. Hijab’s spokesman, Muhammad el-Etri, denied claims that Hijab was dismissed, stating the announcement followed his escape. The defections marked a significant blow to the Assad regime, as Hijab was the highest-ranking official to defect at the time.

In April 1993, Valacich et al. suggested that in light of new media, concurrency be included as an additional characteristic to determine a medium's richness. They define environmental concurrency to represent "the communication capacity of the environment to support distinct communication episodes, without detracting from any other episodes that may be occurring simultaneously between the same or different individuals." Furthermore, they explain that while this idea of concurrency could be applied to the media described in Daft and Lengel's original theories, new media provide a greater opportunity for concurrency than ever before.

Luca Scapuzzi joined Italian Serie B side Varese on loan from Manchester City on 8 January 2013, with the loan lasting until the end of the season. He debuted on 22 January 2013 as a substitute against Brescia. In August 2013, he signed on loan for Serie B side Siena.

John Dubois emigrated to America in 1791, landing in Norfolk, Virginia, with commendatory letters addressed to prominent figures, including James Monroe. These letters facilitated his warm reception and initial settlement. Dubois stayed as a guest in Monroe's home until he moved to Richmond, where he rented a house and opened a school. At this school, he taught subjects such as French and mathematics, contributing to the education of the local community. During his time in Richmond, Dubois took English lessons from Patrick Henry, a key figure in American history. Virginia, having passed the Statute for Religious Freedom in 1786, provided a more tolerant environment for religious practice, which likely influenced Dubois's missionary work. While in the capitol, Dubois befriended two priests, Francis Matignon and Stephen Badin, who shared his dedication to serving the Catholic Church in the United States. This period marked the beginning of Dubois's significant contributions to the Catholic Church in America, as he worked to establish a foundation for his ministry in a new and diverse cultural landscape.

In addition, the cost of CO2 emissions avoided through Ca-looping is lower than the cost of emissions avoided via an oxyfuel combustion process (~US$23.8/t). This can be explained by the fact that, despite the capital costs incurred in constructing the carbonator for Ca-looping, CO2 will not only be captured from the oxy-fired combustion, but also from the main combustor (before the carbonator). The oxygen required in the calciners is only 1/3 that required for an oxyfuel process, lowering air separation unit capital costs and operating costs. Sensitivity Analysis: Figure 3 shows how varying 8 separate parameters affects the cost/metric ton of CO2 captured through Ca-looping. It is evident that the dominant variables that affect cost are related to sorbent use, the Ca/C ratio and the CaO deactivation ratio. This is because the large sorbent quantities required dominate the economics of the capture process.

Correa lawrenceana var. genoensis is a shrub that typically grows to a height of up to 2 metres. Its ovate leaves range from 3 to 6 cm in length and up to 4 cm in width, with a fawn-tomentose texture on the underside. The flowers are usually solitary and axillary, borne on peduncles approximately 1 cm long. The calyx is cup-shaped, while the tubular corolla is yellow-green, varying in size. Flowering predominantly occurs in winter and spring, with sporadic blooms throughout the year.

Switzerland became a popular destination for winter vacations in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, attracting visitors from across Europe. Winter sports such as skiing, sledding, and bandy were played in cities like Geneva and other towns. Students from Oxford and Cambridge traveled to Switzerland to play bandy, with a notable match in 1885 in St. Moritz, which served as the predecessor to the Ice Hockey Varsity Match. The European Championship was also held in Switzerland in 1913, reflecting the country's prominence in winter sports.

The Deucha Pachami coal block in Birbhum Coalfield has been allotted jointly to West Bengal (share: 28%), Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Satluj Jal Vidyut Nigam. A new company Bengal Birbhum Coalfields Limited has been formed to mine the new block. The block now has estimated reserves of 2 billion tonnes, but inferred reserves could stretch up to 20 billion tonnes. According to newspaper reports, the block is spread across an area of 1,230 hectares with a 70–80 m thick basalt rock covering the coal seam and it would be difficult to mine the coal. However, the area where it is located is non-agricultural waste land.

In 2010, three years after the dissolution of its franchise in Israel, Burger King announced plans to re-enter the Israeli market. This move followed the acquisition of the chain by Brazilian private equity firm 3G Capital for $4 billion in late 2010. Under its new ownership, Burger King adopted an aggressive expansion strategy aimed at increasing its global market share and competing with rivals like McDonald's. Discussions for a new franchise owner in Israel involved Burger King's European arm, which oversees operations in the Middle East. If negotiations succeeded, the chain planned to open 12 restaurants within the first year of its return, with 15 more the following year. This re-entry attempt was part of 3G Capital's broader strategy to expand into markets where Burger King had no presence or had previously exited.

On 1 August 2014, Hangeland joined Crystal Palace on a free transfer, signing a one-year deal. On the 16th, he made his competitive debut for the club, on the opening day of the Premier League season away to Arsenal. Hangeland opened the scoring by heading in Jason Puncheon's corner, but Palace eventually lost 2–1. He scored twice in 17 appearances as Palace finished the season in 10th place under Alan Pardew.

Bruce Rosen, MD, PhD, has received numerous prestigious awards and recognitions for his contributions to the field of functional MRI. In 2011, he was honored with the Outstanding Researcher Award from the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA) and the Rigshospitalets International KFJ Prize from the University of Copenhagen/Rigshospitalet. He is a Fellow and Gold Medal recipient from the International Society for Magnetic Resonance in Medicine for his groundbreaking work in functional MRI. Dr. Rosen is also a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering and a member of the Institute of Medicine of the National Academies. Additionally, in 2021, he was elected a Fellow of the National Academy of Inventors for his innovative contributions to science and society.

The 2019 Uckfield town council elections were held alongside the elections for Wealden District Council on 2 May 2019 on new ward boundaries. 9 seats were up for election, as only 2 candidates for 3 seats were nominated in Uckfield North ward and 2 candidates for 3 seats in Uckfield Ridgwood ward, with incumbent Councillor Keith Everett having submitted his nomination papers, but passing away before the close of nominations. At the first meeting of the new Town Council, Karen Bedwell was co-opted as a Councillor for Ridgewood ward and Colin Snelgrove co-opted as a Councillor for North ward, filling all 15 seats on the council. Both Councillors Bedwell and Snelgrove subsequently joined the Trust Independent group. Diane Ward, who ran as an Independent councillor, also sits with the Trust Independent group.

Vicente Greco was born on February 3, 1888, in Buenos Aires, Argentina, to Italian immigrants Genaro and Victoria. He had several siblings, all passionate about learning and music. The family’s poor economic status required Vicente to leave elementary school and work as a newspaper seller in the streets. Despite these challenges, he began his musical career in 1908, playing the bandoneon and composing tangos. Initially unable to write music, he relied on fellow musicians to transcribe his compositions.

The first article about this species was published in 1875 by Edward Drinker Cope who wrote of the toad as a particular but unnamed variety of Bufo valliceps from eastern Costa Rica. It was described as a novel species in its own right in 1877 by Cope under the name B. melanochlorus. The holotype is a specimen (undesignated in Cope's original publication, but thought to be USNM30592) taken in Limón Province sometime before 1877 by W. W. Gabb. The specific epithet melanochlorus is compounded from the Ancient Greek μελανω (melanō), meaning "blackened" and χλωρός (khlōrós) meaning “(yellowish) green”. The English name invented by Frank and Ramus is thus a simple transliteration.

Witold's son donated his father's diary to the University of Bydgoszcz. The book was bought by Witold's father in 1910. Inside, one can discover articles, watercolours, sketches, thoughts of the librarian, but also mementoes from famous scholars he met: lines from Teofil Lenartowicz, Aleksander Brückner or Stanisław Przybyszewski, a photo of William Morfill while in Oxford in 1909, a sketch by Konstanty Laszczka, fragments of music scores by Feliks Nowowiejski, Ludomir Różycki, words of appreciation from Adam Grzymała-Siedlecki, a sketch of a poem by Józef Weyssenhoff.

Football was introduced to Turkey in the late 19th century by Englishmen living in Salonica, then part of the Ottoman Empire. The first league competition in the country, the Istanbul Football League, was established in the 1904–05 season. Over the following decades, regional leagues emerged, including those in Ankara (1922), Trabzon (1922), Adana (1924), Izmir (1924), Bursa (1924), and Eskisehir (1924). The first national competition, the Turkish Football Championship (Türkiye Futbol Şampiyonası), began in 1924 and continued until 1951. It followed a knockout format, featuring the winners of the top regional leagues. In 1937, the National Division (Millî Küme) was introduced as the first national league competition, bringing together the strongest clubs from the Ankara, Istanbul, and Izmir leagues. This competition lasted until 1950. The modern era of Turkish football began with the creation of the Millî Lig (now known as the Süper Lig) in 1959, marking the establishment of a unified national league.

Some programmes are in place to preserve or maintain species which are challenged in their natural environment by raising them in a protected environment. One such example in the United Kingdom is the freshwater pearl mussel (Margaritifera margaritifera) which can live for over 100 years, but have seen a loss of their habitat through changes in water quality and being fished for their pearls. The species was given protected status in the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981, and hunting, selling or buying the pearls has been illegal since 1998, hasn't stopped illegal activity in removing them from rivers. Another problem in the breeding cycle of the freshwater pearl mussel is that when the young are released by the female, they attach to the gills of passing salmon and trout (without causing any harm to the host fish) and as these fish are now rarer in some rivers, this too has had an effect on the survival of the species.

The river flows in the southwest area of Java with predominantly tropical monsoon climate (designated as Am in the Köppen-Geiger climate classification). The annual average temperature in the area is 23 °C. The warmest month is October when the average temperature is around 26 °C, and the coldest is January, at 18 °C. The average annual rainfall is 3834 mm. The wettest month is December, with an average of 578 mm rainfall, and the driest is September, with 35 mm rainfall.

Love Hina was adapted into a 24-episode anime television series by Xebec, a division of Production I.G. The series aired on TV Tokyo April 19 through September 27, 2000. The opening theme was Sakura Saku and the closing theme was Kimi Sae Ireba. Both songs were written by Ritsuko Okazaki and performed by Megumi Hayashibara. The two themes were released as a CD single, which debuted on the Oricon charts at Number 7. A bonus 25th episode was later created and released as a DVD bonus. The series and bonus episode were directed by Yoshiaki Iwasaki, written by Shō Aikawa and featured character designs by Makoto Uno.

As the crisis unfolded, attempts were made to negotiate the release of the hostages. According to declassified diplomatic documents, the Egyptian government under Sadat tried to negotiate with both the PLO and the Ugandan government. PLO chairman Yasser Arafat sent his political aide Hani al-Hassan to Uganda as a special envoy to negotiate with the hostage takers and with Amin. However, the PFLP-EO hijackers refused to see him.

In 1982, the Feinberg Realty and Construction Company purchased 1,850 square feet of land on 57th Street, which included the Little Carnegie Theater. The theater closed abruptly in mid-1982 following the acquisition. Feinberg hired the architectural firm Schuman Lichtenstein & Claman to design a 350,000-square-foot mixed-use tower on the site.

Much of the Oregon and California (O&C) Railroad land was rugged and unsuitable for farming, but its rich timber resources significantly increased its value, with timber companies willing to pay far more than the $2.50 per acre limit set by the land grant terms. Edward Harriman, president of the Southern Pacific Railroad, which controlled the O&C lands, hired Stephen A. Douglas Puter to circumvent these requirements. Puter recruited individuals, often from urban areas, to fraudulently register as "settlers" for 160-acre parcels. Once registered, the parcels were transferred to timber companies or speculators in violation of the grant's conditions, allowing large-scale accumulation of valuable timberland and bypassing the intended small-scale settlement.

Zharman is a shallow, hypersaline lake located in Kazakhstan. To the north lie Saryqopa Koli and Balyksor, while to the south is Big Saryoba. The lake features an irregular coastline, with sandy and saline shores and a flat, sediment-rich bottom. The meadows near its shores are utilized for grazing livestock, reflecting the region's agricultural practices.

Under the Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA), wages paid to a child under 18 by a sole proprietorship or a partnership where both parents are the only partners are exempt from FICA tax. However, this exemption does not apply to corporations or partnerships with non-parent partners. For domestic service, the exemption applies to children under 21 employed by a parent, but not in corporate settings.

AllMusic reviewer Alex Henderson praised *Lightnin'*, an album delivered by Lightnin' Hopkins in the 1960s when he was in his early fifties, for its raw and intimate session quality, likening the listening experience to sitting in a small Texas club with the blues legend himself. The Penguin Guide to Blues Recordings awarded the album three stars, noting that while it may not represent Hopkins at his absolute peak, it captures the essence of his unpolished, authentic style. This release exemplifies Hopkins' ability to blend relaxed yet passionate performances, showcasing his virtuosic guitar work and evocative storytelling that defined his enduring legacy.

Iraq holds the fifth-largest proven crude oil reserves globally, with 145 billion barrels as of 2023, though estimates vary between 112 and 300 billion barrels. By the end of 2017, Iraq exported approximately 4 million barrels per day out of a total production of 4.3 million barrels, making it the second-largest oil producer in OPEC and one of the top global oil exporters. Despite ongoing civil war, Iraq increased production significantly in the mid-2010s, becoming the second-leading contributor to global oil supply growth in 2015. However, production dipped by 2% in 2017 due to infrastructure constraints, export bottlenecks, and compliance with OPEC production cuts. Iraq’s oil industry has faced decades of challenges, including the Iran-Iraq War, the Gulf War, UN sanctions following the 1990 invasion of Kuwait, and the U.S.-led invasion in 2003, which severely damaged infrastructure. Despite this, the sector has shown remarkable recovery and remains central to Iraq’s economy.
Medary is located in Brookings County, approximately 4.5 miles south of Brookings. Its coordinates are 44°12′35′′N 96°47′11′′W﻿ / ﻿44.20972°N 96.78639°W. The Big Sioux River is located nearby.

Spiro earned dual bachelor's degrees in Political Science and Communication Arts from the University of Wisconsin–Madison. He then attended the University of Texas at Austin, where he received a master's degree in Film Production. His early work includes film adaptations of The Convict, a short story by James Lee Burke, and The Suicide Club, a story by Robert Louis Stevenson, which starred Jonathan Pryce and Paul Bettany.

Julian F. Walker retired in 1971 and moved to Devon, England. As a political officer in the Persian Gulf Residency during the 1950s and 1960s, he played a pivotal role in negotiating borders and fostering relationships with local leaders, including Sheikhs Zayed and Shakbout. Known for his deep understanding of the region, Walker personally met tribal leaders and sheikhs during border demarcations. In retirement, he pursued hobbies such as gardening and writing, reflecting his lifelong interest in the Arabian Gulf and its history.

The "Intelligence Management System" in *Voyage: Inspired by Jules Verne* is an ironic feature where the Selenites, the Moon's intelligent inhabitants, assign a "lunar IQ" score to the player. Actions like solving puzzles and interacting with the environment increase the score, leading to improved treatment by the Selenites. Alexis Lang, the game's Lead Designer, described this system in an interview with GamersInfo, emphasizing its humorous intent.

Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again was announced in May 2017, with Ol Parker writing and directing the sequel. The film was released in U.S. cinemas on July 20, 2018, and in the U.K. on July 27, 2018. On September 27, 2017, Benny Andersson confirmed that ABBA songs "Angel Eyes," "I Wonder (Departure)," and "When I Kissed the Teacher" would be featured, though "I Wonder (Departure)" was ultimately cut from the film. However, the song is included on the official soundtrack album.

Histamine is present in a variety of fermented products such as wine, aged cheeses, and sauerkraut. Red wine has 20–200% more histamine than white wine, and those who react to it may be deficient in the enzyme diamine oxidase. Experts believe that in some individuals, alcohol consumption may lead to elevated plasma histamine levels even in the absence of histamines in the beverage consumed. A study of 16 people with an intolerance to red wine found no difference in reactions to low and high histamine wines. Other biogenic amines may also have an effect.

Connecticut Raised Bill 1098 was proposed in response to the 2006 fraud case involving Rev. Michael Jude Fay, a Roman Catholic priest accused of embezzling $1.3 million from his parish, St. John Roman Catholic Church in Darien, to fund a lavish lifestyle. The bill explicitly targeted the Roman Catholic Church, proposing to shift parish administration from priests and bishops to boards of lay trustees. On August 19, 2024, a scheduled protest by 3,500 marchers, initially planned to oppose the bill, turned into a celebration after the bill was withdrawn, reflecting widespread opposition to its proposed governance changes.

In round 18 of the 2022 NRL season, Walker scored one try and kicked nine goals in the Sydney Roosters 54-26 victory over St. George Illawarra. In round 23, Walker kicked twelve goals from twelve attempts in the clubs 72-6 victory over the Wests Tigers. Walker played a total of 25 games for the club in the 2022 season and finished as their top point scorer with 211 points. Walker played for the Sydney Roosters in their elimination final loss to South Sydney.

Eagle Point Park in Dubuque, Iowa, was established following the recommendation of Charles Mulford Robinson, a prominent city planner and advocate of the City Beautiful Movement, who selected the clifftop site in 1907 and detailed his vision in the "Report on the Improvement of the City of Dubuque, Iowa." A citizens' committee, chaired by Judge Oliver Shiras, was formed in 1908 to acquire the property, and with support from the Civic Division of the Dubuque Women’s Club, 100 acres were purchased from A.L. Rhomberg and deeded to the city. The park officially opened to the public in May 1909. During the Great Depression, Eagle Point Park underwent significant expansion and renovation as part of the Works Progress Administration (WPA) program, with architect Alfred Caldwell overseeing the construction of numerous limestone structures, including pavilions, staircases, and retaining walls. In 1936, President Franklin D. Roosevelt visited the park and praised the WPA efforts, calling it a "worthwhile boondoggle." A streetcar turnaround and waiting shelter were built near the entrance, and the shelter, originally for bus passengers, is now used as a picnic area. The park remains a historic and scenic landmark overlooking the Mississippi River.

Norman Joseph became the 17th head football coach at Mississippi College in the 2005 season, leading the Choctaws to a 4-6 record in his first year. The team improved to 5-5 in 2006 and 8-2 in 2007. In 2008, the Choctaws were ranked 25th in the NCAA Division III preseason poll but lost their opening game to Millsaps. They finished the season 5-5. In 2009, Joseph guided the Choctaws to an 8-3 record, winning the American Southwest Conference championship and reaching the NCAA Division III Playoffs for the first time since 1991. Joseph stepped down as head coach following the 2013 season.

As of the census of 2000, there were 1,174 people, 426 households, and 322 families residing in the township. The population density was 34.2 inhabitants per square mile (13.2/km2). There were 588 housing units at an average density of 17.1 per square mile (6.6/km2). The racial makeup of the township was 93.70% White, 2.56% African American, 0.34% Native American, 0.26% Asian, 1.79% from other races, and 1.36% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 3.75% of the population.

Before industrialization, agriculture was a primary driver of land reclamation. In South China, farmers reclaimed paddy fields by enclosing areas with stone walls, particularly near river deltas and estuaries. These reclaimed lands are used for wet rice cultivation, benefiting from the region's mild climate. Additionally, enclosed areas often serve as fish ponds, a common practice in places like the Pearl River Delta. These reclaimed zones attract diverse wildlife, including waterfowl, shorebirds, and other species dependent on wetland habitats.

Willow Garage, a Silicon Valley tech incubator active from 2007 to 2013, played a pivotal role in advancing robotics through its Personal Robotics Program. After scrapping its initial projects, the company focused on developing the PR2 robot as a follow-up to the PR1. The PR2, designed for safe and modular mobile manipulation, was powered entirely by the Robot Operating System (ROS), an open-source framework also developed at Willow Garage. Contributions to ROS came from over 20 institutions, including Stanford University’s STAIR project, which helped establish ROS as a multi-robot platform from the outset. The PR2 and ROS aimed to create a standardized platform for personal robotics, enabling researchers to collaborate and innovate in autonomy, manipulation, and human-robot interaction, while fostering an open-source robotics ecosystem.

Mark Levine announced his candidacy for Manhattan Borough President in early 2021, earning endorsements from officials in the Bronx and Harlem. He defeated State Senator Brad Hoylman by over 7 percentage points in the Democratic primary and won the November 2021 general election against Louis Puliafito and Michael Lewyn.

Sinezona brevis is a species of minute sea snail with a white, solid shell reaching a height of approximately 0.8 mm. The shell is described as depressed and perforated, with a distinct umbilicus. Its sculpture features prominent, rounded, and elevated spiral ribs, with the interstices between the ribs containing fine axial threads. The protoconch consists of 1½ smooth whorls, while the teleoconch comprises three additional whorls that are convex and gradually increase in size. The aperture of the shell is rounded, complementing the overall compact and intricate structure of this micromollusk.

Jarvis began riding at the age of three and her parents purchased her a pony when she was four. In 2006, she competed in her first para-equestrian event and is classed as a Grade III rider. Her first international event was the 2007 FEI World Para Dressage Championships in England where she finished sixth and eight. At the 2008 Beijing Paralympics, she finished fourth in the Mixed Dressage – Championship grade III, seventh in the Mixed Dressage – Freestyle grade III and sixth in the Mixed Dressage Team Open. Jarvis won bronze medals in the individual test and freestyle test events at the 2010 World Equestrian Games at Lexington, Kentucky. She was unable to attend the 2012 London Paralympics and 2014 World Equestrian Games due to transportation and horse injury problems.

In April 1947, the Albany school board granted permission for a concert by Paul Robeson, primarily featuring Negro spirituals. However, Mayor Erastus Corning 2nd, then five years into his tenure, overrode the decision, citing concerns about Robeson's alleged association with the Communist Party. Corning stated that while Robeson was free to perform elsewhere, school buildings should not be used for controversial affairs. He suggested finding a private venue for the concert instead. Corning emphasized that his decision was not about censorship but about maintaining neutrality in public spaces, reflecting his broader stance against using public facilities for events that could provoke political or social discord.

Essert-Pittet had a population (as of 2015) of 164. As of 2008, 20.3% of the population are resident foreign nationals. Over the last 10 years (1999–2009 ) the population has changed at a rate of 11.9%. It has changed at a rate of 11% due to migration and at a rate of 2.5% due to births and deaths.

The West Midlands is the second most populous city-region of the United Kingdom after Greater London. The position of Mayor of the West Midlands was created in 2017 following a devolution agreement between the UK government and the West Midlands Combined Authority (WMCA). The Cities and Local Government Devolution Act 2016 required a directly elected metro mayor for combined authorities to receive additional powers from central government. Under the terms of the devolution deal, the first mayoral term was set to last until 2020, followed by elections every four years. In the inaugural election in 2017, the Conservative candidate Andy Street defeated Labour's Siôn Simon in the final round with 50.4% of the vote.

The sequel anime film of the *Given* television series was announced in 2020, adapting the manga's storyline focusing on adult band members Haruki, Akihiko, and Ugetsu. The cast reprised their roles, with Hikaru Yamaguchi returning as director. The film was developed under Fuji TV's Blue Lynx boys-love anime label, continuing the collaboration with Lerche studio and the creative team from the series.

In Russia bandy is known as hockey with a ball or simply Russian hockey. A similar game became popular among the Russian nobility in the early 1700s, with the imperial court of Peter the Great playing a predecessor of modern bandy on Saint Petersburg's frozen Neva river. Russians initially played this game using ordinary footwear with sticks made out of juniper wood, but it wasn't until later that ice skates were introduced. Bandy did not became popular among the masses throughout the Russian Empire until the second half of the 19th century. The predecessor of the current Russian Bandy Federation was founded in 1898. Bandy is considered a national sport in Russia and is the only discipline to have official support of the Russian Orthodox Church.

After the war, Whipple became an associate justice of the Superior Court of New Hampshire. On November 28, 1785, He suffered from a heart ailment and died after fainting from atop his horse while traveling his court circuit. He was buried in what is now the North Cemetery in Portsmouth, New Hampshire. His headstone was replaced with a new memorial in 1976 in conjunction with the United States Bicentennial.

Artifacts recovered from underwater sites require stabilization to manage water removal and conservation. This can be achieved by carefully drying the artifact or replacing the water with an inert medium, as demonstrated with the Mary Rose. Materials like metals and glass from salt water need stabilization due to salt absorption or metal leaching. In-situ conservation of underwater structures is possible but must account for the site's dynamic nature. Intrusive investigations or artifact removal can cause scouring, which exposes the site to further deterioration. Proper conservation methods are essential to preserve the integrity of these submerged cultural heritage sites.

In N,N-dialkylhydrazones the C=N bond can be hydrolysed, oxidised and reduced, the N–N bond can be reduced to the free amine. The carbon atom of the C=N bond can react with organometallic nucleophiles. The alpha-hydrogen atom is more acidic by 10 orders of magnitude compared to the ketone and therefore more nucleophilic. Deprotonation with for instance LDA gives an azaenolate which can be alkylated by alkyl halides. The hydrazines SAMP and RAMP function as chiral auxiliary.

Richard Mentor Johnson was born in the settlement of Beargrass on the Kentucky frontier (present-day Louisville) on October 17, 1780, the fifth of Robert and Jemima (Suggett) Johnson's 11 children, and the second of eight sons. His brothers John and Henry Johnson survived him. His parents married in 1770. Robert Johnson purchased land in what is now Kentucky, but was then part of Virginia, from Patrick Henry and from James Madison. He had worked as a surveyor and was able to pick out good land. His wife Jemima Suggett "came from a wealthy and politically connected family."

As military operations in Vietnam escalated in the mid-1960s, the demand for air refueling increased, with the 92nd Air Refueling Squadron actively supporting Operation Young Tiger starting in 1965. Their primary task was refueling combat aircraft, including B-52s during Operation Arc Light. After 1966, the 43rd Air Refueling Squadron joined them in Southeast Asia. The 92nd also supported bombing missions and maintained operations in the region until 1975, focusing on Southeast Asia's airspace to sustain U.S. combat efforts.

William Henry Seaman was born in New Berlin, Wisconsin Territory, on November 15, 1842. His father, William Seaman, had brought the family to the Wisconsin Territory from Buffalo, New York, the previous year. His mother, Arelisle (Crane) Seaman, was descended from some of the earliest Puritan settlers at the Massachusetts Bay Colony. His paternal grandfather, Williams Seaman, was a Democratic state senator in the New York State Legislature.

As of 2010, Boussens had an unemployment rate of 3.4%. As of 2008, there were 18 people employed in the primary economic sector and about 10 businesses involved in this sector. 8 people were employed in the secondary sector and there were 4 businesses in this sector. 150 people were employed in the tertiary sector, with 23 businesses in this sector. There were 396 residents of the municipality who were employed in some capacity, of which females made up 44.7% of the workforce.

Buffalo Bill State Park, located in Park County, Wyoming, is named after Colonel William F. "Buffalo Bill" Cody, a legendary Wild West showman, scout, and developer. Cody first came to the Shoshone Canyon area in the 1870s as a guide for a survey expedition and spent the next two decades leading hunting parties and promoting the region. He played a key role in agricultural development through irrigation projects and founded the town of Cody in 1896. Cody once owned land now part of the park, which he sold to the federal government for the construction of Buffalo Bill Dam, completed in 1910. The dam created Buffalo Bill Reservoir, a landmark of engineering and historical significance.

Recruiting international athletes has grown rapidly in recent years, with many German athletes outside Germany based in the U.S. NCAA institutions offer one of the only options for European athletes to combine academics and athletics. These students often arrive with high aspirations, driven by the belief that they control their destiny, despite facing challenges in adapting to a new system.

An important influence upon Hindu consciousness arises from the time of Islamic empires in India. Entering the 20th century, Hindus formed over 75% of the population and thus unsurprisingly the backbone and platform of the nationalist movement. Modern Hindu thinking desired to unite Hindu society across the boundaries of caste, linguistic groups and ethnicity. In 1925, K.B. Hedgewar founded the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh in Nagpur, Maharashtra, which grew into the largest civil organisation in the country, and the most potent, mainstream base of Hindu nationalism.

The All-Russian Cossack Society (Template:Lang-ru) is responsible for the coordination of the activities of all 11 registered Cossack hosts, particularly in the spheres of patriotic education and the continuity of historical Cossack customs and traditions. Both registered and non-registered Cossack organizations can be part of the society. On 4 November 2019, Russian President Vladimir Putin appointed Kuban Vice Governor and Kuban Cossack Host Ataman Nikolai Doluda as Ataman of the All-Russia Cossack Society. Cossack General Doluda was appointed two years after the atamans and the Cossacks created it in October 2017. The idea was first proposed in 1994. On 27 November 2018, delegates of the Constitutive Assembly voted for the establishment of the society and adopted its official statute. Doluda was then nominated for head of the society, in which he was backed by the Presidential Council on Cossack Affairs.

On February 28, 2012, Michigan and Arizona held their Republican presidential primaries, allocating 30 and 29 delegates, respectively. CNN moderated a debate in Mesa, Arizona, on February 22. Arizona used a winner-take-all method, while Michigan employed a hybrid model, splitting delegates between a proportional primary and a state convention. Mitt Romney narrowly won Michigan and decisively carried Arizona, solidifying his position in the Republican race.

The Hollywood Pop Up Comedy Club held its first stand-up comedy event in Beirut at an undisclosed venue, hosting shows there for several years before expanding to Saudi Arabia. During the first Jeddah Season in June 2019, the club brought U.S.-based comedians, including Eddie Griffin, who performed for one night in the Boulevard zone. Following this, the club produced the LOL Comedy Tour and hosted events in cities like Riyadh and Jeddah. At the 2019 Riyadh Season, Griffin headlined, marking a significant milestone in the club’s international expansion.

Bourbon from Watershed Distillery was first released in 2012 and was two years aged at the time. Current Watershed Bourbon available on shelves is three years aged and the distillery's goal is to eventually have a six-year aged bourbon. They utilize a five grain mash bill (corn, wheat, barley, rye and spelt) and age their bourbon in 53-gallon, char 4 barrels. Watershed Bourbon is bottled at 94 proof.

Acamarachi is known as an Inca mountain sanctuary, among at a minimum 15 other mountain sanctuaries known to have been used by the Inca. Other known sanctuaries are Cerro Colorado, Cerro Lailay, Cerro del Leon, Cerro Mullay, Cerro Toco, Cerro Quimal, Curiquinca, Juriques, Laguna Verde, Licancabur, Linzor, Paniri, Sairecabur, Tatio and Toconce. On its summit, four different ceremonial platforms are arranged in a cloverleaf conformation.

On 1 January 1935, Dr. Freja transferred to retirement. However, he did not enjoy a well-deserved rest for long. He died after a long and serious illness at the age of 59 in the general hospital in Prague from cirrhosis of the liver on 30 April 1937. His remains have been located in the scattered meadow at the Malvazinky Cemetery in Smíchov since the first half of the 1980s.

UnHerd was founded in 2017 by conservative British political activist Tim Montgomerie, who also acted as editor. Following Montgomerie's departure in September 2018, journalist Sally Chatterton, who previously wrote for The Daily Telegraph and The Independent, took over as editor. Freddie Sayers joined the magazine in 2019 as executive editor, having previously been editor-in-chief of YouGov and founder of the British news and current affairs website Politics Home. As of October 2022, the website lists 23 staff.

The fountain was a frequent target for vandals who would repeatedly break off fingers or hands from the sculptures. New hands were later carved and attached to the sculptures. In 1948, the fountain was temporarily removed when a streetcar underpass was built beneath Dupont Circle. When it was moved back to its original location two years later, the fountain's pumping system was replaced. When the new system was installed, workers forgot to connect the pipes to the fountain. The issue was corrected the following year and the fountain became operational.

Jean Bédard, a union advisor, is a perennial candidate for the Marxist-Leninist Party. He has run in multiple federal and provincial elections under the party's banner. During the 2003 election, *Le Soleil* reported being unable to obtain an interview with him.

Agriculture was a driver of land reclamation before industrialisation. In South China, farmers reclaimed paddy fields by enclosing an area with a stone wall on the sea shore near a river mouth or river delta. The species of rice that are grown on these grounds are more salt tolerant. Another use of such enclosed land is the creation of fish ponds. It is commonly seen on the Pearl River Delta and Hong Kong. These reclaimed areas also attract species of migrating birds.

The European Fuel Quality Directive permits up to 3% methanol by volume to be blended with gasoline, provided an equal amount of a higher molecular weight alcohol, such as butanol, is added. In China, methanol is used in blends ranging from M5 to M100, with annual consumption reaching significant levels. Modern gasoline vehicles can utilize alcohol fuels with minor adjustments, such as upgrading fuel system components to prevent corrosion. Alcohol fuels, including methanol, offer benefits like higher octane ratings, improving engine performance and efficiency, while supporting the transition to cleaner, more sustainable energy sources in transportation.

Children's entertainer Washboard Willy is a distinctive and engaging character. Washboard Willy has transformed into a Pied Piper of Rhythm for kids of all ages thanks to his benevolent spirit. Their collection of rhythmic toys inspires creative musical expression. He leads a procession and stroll through the fair, and performs on the stage in the kids' area. "In Washboard Willy's wake, smiles emerge."

The elected Malankara Association, consisting of parish members, manages the church's religious and social concerns. Formerly the Malankara Palli-yogam (മലങ്കര പള്ളി യോഗം; Malankara Parish Assembly, its modern form is believed to have been founded in 1873 as the Mulanthuruthy Synod, a gathering of parish representatives in Parumala. In 1876, the Malankara Association began.

The music video for "Beautiful Liar" was directed by Jake Nava and filmed over two days during the two-week production of the *B'Day Anthology Video Album*. Due to a tight schedule, the production team had limited time for choreography, leading to the dance sequences being choreographed spontaneously. Beyoncé and Shakira rehearsed the routines in just 40 minutes, with Shakira teaching Beyoncé some of the choreography, including belly dancing moves. Beyoncé also originated the concept of playing lookalikes in the video, inspired by an experience where she mistook two dancers for a reflection in a mirror. The music video premiered on MTV's *Total Request Live* on February 28, 2007.

Cossack family values as expressed in 21st century Russia are simple, rigid, and very traditional compared to those of contemporary Western culture. In theory, men build the home and provide an income, and women take care of the family and provide for the children and household. Traditional Russian values, culture and Orthodox Christianity form the bedrock of their beliefs.

Silverstein formed in February 2000 in Burlington, Ontario. Silverstein released two EPs, Summer's Stellar Gaze (2000) and When the Shadows Beam (2002) independently, then on October 22, 2002, it was announced that the band had signed to Victory Records. The band released four albums on Victory in their first ten years after forming: When Broken Is Easily Fixed (2003), Discovering The Waterfront (2005), Arrivals & Departures (2007), and A Shipwreck In The Sand (2009). Liam Cormier of Cancer Bats reprises his guest vocal role on "Vices".

The 4th cadet battalion’s honor guard of the Baku Higher Combined Arms Command School played a prominent ceremonial role, representing the Soviet Armed Forces in official events. Established in the late 1950s under the command of Major V.V. Sermiagov, the unit was one of only two ceremonial units in Baku during the late 1960s, alongside the honor guard of the Soviet Navy. The 4th battalion’s honor guard participated in military parades, wreath-laying ceremonies, and the reception of high-ranking officials, including Soviet leaders such as the Chairmen of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet, the Chairmen of the Council of Ministers, and the Minister of Defense. The unit also welcomed foreign dignitaries, including leaders from Turkey, Iran, and Egypt, at Binah Airport, where they performed meticulously rehearsed drills. The cadets, selected for their height, physique, and appearance, wore uniforms based on the traditional cadet gray, symbolizing the prestige of the Soviet military. Notably, the honor guard participated in the 1982 visit of Leonid Brezhnev to Baku, showcasing their precision and discipline. Their activities reflected the school’s commitment to upholding Soviet military traditions and fostering a sense of pride and professionalism among its cadets.

Universities have a certain level of autonomy and are allowed to receive funds from other sources, including private donations and from their research (e.g. patents) and other marketable pursuits, as long as this does not exceed the funding received from the Ministry. The ultimate responsibility for the management of the university also belongs to the Ministry, so universities cannot be independent from government control and the Minister for Education is able to take charge of all university activities if it deems necessary, including curriculum, research, policy, and the university management itself.

Unaegbu became an Executive Officer of the Lagos State Council of Tradesmen and Artisans in the Ministry of Commerce at Alausa, Ikeja. Based upon his experiences, he wrote his first book, This Lagos Na Wa (Ode on Lagos) and Other Poetic Portraits which was published in January 2006 by Prize Publishers. It is 700 lines long, with 668 lines in iambic pentameter, with rhyme scheme ABABCDCD. The book was later said to contain a poem that was reviewed as "The longest poem by a Nigerian" in Nigeria's Newswatch magazine on April 2, page 57, 2007. Another magazine, The Roar, declared that it is, "the longest metrical poem in Africa". In 2010, a new edition of the book of poems was published, with an audio CD of the poems. Its new title was Ode on Lagos (This Lagos Na Wa) and Other Poetic Portraits.

As of 2010, Boussens, Switzerland, had an unemployment rate of 3.2%. In 2008, 18 people were employed in the primary economic sector across 6 businesses, while the secondary sector employed 33 people in 7 businesses. The tertiary sector was the largest, employing 123 people in 27 businesses. Overall, 227 residents were employed, with females comprising 43.6% of the workforce. The local economy reflects a diverse structure, with significant contributions from agriculture, industry, and services.

The neural pathway of JAR in Gymnarchus is nearly identical to that of the Gymnotiformes, with a few minor differences. S-units in Gymnarchus are time coders, like the T-units in Gymnotiformes. O-units code the signal's intensity, like P-units in Gymnotiformes, but respond over a narrower range of intensities. In Gymnarchus, phase differences between EOD and stimulus are calculated in the electrosensory lateral line lobe rather than in the torus semicircularis.

On December 17, 2020, Feliz signed a minor league contract with the Philadelphia Phillies organization. He was assigned to the Triple-A Lehigh Valley IronPigs to begin the 2021 season, where he recorded a 2–1 record and 1.26 ERA in 15 games. On June 25, 2021, Feliz was selected to the active roster.
