On July 1, Feliz was designated for assignment by the Phillies after struggling to a 36.00 ERA across 2 appearances. On July 3, Feliz elected free agency.

Post-Euro-Canadian contact with the Copper Inuit was marked by both exploration and conflict. According to Robin McGrath, Inuit oral histories reveal a longstanding history of conflict between the Inuit and the Dene, often attributed to trade disputes or raids for women. One of the most infamous incidents was recorded by Samuel Hearne, who in 1771 became the first European to explore the Coppermine River region. During this expedition, Hearne witnessed a massacre at Bloody Falls, where Matonabbee, the leader of Hearne's Chipewyan Dene guides, and his companions killed over 20 Inuit. This event, which deeply traumatized Hearne, highlighted the violent tensions between the two groups. Further exploration of the Coppermine River region did not occur until 1820–1853, a period that included Sir John Franklin’s expeditions in 1821 and 1825. In the mid-19th century, John Rae, a Hudson's Bay Company surgeon and surveyor, encountered Copper Inuit at Rae River in 1847 and later at Cape Flinders and Stromness Bay in 1851. Rae’s interactions with the Inuit, along with his reliance on Indigenous knowledge, provided valuable insights into the Arctic and its peoples. These early encounters, though often fraught with violence and misunderstanding, marked the beginning of sustained Euro-Canadian contact with the Copper Inuit.

The Vietnam War introduced serious tensions into the general's family life. His son Dennis fled to India to escape service. Nevertheless, General Carroll donned his uniform to represent Dennis, after he returned from India, before the Selective Service board and succeeded in getting his son recognized as a conscientious objector. There were also serious disagreements with his son James, who was ordained to the Roman Catholic priesthood in 1969. Father and son first clashed over the Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.; the general suspected several of King's aides were Communists, whereas James admired King as a champion of the poor. James' anti-war activity was an embarrassment to his father. After leaving the priesthood, James became a journalist and author. Several of his novels, including Prince of Peace (1984) and Memorial Bridge (1991), bear traces of the general's fraught relations with his sons, albeit in fictionalized form. James Carroll's memoir, An American Requiem: God, My Father, and the War That Came Between Us, winner of the National Book Award in 1996, addressed his differences with his father directly.

The Hoosiers were ranked #8 when Nebraska arrived, and it was a tall order to hope for victory against an Indiana team that had risen over the past several years to be a formidable powerhouse. The Cornhuskers were outplayed in almost every aspect of the game and fell by a 40-point margin. The one ray of hope taken away from the contest by Nebraska was that they had scored more points on Indiana than any other team so far in the season. Once upon a time, Nebraska had held the series lead at 3–0–2, but the Hoosiers had since run off five straight wins to take the series lead back, and dealt Nebraska their third consecutive year of three losses to open the season. Indiana would go on to finish the season as Big 9 champions, undefeated at 9–0–1, and ranked 4th in the AP Poll.

The official state motto of West Virginia, "Montani Semper Liberi" (Latin for "Mountaineers Are Always Free"), was suggested by Joseph H. Diss Debar, the designer of the state's Great Seal. The motto was adopted in Article II, Section 2-7 of the West Virginia state constitution, signed in 1872. This article specifies that the seal, bearing the motto, is the Great Seal of the State of West Virginia and must be kept by the Secretary of State for official use. Diss Debar, a Swiss immigrant and supporter of West Virginia's creation, incorporated the motto into the seal, symbolizing the state's independent spirit and steadfastness.

The "Siege of Balfour" began in 2020 when Uri Nachman, unaffiliated with any political party, initiated protests outside Beit Aghion, the official residence of the Israeli Prime Minister on Balfour Street, Jerusalem. The site, symbolic of political power, became the focal point of demonstrations against Benjamin Netanyahu. Nachman returned on 10 June for a 24-hour siege, joined by veteran protester Amir Haskel, who organized a well-stocked protest tent and promoted a sit-down strike to sustain the movement. Initially, the public response was mixed, with some residents expressing frustration over disruptions, while others supported the protests, which highlighted dissatisfaction with Netanyahu's leadership amidst corruption allegations and the handling of the COVID-19 pandemic.

The Anglo-Saxon Chronicle records the town of Chippenham as *Cippanhamme*, which may refer to "Cippa's hamm," an enclosure in a river meadow belonging to a person named Cippa. An alternative theory suggests the name derives from the Anglo-Saxon word *ceap*, meaning "market." Over time, the name evolved through various forms, including *Cippanhamm* (878), *Cepen* (1042), *Cheppeham* (1155), *Chippenham* (1227), *Shippenham* (1319), and *Chippyngham* (1541). On John Speed's 1611 map of Wiltshire, the name appears as "Chippenham" for the hundred and "Chipnam" for the town itself. Other locations with similar names include Chippenham in Cambridgeshire, Chippenham in Monmouthshire, and Cippenham in Buckinghamshire. While there is no direct evidence, some have speculated a toponymic connection between Chippenham and Copenhagen, as both names may share roots in the Old English or Old Norse word for "market."

The Minot Public Schools system operates ten elementary schools (K–5) within the city: Bel Air, Edison, John Hoeven, Lewis and Clark, Longfellow, McKinley, Perkett, Roosevelt, Sunnyside, and Washington. Bell Elementary, located about five miles southeast of Minot, also serves the area. Jefferson Elementary closed in 2003, and the old Washington Elementary building closed in 2007, with students moving to a renovated facility in a former health care center. Additionally, there are two elementary schools (K–6) on the Minot Air Force Base: Dakota and North Plains. The 2011 flood caused the relocation of Erik Ramstad Middle School and the closure of Lincoln Elementary. Longfellow Elementary was expanded after the flood, and children from the Lincoln neighborhood attended Longfellow. Lincoln students initially attended classes at a local church before moving to Longfellow when its new addition was completed.

A total of 2 candidates registered with the Independent National Electoral Commission to contest in the election. SDP candidate Tajudeen Adefisoye won the election, defeating PDP Kayode Akinmade. Adefisoye received 53.28% of the votes, while Akinmade received 46.72%.

His rise to power as the Sultan was disliked by most of the population of Brunei and ignored royal Bruneian tradition. In an attempt to shift public opinions on him, he began to remove people who went up against him but it backfired and made him even more unpopular. The Second Civil War broke out after the population began to rebel in support of his nephew Pengiran Omar Ali Saifuddin in becoming the Sultan.

Security considerations in iris recognition focus on ensuring the reliability and integrity of the biometric identification process. The reliability of such systems depends on the quality of the acquired signal, which must be free from interference like glare, occlusion, or environmental factors. An important consideration is the need for live-tissue verification to prevent spoofing, as many commercially available iris-recognition systems can be fooled by high-quality images or synthetic replicas. This makes them unsuitable for unsupervised applications, such as financial transactions, where robust anti-spoofing measures are critical. In contrast, supervised applications, like controlled access to secure facilities, face fewer challenges with live-tissue verification. Additionally, the Fourth Amendment does not protect physical characteristics exposed to the public, such as the iris, raising privacy concerns. Ensuring state-of-the-art encryption and presentation attack detection is essential to mitigate risks, as no biometric system is 100% reliable due to the statistical nature of biometric algorithms.

Nikta Fakhri grew up in Tehran, Iran, and completed her undergraduate degree in physics at Sharif University of Technology. She then pursued her PhD at Rice University, where her doctoral research focused on the properties of single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWNTs) and their use as near-infrared fluorescent probes to track proteins within cells. Following her PhD, she joined the University of Göttingen as a Human Frontier Science Program postdoctoral fellow.

Marjorie Franklin, a pioneering psychoanalyst, developed planned environmental therapy to address the interplay between a patient’s environment and mental illness. She emphasized strengthening well-functioning aspects of the personality, "side-tracking" symptoms to foster growth. This approach, encouraging responsibility and broader development, is considered a forerunner of modern therapeutic community practices.

Referral networking organizations may encourage taglines to be used as the conclusion to an introduction by each attendee. The purpose would be to make the introduction and that speaker more memorable in the minds of the other attendees after the meeting is over. Other terms for taglines are "memory hooks" (used by BNI®) and "USP" or "Unique Selling Proposition" which is a more commonly known term.

Flexible manufacturing systems containing more than one AGV may require it to have traffic control so the AGV's will not run into one another. Traffic control can be carried out locally or by software running on a fixed computer elsewhere in the facility. Local methods include zone control, forward sensing control, and combination control. Each method has its advantages and disadvantages.

ISG15, a 15-kDa protein, was first identified in 1979 by Peter Lengyel and colleagues and was initially termed the "15-kDa protein induced by interferon (IFN)" due to its molecular weight. In 1987, James E. Darnell and colleagues renamed it "interferon-stimulated gene 15" (ISG15) after identifying its gene as transcriptionally driven by IFN-β. That same year, ISG15 was found to cross-react with anti-ubiquitin antibodies, revealing its structural similarity to ubiquitin. This led to the coining of the term "ISGylation" to describe its ubiquitin-like conjugation to cellular proteins. In subsequent decades, studies highlighted ISG15's critical antiviral activity against viruses such as influenza, herpes, and Sindbis, emphasizing its role in the IFN response. Additionally, ISG15 was discovered to exist extracellularly, secreted by human neutrophils, monocytes, and lymphocytes, despite lacking a signal peptide. It was also detected in human serum samples, suggesting a broader immunomodulatory role beyond its intracellular functions.

Select Registry, originally founded as the Independent Innkeepers' Association in 1972 by travel writer Norman Simpson, emerged from his exploration of North America in search of unique accommodations offering "good honest lodging, good honest food, and good honest feeling." Documented in his influential book *Country Inns and Back Roads*, Simpson's work laid the foundation for what is now known as Select Registry.

Fellowes served as a Brookings Institution fellow from April 2004 to April 2008. His academic research focused on consumer finance, particularly with regard to poverty, household debt, and retirement planning. In 2008, he left Brookings to found financial planning technology company HelloWallet with a $1 million grant from the Rockefeller Foundation. Following the acquisition of HelloWallet by Morningstar, Inc. in 2014, Fellowes served as Chief Innovation Officer at Morningstar. In 2016, with $5 million in seed funding from his own resources, Morningstar, and eBay founder Pierre Omidyar's Omidyar Network, Fellowes founded online financial planning and investment management company United Income. United Income was acquired by Capital One Financial in August 2019.

Ghate was born in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh. He started learning his art at an early age of three in Jabalpur. He then moved to Mumbai, and learned for over twelve years under Taalyogi Pandit Suresh Talwalkar.

At an early age Cai Jinbiao would be accepted in a specialist sports school within Xingning, Guangdong before he started his career playing for the Guangdong youth team where he would soon be promoted to the senior team in 1974. Despite his relatively short height for a defender he would go on to establish himself as an extremely effective defender and receive the nickname "Steel head" by the local fans. He would soon receive his first call-up to the Chinese national team by the manager Nian Weisi while back at his club Cai's free-kick skills and jumping ability would then go on to see Guangdong FC win their first ever Chinese league title at the end of the 1979 league season.

Sant Hirdaram Sahib, born as Ramchand on 21st September 1906 to Indumati and Shri Sahajram Ji in the village of Bhangun, Nawabshah district (Sindh), was a revered Sindhi saint and spiritual leader. A disciple of Baba Hariram, he was renamed Hirdaram by his guru. After migrating to India in 1948, he initially settled in Jodhpur, later moving to Ajmer and Pushkar, where his spiritual mission flourished. In 1965, he relocated to Bairagarh, Bhopal, which was renamed Sant Hirdaram Nagar in 1995 by the Madhya Pradesh government to honor his selfless service. Jeev Sewa Sansthan, inspired by his teachings, conducts numerous benevolent activities. His successor, Rev. Siddh Bhauji, a Bal Brahmachari like his guru, has devoted his life to fulfilling Sant Hirdaram Sahib’s mission of uplifting society through education, morality, and cultural values.

As of the census of 2010, there were 767 people, 280 households, and 213 families living in the village. The population density was 723.6 inhabitants per square mile (279.4/km2). There were 303 housing units at an average density of 285.8 per square mile (110.3/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 98.3% White, 0.5% African American, 0.1% Asian, 0.3% from other races, and 0.8% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 2.0% of the population.

Pope Pius VII, who reigned from 1800 to 1823, faced continual conflict with Napoleon Bonaparte, whose demands for control over the Church defined much of the pope's tenure. Napoleon sought to subordinate the Church to the French state, insisting that all ecclesiastical matters, including the appointment of bishops, be subject to his authority. Pius VII, however, resisted these demands, striving to maintain the Church’s independence and seeking the return of territories annexed by Napoleon, including parts of the Papal States. The pope also advocated for the release of the "Black Cardinals," a group of clergy exiled and stripped of their property for refusing to attend Napoleon’s 1810 marriage to Marie Louise of Austria. Their impoverishment symbolized the broader struggle between Napoleon’s ambitions and the Church’s autonomy.

Tim Pawlenty was elected to the Minnesota House of Representatives in 1992, representing District 38B in Dakota County. He was reelected four times and became House Majority Leader in 1999 after Republicans gained control of the House. Pawlenty authored bills focused on education, tax reform, and housing. During a state budget surplus, he advocated for tax cuts and fiscal restraint. His leadership and ability to navigate legislative priorities contributed to his selection as Majority Leader, marking a significant step in his political career.

The Acelhuate River, which crosses San Salvador, is heavily polluted with industrial waste, raw sewage, and solid trash, making it one of the most contaminated rivers in El Salvador. Its water, classified as a toxic mix of liquids and solids, cannot be treated effectively. The polluted water flows into Lake Ilopango, further exacerbating environmental degradation.

Pokémon Sun and Moon are role-playing video games with adventure elements, set in the fictional Alola region (loosely based on Hawaii), presented in a third-person, overhead perspective. The player controls a young trainer who goes on a quest to catch and train creatures known as Pokémon, and win battles against other trainers. By defeating opposing Pokémon in turn-based battles, the player's Pokémon gains experience, allowing them to level up and increase their battle statistics, learn new battle techniques, and in some cases, evolve into more powerful Pokémon. Players can capture wild Pokémon, found during random encounters, by weakening them in battle and catching them with Poké Balls, allowing them to be added to their party. Players are also able to battle and trade Pokémon with other human players using the Nintendo 3DS' connectivity features. As with previous games in the series, certain Pokémon are only obtainable in either Sun or Moon, with players encouraged to trade with others in order to obtain all Pokémon from both versions.

According to the 2010 census, Lugoff, South Carolina, had a population of 7,434. The population density was approximately 326.3 people per square mile. The racial composition was 69.8% White, 23.3% African American, 0.7% Asian, 0.0% Pacific Islander, 3.9% Hispanic or Latino of any race, 2.3% from two or more races, and a small percentage Native American. There were 2,800 households and 2,100 families residing in the area. Lugoff had 3,000 housing units, with a vacancy rate of approximately 6.7%. The community was predominantly rural, reflecting its location within Kershaw County and its proximity to Camden and the Wateree River.

As with most other biometric identification technology, an important consideration is live-tissue verification. The reliability of any biometric identification depends on ensuring that the signal acquired and compared has actually been recorded from a live body part of the person to be identified and is not a manufactured template. Besides a person's physical characteristics, which includes the eyes, one's voice and handwriting too, are not protected by the Fourth Amendment even though they are all constantly exposed. Many commercially available iris-recognition systems are easily fooled by presenting a high-quality photograph of a face instead of a real face, which makes such devices unsuitable for unsupervised applications, such as door access-control systems. However, this is not the case with all iris recognition algorithms. The problem of live-tissue verification is less of a concern in supervised applications (e.g., immigration control), where a human operator supervises the process of taking the picture.

Peter was born and raised in Alexandria. The Coptic Orthodox Church believes that Peter was given by his parents to Theonas to be brought up as a priest, similarly to the story of Samuel in the Old Testament. He rose through the ranks of holy orders, first becoming a reader, then a deacon, then a priest. Highly educated, Peter became head of the school of Alexandria.

The Richmond Flying Squirrels unveiled their logo in 2010, featuring a sleek, angular flying squirrel in mid-flight. Designed by Brandiose, the logo incorporates Radiant Red, Squirrels Silver, traditional Black, and White. At its center is an acorn-shaped patch with the letter "R." Ballpark Digest named it the Logo of the Year in 2010, and Sports Illustrated recognized it as the best minor-league logo in 2015, highlighting its dynamic and superhero-like design.

Phelps's business losses were substantial in the great fire of May 1851, but Phelps rebuilt and soon recovered his loss. It was during this period of recovery that he purchased 3,500 acres (1,400 ha) of land in what is now San Mateo County, California. He used the land for farming and soon decided to make the area, now the city of San Carlos, his home.

Karina Chenelle LeBlanc was born in Atlanta, Georgia, to Vans LeBlanc, from Dominica, and Winsome LeBlanc, from Jamaica. Her parents were temporarily stationed in Atlanta for three weeks due to a hurricane in the Caribbean. She grew up in Roseau, Dominica, until the age of eight, when her family immigrated to Maple Ridge, British Columbia, Canada. LeBlanc began playing soccer at 12 and was later named one of the top 20 Division I college recruits in 1997, despite her high school lacking a girls' soccer team. That same year, she also earned all-star honors in basketball, showcasing her athletic versatility.

Matt Fellowes began his career as a fellow at the Brookings Institution in 2004, focusing on the finances of lower-income families. In 2008, he founded HelloWallet, initially funded by his own savings and a grant from the Rockefeller Foundation. Morningstar, Inc., an early investor, acquired HelloWallet in 2014 for $52.5 million, where Fellowes joined as an executive. In 2017, he founded United Income, a wealth management platform backed by seed funding from Morningstar and eBay founder Pierre Omidyar. United Income was acquired by Capital One in 2019, with Fellowes continuing to lead the company as it operated independently under its new ownership.

Coming into his freshman season, Allen was selected to the Preseason All-Atlantic 10 Rookie Team. He was seen as an answer for Temple's rebounding and interior scoring struggles from the previous 12–18 season. In his first college basketball game, Allen scored eight points and grabbed nine rebounds in a loss to #7 Tennessee. From November 18 through December 9, 2007, he scored in double figures in six consecutive games. On February 10, 2008, Allen's season-high 20 points led Temple to an 80–70 overtime victory over the UMass Minutemen. Allen was benched in the February 17 game against Dayton due to missing a study assignment.

The Battle of Chelsea Creek, fought on May 27, 1775, saw the involvement of HMS Somerset, whose tender, Britannia, played a key role. During the battle, the armed schooner HMS Diana, commanded by Lieutenant Thomas Graves, was tasked with cutting off the American retreat but ran aground in Chelsea Creek. American rebels set the Diana ablaze, marking the first capture of a British warship by colonial forces. Britannia, under the command of Lieutenant John Graves, rescued Diana's crew amidst heavy fire. This engagement marked a significant early victory for the American forces in the Revolutionary War.

Color is a visual characteristic that is described by terms like red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple, etc. Typically, it is the color of an object that attracts the most attention. Color is one of the primary properties that is noticed when a consumer makes a decision to buy a dress. The colors are distinctive and distinguishable; we frequently refer to clothing by its color, for instance, a "blue shirt."

In 1969, Avalon Hill dominated the wargame market, producing on average, one game per year with well-produced but expensive components. At the newly founded wargame publisher SPI, Jim Dunnigan and his design team decided to go in the opposite direction, marketing a number of very cheaply made "test games" to prove that producing many games a year could also be a viable business model. These test games featured typewritten pages with hand-drawn maps and graphics and thin paper counter sheets, packaged in a plain envelope. Korea, designed by Dunnigan, was the twelfth of these games, published in 1969.

Piła, whose name derives from the Polish word for "saw," referencing its historical wood-sawing industry, saw German colonization in the 13th century following the devastation of the First Mongol invasion, which ended with the Battle of Legnica in 1241. Under King Casimir IV Jagiellon (1447–1492), Polish immigration policies shifted as he defeated the Teutonic Order in 1466. Located in Poland’s flat, river-laden central plains, Piła’s geography shaped its development and historical significance.

In the 2022 NRL season, Sam Walker played 25 games for the Sydney Roosters, scoring 202 points to finish as the club's top point scorer. In round 18, he scored one try and kicked nine goals in a 54-26 win over the St George Illawarra Dragons. In round 23, he kicked 12 goals in a 72-6 demolition of the Wests Tigers. The Roosters' season ended with a 30-14 elimination final loss to the South Sydney Rabbitohs.

In the wake of Labour's second defeat at the Scottish Parliament election of May 2011, which saw the Scottish National Party (SNP) form its first majority government, Gray announced his intention to step down as leader of the Labour MSPs later that year. A review chaired by Jim Murphy and Sarah Boyack into the party's structure in Scotland subsequently concluded that the next leader should lead the entire Scottish Labour Party rather than just its MSPs, as previous leaders had. Lamont declared her candidacy for the leadership election in September 2011 and launched her campaign on 7 November at Stirling University. She told delegates that Labour needed to reengage with the electorate if it wanted to govern again: "We must listen and learn, show humility and seek again to talk for and to people's ambitions and concerns. Our real challenge is that we in Labour lost our way, lost our confidence and lost Scotland."

The Paris Air Show-2019, held from June 17 to June 23 in Le Bourget, France, was organized under the patronage of the French Aerospace Industries Association. During the event, Farrukh Vezirov, Counsellor to the Minister of Defence Industry of Azerbaijan, met with Thomas Lorne, Head of International Cooperation at France's Ministry of Defence, to discuss establishing military-technical cooperation between the two countries.

Prior to 2007, PA 830 ran further west into Washington and Pine Creek Townships. The route began at US 322 near Emerickville. It had an interchange with I-80 at Exit 86 and traveled a winding route heading northeast. At Red Barn Road, it made a right turn and passed the old entrance to the DuBois Regional Airport. It made a sharp right curve at Allens Mills Road where the present routing continues today. The route between US 322 and Red Barn Road is still state-maintained as SR 1830 and the section of Red Barn Road between SR 1830 and the modern-day PA 830 is SR 1013. Parts of the old PA 830 are still drivable between the PA 830 / SR 1830 intersection and Allens Mills Road, but it is not completely passable due to breaks in the pavement and the new parking lot for the airport.

Harmanus Bleecker maintained numerous friendships and professional relationships, many of which transcended political leanings. For many years he practiced law with Theodore Sedgwick (1780–1839), the son of Federalist legislator and judge Theodore Sedgwick (1746–1813), and he was once engaged to Judge Sedgwick's daughter Catharine Sedgwick. He was also a close friend of Federalist Congressman and Boston Mayor Josiah Quincy (1772–1864).

Co-written by all members of The Birthday Party, *The Bad Seed* E.P. forecasted Nick Cave's future as a master of gothic storytelling, with Mick Harvey expected to remain a pivotal collaborator. Rowland S. Howard's guitar work was particularly praised, especially on "Deep in the Woods," which ends on a perfectly outrageous note. In this track, Cave is described as a menacing figure, conjuring a scene of ritual murder in an isolated forest. Its delivery and lyrics redefine the macabre, setting the stage for Cave's later work with The Bad Seeds.

The Srebrenica massacre occurred during the Bosnian War, with over 8,000 Bosniak men and boys killed by Bosnian Serb forces over at least 72 hours beginning on July 13, 1995. The International Criminal Tribunal for the Former Yugoslavia (ICTY) later recognized it as an act of genocide. In 2015, the United Kingdom sponsored a UN resolution to commemorate the 20th anniversary and label the Serbian people genocidal, prompting Serbian President Tomislav Nikolić to request Russian intervention. On July 8, 2015, Vitaly Churkin, representing Russia, vetoed the resolution, citing its perceived political motivations, making Russia the sole Security Council member to oppose it.

The treatment of performers involved in Gulag theater varied widely over time and among the different camps. Solovetsky's earliest theater group was completely voluntary and self-organized, and the actors (all amateurs) were afforded no special privileges. Like other prisoners, they put in full workdays clearing forests, and they had to schedule rehearsals during the few hours they had free. The musicians who comprised the band in Kazimierz Zarod's camp were also treated like non-performers: after the prisoners had passed through the gates on their way to work in the forest, the musicians simply put down their instruments and joined the march at the end of the line.

In 2021, Khalil Iverson competed in The Basketball Tournament (TBT) with Kohl Blooded, a team composed primarily of Wisconsin alumni. Despite being the No. 2 seed in the Columbus region, Kohl Blooded was upset in the first round by No. 7 seed Mid-American Unity, with Iverson scoring 17 points. In 2019, Iverson participated in TBT's Dunk Contest, finishing second behind William Coleman and earning $10,000. His dunk over Zach Jones garnered over 1.2 million views on Bleacher Report's Twitter.

Since 1998 the "Grodzka Gate" has been implementing the project «Historia Mówiona» (Template:Lang-en). It is based on collecting of audio and video recorded memories of witnesses of various historical events. Pursuant to oral history guidelines, the "Grodzka Gate" collected memories of almost 2,000 people and recordings of more than 3,000 hours of audio and video materials. The main topics of the interviews are mundane life, education, intercultural reciprocity, World War II, The Holocaust, Polish-Jewish relations during the war and opposition and underground publishing during the years of The Polish People's Republic. Gathered materials constitute the largest collection of this kind in Poland, which is available in the Internet. Every file fulfills the requirements of The Dublin Core Metadata Initiative and is stored in the system dLibra.

An ITV documentary, Exposure: The Other Side of Jimmy Savile, researched and presented by former police detective Mark Williams-Thomas, was broadcast on 3 October 2012, almost a year after Savile's death. The programme contained several allegations by women who said that, as teenagers, they had been sexually abused by radio and television personality Jimmy Savile, who had gained access to them through the television programmes he had presented and his charity work. Following the broadcast, many other people came forward to make allegations about Savile's conduct towards young people, including sexual abuse that had taken place on BBC premises and in hospitals to which Savile had access.

P. radiata is widely used in private gardens and public landscapes in temperate California, and similar climates around the world. It is particularly commonly grown as a landscape tree in coastal areas of California outside of its native range, where the climate is virtually identical to its native range. It is fast-growing and adaptable to a broad range of soil types and climates, though it does not tolerate temperatures below about −15 °C (5 °F). Its fast growth makes it ideal for landscapes and forestry; in a good situation, P. radiata can reach its full height in 40 years or so. Though a combination of biotic and abiotic factors determines the natural distribution of P. radiata, humans have broadly expanded its distribution up and down the California coast.

Adler-Milstein was born and raised in San Francisco and also spent much time growing up at a family home in Sonoma, California. She earned a Bachelor of Science degree from Stanford University and a PhD from Harvard University. Before enrolling at Harvard, Adler-Milstein worked at the Center for IT Leadership and in the Health and Life Sciences Division of Accenture at Partners Healthcare.

Beyoncé was backed onstage by her 10-member all-female band and a group of 10 dancers during the performances. The stage featured a dynamic setup with "a seemingly endless number of screens and levels," as noted by Jim Farber of the *Daily News*. LED screens displayed bold, abstract visuals, including grainy, Super 8-style videos and interstitial clips of dancers performing to fragmented melodies. The light shows, described by Ben Ratliff of *The New York Times* as "modest," complemented the visuals. The graphics, featuring modern and striking designs, were described by Farber as having a "soft revolution" aesthetic. Together, these elements created a visually immersive experience that enhanced the high-energy performances.

As of December 2020, Gland had a population of 13,507. In 2008, 32.8% of its residents were foreign nationals. Between 1999 and 2009, the population grew by 21.4%, with 75% of the increase due to migration and 25% due to births and deaths.

A novel belonging to The Golden Age of the Tagalog Novel (1905-1921), the language used by Iñigo Ed Regalado was described by Roberto T. Añonuevo as a Tagalog-language that challenges the reader to study the language and the history of the Philippines, particularly in Tagalog literature. Regalado wrote the novel when adultery was a sensitive topic in Philippine literature, thus he used language that was not obvious or vulgar, although may still be considered by Philippine society of the time as "controversial and dense in content". Regalado tamed his language by using words and descriptions that were metaphorical, parable-like, and allegorical, particularly those that are pertaining to women.

On June 26, 2009, General Electric CEO Jeff Immelt announced plans for a new Advanced Manufacturing and Software Technology Center to be located in Van Buren Township in the heart of the Detroit Region Aerotropolis. The facility was expected to bring approximately to 1,200 jobs to the region, and was constructed in Visteon Village home to the Visteon Corporation, ironically in Chapter 11 at the time of the announcement. GE's plans included leasing current office space as well as construction of a new $100 million, 100,000-square-foot (9,300 m2) research and development facility to house GE engineers who will focus on advanced manufacturing for alternative energy as well as aircraft engine and gas turbine technologies. The software center will develop applications to support GE business systems and advanced technology groups. It will also be a training hub for GE IT personnel from around the world.

The village of Wissahickon was founded by officials of the Pencoyd Iron Works in the late nineteenth century. Beginning in the 1880s, growing numbers of mill owners and wealthy business owners from neighboring Manayunk sought elegant homes on ample lots; they set their eyes on land previously owned by prominent Philadelphia families – including the Camac, Dobson, Salaignac, and Wetherill families – along the Wissahickon Creek. Grand single-detached and semi-detached homes with ample side and rear yards were built, creating "a unique park side Victorian wonderland." The neighborhood was considered a highly desirable place to live as it had proximity to the Wissahickon Valley, tree-lined streets, new sewers and water services, and grand homes in high Victorian styling.

Nicole Bilderback was born in South Korea and raised in Tulsa, Oklahoma, and Dallas, Texas. She was adopted through Holt International Children's Services by Lois and Jim Bilderback and has two older brothers. In junior high school, she was a cheerleader and studied dance from an early age. At 14, she enrolled at the Dallas Young Actor's Studio to pursue acting.

Sora, the protagonist of *Kingdom Hearts*, was designed by Tetsuya Nomura, who envisioned him as an original character to bridge the collaboration between Disney and Square. During early development, Disney proposed Donald Duck as the main character, while Square wanted Mickey Mouse. Nomura rejected both ideas, opting for a unique protagonist. Sora’s design incorporated elements of Mickey Mouse, such as his red shorts and oversized shoes, to reflect Disney’s influence. Initially, Sora was conceived as a lion-like character with a tail, resembling Zidane from *Final Fantasy IX*, but this was removed to avoid overlap. His original weapon was a chainsaw-like blade, which was redesigned into the iconic Keyblade to better suit the game’s tone and appeal to younger audiences. Final Fantasy characters were included in the first game to support Sora, as he was a new character at the time, and to help establish the series by connecting it to Square’s existing fanbase.

In May 1841, while visiting Moscow, Mikhail Lermontov composed eight poetic invectives aimed at Alexander Benckendorff. On May 9, he arrived in Stavropol, where he introduced himself to General Grabbe and requested permission to remain in the town. However, he soon changed his course and traveled to Pyatigorsk, informing his superiors that he had fallen ill. The regiment's special commission subsequently recommended treatment at Mineralnye Vody. During this time, Lermontov confided in a friend on July 8, 1841, that he felt he had very little time left to live. This foreboding sentiment would tragically prove true later that month.

Publicists are usually skilled writers, as well as motivated to promote individuals. Furthermore, publicists need to be able to handle the stress associated with crisis. For example, if a client is arrested for a DUI, they need to release press coverage with details explaining how the client will resolve the situation. In terms of education, publicists will often major in communications, journalism, or public relations in college. When starting out as a publicist, one will have to work their way up in regards to position. Usually, it is helpful that aspiring publicists intern at a public relations firm while at college to gain experience. Schools that offer communication and public relations degrees consist of Ashford University, Colorado Technical University, Seton Hall University, American University, and the University of Florida.

In 1816, during the reign of King Ferdinand VII of Spain, the citizens of San Cristóbal de Alvarado sought and were granted the title of Town (Villa) through a Royal Decree issued on September 8. The decree cost 92,800 maravedíes. Two years later, on September 5, 1818, Ferdinand VII authorized the establishment of the first Honorable Ayuntamiento (City Council), presided over by Tomás del Hondal, who became the first Mayor of San Cristóbal de Alvarado.

The Military-Technical Institute of the National Guard serves as the main military academy for national guard troops. The Tashkent Higher Military Technical School of the Ministry of Internal Affairs was established on the basis of the Higher Courses of the Ministry of Internal Affairs (military unit 5375). On January 10, 1992, it was transferred to the jurisdiction of the system of the Ministry of Internal Affairs. The university first accepted cadets in 1991 and in 1995 prepared the first graduates. In 2017, it was transferred to the National Guard.

The F4F-4 Wildcat entered service in 1941 and became the definitive version of the aircraft, seeing the most combat service in the early years of World War II, including the Battle of Midway. It was equipped with six 0.50 caliber machine guns and featured the Grumman-patented Sto-Wing folding wing system, which allowed more aircraft to be stored on an aircraft carrier, increasing the number of fighters that could be parked on a surface by more than a factor of two. Navy F4F-3s were replaced by F4F-4s in June 1942, though VMF-221 still operated F4F-3s during the Battle of Midway. VF-42 of the USS Yorktown was the last carrier group converted to the F4F-4, receiving the aircraft as it departed Pearl Harbor for the battle.

Singh graduated from Sandhurst in 1933 and was commissioned as a second lieutenant and placed in the unattached list of the Indian Army. Newly commissioned Indian officers were attached with a British unit before being sent to an Indian unit. He was attached with the Royal Berkshire Regiment. After an year's attachment, he was posted to the 6th Royal Battalion (Scinde), the 13th Frontier Force Rifles which was stationed at Kohat in the North-West Frontier Province (NWFP). Subsequently, the battalion moved to Razmak and join the Razmak Brigade. He spent three years in NWFP, till 1937, participating in multiple active operations.

James Hinchcliffe joined NBC Sports as an analyst for IndyCar races in 2020, also covering select IMSA WeatherTech Championship Series events that year. He rejoined NBC Sports as a full-time analyst in 2022, joining play-by-play voice Leigh Diffey and analyst Townsend Bell. Hinchcliffe replaced fellow Canadian Paul Tracy in the broadcast booth and continued to analyze IMSA races alongside IndyCar. In October 2022, Hinchcliffe joined the crew of F1's "Weekend Warmup" for the United States and Mexico City Grands Prix.

Emperor Menelik's health began to deteriorate in 1906, leading to increased rivalries within the government. Empress Taytu asserted her authority by undermining the crown council of ministers, frustrating the Shewan faction, which managed daily governance. She maneuvered her candidates into key positions, further straining relations. Welde Giyorgis, commanding thirty-one thousand men, refused to align with either the Empress or the Shewan establishment, ignoring orders and correspondence from the central government, exacerbating the political instability during this period.

The three-way baseball game was attended by over 50,000 fans. A total of 49,605 people paid for tickets by buying war bonds. In addition, 500 injured veterans were given free admission. Ticket sales from the game raised about $4.5 million. In addition, the mayor of New York City, Fiorello La Guardia, pledged $50 million of bonds, and an additional $1 million was contributed by Bond Clothing Stores in exchange for an autographed program. Overall, more than $56 million was raised. New York Daily News sportswriter Dick Young described the event as "the wackiest diamond battle ever conceived".

Margaret Louise Cuninggim was born in Nashville, Tennessee. Her father, Jesse Lee Cuninggim, was a Methodist clergyman and the President of Scarritt College for Christian Workers in Nashville. Her brother, Merrimon Cuninggim (1911-1995), was also a Methodist clergyman, and served as the President of Salem College in Winston-Salem, North Carolina. She graduated from Duke University in Durham, North Carolina in 1936 and went on to earn a master's degree from Columbia University in New York City in 1947 and a Doctorate of Education from Northwestern University in Evanston, Illinois in 1958.

In 2013, Mark Carney, then Governor of the Bank of England, announced the introduction of a newly designed £10 banknote made from durable polymer material. The note, featuring early 19th-century novelist Jane Austen, replaced the image of naturalist Charles Darwin and was issued on 14 September 2017. This decision followed public campaigns, including one highlighting the lack of female representation on banknotes, as Elizabeth Fry had been the only woman, aside from Queen Elizabeth II, previously featured. The polymer design aimed to enhance durability and security, marking a significant evolution in the Bank of England's currency production.

K. N. Vijiyin of New Straits Times opined that "Catch this film if you are looking for a story with a difference". A critic from Deccan Herald wrote "Housefull is awfully silly, and seems to serve no other purpose than to show Parthiban, as being capable of political authority". Kalki praised Parthiban for attempting a Speed kind of thriller in Tamil.

On 22 March 2017, the Community Development Program (CDP) was referred to the Senate Finance and Public Administration References Committee for inquiry, with findings handed down in September 2017. The report included 96 recommendations, with 22 addressing topics such as joblessness, policy design, and community empowerment. The committee concluded that the CDP should not continue in its current form, criticizing its centralized, punitive, and compliance-heavy administration. Instead, it recommended a new program focused on rewarding participation, reducing administrative burdens, and empowering local communities through a decentralized decision-making model. The report emphasized the need for local jobs plans and active consultation with Indigenous communities and stakeholders to ensure effective reform.

World basketball was growing, but it was on June 18, 1932 that a real international organization was formed, to coordinate tournaments and teams: that day, the following representatives of the 8 national federations: Attilio PONISIO (Argentina), Simeon MAVROSKOUFIS (Greece), Count Giorgio ASINARI DI SAN MARZANO (Italy), Joseph SHADEIKO (Latvia), Henry BRANDT (Portugal), D.D. TEICA (Romania), Léon BOUFFARD (Switzerland), Ladilslav KAPUCIAN (Czechoslovakia) founded the International Basketball Federation (Fédération internationale de basketball amateur, FIBA) in Geneva. Its work was fundamental for the first inclusion of basketball in the Berlin Olympic Games in 1936. The first Olympic title was won by the U.S. national team: Sam Balter, Ralph Bishop, Joe Fortenberry, Tex Gibbons, Francis Johnson, Carl Knowles, Frank Lubin, Art Mollner, Donald Piper, Jack Ragland, Willard Schmidt, Carl Shy, Duane Swanson, Bill Wheatley and the trainer James Needles. Canada was runner-up; the games were played on an outdoor clay court. The first World Championship was held in Argentina in 1950.

The Canucks completed their second season with the Chicago Wolves as American Hockey League affiliate, the final one under the two teams' contract. The Wolves started the season by winning their first four games for the second time in franchise history. The following games were not as fruitful as they lost five of their next six games and their starting goaltender Eddie Lack suffered a hip injury. The injury required surgery, and Lack missed the remainder of the season. With Lack out the Wolves finished fifteenth in the league for fewest goals allowed. The previous season they allowed the fifth fewest goals in the league. Lack and back-up Matt Climie set a franchise record for lowest team goals against average. With the league's nineteenth best offense, the Wolves were unable to overcome the goals against them and finished fourth in their division. Thus missing the playoffs. It was the second time in three years that the Wolves missed the playoffs, but only the fourth time in franchise history. Despite missing the playoffs, Chicago finished second in league attendance.

Deveron Harper played 27 games for the Carolina Panthers from 2000 to 2001 before being waived/injured on August 1, 2002. He later joined the New Orleans Saints, appearing in 14 games during the 2004 season, before being released on October 12, 2004. Harper signed with the Miami Dolphins on August 10, 2005, but was released on August 15, 2005. He subsequently joined the Austin Wranglers of the Arena Football League, signing on October 11, 2005, and played for the Kansas City Brigade in 2007. Currently, Deveron Harper serves as the dean of students at a high school in Georgia, where he focuses on student development and leadership.

Game II of 1991 is remembered for a number of dramatic incidents. Played in drenching rain, the game exploded into violence before half-time when volatile New South Wales forward Mark Geyer chopped down on Queensland hooker Steve Walters in a tackle. Prop Andrew Gee ran in to assist his teammate, sparking a brawl which involved most players from both teams. When the dust settled, referee David Manson issued a handful of cautions, but as players left the field for half-time, Queensland captain Wally Lewis continued to goad Geyer, clearly expecting the New South Wales forward to be antagonised into a violent response right in front of Manson that would have him sent off. This became a lasting image in Australian sporting folklore.

Blunt met her future husband Earle Page while serving as a theatre nurse at RPA; he was a medical resident there. Their first encounter came while she was assisting him with an unorthodox fire cupping procedure on a kidney patient. He accidentally threw a lit piece of alcohol-soaked paper onto her dress, causing it to catch fire. He quickly threw a nearby blanket over her, and she escaped with only minor burns. Page – who was almost five years her junior – soon began courting her, and in 1904 convinced her to become the matron at his new private hospital in Grafton. They married in Sydney on 18 September 1906, and would have five children together – Mary (b. 1909), Earle Jr. (b. 1910), Donald (b. 1912), Iven (b. 1914), and Douglas (b. 1916).

Heather Dale discovered Medievalism at the age of eighteen while a student at the University of Waterloo, through the Society for Creative Anachronism. Inspired by Arthurian legend and fantasy books she loved, she began composing songs and soon connected with the science fiction community at conventions in Toronto. She made her first recordings in 1992 and released her debut album, *The Trial of Lancelot*, in 2000. The album features her most popular song, "Mordred's Lullaby," which went viral and amassed over twenty million views on YouTube by August 2020, cementing her reputation as a prominent figure in Celtic and fantasy-inspired music.

Parents with intellectual disabilities often face discrimination, particularly in custody decisions, and encounter challenges managing housing and financial issues due to socio-economic disadvantages, such as poverty and low incomes. These disadvantages can lead to limited resources for their children, who may require additional support as they surpass their parents' intellectual abilities. A 2018 systematic review found limited high-quality evidence but highlighted that parents with intellectual disabilities can effectively parent when provided with appropriate training and support systems.

Iraq is one of the most oil-rich countries in the world. The country holding the fifth largest proven crude oil reserves,: 5 totalling 147.22 billion barrels at the end of 2017. Most of this oil—4 million barrels per day out of 4.3 million barrels produced daily—is exported, making Iraq the third-largest exporter of oil.: 5 Despite its ongoing civil war, Iraq was able to increase oil production during 2015 and 2016, with production dipping by 3.5 per cent in 2017 due to conflict with the Kurdistan Regional Government and OPEC production limits.: 5 By world standards, production costs for Iraqi oil are relatively low. However, four wars—the 1980–1988 Iraq-Iran War, 1991 Gulf War, the 2003-2011 War in Iraq, and the civil war—and the 1991–2003 UN sanctions have left the industry's infrastructure in poor condition, and the de facto independence of oil-rich Kurdistan Region have limited production.: 5–6

Citizens' Gavel operates from offices in Ibadan, Lagos and Abuja. The head of the organization is addressed as the Team Leader and he also happens to be the founder of the organization. Gavel has 16 full-time staff and works with over 150 lawyers across 19 states of Nigeria. Gavel set up 2 arms to create diversification in their approach. These arms are JusticeLab and Podus. JusticeLab is focused on addressing systemic issues by responding to issues around weakness in the judicial system of Nigeria especially in areas it affects citizens. Podus is focused on linking citizens who have suffered or are suffering from security agency brutality to lawyers. Podus is already on the Google Play Store and it works like Uber such that victims can log into the app, enter in their issue/case and a lawyer within their vicinity can be alerted to assist them.

On July 20, 2006, the newly formed Indian Motorcycle Company, owned largely by Stellican Limited, a London-based private equity firm, announced its new home in Kings Mountain, North Carolina, where it restarted the Indian motorcycle brand, manufacturing Indian Chief motorcycles in limited numbers, with a focus on exclusivity rather than performance. Starting out where the defunct Gilroy IMC operation left off in 2003, the "Kings Mountain" models were continuation models based on the new series of motorcycles developed in 1999. The 2009 Indian Chief incorporated a redesigned 105-cubic-inch (1,720 cc) Powerplus V-twin powertrain with electronic closed-loop sequential-port fuel injection, and a charging system providing increased capacity for the electronic fuel injection.

The Herndon Home is located in Atlanta's Vine City neighborhood, adjacent to the campus of Morris Brown College (formerly the campus of Atlanta University) on the north side of University Place NW. It is a two-story rectangular structure, faced in brick, with a flat, balustraded roof, porches projecting from the sides, and a massive neoclassical entrance portico, supported by large paired columns. The entrance is framed by wide sidelight windows and topped by a semi-oval transom, all windows displaying etching and tracery. The building is capped by a full entablature and modillioned cornice. The flat roof is usable as an open terrace, although it was reported to be in poor condition in 2000. The building interior is elaborately fitted with original period wood, stone, and plaster. The main living rooms have murals on the wall depicting elements of the Herndon family history.

Herdmanston in East Lothian had been held from the 12th century, when Henry St Clair received a grant of the lands of Herdmanston, from Richard de Morville, Constable of Scotland. It is not known if the Sinclair Lords Herdmanston share a common origin with the Sinclair Barons of Roslin, from whom branch off the Lords Sinclair and Earls of Caithness. According to the website sinclairgenealogy.info, the fact that the first proven Baron of Roslin, William St. Clair (died 1297) was made sheriff of Haddington in East Lothian where Herdmanston also is, suggests that he was appointed there to cover his own home area, and given that the name William appears frequently in the St Clair of Herdmanston family suggests that he may have been part of their extended family. However, according to the website clansinclairusa.org, William St Clair of Roslin was probably unrelated to the St Clairs of Herdmanston. According to Archibald Allan writing in 1900, Henry St Clair of Herdmanston appears to have been a son of the first William de St Clair of Roslin of the 11th century, but this William's existence cannot be proved by contemporary evidence and according to Roland Saint-Clair, William St. Clair who died in 1297 was the first proven Baron of Roslin.

Superdelegates, or automatic delegates, are unpledged delegates at the Democratic National Convention, comprising 15-20% of total delegates. They include Democratic leaders such as members of Congress, governors, and former presidents, seated based on their positions.

As of the 2000 census, Fairview Township, Cass County, Minnesota, had a population of 730 people, 265 households, and 205 families. The population density was 12.2 inhabitants per square mile (4.7/km²), with 330 housing units at an average density of 5.5 per square mile (2.1/km²). The racial makeup was 97.95% White, 0.96% Native American, 0.27% Asian, and 0.68% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino individuals of any race comprised 1.51% of the population. There were no reported African American residents.

After retirement as a football player, Oblak started coaching at various Slovenian clubs, including three times at Olimpija Ljubljana, NK Naklo and FC Koper. He proved to be a good youth coach, so in February 2004 he was named the new Slovenia under-21 national coach. On 24 May 2004, he replaced Bojan Prašnikar as the head coach of the Slovenia national football team. The team started brightly under his guidance, with the highlight being a 1–0 over Italy (eventual World Champions) in a World Cup qualifying match. However, the team soon ran into a series of bad results, and the pressure mounted on Oblak, until finally in late 2006, he was fired from the job. He was succeeded by Matjaž Kek. In 2009, he again took the managers job at his home club NK Olimpija Ljubljana. He was relieved of his duties after only four games.

In August 2018, Major League Wrestling (MLW) announced that they had started a working agreement with Mexican-based promotion Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA). Nearly two years later on February 5, 2020, MLW announced that it would be holding its first co-promoted event with AAA on March 13, held in conjunction with AAA's local Tijuana partner Promociones EMW. This marked the second MLW event promoted in Mexico, following the Crash/Major League Wrestling show held on October 5, 2019.

In most parts of Australia, tuna casserole is known as Tuna Mornay. It features a creamy white Mornay sauce, often enriched with cheese, peas, corn, or herbs, and is typically made with pasta instead of egg noodles.

Cossack family values in 21st century Russia are described as simple yet rigid, rooted in tradition. Men are expected to build homes and provide income, while women focus on cooking, cleaning, and raising children. These values are grounded in traditional Russian culture, Orthodoxy, and a strong emphasis on preserving their cultural and religious heritage.

At the time of the disaster it was said that there were only 4 companies able to insure risks of such size. The impact of Deepwater Horizon on insurance, reinsurance and other global markets due to the shift in systemic risk is as yet unknown. Until this incident loss of an entire semi-submersible rig in this way was considered "an unprecedented tragedy" with an underwriter at Pritchard Capital commenting "It's never happened that a semi could burn into the sea and completely sink. Now underwriters have to include that as a risk. That’s probably $10,000 to $15,000 more per day in rig insurance. They’ll make it up by charging more on a per-rig basis."

The PCT was established as a national scenic trail by the National Trails Systems Act of 1968. In 1971, the Pacific Crest Trail Club was founded, and in 1977 the Pacific Crest Trail Conference was incorporated. The two groups merged in 1987, and in 1992, the name was changed to the Pacific Crest Trail Association. The Association hired its first paid staff in 1993.

Erich Barnes, known for his fiery intensity and savvy defensive play, was ranked No. 63 in Cleveland Browns history by Mike Pettica of Cleveland.com in 2022. A six-time Pro Bowler, Barnes was also named to the Hall of Very Good Class of 2013 by the Pro Football Researchers Association.

Robby Wells filed with the Federal Election Commission in 2018 to run for President in the 2020 Democratic Party primary. On November 7, 2019, he filed in person at the New Hampshire Secretary of State’s office, led by Bill Gardner. After New Hampshire, Wells appeared on Democratic primary ballots in Louisiana and Colorado.

"Little Istanbul" (Template:Lang-tr) is an area of South Paterson centered on Main Street. This rapidly growing Turkish American enclave is the largest in the United States in addition to housing many Palestinians, Lebanese, Syrians, Jordanians, and Arab immigrants from a variety of other countries. Paterson is home to the largest Turkish-American immigrant community in the United States (Little Istanbul) and the second largest Arab-American community after Dearborn, Michigan. Therefore, it also called "Little Ramallah" (Template:Lang-ar Rām Allāh al-Ṣaḡīra). The Paterson-based Arab American Civic Association runs an Arabic language program in the Paterson school district. Paterson's Arab American population was estimated as high as 20,000 in 2015.

Founder and former editor-in-chief Rodrigo Gudiño serves as the company president. As of March 2017, the executive editor is Andrea Subissati; contributing editor Monica S. Kuebler oversees book features and reviews; online managing editor William J. Wright handles news and reviews on Rue-Morgue.com; operations manager Mariam Bastani handles customer service and ensures that all weekly office operations are running smoothly; editorial assistant Maddi McGillvray reviews and edits the final prints of the magazine during production and is a frequent contributor to the website; music editor Aaron von Lupton oversees music features and reviews, and games editor Evan Millar oversees video game and tabletop game features and reviews. The art director is Andrew Wright.

Sikhism is a religion that began in India in the mid-fifteenth century with the first Guru, known as Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469-1539 C.E.). The essence of being a Sikh is that one lives one’s life according to the teachings of the Sikh Gurus, devoting time to meditating on God and the scriptures, chanting, and living life in a way that benefits other people and the world. Kaur’s teacher, Yogi Bhajan (1929-2004), was influential in helping promote the Sikh tradition in the West.

The first international annual competition contested by women's champion clubs was the International Women's Club Championship, founded and organized in Japan. The inaugural edition took place in November 2012, featuring four teams: European champions Lyon, Australia's Canberra United, and Japan's INAC Kobe Leonessa and NTV Beleza. Lyon represented Europe, while Canberra United represented Australia. Lyon won the tournament, defeating INAC Kobe Leonessa 2-1 in the final after extra time, with Sonia Bompastor scoring the decisive penalty.

The Minnesota–Wisconsin football rivalry, the most-played rivalry in NCAA Division I FBS history, began in 1890 with the first game held in Minneapolis, where Minnesota defeated Wisconsin 63–0. Wisconsin secured its first victory in 1894 under coach Hiram Stickney. The rivalry became a conference matchup in 1896 with the formation of the Big Ten Conference. In 1906, President Theodore Roosevelt suspended college football, including this rivalry, due to safety concerns over injuries and fatalities, marking the only year since 1890 the teams did not play. The game alternates between Minneapolis and Madison and has been played annually since 1907, except for the 1906 hiatus, solidifying its place as a historic and enduring tradition.

In 1973, the NCAA reorganized its membership into three divisions—Division I, Division II, and Division III—to address the varying levels of emphasis in college athletics and ensure fair competition. Five years later, in 1978, Division I members voted to create subdivisions I-A and I-AA for football. These subdivisions were renamed in 2006 to the Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) and Football Championship Subdivision (FCS), reflecting their distinct structures and competitive formats.

FuboTV was co-founded in January 2015 by David Gandler (CEO), Alberto Horihuela (CMO) and Sung Ho Choi. At launch, FuboTV cost $7 per month and offered live streams from soccer-centric channels. In early 2017, Fubo pivoted to become a broader streaming service, adding entertainment and news programming in addition to soccer and sports from NFL, NBA, MLB and NHL. During its first five years, investors in FuboTV include AMC Networks, Luminari Capital, Northzone, Sky, and Scripps Networks Interactive. FuboTV was named to Forbes’ Next Billion-Dollar Startups list in 2019.

"Piece of Me" received widespread acclaim from music critics, most of whom considered it the standout track from Blackout. Alex Fletcher of Digital Spy gave "Piece of Me" four stars, calling it "a two fingered-salute to the media hounds and an electro-thudding cry of defiance, warning us that this popstrel is not for turning. [The opening line] poops from a great height on anything Lily Allen has ever penned and reveals that it's been Spears who's been laughing hardest during her year of zany media antics". Peter Robinson of The Observer and Margeaux Watson of Entertainment Weekly named "Piece of Me" one of the standout tracks of the album. Dennis Lim of Blender called it one of the best tracks of Blackout along with "Gimme More". Laura Herbert of BBC News said that the song is "without doubt the best track on the whole album. [...] It's a masterpiece." Kelefa Sanneh of The New York Times said "[Bloodshy & Avant] evoke the horror, the exhilaration and (finally) the boredom of [Spears's] overexamined life. It’s brilliant".

The Mandiri River flows through the Menoreh Hills in the Yogyakarta region of Java, Indonesia. The area experiences a tropical climate with an annual average temperature of approximately 27°C. The warmest month is February, with an average temperature of 28°C, while the coldest month is July, averaging 26°C. Annual rainfall is significant, with the wettest month being December, receiving around 300 mm of rain, and the driest month being August, with an average rainfall of 50 mm.

Dahlén began his professional career 2003 with Brynäs IF before joining IFK Norrköping in January 2004. He played for Norrköping from January 2004 to June 2005. In July 2005, he signed for Umeå FC. He played for Umeå in few games before signing for Gefle IF in January 2007. On 10 December 2009, he announced his departure from Gefle and signed a two-and-a-half-year contract with Hansa Rostock. On 13 June 2010, he left Hansa Rostock and signed for FSV Frankfurt. After having only played one match for his club in the 2011–12 season, his contract was terminated by mutual consent on 16 January 2012.

Anthony W. Miller began his career at Delco Electronics before spending 10 years as a partner at McKinsey & Company, specializing in growth strategies, operating performance improvement, and restructuring across the United States, Europe, and Asia. From 2003 to 2006, he served as executive vice president of operations at LRN Corporation, a provider of governance, risk, ethics, and compliance software and eLearning services. In 2006, he joined Silver Lake Partners as an operating partner, helping launch the firm's Value Creation Team.

Since the 1970s Yiguandao spread to Southeast Asia. In Thailand (where it is named อนุตตรธรรม Anuttharatham) it has grown so strong in recent decades to come into conflict with Buddhism; as of 2009 there were over 7000 worship halls, and it is reported that 200.000 Thais each year convert into the religion. In Singapore the Yiguandao has three great public halls (white multiple-storied buildings with traditional Chinese architectural features) and more than 2000 house churches.

Brookfield, located in present-day Waukesha County, Wisconsin, was originally inhabited by the Potawatomi people. The first white settler, William Howe, arrived in 1820 with a Presidential Land Grant. In 1836, Robert Curren purchased a claim and established a tavern and inn in the area.

Emperor Menelik's health deterioration since 1906 led to increasing rivalries in the government. Empress Taytu asserted her authority by undermining the crown council of ministers. The Shewan faction running the daily governance was frustrated by the decisions of Empress Taytu, after she maneuvered her own candidates into positions of power. Welde Giyorgis with an army of thirty thousand men under his command did not commit himself to the Empress or the Shewan establishment, rather he ignored to obey orders or to answer letters from the central government.

Williams is originally from Knoxville, Tennessee, the daughter of two clinical psychologists. She graduated from Kenyon College in 2010 with degrees in English and studio art. While a student, she interned in New York City for Art + Commerce, Harper's Bazaar, and Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week. Williams worked as a photo editor and producer at Vanity Fair, assisting in and producing photoshoots. She worked on shoots for Caitlyn Jenner, Rihanna, and Patti Smith. Williams was laid off in 2019.

D-Black, born Desmond Kwesi Blackmore, began his career as an independent artist in 2010 after parting ways with his rap partner Kwaku T, with whom he formed the group D-Black & Kwaku T in 2009. The duo released the album *Target Practice* before going solo. As a solo artist, D-Black has released multiple albums, including *Music, Love and Life* and *Loyalty*, as well as an E.P. and a mix-tape. In 2011, he won a BET Award, solidifying his international acclaim.

Kihikihi School is a co-educational state primary school in the Waipa district, providing dual medium education for Year 1 to 8 students. As of November 2024, it has a roll of 210 students across an English-medium Aoraki stream and a Te Reo Māori Rumaki stream. The school opened in 1873, moved to its current site in 1884, was destroyed by fire in 1938, and rebuilt in 1952.

CSIS is Canada's lead agency on national security matters and for conducting national security investigations and security intelligence collection. CSIS collects and analyzes intelligence, then advises the Government of Canada on issues and activities that may threaten the security of Canada and its citizens. These threats include terrorism, espionage and foreign interference in Canadian affairs, proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, and information security threats. The agency is also responsible for the security screening program.

Feature search, also known as parallel search, is a type of visual search where the target differs from all distractors in a single, unique feature, such as color, size, or orientation. For example, identifying a red circle among green circles is a feature search task involving color. In this process, distractors are uniform and lack the defining feature of the target, allowing for efficient detection. Feature search is characterized by parallel processing, where the target is detected simultaneously across the visual field, independent of the number of distractors. Reaction times remain consistent regardless of set size, as peripheral vision can easily discriminate the target from distractors. This efficiency is influenced by the distinctiveness of the target feature and the minimal similarity between the target and distractors.

The 5 Year MD Program is designed for recent high school graduates seeking direct admission into the study of medicine. The program consists of 4 semesters of premedical courses, including Biology, General Chemistry, Organic Chemistry, Physics, Math, and Introductory Anatomy. This is followed by the structured curriculum in the 4 Year MD Program.

The village consists of around 300 families living on land bought by the Catholic Church in 1938. Franciscan Father Ken Viegas was the parish priest in 2004. The Faithful Companions of Jesus Sisters also run a dispensary out of their St. Clare Convent. The St Isidore primary school run by the Franciscan Sisters of the Heart of Jesus provides education to the community up to the eighth grade. In 2011 some 200 disadvantaged children were receiving an education at the school funded by Catholic Missions.

In the game's backstory, an event called the Rise of Morastrum occurs once every three centuries; a solar eclipse occurs, with every newborn of that year except one destined to die; this survivor is called the Child of Destiny and receives great power. 600 years prior, the Child of Destiny became an evil ruler called the Archfiend, while 300 years prior the Child of Destiny was a good hero called the Matriarch who toppled the Archfiend. Prior to the game's opening, the Rise of Morastrum occurs again, and people begin seeking the Child of Destiny to discover their eventual fate. The eight main characters, all known to each other, set out on their own quests and end up involved in the hunt for the Child of Destiny. They are Julian Nohl, a reckless and just swordsman; the tomboyish Ellen Carson; the shy Sarah Carson; Thomas Bent, friend to Julian, Ellen and Sarah; Monika Ausbach, sister to the Marquis of Loanne; Monika's brother Mikhail Ausbach von Loanne; Katarina Lauran, Monika's attendant; and Khalid, a wandering mercenary. They all cross paths in the village of Sinon.

George A. Zentmyer was born on August 9, 1913, in North Platte, Nebraska, to parents whose names are not mentioned in the provided context. While an undergraduate at UCLA, he studied botany and later pursued graduate work at UC Berkeley, focusing his master's and doctoral theses on plant pathology, particularly phytophthora species.

The fluorinase enzyme catalyzes the formation of a carbon-fluorine (C-F) bond at the C-5' position of S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM), producing 5'-fluoro-5'-deoxyadenosine (5'-FDA) and L-methionine. L-methionine is released as a byproduct. The reaction is approximately 10^12 times faster than the uncatalyzed process, despite the fluorinase being considered a slow enzyme with a turnover number (kcat) of up to 0.4 min⁻¹. The high kinetic barrier arises from the transformation of the fluoride ion into a nucleophile within the enzyme's active site, which requires significant activation energy due to the fluoride ion's strong solvation and low nucleophilicity in aqueous conditions.

Perras debuted as an Irish rogue villain character named Red McNulty, billed from Dublin, Ireland and wrestling with an eyepatch. For the next three years, he wrestled around the Toronto area, eventually quitting his regular job to wrestle in the north-western area of Canada. There, Perras acquired much wrestling experience, and from there he made his first trip to Japan.

Eversz is best known as the author of the award winning series of crime novels featuring the paparazza Nina Zero, set in Southern California. The first novel in the series, Shooting Elvis, was Oslo Aftenposten’s Crime Novel of the Year in 1997, and the Best Humorous Crime Novel of 1996 in The Guardian (awarded by Val McDermid). The next in the series, Killing Paparazzi, was a The Washington Post Book World Rave in 2001. The Washington Post has written favorable reviews for the following titles in the series. His novels have been translated into fifteen languages.

"The Things That I Used to Do," a seminal blues track by Guitar Slim, was included on the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame's list of "500 Songs That Shaped Rock and Roll." The song played a pivotal role in the development of electric blues and influenced the commercial success of rock and roll by showcasing the potential of amplified instruments. Its innovative use of the electric guitar, particularly its distorted tone, helped shape the 'electric sound' of rock music. A favorite of Jimi Hendrix, he recorded an impromptu version of the song with Johnny Winter in 1969. This rendition was officially released on the 2004 compilation *Martin Scorsese Presents the Blues: Jimi Hendrix*, further cementing the song's enduring legacy in rock history.

Willow Garage began developing the PR2 robot as a follow-up to the PR1, and ROS as the software to run it. Groups from more than twenty institutions made contributions to ROS, both the core software and the growing number of packages which worked with ROS to form a greater software ecosystem. That people outside of Willow were contributing to ROS (especially from Stanford's STAIR project) meant that ROS was a multi-robot platform from the start. While Willow Garage had originally had other projects in progress, they were scrapped in favor of the Personal Robotics Program: focused on producing the PR2 as a research platform for academia and ROS as the open-source robotics stack that would underlie both academic research and tech startups, much like the LAMP stack did for web-based startups.

Welsh Kale are Welsh Romani, they are found in the Welsh-speaking parts of Northwestern Wales, and they speak Welsh Kalá. The Romani population in Wales is estimated to be around 3,000. The Roma first arrived in Wales during the 16th century.

Harmanus Bleecker practiced law for many years with Theodore Sedgwick (1780–1839), the son of Federalist legislator and judge Theodore Sedgwick (1746–1813). Bleecker was once engaged to Catharine Sedgwick, the judge’s daughter. He was also a close friend of Federalist Congressman and Boston Mayor Josiah Quincy III, maintaining strong personal and professional relationships throughout his life.

At the time of the Deepwater Horizon disaster, few companies could insure risks of such magnitude, highlighting a systemic shift in global insurance and reinsurance markets. The incident, described as an "unprecedented tragedy" by industry experts, surpassed previous disasters like Piper Alpha in scale and complexity. An underwriter at Pritchard Capital noted the extraordinary nature of a semi-submersible rig burning and sinking entirely, a risk previously deemed improbable. This event led to a significant rise in rig insurance costs, with an estimated additional $25,000 to $50,000 per day to account for such risks. Underwriters are expected to offset these increased risks by raising premiums and reassessing coverage terms, reshaping the offshore energy insurance landscape.

Colt Lyerla went undrafted in the 2014 NFL Draft and was signed by the Green Bay Packers as an undrafted free agent on May 19, 2014. During the Packers' Family Night practice on August 2, 2014, Lyerla attempted to hurdle a defender and tore the medial collateral ligament (MCL) and posterior cruciate ligament (PCL) in his right knee. Initially placed on injured reserve, Lyerla was waived with an injury settlement on August 26, 2014, ending his brief tenure with the team.

Eileen Kramer was born and grew up in Mosman Bay with one other sibling, a brother. Her father, a car salesman, began showing signs of alcoholism when Kramer was about 10, leading to her mother leaving and secretly relocating with the children to Coogee when she was 13. Her mother then began working as a store detective at Farmers (now owned by Myer), a department store on George Street.

Charlotte Teuber-Weckersdorf was barred from attending the Gymnasium in 1938 due to the Nazi regime's policies. During World War II, she served with the Red Cross. In 1945, she completed her Matura exams. She studied Archaeology and History of Art in Innsbruck, earning degrees in these subjects in 1949. She later pursued Political Science studies in Vienna, completing her master’s degree in 1951. In 1956, she earned her PhD, further solidifying her academic achievements.

Soon after Pafawag started the production of a universal EU07 locomotive, The Rolling-stock Industry Central Bureau of Construction (Pl.: Centralne Biuro Konstrukcyjne Przemysłu Taboru Kolejowego) began to develop a new heavy Co-Co freight locomotive. The electrical part of the locomotive including the traction motors was based on the equipment used in EU07 locomotive. The new locomotive had some modern features, including its body.

Melanie Oxley, daughter of Eric and Jan Oxley, grew up in Kingscliff, New South Wales, where her family settled in 1965 after leaving Sydney. Eric, originally from Manchester, was an art teacher. Melanie's older brothers, Peter and Jeremy, co-founded the band Sunnyboys, while her younger brothers, Tim and Damien, also pursued music, with Tim performing as a singer, guitarist, and drummer in various bands.
