The Oxley children enjoyed a coastal upbringing, shaped by their parents' move to the small, then sparsely populated town.

The pre-pathogenic period refers to the stage before disease onset, where risk factors and vulnerabilities develop. Musculoskeletal pathologies like osteoarthritis and tendinopathy affect joints, tendons, and surrounding areas, such as hips and knees. Common causes include cartilage damage, tendon fiber disorganization, and overuse. Most humans eventually exhibit evidence of these conditions.

Pascual de Gayangos y Arce was born on June 21, 1809, in Seville, Spain, to Brigadier José Gayangos y Nebot. He completed his primary education in Madrid and, at age 13, was sent to study at Pont-le-Voy near Blois, France. In 1828, he began studying Arabic under Silvestre de Sacy in Paris.

The principal focus of Schütz's research was on "ab initio calculations" of the electronic structures of extended molecules (local correlation methods), excited electronic states, and intermolecular forces. During his time at Stuttgart he successfully developed correlation methods scaled in a linear sequence according to molecule size, building on work undertaken by Peter Pulay during the 1980s. The work that Schütz undertook during the first couple of years of the third millennium invokes Post–Hartree–Fock methodologies, applying coupled cluster techniques, and extending to molecules with triple substitutions. Previously they had been limited to small molecules because of the absence of scalability of the methods conditioned by cononinical (and therefore not localised) orthogonal molecular orbital bases.

Darton was one of the first British women to be allowed to vote. She first appears in the Electoral Register for 1919, living at Woodhouse Parade, High Road, North Finchley. She went on to run a photographic materials business with Dorothy Jolly in Crouch End, London. She died at the Royal Cancer Hospital in London on 3 January 1945, aged 62. Probate records show her leaving her effects of £1,181 to Jolly.

The Bellaire Texan, serving the Willowbend community in the mid-20th century, was headquartered in Bellaire and published by the Texan Publishing Corporation. By 1975, it became the Bellaire & Southwestern Texan, published by Preston Publishing Company and headquartered in Houston.

Brian Ibbott, who dreamed of becoming a DJ as a child, began his career spinning music at weddings but found the repetitive playlists uninspiring. In August 2004, he discovered podcasting, which reignited his passion for creating a unique music experience. He launched *Coverville*, a podcast dedicated to cover songs, later that year. The show reached its 300th episode on March 4, 2007, and its 500th on August 2008, celebrated with a gala in Las Vegas. *Coverville* became a pioneer in podcasting, attracting listeners worldwide.

Labour remained in control of the council with 23 councillors after holding 7 of the 11 seats contested, despite losing 1 seat to the Conservatives. The Conservative gain came in Furnace Green, where Brenda Blackwell defeated the former Labour mayor Ray Calcott by 1,269 votes to 895. This took to the Conservatives to 7 seats, as they also held the 2 seats contested in Pound Hill North, while the Liberal Democrats remained on 2 seats after holding Northgate.

The last part is the pintado ('painting') process. Very fine-tipped brushes are used, made of turkey feather at the tip of which a cat's hair is inserted. Thanks to this, very elaborate motifs can be painted, which cover the entire surface of the box or furniture. Natural powder pigments mixed with sisa (oil based preparation for gluing gold) are used, that is, oil paint. In this case, each painter also has his own style, although flowers are usually the most frequent motif. Abstract, geometric and animal shapes are also very common. The drawings are simple in a naïve style and very colorful, which makes a beautiful contrast to the glossy black of the case.

Senator McDonald and Representative Lawlor stated that the bill was proposed as a response to the 2006 fraud case in which Rev. Michael Jude Fay, a priest from the Diocese of Bridgeport, was accused of stealing $1.4 million from his parish. Raised Bill 1098 explicitly referred only to the Roman Catholic Church, and would require the creation of administrative corporate boards of lay people as heads of parishes, replacing the Church's normal oversight by priests and bishops. On March 11, 2009, a scheduled protest by 3500 marchers became a celebration following the news that the bill had been tabled.

The Acelhuate River is an important drainage system for El Salvador's capital, San Salvador, and is severely contaminated with heavy metals along with domestic and industrial waste. This water is considered a biohazard, and the contamination is so severe that it is rendered untreatable by treatment methods such as reverse osmosis. Contaminated water from the Acelhuate River flows directly into the Cerron Grande reservoir.

The Dupont Circle Fountain has faced frequent vandalism, with sculptures often targeted, particularly their hands, which were repeatedly broken off. After such damage, the sculptures were repaired. In 1948, the fountain was temporarily removed due to the construction of a streetcar underpass. It was returned two years later with a new pumping system installed. However, workers failed to connect the pipes properly, leaving the fountain non-functional. This issue was resolved the following year, restoring the fountain to full operation.

In June 2011, the FBI served Dallas County Commissioner John Wiley Price and multiple associates with search warrants as part of a public corruption investigation. The warrants cited potential violations of U.S. Code Titles 18, 26, and 31, including theft or bribery concerning programs receiving federal funds, tax evasion, fraud, false statements, money laundering, and conspiracy. Agents raided Price's home, vehicles, and county offices, seizing documents, cash, and valuables. On July 25, 2014, Price was arrested by the FBI, accused of accepting nearly $1 million in bribes in exchange for political favors. His trial was scheduled for January 2016, and he was ordered to contribute $80,000 toward his defense, with an initial $20,000 payment due by March 15, 2015, followed by $10,000 bi-monthly payments and $500 monthly installments until the trial's conclusion.

The All-China Federation of Trade Unions (ACFTU) was established in 1925 to represent the interests of national and local trade unions and trade union councils. The ACFTU reported a membership of 130 million, out of an estimated 248 million urban workers, at the end of 2002. Chinese trade unions are organized on a broad industrial basis. Membership is open to those who rely on wages for the whole or a large part of their income, a qualification that excludes most agricultural workers. In theory, membership is not compulsory, but in view of the unions' role in the distribution of social benefits, the economic pressure to join is great. The lowest unit is the enterprise union committee. Individual trade unions also operate at the provincial level, and there are trade union councils that coordinate all union activities within a particular area and operate at county, municipal, and provincial levels. At the top of the movement is the ACFTU, which discharges its functions through a number of regional federations.

In March 2017, Junior Fa prepared for his first title fight against veteran boxer Daniel Tai for the vacant New Zealand Professional Boxing Association (NZPBA) heavyweight title. The bout took place on March 18 at the ABA Stadium in Auckland, New Zealand. Tai, who had been fighting professionally since 2000, entered the fight with a record of 22 wins, 21 losses, and 2 draws, having only been knocked out once in his career. The NZPBA heavyweight title had been vacant for nearly five years, last held by Sonny Bill Williams. During the fight, Fa faced significant challenges, including swelling and damage to his face, but relied on his endurance and strategic approach to outlast Tai in a grueling 10-round battle. The judges scored the fight 100-90, 98-94, and 95-95, awarding Fa a majority decision victory and his first professional title, extending his undefeated record to 11 wins.

As military operations in Vietnam escalated, the demand for air refueling of attacking aircraft increased. Crews from the squadron became actively involved in Operation Young Tiger starting in 1965, refueling combat aircraft in Southeast Asia (after 1966, it was joined by its sister at Fairchild, the 43d Air Refueling Squadron). Shortly thereafter, the mission of refueling B-52s participating in Operation Arc Light was added. The 92d furnished tankers and crews to support combat in Southeast Asia until 1975.

Gabrielle Jackson was diagnosed with endometriosis in 2001. As The Guardian's opinion editor, she launched an investigation into the condition in 2015 after publishing a personal article detailing her experiences with the disease. Prompted by the overwhelming response—600 stories within 24 hours and one million readers—Jackson was inspired to explore the widespread neglect of endometriosis, a condition affecting millions globally but receiving minimal medical and media attention.

For small or backyard growers, collecting and destroying all leaf debris on the ground is an absolute necessity due to the potency of this disease because the fungus overwinters in this leftover leaf litter. This is its main form of survival. By removing and destroying these leaves, a grower can significantly decrease the amount of primary inoculum available in the spring. It will greatly decrease the apparent infection rate. There has also been a study done on the addition of a straw mulch bedding to the ground after all the leaves have been picked up. The addition of this mulch further reduced the spring infection rate. Leaf litter removal is not very practical for large commercial growers due labor needs and number of trees but if at all possible, a majority of the old leaves should try to be collected.

Esa-Pekka Salonen's *Cello Concerto*, his fourth concerto, follows his Alto Saxophone Concerto (1980–81), Piano Concerto (2007), and Violin Concerto (2009). In the program notes, Salonen revealed that some ideas for the *Cello Concerto* trace back three decades, though the material was primarily developed in the summer of 2015, during which he explored new textures without a concrete plan. The second and third movements of the concerto quote his earlier works, including the *cello concerto from 2016*. This piece reflects Salonen's evolving compositional voice, blending innovative textures with a balance between soloist and orchestra, and continues his legacy as both a conductor and composer.

On October 3, 2017, Dutch Defense Minister Jeanine Hennis-Plasschaert resigned following a Dutch Safety Board report on a July 2016 training accident in Kidal, Mali, where two soldiers died due to defective, improperly stored mortar shells. The report criticized the military's safety standards. Chief of Defence General Tom Middendorp also resigned over the incident.

Nash is the younger of two sisters. She married PFR drummer Mark Nash in May 1996, whom she met while both bands were performing at the Cornerstone Festival in Illinois. They have one child (a son) together, who was born in 2004. The couple divorced in 2007. Nash married musician Stephen Wilson in 2011.

In late 1966, Keith Barber and Les Gilbert reformed The Wild Cherries, recruiting former Weird Mob bassist and vocalist Danny Robinson as their new singer. Peter Eddey, also from the Weird Mob, joined as bassist, while Gilbert switched to organ, playing a Hammond. In January 1967, the lineup was completed with the addition of guitar virtuoso Lobby Loyde (formerly of the Purple Hearts), solidifying the band’s reputation as a pioneering force in Melbourne’s psychedelic music scene.

In April 2011, Polaris Industries, the off-road and leisure vehicle maker and parent company of Victory Motorcycles, announced its intention to acquire Indian Motorcycle. Indian's production facilities were moved to Spirit Lake, Iowa, where production began on August 5, 2011. In March 2013, Indian unveiled their new 111 cu in (1,820 cc) "Thunder Stroke" engine, and began to sell their newly designed motorcycles based on it in August 2013.

Brand name products are available at Loeb's. Products range from outdoor inspired clothing and travel gear for men, women, and kids to a large selection of men's suits and dress clothes. Loeb's carries brands by Loeb's own brand LOEB'S, and Loeb's own Alex Loeb Heritage Collection brand, On Running shoes, Fish Hippie, Olukai, Peter Millar, Duck Head, Sorel, The North Face, Patagonia, goodr sunglasses, KREWE sunglasses, Columbia, Eagles Nest Outfitters, Costa Del Mar, Ralph Lauren, Chaco, Teva, Bostonian, H.S. Trask, Kavu, Jack Victor, and Clarks.

Approximately 50,000 fans attended the Tri-Cornered Baseball Game on June 26, 1944, at the Polo Grounds, with many purchasing tickets through war bonds. Admission raised $5.5 million, while 5,000 injured veterans were granted free entry. New York City Mayor Fiorello LaGuardia pledged $50 million in bonds, and Bond Clothing Stores contributed $1 million for an autographed program. In total, the event raised $56.5 million for the war effort. New York Daily News sportswriter Jimmy Powers described the game as "the wackiest diamond battle ever conceived," highlighting the unique nature of the three-team exhibition game held to support the Allied forces.

Smith's first academic post was as an Assistant Lecturer at the University of Exeter from 1959–60. He then became Research Officer for the Acton Society Trust in 1960–2, a trust set up by the Joseph Rowntree Social Service Trust in the 1940s "to analyse the implications of the welfare state for liberty and the individual." In 1962, he became a Lecturer in Politics at the University of Hull, where he would remain for the next five years.

On 1 November 2017, the US Navy issued a "Memorandum for distribution". The memorandum defines the legal privilege of such reports, but in the interests of transparency authorizes the release of the two reports covering collisions of Fitzgerald and John McCain. The two reports are annexed to the memorandum. The reports are intended to be authoritative, but are not balanced; they specifically exclude a detailed examination of the actions of ACX Crystal and Alnic MC respectively. The report for John S. McCain detailed the following;

During his tenure as President of Wilmington University, Varsalona was awarded the inaugural Rev. Roberto Balducelli Appreciation Award, given to Italian-American community leaders who use their position to promote Italian culture and values in the communities they serve. Varsalona also received the Community Leadership Advocate award on behalf of the Goodwill of Delaware and Delaware County. The award was given to Varsalona in appreciation of Wilmington University's scholarship programs, many of which directly benefitted employees and volunteers involved with the local chapter of the Goodwill.

The golden parakeet is listed as vulnerable on the IUCN Red List, with an estimated population of 10,000 to 20,000. Its decline is driven by deforestation, habitat loss, and capture for aviculture, where its striking plumage makes it highly sought after. Locally, it is often viewed as a pest for feeding on crops and is hunted for food or sport, further threatening its survival.

A well-known statement is "Truth is one, sages call it by various names." It is derived from Rig Veda Samhita 1.164.46: "They call him Indra, Mitra, Varuna, Agni, and he is heavenly nobly-winged Garutman. To what is One, sages give many a title. They call it Agni, Yama, Matarisvan.". According to William A. Graham, "the one" in verse 1.164.46 refers to Vāc, goddess of speech, appearing as "the creative force and absolute force in the universe." In later Vedic literature, "Speech or utterance is also identified with the supreme power or transcendent reality," and "equated with Brahman in this sense." Frauwallner states that "many gods are traced back to the one Godhead. The one (ekam) is not meant adjectivelcenter quality but as a substantive, as the upholding centre of reality."

The history of OFD reaches back to its predecessor, which was founded in November 1958 as Ostfriesische Lufttaxi - Dekker und Janssen OHG - or OLT for short - to offer ad-hoc charters and sightseeing flights in East Frisia. By 1961, the demand had increased heavily and by 1968, OLT was the largest German regional airline. During the 1970s, as new investors were found, OLT expanded its services with scheduled flights between German cities such as Münster/Osnabrück-Frankfurt.

Andhra Pradesh, a state rich in natural and cultural heritage, offers diverse tourist attractions. Coastal districts feature beaches like Rishikonda and Mypadu. The state is home to the Belum Caves, India's second-longest caves, and the rock-cut Undavalli Caves. Scenic spots include Araku Valley, Horsley Hills, and Nagari Hills. Arma Konda, the state's highest peak, lies in the Eastern Ghats, enhancing its geographical allure.

Her father was a military feldsher and her mother was the daughter of a pharmacist. She was born in the barracks where her father was employed. In 1762, her family moved to Ludwigsburg, where she grew up with Friedrich Schiller and his sisters. Christophine, who was the first to recognize Friedrich's talent, became her life-long friend.

Silloth's largest church, Christ Church, is located on Criffel Street in a prominent position within the town. The body of the church was completed in 1870, with the porch tower and large broach spire added in 1878. The spire houses a ring of eight bells, cast in 1883 by J Taylor & Son of Loughborough. Designed by Charles Ferguson in the Victorian Gothic style, the church features a mix of materials, including granite sourced from shipping ballast and red and yellow brickwork internally. Its commanding spire serves as a significant landmark across the Solway Plain, reflecting the ambitious design and strategic placement of the church in Silloth's original town plan.

The Bellaire Texan, which served the Willowbend community in the mid-20th Century, was headquartered in Bellaire and published by the Texan Publishing Corporation. By 1975 it became known as the Bellaire & Southwestern Texan and was published by the Preston Publishing Company. It was then headquartered in Houston.

As a freshman in 2009, Lester played in eight games, recording eight tackles. He played on special teams in the national championship game against Texas, but did not record a tackle. As a sophomore in 2010, Lester took over as a starter and had 52 tackles, eight interceptions, and a sack.

The 4th cadet battalion maintained a special honour guard unit that served as the official ceremonial ambassador of the military forces of the republic. It participated in the march on of the school battle banner, the welcoming of the Soviet leadership (the General Secretary, the head of state and head of government) and foreign delegations to Baku, as well public events such as wreath laying ceremonies. In the late 60s, the only ceremonial units in Baku were an army and navy guard, a motorcycle detachment, and a military band. The uniform of the unit was based on that of the 154th Preobrazhensky Independent Commandant's Regiment in Moscow. Its first commander was Major S. Akopdzhanyan. It had participated in the welcoming ceremonies of the leaders of Turkey, Iran, Senegal (Prime Minister Mamadou Dia) and Egypt at Binah Airport (now Heydar Aliyev International Airport). It also took part in the visit of Leonid Brezhnev to Baku in 1982 and ruby jubilee celebrations of Victory Day in 1985. In 1965, the unit had the honor to represent the academy by opening a military parade for the first time.

Sora was designed by Tetsuya Nomura as the protagonist of Kingdom Hearts. He was not originally slated to be the protagonist, as Disney wanted Donald Duck to fill the role, while Square wanted Mickey Mouse. Nomura designed Sora with the concepts of the Disney characters in mind, basing his outfit on Mickey Mouse's trademark white gloves, red shorts and giant yellow shoes. Sora originally wielded a weapon resembling a chainsaw; however, the weapon was not well received by Disney, which led Nomura to redesign the weapon into a Keyblade. He was also designed with lion-like features, which were removed as the staff found them to be similar to those of Final Fantasy IX protagonist Zidane Tribal. The design was further reworked after a talk with the Disney staff, and Nomura finished it after a night's work. Because Sora was a new character in the first Kingdom Hearts, Square added Final Fantasy characters who support the protagonist.

The CDP was referred to the Finance and public administration references committee for a Senate inquiry and report on 22 March 2017. The findings of the inquiry were handed down on 14 December 2017. The Senate inquiry handed down 22 recommendations, ranging from financial penalties to government support for remote communities. The report also concluded that "The committee is of the view that CDP cannot and should not continue in its current form. A new program needs to be developed which moves away from a centralised, top-down administration in which communities are told what to do and move towards a model where the local communities are empowered to make decisions that are best for them."

Van Gogh died about a year after creating the paintings. His brother, Theo, died a few months later and the two later paintings came to the possession of Theo's widow, Johanna van Gogh-Bonger (later Johanna Cohen Gosschalk-Bonger). The original study was in 1890 either gifted to Paul Gauguin or traded in exchange with the French artist. In 1899, the painting was acquired by Ambroise Vollard from Gauguin's art dealer Georges Chaudet. The piece was then acquired by the art collector Émile Schuffenecker who passed it down to Amédée Schuffenecker, who in turn sold it to Helene Kröller-Müller in April 1912. It has since been in the collection of the Kröller-Müller Museum.

In 1938 Charlotte was not allowed to attend the Gymnasium anymore, because of her pro-Austrian catholic-conservative beliefs and family. During World War II she served within the German Red Cross. In 1945 she took general qualification exams for the university entrance and studied Archaeology and History of art in Innsbruck. In 1956 she took her degrees. She studied Political Science in Harvard and made her master's degree in 1960 and her PhD in 1973. Between 1960 and 1973 she was also a college teacher. From 1982 to 1992 she taught as a visiting professor in the Institute of Political Science of the University of Vienna and worked also after 1992 with students in Vienna. She also worked with the United Nations.

On Rotten Tomatoes, *Final Account* holds an approval rating of 92% based on 91 reviews, with an average rating of 7.6/10. The site's consensus describes the film as a "chillingly valuable viewing" despite not being a definitive statement. Metacritic sampled 20 critics, assigning the film a weighted average score of 70 out of 100, indicating "generally favorable reviews." The documentary has been praised for its sobering exploration of complicity and accountability during the Holocaust.

Trevor Smith began his academic career as an Assistant Lecturer at the University of Exeter, serving from 1959 to 1961. He then worked as a Research Officer for the Acton Society Trust, established by the Joseph Rowntree Social Service Trust, from 1961 to 1962, focusing on advancing social justice and influencing public thought. In 1962, he became a Lecturer in Politics at the University of Hull, marking the start of his long academic tenure.

A study published in 2017 by the IEEE and the ACM has analysed the main software products used for the teaching of modeling. It was based on an international sample of 150 academics. StarUML was the third most used product with 23.9% of the respondents having used it in their courses.

The Gibson Custom Shop launched the Collector's Choice series in 2010, featuring meticulous recreations of rare vintage guitars, including a limited run based on Greeny. An Artist's Proof version, owned by Gary Moore, was auctioned in 2021. In 2023, Gibson released the Gary Moore Les Paul Standard, inspired by Greeny, featuring a Lemonburst finish, weight-relieved body, and a quarter-sawn Grade-A mahogany neck with a rounded '50s profile measuring 0.818" at the 1st fret and 0.963" at the 12th.

In 2007, under the leadership of Sister Dawn Tomaszewski, General Superior of the Sisters of Providence, the congregation founded Providence Cristo Rey High School in Indianapolis, Indiana. The school, part of the Cristo Rey Network, focuses on providing economically disadvantaged students with a college-preparatory education through a unique work-study program. Students are placed with corporate partners to earn part of their tuition, and the school opened at the former site of IPS School 50. Providence Cristo Rey graduated its first class in 2011. School buses offer curb-to-school service for students with special circumstances, and the school maintains a collection showcasing the student experience through academics and professional work.

Ball was a regular fixture on children's television from the mid 1970s and throughout the 1980s, presenting several series of science and technology programmes intended for children (including Think of a Number; Think Again; Think Backwards; Think...This Way and Johnny Ball Reveals All). He was also one of the hosts of pre-school programme Play School beginning in 1967 and continuing throughout the 1970s and beyond. As well as appearing on screen Ball wrote jokes for some shows including Crackerjack. All of these shows (except the ITV programme ...Reveals All) appeared on the BBC. Ball's shows were known for presenting scientific and technological principles in an entertaining and accessible way for young people.

Dixon was born on 22 March 1828 in Caistor, Lincolnshire, England. His forebears owned a large estate in Lincolnshire and were involved in draining The Fens. Dixon received his education at Caistor Grammar School and went to sea aged 14, working for Robert Brooks and Co. On his first voyage, he was wrecked at Pernambuco in Brazil and returned to England on the clipper Swordfish. Dixon went to Australia on several journeys and in 1844 or 1845, a journey took him to New Zealand. As first mate, he went into Port Phillip and they found that 400 ships had been abandoned by their crews for one of the Australian gold rushes, but due to his tact he managed to hold on to his crew. Bishop Selwyn offered Dixon command of his mission yacht but by then, he had decided to take up land in New Zealand on the advice of an Australian squatter, John Murphy, who had established himself at Cust. He left Robert Brooks and Co's employment in 1851 and came to New Zealand on the Samarang, with John Hall as a fellow passenger, which arrived in Lyttelton Harbour on 31 July 1852.

The nobles, spurred by Russian promises of support, formed the Tarnogród Confederation on 26 November 1715. The Confederation's marshal was Stanisław Ledóchowski. The Tarnogród Confederation was only the last and most notable of several confederations formed against Augustus at that time. The Confederates were supported by most of the Commonwealth's own army.

Somerset was the backdrop to another brief but important incident during the war, the Battle of Chelsea Creek. On the night of Saturday 27 May 1775, HMS Armed Schooner Diana, under the command of Lieutenant Thomas Graves, ran aground in Chelsea Creek while attempting to keep Americans from driving British livestock from Noddle's Island in Boston Harbor, at which point the American rebels set fire to the ship. HMS Somerset's tender, Britannia (under the command of Thomas Graves' brother Lieutenant John Graves), was able to rescue the Diana's company.

As of 31 December 2020, Zollikon had a population of approximately 13,000. In 2007, 17.1% of the population were foreign nationals. By 2008, the gender distribution was 47.9% male and 52.1% female. Over the past decade, the population grew at a rate of 10.1%. In 2000, 85.9% of residents spoke German, making it the most common language, followed by English (3.9%) and French (2.0%). These demographics reflect Zollikon's position as a predominantly German-speaking municipality with a modest but diverse international community.

Cleveley was the son of John Cleveley the Elder. He and his twin brother Robert were both, like their father, marine painters. John and Robert were both brought up and trained in dockyards, but (particularly by producing pictures especially for print reproduction) addressed much wider audiences with their art than their father had done.

She was the daughter of Dejazmatch Wube Hayle Maryam, an ethnic Amhara, and the prince of Semien province who became the virtual ruler of all of northern Ethiopia after his conquest of Tigray province and parts of modern day Eritrea during the 1830s and 1840s. Her mother was Woizero Lakiyaye, a noblewoman of Tigray who ruled in Tigrai region. Tiruwork Wube was a descendant of Ras Wolde Selassie who ruled much of the region and established his capital at Chalacot in the late 1700s. The family of Dejazmatch Wube were descended from Emperor Fasilides, and the Dejazmatch had long harbored ambitions of becoming Emperor himself. However, Tewodros II defeated Wube, and had himself crowned at the church of Derasge Maryam that Wube is said to have built for his own planned coronation. Tewodros is said to have especially hated Dejazmach Wube among his enemies and ordered that he be chained and imprisoned for life along with his sons. A deeply religious woman, Tiruwork Wube endured her father's condition by resorting to prayer and fasting, and planned on entering a convent.

By 1923, Vancouver had become the primary cultural, social, and religious centre of Punjabi Canadians and was home to the largest ethnic Indian population in North America. Throughout the mid-20th century, the Punjabi population in Canada faced restrictive immigration policies, which reflected broader discrimination against non-European groups. After World War Two, societal introspection led to policy changes, and in 1947, the Canadian government re-enfranchised the Indo-Canadian community, granting them the right to vote.

The Salesian Sisters Convent and School in the Cremisan Valley is part of the Salesian Society, founded by St. John Bosco in the late 19th century. Opened in 1960, it serves over 450 students across its primary school, kindergarten, and learning disabilities program, offering enrichment activities, arts, and outdoor recreation in afternoons and summer.

The main military academy for Uzbekistan's National Guard is the Academy of the National Guard. It was established on the basis of the Tashkent Higher Military Technical School of the Ministry of Internal Affairs, which originated from military unit No. 64405. The school was transferred to the Ministry of Internal Affairs on September 2, 1994, accepted its first cadets in 1994, and prepared its first graduates in 1997. In 2019, the Tashkent Higher Military Technical School was transferred to the National Guard and renamed the Academy of the National Guard.

Abbasi has written more than 100 books and 2,000 articles. In 1990, he invented the mitraic diary which he publishes each year. He has also written three books on this subject in multiple languages. He organized and produced more than 200 artistic programs, plays and festivals about the Eiffel Tower, Bois de Boulogne, Palais des congrès of Versailles, Théâtre de Paris, Casino de Paris, Theatre ADYAR, and Auditorium of Châtelet.

Eric Geboers died on May 6, 2018, in a drowning accident at Lake Miramar near Mol, Belgium, after jumping into the cold water to save his Japanese Shiba Inu, a gift from his wife. His body was recovered the following day. The dog survived the incident.

In 2011, Sanjeev Rajput won a gold medal at the ISSF World Cup in Changwon, South Korea, breaking his own national record for the third time and securing a quota for the 2012 London Olympics. He later finished 8th in the 50m Rifle 3 Positions event at the ISSF World Cup finals in Poland. At the 34th National Games in Ranchi, Bihar, he claimed five gold and one silver medal. Rajput competed in the 2012 Summer Olympics at the Royal Artillery Barracks in London but was unable to advance to the finals in the men’s 50m Rifle 3 Positions event.

Bandy in its original, informal manner disappeared from the North American continent entirely once it and elements from the early game had become absorbed into a new sport of ice hockey. While ice hockey was growing and organizing in the United States, bandy was doing the same, but only in Europe and Scandinavia. It would not arrive in its organized format in the United States until the 1970s, almost a century after its initial development.

The town is named for the Ngāti Tamaoho settlement in the area called Pokino, located to the northwest of the current town centre. A military installation, Pokino Camp, was set up in the area in December 1861 after Governor George Edward Grey called for a road to be completed from Drury to the Waikato River, Great South Road. In mid-1862, work began on Queen's Redoubt, a military base which would become General Cameron's base of operations for the Invasion of the Waikato, housing 450 soldiers. The Māori village of Pokino to the north of Queen's Redoubt was deserted in July 1863 just prior to the start of the invasion (officially the village was listed as abandoned, though likely it had been ransacked by unauthorised soldiers). Queen's Redoubt was abandoned for military use in March 1867, with the buildings of the redoubt sold off by auction soon after.

Jeff Unaegbu served as an executive officer at the Lagos State Council of Tradesmen and Artisans, which operates under the Ministry of Commerce in Alausa, Ikeja. His first book, *Ode on Lagos (This Lagos Na Wa) and Other Poetic Portraits*, was published in January 2006 by Prize Publishers, with a later edition released in 2010 by Pitmak Books, Abuja, under the revised title *This Lagos Na Wa (Ode on Lagos) and Other Poetic Portraits*. The lead poem in the collection, spanning approximately 4,895 words and 78 stanzas across 25 pages, was praised for its length and structure, featuring a prologue and epilogue as long as average full-length poems. Newswatch magazine reviewed the poem on April 2, 2007, calling it "the longest poem by a Nigerian," while *The Roar* magazine described it as "the longest metrical poem in Africa." The 2010 edition included additional features, enhancing the work’s literary significance.

Bilderback was born in Seoul, South Korea and raised in Tulsa, Oklahoma and Dallas, Texas. She was adopted by American parents Jim and Lois Bilderback, through the Holt International Children's Services program. She was a cheerleader in junior high school and has two older brothers. She studied dancing from a very early age and at the age of 14 enrolled at the Dallas Young Actor's Studio.

The original Broadway production opened at the Globe Theatre on October 15, 1931, moved to the George M. Cohan Theater on May 24, 1932, and ran for a total of 395 performances, an unusual success for the Depression years. It was produced by Max Gordon and staged by José Ruben. The show featured Bettina Hall as Shirley Sheridan, Georges Metaxa as Victor Florescu, George Meader as Pompineau, Odette Myrtil as prima donna Odette, and Flora Le Breton as Maizie Gripps. Eddie Foy Jr. and Lawrence Grossmith were also in the original cast. Ensemble dances were staged by Albertina Rasch and featured the Albertina Rasch Dancers.

Generalbezirk Estland was the last of the four districts to be formally established on 5 December 1941. It was organized on the territory of German-occupied Estonia, which had until then been under the military administration of the Wehrmacht's Army Group North. The capital of Generalbezirk Estland was Tallinn (Reval).

The highest peaks of the Tofane group are Tofana di Mezzo (3,244 m (10,643 ft)), Tofana di Dentro (3,238 m (10,623 ft)), and Tofana di Rozes (3,225 m (10,581 ft)). Tofana di Mezzo is the third highest peak in the Dolomites, after Marmolada (3,343 m (10,968 ft)) and Antelao (3,262 m (10,702 ft)). All three peaks were first climbed by Paul Grohmann along with local mountain guides, in 1863 (Tofana di Mezzo - with Francesco Lacedelli), 1864 (Tofana di Rozes - with Francesco Lacedelli, Angelo Dimai and Santo Siorpaes) and 1865 (Tofana di Dentro - with Angelo Dimai).

On July 28, 2011, the Washington Redskins signed Smith as an undrafted free agent. Due to the suspension of Trent Williams, he played his first professional game in Week 14 of the 2011 season, rotating the left tackle position with Sean Locklear. In Week 15 against the New York Giants, Smith would start his first game instead of Locklear. He continued to start as the left tackle for the rest of the 2011 season. Smith has received praise for his ability to defend against elite pass rushers Jason Pierre-Paul, Jared Allen, and Trent Cole, despite being a rookie.

Ferguson trademarks in the UK are registered under class 09 for audio-visual equipment. Registration EU010787471 by Ferguson Sp. z.o.o. dates to 04/04/2012, while EU003131927, registered by Thomson Multimedia (now Technicolor), dates back to 26/09/1946.

William Henry Seaman was born on November 15, 1842, in New Berlin, Wisconsin Territory. He was the son of Williams Seaman, who brought his family to Wisconsin from Ceresco in 1840, and Arelisle (Crane) Seaman, a descendant of the Crane family. His paternal grandfather, also named Williams Seaman, served in the New York State Legislature.

Stability in marriage has been a topic of significant interest, particularly when comparing arranged and autonomous unions. In the United States and parts of the European Union, where autonomous marriages dominate, divorce rates have shown varying trends. While the U.S. has experienced a decline in divorce rates since the 1980s, some European countries have seen increases, though rates in others, like the United Kingdom, have declined between 2004 and 2015. Cultures with high rates of arranged marriages, such as those in India and parts of the Orthodox Jewish community, exhibit some of the lowest divorce rates globally. For instance, the divorce rate among Hindus in India is approximately 2.0 per 1,000 married individuals, while the global divorce rate for arranged marriages is just 6%, according to a 2012 study by Statistic Brain. Among the Amish in the U.S., divorce is virtually nonexistent due to cultural and religious prohibitions. In Israel, however, divorce rates among Ultra-Orthodox Jews are rising, approaching those of the secular population. Scholars continue to debate whether the stability of arranged marriages stems from shared values and societal expectations or limitations on personal agency compared to autonomous marriages.

In July 2011, Price and multiple associates were served with search warrants obtained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation arising out of acts of alleged public corruption. The warrant on Price's residence specified a search for data related to violations of the U.S. Code Titles 18, 26, or 31, specifically "Theft or Bribery Concerning Programs Receiving Federal Funds," tax evasion, fraud, false statements, money laundering, and "aiding and abetting and conspiracy to violate these statutes." The FBI seized multiple assets belonging to Price. Price is currently being investigated by the FBI. According to Federal sources, Price was arrested by the FBI on Friday, July 25, 2014. His trial has been scheduled for January 2016 and will have to contribute $80,000 towards his own defense.

Škriniar began his career in the youth structures of FK Žiar nad Hronom before joining the youth setup of MŠK Žilina at age 12. He made his official Slovak Super Liga debut for the first team on 27 March 2012 aged 17 years and 49 days, in a match against ViOn Zlaté Moravce. On 23 November 2012 he scored his first goal in the Slovak Super Liga against ViOn during a 4–1 victory.

At 16 years old Sak began to play regularly in senior teams. In 2006, he joined Drawa Thule Krzyż Wlkp. After six months he was promoted to the Polish fourth division played for Sokół Pniewy until January 2008. He went on a one-week trial at Championship side Cardiff City and eventually signed for the club. On 19 February he was included in the first team for the first time when he was named as a substitute in the FAW Premier Cup semi-final against Newport County.

Sheldon became a "missionary-deaconess" of the Methodist Episcopal Church in 1889, to serve in Darjeeling. After six years, she and Annie Budden moved to a mission post in the Pithoragarh district, near the India-Nepal border (in present-day Nepal). There she served from 1895 to 1912, most of those years with Eva C. M. Browne. The couple ran a small farm with vegetables, fruits, and cows. She learned the spoken Bhotiya language, devised a written version to record it, and composed translations of Christian texts for local use. She and Browne began a kindergarten, organized schools for girls and women, opened a clinic, built a church, and hosted visiting missionaries.

Hext is from Truro, Cornwall. and has also lived near Sherston, Wiltshire. In 2012, she was paralysed in a horse riding accident, and required the use of a wheelchair. She watched the 2012 Summer Paralympics from her hospital bed. In November 2021, she had a cochlear implant, which allowed her to hear again. In her spare time, Hext volunteers for the Wiltshire Air Ambulance.

Nair is a member of the World Economic Forum Global Agenda Council for Sustainability and has argued at numerous forums including the WEF, APEC and OECD about the need for radical reform of the current economic model and strict limits on consumption. Nair was previously Chairman of Environmental Resources Management (ERM), building the company to be the leading environmental consultancy in Asia Pacific. He left in March 2004.

In 1996, midway through the inaugural MLS season, Lagos was signed by the MetroStars. He impressed right away, but his season was cut short after he tore his left-knee's ACL, MCL, and LCL in a single horrific injury just six games in. Lagos made a comeback in the next season, playing in 15 league matches, but still impaired and less effective than before. Frustrated with his injuries, the Metros exposed Manny in the 1997 MLS Expansion Draft, where he was taken by the Chicago Fire. Nowhere near fully recovered and considering retirement, he only played one game for the MLS Cup champions in 1998, and went on to make only 9 appearances in 1999, always as a substitute, excluded from the first eleven due to his unreliable health and the Fire's surplus of attacking players.

"May Pagsinta’y Walang Puso," a novel by Iñigo Ed. Regalado from the early 20th century, showcases the depth of the Tagalog language during a time when adultery was a sensitive topic in Philippine literature. Roberto T. Añonuevo described the language as challenging, urging readers to delve into the intricacies of Tagalog and the nation’s history. Regalado employed subtle, metaphorical expressions to navigate societal taboos, particularly in portraying women with sensitivity and nuance. By using refined and evocative language, he avoided vulgarity while engaging readers in the complex themes of love, betrayal, and forgiveness, reflecting the cultural and moral tensions of the colonial era.

Many Hmong immigrated to Michigan after the end of the Vietnam War in the 1970s. The Hmong had moved to Detroit in order to obtain employment and so members of the same families could live in the same area. Hmong people had migrated to Detroit from various places in the United States. As of that year, Metro Detroit's Hmong population is smaller than the major Hmong populations in California, Minnesota, and Wisconsin.

Keith Lambert began his professional cycling career in 1972, signing his first contract with the Falcon cycling team under the management of Ernie Clements. His first professional victory came in the Grand Prix of Essex. On 6 August 1972, Lambert competed in the Milk Race, finishing as the top British rider. In 1974, he joined the Holdsworth team, where he raced alongside notable teammates such as Les West and Colin Lewis. That same year, Lambert claimed his first British National Road Race Championship title. He also competed in the 1976 Paris-Roubaix, finishing the grueling event. In 1978, Lambert signed with the TI-Raleigh team and placed 12th at the 1979 World Championship in Valkenburg aan de Geul. He joined Weinmann in 1980, finishing second in the Druivenkoers Overijse, and later that year, he narrowly defeated Bill Nickson to win his second British National Road Race Championship in Redditch. Lambert rejoined Falcon in 1983, where he won the British Professional Circuit Race Championship, and competed in the 1982 World Championship in Goodwood. Over his 15-year professional career, spanning from 1972 to 1987, Lambert achieved 61 professional victories, cementing his legacy as one of Britain’s most accomplished cyclists.

The gameplay of *Tales of the Heroes: Twin Brave* resembles the *Dynasty Warriors* series, featuring action-packed, beat-'em-up combat. Players control pairs of characters from 15 *Tales of* titles, battling waves of enemies and bosses. Antagonists like Elraine and Schwartz can be unlocked, offering additional challenges. The game supports both single-player and two-player modes.

Hildegard of Bingen was one of the first persons for whom the Roman canonization process was officially applied, though four attempts, the last in 1244 under Pope Innocent IV, were not completed, leaving her at the level of beatification. Her name was included in the Roman Martyrology in the late 16th century, and her feast is celebrated on September 17. Popes John Paul II and Benedict XVI referred to her as a saint. Her parish and pilgrimage church in Eibingen near Rüdesheim houses her relics, including her encased earthly remains.

As of the 2001 India census, Jaitwara, a small town in northeastern Madhya Pradesh near the Uttar Pradesh border, had a population of approximately 8,500. Males constituted 52% of the population, while females made up 48%. The average literacy rate was 64%, higher than the national average of 59.5%, with male literacy at 72% and female literacy at 55%. About 15% of the population was under 6 years of age. The nearest city to Jaitwara is Satna.

After the Second World War, No. 617 Squadron RAF replaced its Avro Lancasters with Avro Lincolns, later transitioning to the English Electric Canberra jet bomber in 1952. The squadron was deployed to Malaya for four months in 1955 before returning to RAF Binbrook, where it was disbanded on 15 December 1955. It reformed at RAF Scampton on 1 May 1958 as part of RAF Bomber Command's V-bomber force, tasked with maintaining the UK’s strategic nuclear deterrent. From August 1960, the squadron operated the Avro Vulcan B1, which was upgraded to the Vulcan B1A with an electronic countermeasures (ECM) tail pod by 23 May 1961. The squadron’s role involved high-level strategic bombing, equipped with a variety of free-fall nuclear bombs, including British designs such as Yellow Sun Mk2. For a short period, American nuclear bombs were also supplied under Project E arrangements. This period marked the squadron’s transition to a key role in the Cold War, contributing to the UK’s nuclear deterrence strategy within the framework of RAF Bomber Command.

Born in the Auckland suburb of Milford, Penney attended Westlake Boys High School and played junior basketball for the North Harbour Basketball Association, joining their New Zealand NBL team, the North Harbour Kings, in 1998 as a 17-year-old. He earned NZNBL Rookie of the Year honours that year and helped the Kings reach the grand final. He also played for the Kings in 1999 and 2000.

Delzie Demaree was born on September 15, 1889, in Benham, Ripley County, Indiana. He attended Danville High School and later served in the U.S. Marine Corps during World War I from 1917 to 1919, enduring injuries and exposure to gas. In 1920, he earned a B.S. in botany from Indiana University. Demaree began focusing on botanical specimen collection in 1922 while teaching at Hendricks College. He completed his PhD at Stanford University in 1932 and subsequently taught at institutions in Arkansas and Texas.

Ruth, born to an abusive father she called Tateh (Yiddish for "father"), rarely discussed the details of her early family life, shrouding it in secrecy. Tateh, a deeply racist and arrogant man, mistreated Ruth’s gentle and kind mother, Mameh (Yiddish for "mother"), who was crippled by polio and never learned English despite living in America for years. Ruth’s decision to leave her Jewish family was driven by her desire to escape Tateh’s oppressive household. Her family disowned her after she married Andrew McBride, a Black Christian man, defying their strict racial and religious prejudices. Though Ruth found freedom in her new life, she carried guilt over leaving Mameh, whose health had deteriorated. Ruth’s story reflects her struggle to reconcile her past with her present, as well as her resilience in overcoming societal and familial constraints.

The Hindu consciousness, shaped significantly during the Classical period (circa 800 A.D.–1700 A.D.), entered the 20th century with Hindus forming about 80% of India’s population. Hindus played a central role in the nationalist movement, seeking to unify and reform Hindu society. In 1925, Dr. Keshav Baliram Hedgewar founded the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) in Maharashtra to promote Hindu unity and discipline. Over time, the RSS grew into a powerful organization, with over 585,000 members and influence across various sectors of Indian society.

Retaj Al Rayyan Mecca, a 4-star hotel in Mecca, Saudi Arabia, features 22 stories and 670 rooms and suites. The hotel offers a restaurant, fitness center, and meeting rooms. According to Trade Arabia, it marks the Retaj Group's first step in its GCC expansion plan, particularly in Saudi Arabia.

The case *Marcus v. Search Warrant* was handed down in 1961, establishing First Amendment protections requiring particularity in warrants targeting expressive material. In *United States v. Espinoza* (1992), the Eleventh Circuit rejected a defense claim that operating an unlicensed money services business was protected under the First Amendment. The Eighth Circuit, in 1983, affirmed Marcus's protections, emphasizing that searches targeting expressive material must meet heightened particularity standards. Forty years later, in 2023, the Fifth Circuit ruled that Marcus's protections did not apply to seizures of material when the search's purpose was unrelated to the ideas contained within, such as corroborating victim testimony.

The 2004 UN report on human population projections assumed continued progress in life expectancy, with global averages expected to exceed 95 years by 2300. By 2100, life expectancy was projected to range between 85 and 90 years, rising further to over 100 years in some countries, such as Japan, by 2300. These increases were anticipated to slow population growth, with annual growth rates declining to near zero by 2300. However, hypothetical factors, such as sustained high fertility or advancements in life-extending technologies, could drive long-term population growth beyond 2100. The report highlighted the significance of technological innovations in extending life spans, which, alongside behavioral improvements, could further influence demographic trends and the global population trajectory over the coming centuries.

Acamas was a strong, deep-bodied bay horse with a white coronet on his left hind foot bred in the United Kingdom by Marcel Boussac. He was one of many successful racehorses sired by Mill Reef, an American-bred horse who won the Epsom Derby, King George VI and Queen Elizabeth Stakes and Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe in 1971. His dam Licata, was a very good racemare who won the Prix Cléopâtre and Prix de Malleret in 1972. She was even better as a broodmare, going on to produce the Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud winner Akarad and the 1982 Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe winner Akiyda. Boussac sent his colt into training with Guy Bonnaventure.

On December 12, 2013, at the Central Urbanization Work Conference, Xi Jinping, General Secretary of the Communist Party of China and the country’s top political leader, emphasized the need to improve urban drainage systems by building sponge cities. He proposed cities that prioritize natural accumulation, natural infiltration, and natural purification to manage rainwater effectively. Following this, the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development issued the "Provisional Technical Guidelines for Sponge City Construction" on October 22, 2014, which highlighted low-impact development (LID) strategies for urban rainwater management, marking a significant step in implementing the sponge city concept nationwide.

The "Intelligence Management System" featured in Voyage is a score assigned to the player by the Selenites, the natives of the Moon. For each puzzle the player solves, and for certain actions, this score is increased, and the Selenites treat the player with more respect. During an interview with GamersInfo, Benoît Hozjan, managing director and co-founder of Kheops Studio, described the system, saying:

After retiring as a football player, Branko Oblak embarked on a coaching career, managing several clubs, including NK Olimpija Ljubljana, where he had two separate stints as head coach. He first took charge of Olimpija in the 1994/95 season, leading the team to a national championship title, and returned to manage the club again in 2009. Oblak was named the Slovenia under-21 national coach in 2002 and later replaced Bojan Prašnikar as the head coach of the Slovenia national football team in May 2004. The highlight of his tenure was a victory over Italy during the 2006 World Cup qualifiers. However, after failing to qualify for the tournament, Oblak was dismissed in November 2006. During his time as Slovenia's coach, he utilized over 40 players. He was succeeded by Matjaž Kek as the national team coach. Oblak’s contributions to Slovenian football remain significant, both as a player and a manager.

Music Box received mixed reviews from critics, many of whom found the performances uninvolved and devoid of substantial songwriting. In Rolling Stone, Stephen Holden said the lyrics were "made up entirely of pop and soul clichés" on an album "so precisely calculated to be a blockbuster that its impact is ultimately a little unnerving". Christopher John Farley from Time called it "perfunctory and almost passionless" in spite of highlights in "Anytime You Need a Friend" and the title track. In Entertainment Weekly, David Browne found Carey's performance low on energy and her voice no longer soaring above the backup chorus. Instead, he wrote in Entertainment Weekly, she "drips over them like syrup instead of overpowering them; she lets the melodies speak for themselves." Dennis Hunt was particularly critical in the Los Angeles Times, writing that Carey's pop-soul songs still lacked emotion even though she had "toned down her vocal showboating". He accused Music Box of being geared toward an adult contemporary audience that "likes its soul whitewashed and in small doses". Village Voice critic Robert Christgau labeled it a "dud", indicating "a bad record whose details rarely merit further thought".

With all the songs featuring co-writes from Cave and Harvey, it was said The Bad Seed, "forecasts the imminent future of Cave as a solo artist and Harvey as his right-hand man in more than just title." Howard's guitar work on the E.P. was particularly praised. The song "Deep in the Woods", "ends in all on a perfectly outrageous note, with Cave at his most sepulchral conjuring up a rural scene of devastation. The delivery and lyrics redefine the phrase "over the top.""

On September 17, excerpts from a video recorded on hidden camera were published by Mother Jones showing Romney speaking at a private $50,000-a-plate fundraiser held at hedge fund manager Marc Leder's mansion in Boca Raton, Florida. The magazine learned of the video from James Earl Carter IV, a Democratic opposition researcher (and a grandson of former U.S. president Jimmy Carter), who found the video online and got the videographer in touch with a writer at the magazine. On March 13, 2013, on MSNBC's The Ed Show, Scott Prouty, a bartender at the event, revealed himself as the videographer.

In 1963, Ruth Ella Moore published her research on the sensitivity of gut microorganisms to antibiotics, focusing on microbes isolated from the gut of the death's head cockroach. She cultured the isolated microbes using trypticase soy agar, a nutrient-rich medium suitable for bacterial growth. To assess antimicrobial susceptibility, Moore employed the disk diffusion method, placing antibiotic-impregnated disks at various concentrations onto the agar plates. After incubation, she evaluated bacterial sensitivity by observing zones of inhibition—clear areas around the disks where microbial growth was suppressed. Bacteria were considered resistant if no zone of inhibition formed around the antibiotic disk. While the study primarily focused on identifying antibiotic resistance and sensitivity, Moore's work also contributed to understanding the differentiation between bacteriostatic antibiotics, which inhibit bacterial growth, and bactericidal antibiotics, which kill bacteria. Her meticulous methodology and innovative approach provided valuable insights into the interactions between gut microbes and antibiotics, advancing the field of microbiology and laying groundwork for future research on microbial resistance.

The fourth season was produced by ABC Studios, Bad Robot Productions and Grass Skirt Productions. Damon Lindelof and Carlton Cuse served as the season's show runners. The show was primarily filmed in Hawaii with post-production in Los Angeles. Lindelof and Cuse's fellow executive producers were co-creator J. J. Abrams, Bryan Burk and Jack Bender. The staff writers were Lindelof, Cuse, co-executive producers Edward Kitsis, Adam Horowitz, and Drew Goddard, supervising producer Elizabeth Sarnoff, co-producer Brian K. Vaughan and executive story editor Christina M. Kim. The regular directors were Bender and co-executive producer Stephen Williams.

The term "cement" originates from the Latin word *caementum*, meaning "rough-cut stone" or "hewn stone piece," later associated with binding. Cement, a key construction material, reacts with water through hydration, forming a hardened paste that binds aggregates. Fibre cement incorporates cellulose fibre reinforcement with Portland cement, enhancing strength and durability. Combined with a precise production process, fibre cement becomes a robust, long-lasting material used for cladding, lining, and decorative construction applications.

The average amount of time people spend commuting with public transit in Bogotá, for example to and from work, on a weekday is 97 min. 32% of public transit riders, ride for more than 2 hours every day. The average amount of time people wait at a stop or station for public transit is 20 min, while 40% of riders wait for over 20 minutes on average every day. The average distance people usually ride in a single trip with public transit is 8 km, while 16% travel for over 12 km in a single direction.

Matt Kaeberlein earned a B.S. in Biochemistry and a B.A. in Mathematics from Western Washington University in 1997. He completed his Ph.D. in Biology at MIT in 2002, where he was advised by Leonard Guarente. He later conducted post-doctoral research with Peter S. Rabinovitch at the University of Washington.

In October 2020, Robert F. Smith, then Chairman and CEO of Vista Equity Partners, was named alongside Robert T. Brockman in a tax evasion case. Smith signed a non-prosecution agreement with the IRS, admitting to evading $43 million in taxes and agreeing to pay $140 million in penalties. In November 2020, Vista President Brian N. Sheth departed the firm.

The Moderate party gained official party status and ballot access via a federal lawsuit and the gathering of 34,000 signatures on August 18, 2009. The Moderates fielded candidates for various state offices in the 2010 elections. The Party Founder and 2010 gubernatorial candidate, Kenneth Block, stated that the party's primary focus would be the Rhode Island General Assembly; however, the possibility of further candidates for governor and other state offices was likely.

The most common methods for silver nanoparticle synthesis fall under the category of wet chemistry, which involves the nucleation of particles within a solution. This process begins when a silver ion complex, such as AgNO3 or AgClO4, is reduced to colloidal silver (Ag) in the presence of a reducing agent. As the concentration of dissolved metallic silver ions increases, the ions bind together to form a stable surface. However, when the cluster is small, the surface is energetically unfavorable because the energy gained by reducing the concentration of dissolved particles is outweighed by the energy required to create a new surface. Once the cluster reaches a specific size, known as the critical radius, it becomes energetically favorable and stable enough to continue growing. At this stage, the nucleus remains in the system and grows as additional silver atoms diffuse through the solution and attach to its surface. When the dissolved concentration of atomic silver decreases below a certain threshold, new nanoparticles can no longer form, and the remaining silver is absorbed by diffusion into the existing nanoparticles, halting further nucleation.

In April and May 2012, France held a presidential election in which the winner, François Hollande, had opposed austerity measures, promising to eliminate France's budget deficit by 2017 by canceling recently enacted tax cuts and exemptions for the wealthy, raising the top tax bracket rate to 75% on incomes over one million euros, restoring the retirement age to 60 with a full pension for those who have worked 42 years, restoring 60,000 jobs recently cut from public education, regulating rent increases, and building additional public housing for the poor. In the legislative elections in June, Hollande's Socialist Party won a supermajority capable of amending the French Constitution and enabling the immediate enactment of the promised reforms. Interest rates on French government bonds fell by 30% to record lows, fewer than 50 basis points above German government bond rates.

In 2018, Lima made the move to the United States to play college soccer at Providence College. Here he went on to make 50 appearances for the Friars, scoring 13 goals and tallying six assists. In 2019, he was named to the All-Big East second team and was named the team's Offensive Most Valuable Player, and in his junior and senior years Lima was named to the All-Big East first team.

In Binningen about 5,523 or (39.5%) of the population have completed non-mandatory upper secondary education, and 3,031 or (21.7%) have completed additional higher education (either university or a Fachhochschule). Of the 3,031 who completed tertiary schooling, 53.8% were Swiss men, 28.1% were Swiss women, 10.5% were non-Swiss men and 7.7% were non-Swiss women. As of 2000, there were 206 students in Binningen who came from another municipality, while 643 residents attended schools outside the municipality.

Alcohol is metabolized by a normal liver at the rate of about 8 grams of pure ethanol per hour. 8 grams or 10 mL (0.34 US fl oz) is one British standard unit. An "abnormal" liver with conditions such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, gall bladder disease, and cancer is likely to result in a slower rate of metabolism.

Willie Smith was signed by the Washington Redskins as an undrafted free agent on July 26, 2011. He played his first professional game in Week 14 of the 2011 season, rotating at left tackle with Sean Locklear. In Week 15, Smith made his first start at left tackle against the New York Giants, replacing the injured Trent Williams. For the remainder of the season, Smith continued to start at left tackle, earning praise for his performance against elite pass rushers like Jason Pierre-Paul and Jared Allen. His athleticism and quick adaptation to the NFL highlighted his potential as a reliable offensive lineman.

Ludwig Kietzmann of GamesRadar+ stated that he liked the character of Kat, calling her an "exuberant girl in a scarf" that stood in contrast to most superheroes, who were a "tortured dude in a cape". He also praised how she was a "delightful klutz", which tied into the game's initially imprecise controls. Stating "there's an art to being utterly graceless in Gravity Rush", he stated that he imagined her apologizing to the people caught up in her gravity powers as she passed by and said that "Kat's optimism in the face of her innate clumsiness is what makes her special".

Dwight D. Eisenhower appointed five justices to the Supreme Court of the United States, including Earl Warren, whom he nominated in 1953 following the death of Chief Justice Fred Vinson. Warren, a former Republican Governor of California, was initially appointed via recess appointment and formally confirmed by the Senate in March 1954. Warren presided over the landmark *Brown v. Board of Education* decision that year, which declared segregation in public schools unconstitutional. Although Eisenhower enforced desegregation during the 1957 Little Rock crisis, he was unenthusiastic about the *Brown* ruling. The death of Justice Robert H. Jackson in late 1954 created another vacancy, and Eisenhower nominated John Marshall Harlan II, who joined the Court’s conservative bloc. Harlan was confirmed in 1955, further shaping the ideological balance of the Court.

Dean Gengle wrote in his book, The Netweaver's Sourcebook, that the book: "..did more to liberate media-created hippies than just about any other work of its time." The book has also been referenced in later sociological evaluations of potential paths for society, including Michael Marien's Societal Directions and Alternatives, and Gurth Higgin's Symptoms of Tomorrow. The book's publisher, Capricorn Press or "Capra Press", would come to be better known, having published the work.

In 1974, The Waiters performed their first annual "Spring Fever" concert. The Waiters performed this show annually until 2016. The show was mentioned on various blogs, including Slope Media, which described the show as "taking [...] A Cappella to another level." In the early 1990s, the Waiters added a December Holiday Show to their yearly concert repertoire. The Holiday Show was performed every December through the 2000s.

Nathan Bennett and Stephen A. Miles, authors of *Riding Shotgun: The Role of the COO*, analyze the COO's responsibilities, success factors, classifications, and their critical relationship with the CEO.

In 1852, Jean-Baptiste Bonnemaison, a quarry worker, excavated the embankment and platform in front of the Cave of Aurignac, uncovering animal fossils and 17 human skeletons sealed within the cave by a stone slab. The local mayor ordered the skeletons re-buried in the parish cemetery, with Dr. Amiel, the town physician, overseeing the process. Years later, Edouard Lartet, a pioneer in prehistoric studies, and others attempted to locate the re-burial site for scientific examination but were met with resistance from church custodians, who refused to disclose its location. The discovery marked the cave as a significant archaeological site, later identified as the type site for the Aurignacian culture.

The treatment of performers in Gulag theaters varied significantly across camps and over time. At Solovki, the earliest theater group, organized in 1923, consisted of both professional and amateur actors who rehearsed after grueling 12-hour days of forced labor. Performances were held in the Assumption Cathedral, with no special privileges afforded to the actors beyond participation in cultural activities. In contrast, musicians in Kazimierz Zarod’s camp were treated slightly better; after passing through the gates, they played instruments to boost morale before joining other prisoners in heavy labor. Despite harsh conditions, these cultural efforts provided brief respite and a semblance of humanity within the brutal camp system.

DNA damage response (DDR) is the overarching mechanism which mediates the cell's detection and response to DNA damage. This includes the process of detecting DSB within the cell, and the subsequent triggering and regulation of DSB repair pathways. Upstream detections of DNA damage via DDR will lead to the activation of downstream responses such as senescence, cell apoptosis, halting transcription and activating DNA repair mechanisms. Proteins such as the proteins ATM, ATR and DNA-dependent protein kinase (DNA-PK) are vital for the process of detection of DSB in DDR, and these proteins are recruited to the DSB site in the DNA. In particular, ATM has been identified as the protein kinase in charge of the global meditation of cellular responses to DSB, which includes various DSB repair pathways. Following the recruitment of the aforementioned proteins to DNA damage sites, they will in turn trigger cellular responses and repair pathways to mitigate and repair the damage caused. In short, these vital upstream proteins and downstream repair pathways altogether forms the DDR, which plays a vital role in DSB repair pathways regulation.

Twenty-three conferences earned an automatic bid. In fourteen cases, the automatic bid was the only representative from the conference. Two conferences, Great Midwest and North Atlantic sent a single representative as an at-large team. Twenty-three additional at-large teams were selected from nine of the conferences.

There are no official policies regarding human by-catch data among research and conservation organizations. Each organization may have its own policies. The technology is still emerging, but there are potential solutions. There are software programs, whose original intent is to ease the burden of the thousands of images that must be checked and logged during a camera trap study. The programs assist in automating the process of filtering out the false positives; photos that were triggered without the target species. Along with filtering out empty frames, this software can detect human presence, and filter out photos of humans. This way, privacy could be ensured if the process was automated, so that no person had access to the photos of the people that were inadvertently taken. Most of the government policies and laws regarding privacy with these technologies are aimed at drones.

The grey fantail is territorial and it is a seasonal breeder. They raise several broods per season, usually each of three or four cream eggs, spotted grey and brown. The incubation period is around two weeks, with incubation and feeding duties shared by both parents. During the breeding season, the mass of testes of male grey fantails grows larger than in the non-breeding season. The growth of testes sizes could be subject to sperm competition, similar to other species exhibiting morphological traits. Male birds also have pronounced cloacal protuberances. This change of morphology is associated with polygynandry and the increase in copulation chance. Despite most grey fantails forming season-long monogamous pairs, a small number of male birds seeking extra-pair copulation have been recorded. To seek extra-pair copulation, the male bird invades neighbouring territories and waits at the nest where the female birds were not incubating. The intruding male might coerce a resident female into accepting extra-pair male copulation. Moreover, the aggression of the male bird could act as a deterrent to the female bird, leading to delayed ovulation, allowing the male to re-mate. This increases the likelihood of the male becoming the sole fertiliser of that female's eggs.

Prior to the expiration of the NHL Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) on September 15, 2012, the Vancouver Canucks reassigned several players to their AHL affiliate, the Chicago Wolves, including Zack Kassian, Eddie Lack, Chris Tanev, and Kevin Connauton. Brendan Gaunce, their 2012 first-round draft pick, was sent to the Belleville Bulls of the OHL. When the CBA expired, the NHL initiated a league-wide lockout. During this period, the Canucks management posted a message to fans expressing hope for a resolution while reaffirming their commitment to the community. To mitigate financial strain, the organization reduced employees' work weeks by one day and implemented a 20% pay cut. These measures mirrored actions taken by other NHL teams as they prepared for the work stoppage.

On September 3, 2014, Karen Yang, a friend of Li's, got a call from him. He said that he had called Shao. For some reason she did not answer the phone, but he could hear her conversing with another boy about him, making disparaging remarks. After calling Yang, he posted "fuck my life" in Chinese to his page on Renren, a social networking site popular with Chinese college students. It was his last post to the site.

Marmaduke Dixon was born in 1828 in Caistor, Lincolnshire, the third son of James Dixon and grandson of Thomas Dixon, a renowned sheep breeder. The Dixon family resided at Holton Park, their ancestral seat for four centuries, and were instrumental in the drainage and reclamation of the surrounding fenlands. Marmaduke received his education at Caistor Grammar School but, being a delicate child, was deemed unsuited to the unhealthy fen environment. At the age of 14, he was apprenticed to a shipping firm, Robert Brooks and Co., and embarked on a life at sea. On his first voyage, he was shipwrecked off the coast of Brazil but returned to England aboard the ship Senator. Over the following years, he made several voyages to Australia, and in 1845, during one of these journeys, he first visited New Zealand. By 1851, as first mate, he experienced the challenges of the Australian gold rush, which left many ships stranded without crews. Bishop Selwyn later offered him command of a mission yacht, but Dixon was persuaded by Australian squatter John Murphy to consider settling in New Zealand. In 1852, he left Robert Brooks and Co., traveling aboard the Samarang, which arrived in Lyttelton Harbour in early 1853.

She started her career in short track speed skating in 2007. She made her debut in the 2012-13 ISU Short Track Speed Skating World Cup. In 2015, she became the first short track skater representing Poland to finish on the podium at a World Cup event when she placed second in the 500m in Montreal, Canada.

Natalia Maliszewska began her short track speed skating career in 2004. She debuted in the ISU Short Track Speed Skating World Cup during the 2010/2011 season. In 2015, representing Poland, she became the first Polish short track skater to podium at a World Cup event, finishing second in the 500m in Montreal, Canada.
Iryna Fedyshyn lives in Lviv, is married to producer Vitaliy Chovnyk, and they have two sons, Yuriy and Oleg.

Rachel DeLoache Williams, originally from Knoxville, Tennessee, is the daughter of two clinical psychologists. She graduated from Kenyon College in 2010 with degrees in English and Studio Arts. While a student, she interned at Art + Commerce and Harper’s Bazaar. Shortly after graduating, she joined Vanity Fair’s photo department, where she worked for nearly a decade, collaborating with leading photographers and iconic celebrities on high-profile shoots. Williams was laid off from Vanity Fair in 2019.

The Chinatown Handy Guides were published during the early 20th century, a period marked by the Progressive Era and the rise of social reform movements. Created by entrepreneur and community leader Fang, the guides focused on promoting Chinatown as a cultural and tourist destination while countering negative stereotypes about Chinese Americans. At the time, African Americans in the southern U.S. faced segregation under Jim Crow laws. San Francisco entrepreneurs recognized the economic potential of Chinatown, using the "model family concept" to both attract tourists and reshape public perceptions of Chinese immigrants.

The current approved maintenance therapies for inoperable locoregional Stage III NSCLC following induction chemotherapy are durvalumab and atezolizumab. Tarceva (erlotinib), developed by Genentech, and Alimta (pemetrexed), manufactured by Lilly, are established treatments for advanced NSCLC but have not been tested in combination with or compared to Tecemotide. Other agents, including vaccines, are under development for this indication. Notably, GSK's NSCLC vaccine is in Phase III trials, while Transgene's myvac platform-based vaccine is in earlier phases. Additionally, multiple vaccines, such as Tecemotide and others targeting neoantigens, are being studied, reflecting growing interest in immunotherapy for NSCLC. The competitive landscape suggests potential for new approvals in maintenance therapy for this patient population.

The predecessor of OFD, Ostfriesische Lufttaxi - Dekker und Janssen OHG, was founded in November 1958 in Emden, initially offering air taxi services to North Sea islands. By the early 1970s, demand had grown significantly, and the airline expanded to German domestic routes, becoming the largest German regional airline. During the 1970s, new investors supported its growth, and OLT introduced regional flights, marking a shift from its original focus on island connections.

Jessie Joe Parker represented Papua New Guinea in 2007, playing matches against France and Wales in Europe. He was part of PNG's squad for the 2008 Rugby League World Cup. In 2009, he scored a try during the Pacific Cup. Parker also played for PNG in the 2010 Four Nations and scored a try against Samoa as part of PNG's 2013 World Cup squad.

The New York Times Building is located at 242 West 41st Street in the Midtown Manhattan neighborhood of New York City. It occupies the entire blockfront on the east side of Eighth Avenue, between 40th and 41st Streets, just a short distance southwest of Times Square. The building's land lot is rectangular, with a frontage of 400 feet along Eighth Avenue and 200 feet along both 40th and 41st Streets. The block is bounded to the east by Seventh Avenue. The site was designed with setbacks of 17 feet along Eighth Avenue and 8 feet along 40th and 41st Streets to improve pedestrian circulation in the area.

De Smet completed a postgraduate diploma at the Université Catholique de Louvain, and then carried out doctoral studies at St John's College, Cambridge, graduating PhD in 1993 with a thesis on Neo-Latin Menippean satire in the Low Countries and France. After that she spent three years as a prize fellow at Magdalen College, Oxford, and commenced a two-year British Academy postdoctoral fellowship in 1995.
