The system was a 560-meter automated people mover with two stations, designed to connect the RER A station Noisy-le-Grand – Mont d’Est to the new business district, Maille Horizon Nord. The project aimed to provide a seamless link between the transit hub and the emerging commercial area, enhancing accessibility and urban development.

In late 1839, William Barnard Rhodes returned to New Zealand in partnership with Daniel Cooper and James Holt to secure land in the new colony. He acquired deeds to over 60,000 acres and established trading stations with stock at strategic landings. Rhodes chose Wellington as his base, building premises in Te Aro in 1840 and completing a significant wharf in 1841. He lived in a large house, The Grange, in Wadestown. By 1843, Rhodes had bought out Cooper and Holt’s share, consolidating his business interests and becoming one of the colony’s most prominent landowners and entrepreneurs.

Around 160-220 thousand years ago, the summit of the Las Cañadas I volcano collapsed, creating the Las Cañadas (Ucanca) caldera. Subsequently, the stratovolcano Las Cañadas II formed near Guajara but later underwent catastrophic collapse. The Las Cañadas III volcano then developed in the Diego Hernandez sector of the caldera. All the Las Cañadas volcanoes reached a maximum altitude similar to that of Teide, which is sometimes referred to as the Las Cañadas IV volcano.

Initially, the construction was supposed to begin in September 2016, but the groundbreaking ceremony was held in January 2018. The terminal is expected to be operational by 2020. The estimated cost of the project stands at P5.20 billion. However, pre-construction activities were temporarily suspended due to the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.

The RADA library contains around 30,000 items. Works include around 10,000 plays; works of or about biography, costume, criticism, film, fine art, poetry, social history, stage design, technical theatre and theatre history; screenplays; and theatre periodicals. The collection was started in 1904 with donations from actors and writers of the time such as Sir Squire Bancroft, William Archer, Sir Arthur Wing Pinero and George Bernard Shaw.

Dioscorea elephantipes, commonly known as elephant's foot due to its large, partially buried, tuberous stem, is a deciduous climbing plant native to South Africa. The caudex, which can grow up to 7 feet in diameter and rise several feet above ground, is rich in starch, earning it the alternative name Hottentot bread. Its outer covering is thick and cork-like, resembling an elephant's skin. The caudex grows slowly over time and must be thoroughly processed to remove toxins before it can be consumed safely.

In February 2000, The New York Times selected Forest City Ratner Companies, led by Bruce Ratner, as the developer for its planned headquarters on Eighth Avenue. The following month, the Times began negotiations with city and state officials, proposing to pay $75 million for the site and seeking a two-thirds reduction in real estate taxes. However, the state sought $125 million for the site, while the city wanted the Times to pay full taxes. Some commentators criticized the Times for seeking tax relief despite its prior opposition to corporate tax breaks. A nonbinding agreement was signed in June 2000, under which the Times agreed to pay $100 million for the development rights. The Times planned to occupy half of the 40-story tower, spanning the second through 28th floors, while the remaining space would be leased to office tenants. At the time, other media companies, including Hearst and Conde Nast, were also developing headquarters nearby in Midtown Manhattan.

The biggest problem with how many CLV models are actually used is that they tend to deny the very idea that marketing works (i.e., that marketing will change customer behavior). Low-value customers can be turned into high-value customers by effective marketing. Many CLV models use incorrect mathematics in that they do not take account of the value of a far greater number of middle-value customers, over-prioritizing a smaller number of high value customers. Additionally, these high-value customers may be saturated (i.e., not have the ability to buy any more coffee or insurance), may be the most expensive group to serve, and may be the most expensive group to reach by communication. The use of survey data is a viable way to collect information on potential customers.

The beta version of Kaspersky Internet Security 2011 was released for all Windows users in mid-2010. It introduced a new feature for Windows Vista and Windows 7 users: enhanced compatibility and performance. PCMag rated it highly for its comprehensive scanning options but criticized its malware detection rates, particularly against new phishing sites. Kaspersky Lab released two critical fixes, with the latest version being 11.0.2.556. Independent labs, however, ranked it near the top for overall protection.

In 2018, the inclusion of "Disease X" on the WHO's list of high-priority illnesses sparked media speculation about its potential creation as a biological weapon. During this time, experts were questioned about the possibility of Disease X originating from advancements in gene-editing technologies, such as CRISPR, which could enable the synthesis of synthetic viruses. A notable example cited was the synthesis of horsepox, which demonstrated the feasibility of creating viruses in a laboratory. Discussions highlighted two potential release scenarios: accidental release from a research facility or intentional use as a bioweapon. John-Arne Røttingen, then chief executive of the Research Council of Norway, acknowledged the possibility of a future Disease X arising from synthetic biology but emphasized that zoonotic spillover remained the most likely source. He warned, however, that an event involving synthetic biology and novel viruses could have severe consequences, particularly in populations with no pre-existing immunity.

This combination of features makes Pokémon Sun and Moon a fresh yet familiar experience for fans of the series.

Capacitor discharge stud welding differs from drawn arc stud welding, in that capacitor discharge welding does not require flux. The weld time is shorter, enabling the weld to bond with little oxidation and no need for heat concentration. It also allows for small-diameter studs to be welded to thin, lightweight materials. This process uses a direct-current arc from a capacitor. The weld time in this process is between 1 and 6 milliseconds. Capacitor discharge stud welding with the latest equipment can create a weld without burn-through showing on the opposite side of very thin metals. CD stud welding is often used for smaller diameter studs and pins, as well as on non-standard materials and for accuracy. Drawn arc stud welding is primarily used for structural purposes and larger diameter weld studs.

"The Marriage" has been the subject of extensive analysis in discussions about the artistic potential of video games, particularly within the framework of proceduralist design. Writers and scholars have taken interest in its minimalist approach, focusing on how its mechanics convey meaning without relying on traditional narrative or aesthetic elements. Ian Bogost categorized *The Marriage* as a proceduralist game, emphasizing that such games rely on "computational rules to produce their artistic meaning," rather than prioritizing visuals, sound, or text. Bogost compared *The Marriage* to other experimental games like Rod Humble's *A Love Story* and Stephen Lavelle's *Home*, highlighting its ability to express complex ideas through gameplay mechanics alone. Scholars have noted that the reductionist design of *The Marriage*—stripping away conventional storytelling and graphical detail—has sparked debate about the balance between rules and player interpretation in games. Theresa Claire Devine further explored this dynamic by developing a methodology for analyzing games as art, using *The Marriage* as a case study to demonstrate how games can function as expressive, interpretive works. Additionally, some writers have argued that *The Marriage* serves as a testament to the artistic potential of games, showcasing how interactive systems can evoke emotional and intellectual responses typically associated with more traditional art forms.

There are many differing religious views surrounding surrogacy with regards to lineage and heritability, motherhood, and marital fidelity. Judaism, Hinduism, Islam, and other Christian denominations outside of Catholicism generally approve of surrogacy, but each with some concerns. With Judaism, there are concerns regarding legitimacy and most tend to believe that motherhood belongs to the person who actively delivers the child. Hinduism views infertility as a curse to be cured by any means necessary, generally approving of surrogacy. There is Islamic religious concern centered mainly around the importance of and confusion of lineage and inheritance. Other Christian denominations have a wide variety of views from encouraging surrogacy as it shares the blessing of parenthood, to viewing surrogacy as a means of confused identity in a child and a disruption in traditional marital practices and procreation. Catholicism; however, generally views any third party involved in marriage or procreation to be an intrusion, and thus commonly views gestational surrogacy as an intrusion to the marital bond. Religion has led to legal bans on surrogacy in some countries. For instance, in Costa Rica the Catholic church successfully lobbied to ban surrogacy.

Microhomology-mediated end joining (MMEJ), also known as alternative nonhomologous end joining, involves five steps: end resection, annealing of microhomologous regions, removal of heterologous flaps, fill-in synthesis, and ligation. The process begins with limited end resection to expose microhomologous sequences. After annealing, 3'-flaps and gaps are processed. MMEJ is Ku-independent and influenced by the extent of end resection, with limited resection favoring MMEJ over classical non-homologous end joining (NHEJ).

Elective spinal injections should be performed with imaging guidance, such as fluoroscopy or the use of a radiocontrast agent, unless that guidance is contraindicated. Imaging guidance ensures the correct placement of the needle and maximizes the physician's ability to make an accurate diagnosis and administer effective therapy. Without imaging, the risk increases for the injection to be incorrectly placed, and this would in turn lower the therapy's efficacy and increase subsequent risk of need for more treatment. While traditional techniques without image guidance, also known as blind injections, can assure a degree of accuracy using anatomical landmarks, it has been shown in studies that image guidance provides much more reliable localization and accuracy in comparison.

The verification tools under the START I Treaty were designed to ensure compliance with its provisions, prevent illicit nuclear activities, and build mutual confidence between parties. Verification regimes in arms control treaties serve to monitor adherence, uncover violations, and account for reductions in nuclear stockpiles. The START Treaty included the most complex verification provisions of its time, featuring twelve types of on-site inspections (OSI), such as baseline data inspections, suspect site inspections, and reentry vehicle inspections, among others. Data exchanges and declarations became mandatory, requiring detailed information on the quantities, technical characteristics, locations, movements, and status of offensive nuclear systems. The national technical means of verification (NTM) provision protected satellites and other information-gathering systems, which played a critical role in monitoring compliance. Additionally, the international technical means of verification provision safeguarded multilateral systems specified in other treaties. Cooperative measures, such as displaying items in plain sight, were established to facilitate verification by NTM. The OSI and Perimeter and Portal Continuous Monitoring (PPCM) provisions further strengthened the treaty by ensuring continuous oversight of facilities. Telemetry from ballistic missile flight tests was also subject to strict requirements, including the exchange of tapes, interpretative data, and acceleration profiles, with encryption and encapsulation prohibited to guarantee transparency.

Building officials can be certified by meeting certain requirements.

In September 2016, it was discovered that the encryption of local iOS backups made with iTunes is weaker for iOS 10 devices than for devices running iOS 9. Russian software firm ElcomSoft discovered that the iOS 10 backup process skips several security checks, making it "approximately 2,500 times" faster to try passwords, enabling 6 million password tries per second compared to the 2,400 password tries per second for the same process ElcomSoft has used on iOS 9. The firm stated that the impact is "severe". Apple acknowledged the problem, said it planned to issue a security update, but also stated that iCloud backups were not affected. The iOS 10.1 update subsequently fixed the issue.

The 2019 Uckfield Town Council elections were held on May 2, alongside district council elections. A total of 15 seats were contested, with insufficient candidates for the three seats in Uckfield North and Ridgewood wards, leading to automatic appointments. Keith Everett, nominated for Ridgewood, passed away before the close of nominations. At the first meeting of the new council, Karen Bedwell and Colin Snelgrove were co-opted to represent Ridgewood and North wards, respectively. Both joined the Trust Independent group, which also includes Independent councillor Jackie Love.

When created in 1959, the national park protected an area of 13,000 ha. This has been reduced to 10,250 ha in 1972 through a presidential decree. In 1992 adjoining the national park, the new Serra Geral National Park has been created, encompassing an additional 17,300 ha. However, according to the Duke University's Center for Tropical Conservation, the current park area, even after the extension with Serra Geral National Park, is still too small to be effective for the protection of representative samples of each distinct environment.

Tanerko was a bay horse with a white star and white markings on all four feet bred in France by his owner François Dupré. He was sired by Dupre's stallion Tantieme the double winner of the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe who went on to be the champion sire in France in 1962 and 1965. Tanerko's dam La Divine was a half-sister to Chanteur who won the Coronation Cup in 1947 as well as numerous good races in France. Tanerko was trained at Chantilly by François Mathet.

Another mass execution was discovered near the eastern city of Deir ez-Zor. The unidentified corpses of 13 men had been discovered shot to death execution-style. This incident raised awareness that the violence in Syria was heading towards an inexorable vicious cycle of tit-for-tat attacks between the different parties involved. According to the opposition, the 13 people who were shot at point blank range and later found in a field were employees at the electricity company in Deir Ezzor, who went on strike in protest of the massacres committed by the Syrian regime. They all had their hands tied behind their backs and were shot in the head according to a UN report. The head of a UN observer mission in Syria was “deeply disturbed” by the killings in Deir Ezzor, calling it an “appalling and inexcusable act.” Several days later, the dead were identified to be military and the opposition than claimed they were army defectors killed by government forces. However, on 5 June, the jihadist group the Al-Nusra Front to Protect the Levant claimed responsibility for the killings, stating that they had captured and interrogated the soldiers in Deir al-Zor and "justly" punished them with death, after they confessed to crimes.

Originally announced as a feature documentary in 2013, The Walt Disney Company CEO Bob Iger announced that the film was being revamped as a series of six one-hour episodes for the upcoming Disney+ streaming service. In April 2019, the series was formally announced. In August 2019, it was announced that Angela Bassett will be narrating the series.

In 2010, the Gibson Custom Shop launched the Collector's Choice series, which included a limited run of replicas based on Greeny. An Artist's Proof version owned by Moore was auctioned off following his death in 2011. In 2013, Gibson released the Gary Moore Les Paul Standard, which included a number of features modeled after Greeny, including a 1950s neck profile, mismatched volume and tone knobs and a mahogany body topped with a figured AA-grade maple top.

Julia Adler-Milstein was born and raised in San Francisco, California, and spent much of her childhood in Denver, Colorado. She earned a bachelor's degree from Stanford University and a PhD in Health Policy from Harvard University. Before pursuing her doctorate, she taught undergraduate courses at Harvard while working on her degree.

Johnny Ball began hosting the pre-school programme *Play School* in 1967, continuing throughout the 1970s and beyond. A regular fixture on children's television from the mid-1970s to the 1980s, he presented engaging science and technology programmes, including *Think of a Number*, *Think Again*, *Think Backwards*, *ThinkThis Way*, and *Johnny Ball Reveals All*. Known for his eccentric and entertaining style, Ball made complex scientific and mathematical concepts accessible and fun for young audiences. While most of his shows aired on the BBC, *Johnny Ball Reveals All* was broadcast on ITV. In addition to presenting, he wrote jokes for shows like *Crackerjack*, showcasing his comedic talent alongside his passion for education.

The prehistoric surroundings of Boca de Potrerillos in Nuevo León, Mexico, feature rock art and portable objects with markings that some interpret as early counting systems. These portable pieces, first found in Europe and later in regions like South-East Asia, are often compared to similar artifacts from Scotland and Sulawesi. Their purposes remain debated, with interpretations ranging from symbolic or ritualistic use to practical applications like record-keeping. Establishing their meaning is challenging due to the abstract nature of the carvings and the lack of direct cultural context. These artifacts highlight a broader artistic tradition shared across diverse prehistoric societies.

The previous (used circa 2004 - 2011) classification system, termed the "Tier System", was used by military planners to designate the various individual aircraft elements in an overall usage plan for integrated operations. The Tiers do not refer to specific models of aircraft, but rather roles the aircraft would fill. The U.S. Air Force, U.S. Marine Corps, and U.S. Army each have their own tier system, and the systems are themselves not integrated.

In the mid- to late 1960s, Browne, a prolific songwriter, was pitching his material to artists and publishing houses. On January 7, 1967, he made some demo recordings for Nina Music Publishing at Jaycino Studio in New York City. Nina collected these songs on a double album which was given to various artists and managers in the hope that other artists would choose to record one. Included in these demos, and the third song on this collection, was "I've Been Out Walking", an early version of "These Days". Yet the song was even older than that. Browne later said he wrote it when he was 16 years old, meaning in 1964 or 1965.

On June 26, 2009, General Electric CEO Jeff Immelt announced plans to open an Advanced Manufacturing and Software Technology Center in Van Buren Township, Michigan, within the Detroit Region Aerotropolis. The facility, located in the Visteon Village corporate park, was expected to create approximately 1,200 jobs and focus on developing technologies for renewable energy, aircraft engines, gas turbines, and software services. At the time, Visteon Corporation, the park's owner, was undergoing bankruptcy proceedings. GE planned to construct a 100,000-square-foot research and development facility at a cost of $146 million, supported by $74 million in state incentives over 12 years. The project included new office space and construction, reflecting GE's commitment to innovation and advanced manufacturing. The center was anticipated to generate significant economic benefits for the region, including $146 million in income taxes and other revenue over the same period.

After the election of Raul Khajimba, he permanently appointed Raul Lolua on 25 October 2014. Logua was re-appointed in April 2015, following the replacement of Beslan Butba as Prime Minister by Artur Mikvabia, but handed in his resignation the following month, after two confrontations between police officers and members of the State Security Service. On the evening of 14 May, President Khajimba accepted Logua's resignation and appointed former Prosecutor General of the Gagra District Beslan Khagba as his successor.

As of the census of 2010, there were 7,434 people, 2,861 households, and 2,130 families residing in the CDP. The population density was 494.6 people per square mile (190.9/km2). There were 3,017 housing units, of which 156, or 5.2%, were vacant. The racial makeup of the CDP was 79.9% white, 16.9% African American, 0.2% Native American, 0.5% Asian, 0.1% Pacific Islander, 1.0% some other races, and 1.4% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 2.7% of the population.

Supporters of austerity measures tend to use the metaphor that a government's debt is like a household's debt. They intend to convince people of the notion that the government's overspending leads to the government's default. But this metaphor has been shown to be inaccurate.

Rizwan Khan was born in Pakistan to parents of Indian Muslim heritage. His father migrated from India during the partition, while his mother’s roots trace back to post-partition Pakistan. At the age of 12, Rizwan moved to London with his family. Identifying as a journalist, Rizwan initially pursued medicine before shifting careers. He practices Islam and has been recognized for his contributions to media and storytelling.

After establishing himself as a premier player in France and an international during the 2004–05 campaign, Benítez declared that Diarra still had a future at Liverpool stating "I think it's still early days for him, but it's good news for us to see a young player doing so well. If he continues to play well for his club and is in the national team, we will be reminding everyone he is our player". However, in April 2005, Diarra revealed that he wanted to stay with Lens for the long-term. After finishing the season, on 24 June 2005, Liverpool granted his request after Lens reached an agreement with the club on a transfer fee, purported to be €3.2 million. Diarra finished his Liverpool career with no first-team appearances and only one club appearance, which came in a pre-season friendly.

Ponsonby was commissioned into the Royal Air Force in April 1983 as a pilot, after nine years service as an infantry officer in the British Army. On 10 April 1983, he was appointed to a permanent commission and promoted to flight lieutenant with seniority from 10 March 1980. On 1 July 1988, he was promoted to squadron leader, and then to wing commander on 1 January 1994. As a group captain, he was appointed an Officer of the Order of the British Empire in the 1999 New Year Honours, and was made an aide-de-camp to Queen Elizabeth II on 1 August 2001. He relinquished this appointment on 3 May 2002, being promoted to air commodore on 1 July that year. Ponsonby was also awarded the Queen's Commendation for Valuable Service in the 2002 Operational Honours List "in recognition of gallant and distinguished services in Northern Ireland during the period 1 October 2001 to 31 March 2002". He was promoted to his final rank of air vice marshal on 4 January 2005.

"Disco 2000" by Pulp tells the story of the narrator falling for his childhood friend Deborah, based on Jarvis Cocker's real-life friend Deborah Bone. The lyrics reflect unrequited young love, with Cocker recalling their childhood in Sheffield. While most of the song is true, Cocker noted that the part about meeting "by the fountain down the road" was fictional, as the fountain was demolished in 1998.

Nadezhda Plevitskaya (born Nadezhda Vinnikova) was born in 1884 in the village of Vinnikovo near Kursk to a peasant family. Her love for singing, inherited from her parents, led her to join the church choir of the Trinity Monastery in Kursk, where she stayed as a novice for over two years. After leaving the monastery, she moved to Kiev, where she became a professional singer and married Edmund Plewicki. The couple later moved to Moscow, where Plevitskaya began performing at the renowned Yar Restaurant. Her breakthrough came in 1909 at the Nizhny Novgorod Fair, where her performance impressed the famous tenor Leonid Sobinov, who invited her to sing at a charity concert, launching her career.

The 1974 Formula One season began with the Argentine Grand Prix, where Graham Hill qualified 20th. During the race, Hill was running in 13th position when his engine failed, forcing him to retire. Colin Edwards, unrelated to Hill, did not participate in Formula One but was active in MotoGP. At the Brazilian Grand Prix, Hill qualified 21st but retired after a collision. Embassy Hill entered only one car for the South African Grand Prix, with Hill as the driver. He qualified 22nd and finished in 6th place, earning a championship point. This marked a determined effort by Hill, who continued to race despite challenges with his newly established team.

Graham continued to maintain his innocence and attempted to secure an appeal that would grant him a release from prison. The South Dakota Supreme Court heard oral arguments regarding his 2010 conviction on 19 March 2012 in Vermillion, South Dakota. Graham's attorney, John Murphy, argued that the government should not have had the authority to transfer his case from federal to state jurisdiction following his extradition to the US, the South Dakota Supreme Court ruled that the state was within its rights to prosecute Graham, there was sufficient evidence to convict Graham, and his life-sentence imprisonment without parole was commensurate with the crime committed. The South Dakota Supreme Court thus dismissed the John Graham Appeal. Graham is currently incarcerated at the South Dakota State Penitentiary in Sioux Falls, South Dakota.

Acamarachi, also known as Pili, is recognized as an Inca mountain sanctuary, one of at least 15 such sanctuaries used by the Inca. Other sanctuaries include Licancabur, Cerro Colorado, and Sairecabur. On its summit, four ceremonial platforms are arranged in a cloverleaf formation. These platforms highlight the mountain's significance in Inca rituals and its role as a sacred site within the Central Volcanic Zone of the Andes.

Shortly after his professional career, Wayne Lee became the coach of the Hamilton Hurricanes in the Canadian Junior Football League, leading them to an undefeated season in 1990, and later taught gym at Vankleek Hill Collegiate Institute.

In January 2015, Netflix COO Ted Sarandos stated that Jessica Jones the series was "eligible to go into multiple seasons for sure" and Netflix would look at "how well [they] are addressing both the Marvel fanbase but also the broader fanbase" in terms of determining if additional seasons would be appropriate. In July 2015, Sarandos said some of the Defender series would "selectively have multiple seasons as they come out of the gate," with series showrunner Melissa Rosenberg saying she was hopeful Jessica Jones would get an additional season before The Defenders. Rosenberg later expanded on this, saying that Marvel Television and Netflix were working out the placement of a potential second season, though "[i]t might not be possible from a logistical standpoint" to have a second season of Jessica Jones debut before The Defenders; Sarandos later confirmed this to be the case, stating that the season would air after The Defenders released in 2017. On January 17, 2016, Netflix ordered a second season of 13 episodes. Raelle Tucker joined the season as an executive producer and writer, replacing Liz Friedman from the first season, who departed the series to work on the pilot for the ABC series, Conviction.

In August 2012, Uganda and Israel commemorated the raid at a sombre ceremony at the base of a tower at the Old Entebbe Airport, where Yonatan Netanyahu was killed. Uganda and Israel renewed their commitment to "fight terrorism and to work towards humanity". In addition, wreaths were laid, a moment of silence was held, speeches were given, and a poem was recited. The flags of Uganda and Israel were flown side by side, symbolising the two countries' strong bilateral relations, next to a plaque bearing a history of the raid. The ceremony was attended by Ugandan State Minister for Animal Industry Bright Rwamirama and the deputy Foreign Affairs Minister of Israel Daniel Ayalon, who laid wreaths at the site. Forty years to the day after the rescue operation, Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu, brother of slain Israeli commando Yonatan Netanyahu, visited Entebbe with an Israeli delegation, and laid the groundwork for further Israeli–sub-Saharan African bilateral relations.

In 2011, ViviSat, a joint venture between Alliant Techsystems (ATK) and U.S. Space LLC, proposed the Mission Extension Vehicle (MEV) concept. The MEV was designed to attach to satellites and take over station-keeping functions, extending their operational life. ATK was responsible for the technical design, production, and operation of the MEV, while U.S. Space handled financing and business development. ViviSat aimed to commercialize this satellite servicing system, but the venture was later shuttered.

The Fields Institute initiated the Fields Institute Fellows program in 2002 to recognize individuals for outstanding contributions to its activities. This lifetime appointment is automatically recommended for winners of the CRM-Fields-PIMS Prize.

DCPA, a pre-emergent herbicide, is released into soil and air during application. In air, it exists in both vapor and particulate phases, reacting slowly with hydroxyl radicals, with an estimated half-life of 1.9 days. Particulate-phase DCPA may be removed via wet or dry deposition. With a high Koc value of 3900, DCPA exhibits low mobility in soil, strongly attaching to organic matter. However, its di-acid degradate (TPA) is highly mobile and persistent, posing risks of leaching into water.

The circuit is unchanged from its heyday, remaining in its 1952 configuration when the chicane was added. No modern vehicles are allowed within the circuit perimeter throughout the weekend, except for modern fire and rescue vehicles. There are also theatrical sets that bring the past back to life, as well as many historic aircraft.

Prior to its collection, Thomson assumed the Inaccessible Island rail was related to other Atlantic "island hens," possibly a gallinule. However, after examining the species, Lowe felt compelled to place it in a new genus, naming it *Atlantisia* after the mythical island of Atlantis, said to have been destroyed by a cataclysmic event. The specific name *rogersi* honors Rev. H.M.C. Rogers, who first collected specimens of the species on Tristan da Cunha.

Buxton's legacy included leaving behind a mission hospital in Burundi that is still there to this day. Additionally, Buxton worked for Mildmay Mission Hospital which was reopened in 1988 as Europe's first hospice for people with AIDS. It was also the first hospital to be guaranteed a private charter from the NHS. Buxton used the knowledge of diseases that he had experienced in Burundi to write articles on issues like peptic ulcerations so that people in Europe were aware of the diseases that he faced while abroad. Buxton went on to become the chairman of the Ruanda Mission from 1965 to 1974 and was Medical Superintendent at Mildmay Hospital from 1954 to 1974.

Cardinal Orsini was very influential in the papal conclave of 1492, held in August of that year. Cardinal Rodrigo Borgia was elected, taking the name Alexander VI. The election is notorious for allegations that Borgia bought the votes of his electors, promising them lucrative appointments and gifts. According to ecclesiastical historian Johann Peter Kirsch, Borgia's election was almost entirely due to Cardinal Orsini. Cardinal Gherardi, Patriarch of Venice, who had pushed for Orsini's election, cast the deciding vote.

Stanisław Ulam returned to academia as a visiting professor at Harvard from 1951 to 1952 and at MIT from 1956 to 1957. He was a visiting professor at the University of California, San Diego, in 1963 and at the University of Colorado at Boulder from 1961 to 1962 and 1965 to 1967. In 1967, his position at the University of Colorado became permanent when he was appointed Professor and Chairman of the Department of Mathematics. Ulam maintained a residence in Santa Fe, New Mexico, allowing him to spend summers at Los Alamos. He was an elected member of the National Academy of Sciences, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and the Polish Academy of Learning.

Watershed Distillery first released its bourbon in 2012, initially aged for three and a half years. The current bourbon is aged for four years in Ohio-made barrels, with a goal of eventually aging each barrel for six years. Made from a five-grain mash bill of corn, wheat, rye, barley, and spelt, it is double distilled and bottled at 90 proof, showcasing the distillery's commitment to quality and craftsmanship.

Joe Scigliano began his youth soccer career with Cliff Avenue United and later joined the Vancouver 86ers in 1996, competing in the USL First Division. After a stint with the 86ers, he played for the Philadelphia KiXX in the Major Indoor Soccer League, winning the league championship in the 2001-02 season. In 2004, Scigliano played for ICSF Inter in the Vancouver Metro Soccer League, where he was named MVP. He later won the Canadian National Championship in 2007 with the same team, solidifying his legacy in both indoor and outdoor soccer.

Malachi Davis was born in Toronto, Canada, and raised in Canada alongside his siblings, including a brother. He was first exposed to athletics at a young age when his mother entered his brother into the Hershey Invitational track meet, an event supporting causes like the Special Olympics and Ronald McDonald House. Inspired, Malachi began participating in local track events, where he quickly excelled, showcasing his natural athleticism. This early success laid the foundation for his passion for sports, eventually leading him to pursue basketball, where he would later thrive as a standout player in both JUCO and NCAA competitions.

In recent years, the theme park industry has increasingly adopted automated guided vehicles (AGVs) for rides, enhancing both functionality and guest experience. One of the earliest AGV ride systems was introduced in Epcot's *Universe of Energy*, which opened on October 1, 1982. The ride's "Traveling Theatre" used wired navigation to guide vehicles through the attraction. Wired navigation remains common in rides where employees frequently walk over the ride path, such as the now-closed *Great Movie Ride* at Disney's Hollywood Studios. Another example of wired navigation is *The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror* at Hollywood Studios, a combined drop tower and dark ride. In this attraction, the elevator cars function as AGVs, locking into vertical motion cabs for drops and unlocking to drive horizontally between floors, showcasing the versatility of AGV technology in theme park attractions.

Jerry Coleman, born in San Jose, California, graduated from Lowell High School in San Francisco. He spent six years in the Yankees' minor league system before debuting with the New York Yankees in 1949, where he played his entire nine-year career. In his rookie season, Coleman hit .275, led American League second basemen in fielding percentage, and was named Rookie of the Year by the Associated Press, finishing third in the BBWAA voting.

The Frank Tea and Spice Company was founded in 1896 in Cincinnati, Ohio. In 1917, owner Jacob Frank contracted for the Estilette Pepper Farm in Louisiana where Adam Estilette and Frank became business partners. They mixed spices, vinegar, garlic and cayenne peppers and aged them, creating the original blend of Frank's RedHot, which entered the market in 1920.

After her marriage, the Marquise des Monstiers-Mérinville moved to Paris, where her notable entertainments and charitable activities defined her life. Her health began to fail in 1904, leading her to live as a recluse thereafter.

Catherine Eddowes was buried on the afternoon of Monday, 8 October 1888, in the City of London Cemetery, Aldersbrook Road, Manor Park, London. The service was conducted by Reverend Dunscombe. Her coffin, made of polished elm with oak mouldings, bore a plate inscribed in gilt letters: "Catherine Eddowes, died Sept. 30, 1888, aged 43 years."

George Hilton served in the Legislative Assembly of Minas Gerais from 1999 to 2007, representing the PSL and PL parties. He was first elected as a federal deputy for Minas Gerais in 2006 and re-elected in 2010 and 2014. From 2009 until 2016, he was affiliated with the Brazilian Republican Party.

As of the census of 2000, there were 567 people, 224 households, and 180 families residing in the township. The population density was 15.9 people per square mile (6.1/km2). There were 410 housing units at an average density of 11.5/sq mi (4.4/km2). The racial makeup of the township was 98.24% White, 0.35% African American, 0.18% Native American, 0.53% Asian, and 0.71% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.71% of the population.

Michigan and Arizona held their presidential primaries on Tuesday, February 28, and allocated 29 and 30 Republican delegates, respectively. CNN moderated a Republican primary debate in Mesa, Arizona on February 22, 2012. The Arizona Republican primary is 'winner-take-all' and the Michigan Republican delegate results are allocated proportionately among the candidates by voting district. Romney won the Michigan primary narrowly, and won the Arizona primary by a wide margin.

A branch to Chippenham off the Wilts & Berks Canal was built in 1798, terminating at a wharf at Timber Street near the marketplace; the main commodity traded was coal. The site of the wharf is now the town's bus station, and part of Pewsham Way follows the line of the branch. The Great Western Railway arrived in Chippenham in 1841, and in turn attracted many new businesses. The arrival of these businesses required new housing which led to the expansion of the town into land north of the railway, which in turn led to the growth of further industries to support the building work.

Diffey has worked on NBC's coverage of the Olympic Games, covering luge, skeleton, and bobsled at the 2014 Winter Games in Sochi. NBC executive Sam Flood had expressed interest in having him serve as an announcer outside of motorsports while negotiating his contract with the network, and he prepared Diffey for the Olympics by having him host the Penn Relays and the Luge World Cup. Diffey remarked of serving in the Olympics: "Never in my wildest dreams did I imagine this is where my career would take me. Just working for NBC, that alone made my life. Now going to the Olympics for NBC? I just pinch myself as if to wonder is this really happening?" He returned to the Olympics to cover rowing at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro, and also called the same events he had in Sochi at the 2018 Winter Games in Pyongchang.

The **All-Russian Cossack Society** (Russian: Vserossiiskoe kazach'e obshchestvo) is a government-sponsored organization in the Russian Federation responsible for coordinating the activities of the 11 registered Cossack hosts. Its main spheres of activity include patriotic education and the preservation of historical Cossack customs and traditions. Both registered and non-registered Cossack organizations are eligible to join the society. The idea for the organization was first proposed in 1994, but it was not until October 2017 that the atamans and Cossacks formally created it. On 27 November 2018, the delegates of the Constitutive Assembly voted to establish the society and adopted its official statute. On 4 November 2019, Russian President Vladimir Putin appointed Nikolai Doluda, former Vice Governor of Krasnodar and Ataman of the Kuban Cossack Host, as the Ataman of the All-Russian Cossack Society.

As the Entebbe crisis unfolded, the Egyptian government under President Anwar Sadat attempted to mediate by negotiating with both the PLO and the Ugandan government. PLO chairman Yasser Arafat sent his political aide, Hani al-Hassan, as a special envoy to Uganda to negotiate with the hijackers and Ugandan authorities. However, the PFLP-EO hijackers refused to meet with al-Hassan, rejecting his efforts.

Holbeach Clough, also known as Saracen's Head, appears on maps from the 17th and 18th centuries under both names. The name Saracen's Head is thought to derive from the medieval ambition of Crusaders to return with the head of a Saracen, or infidel, and is associated with a historic public house that may have operated since at least the 15th century. This inn likely served drovers and travelers in the area. The Anglo-Saxon coastline once ran near Holbeach Clough, highlighting its historical proximity to the sea before extensive land reclamation. In the 14th century, drainage systems were constructed in the surrounding marshes to reclaim land, shaping the landscape and enabling settlement and agriculture in this part of the Fens.

Greer has commented several times on the Me Too movement. In November 2017, she called for women to show solidarity when other women are sexually harassed. Just before she was named Australian of the Year in Britain in January 2018, she said she had always wanted to see women react immediately to sexual harassment, as it occurs. "What makes it different is when the man has economic power, as Harvey Weinstein has. But if you spread your legs because he said 'be nice to me and I'll give you a job in a movie' then I'm afraid that's tantamount to consent, and it's too late now to start whingeing about that." In May that year, she argued—of the high-profile cases—that disclosure was "dishonourable" because women who "claim to have been outraged 20 years ago" had been paid to sign non-disclosure agreements, but then had spoken out once the statute of limitations had lapsed and they had nothing to lose.

The series attracted controversy when Madhya Pradesh Home Minister Narottam Mishra objected to a scene in which the characters Lata (Tanya Maniktala) and Kabir (Danesh Razvi) are shown kissing each other within the premises of a temple. His statement came after a Bharatiya Janata Party Youth leader, Gaurav Tiwari, filed a complaint against the makers on 22 November 2020 for "allegedly hurting religious sentiments". Tiwari also appealed to people at large on Twitter to uninstall the platform Netflix (which bought its global streaming rights) from their phones, as #BoycottNetflix began to trend on Twitter. It was further criticised for portraying Love Jihad. On 25 November, an FIR was filed against two executives from Netflix India, in response to the complaint registered by Tiwari.

David Oldfield was elected to the New South Wales Legislative Council in the 1999 New South Wales election, where One Nation achieved 7.5% of the vote. The party secured one seat in the Legislative Council, with Oldfield winning the seat. The second seat was contested by Brian Burston but was not won, marking One Nation's first parliamentary representation in NSW.

The graphic novel version of The Lurker is inspired by the chamber thriller of the same name, previously released in 2012, and introduces a brand-new type of superhero, completely different to those we know from the popular Marvel comics. The appeal of this mystical story is enhanced by the enchanting backdrop of Prague, and set in an anonymous and wild metropolis where the underground metro is not only the most popular means of transport but the axis in which the fates of its inhabitants are intertwined; the fates of those who have yet to realise how a brief encounter with a strange, taciturn and disfigured man will change their lives forever. ISBN 978-80-87801-06-2

The score for *Eternals* was composed by Grammy-nominated German composer Ramin Djawadi, who previously scored Marvel Studios' *Iron Man* (2008). Two tracks from the soundtrack, "Eternals Theme" and "Across the Oceans of Time," were released as singles on October 22, 2021, ahead of the full album's digital release on November 3, 2021, by Hollywood Records and Marvel Music. The soundtrack also features the song "Nach Mera Hero," performed by Celina Sharma, as its final track. Notable licensed songs include Pink Floyd's "Time," which plays during the Marvel Studios' vanity plate, Black Joe Lewis & the Honeybears' "Sugarfoot," heard during Dane Whitman's birthday party, and Skeeter Davis' "The End of the World," which underscores a scene at Phastos' home. Additionally, Foreigner's "Feels Like the First Time" plays during the credits following the mid-credits scene introducing Eros and Pip.

Born William Breitbard in Babylon, New York, (brother of Ric Roman, character actor in such Hollywood films as Some Came Running and television). Williams himself graduated from Babylon Senior High School and attended Syracuse University for one year before dropping out. In 1944, he was hired as a staff announcer at WAAT in Newark, New Jersey while visiting a friend at the station. According to Williams "the guy who did the all-night show had just been fired for being bombed on the air." Six weeks later, a staffer at WNEW heard Williams on the air and invited him to apply for a job at the station. He was hired at WNEW and worked several time slots before being fired by station manager Bernice Judis in 1947. An article in the New York Daily News suggested that Williams was fired for his aggressive tactics with management in his role as shop steward; however, WNEW's official story was that he was fired after Judis caught him one evening in the studio with his feet propped on the desk clad in bright red socks. She was apparently horrified by his lack of style.

During the Simhastha Kumbh Mela held in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, in 2016, a significant event unfolded involving Dabral Baba and his identification of Mahavatar Babaji. While at his home in Bhairav Niwas, Babaji, seated in deep meditation, asked his disciple to turn on the TV to watch the live telecast of the Kumbh Snan. He directed his wife and a devotee, Yogesh, to observe a young sadhu, approximately 25 years old, taking a dip in the Shipra River amidst the crowd. Babaji identified this young sadhu as Mahavatar Babaji and predicted that the sadhu would shake his head to wash his jata (matted hair). As the live telecast continued, both Yogesh and Babaji’s wife witnessed the sadhu performing the exact action, shaking his head and washing his jata, before the camera shifted to another scene. This moment left a profound impression on those present, further deepening their reverence for Babaji.

Celtic's traditional rivals are Rangers, with the two clubs collectively known as the "Old Firm." This rivalry is one of the most famous and intense in world football, with their first match dating back to 1888. As of May 2022, Celtic and Rangers have won a combined 107 Scottish league championships since the league's establishment in 1890, leaving only 19 titles for other clubs. Celtic, historically associated with Glasgow's Irish Catholic community, has a strong connection to Irish ancestry, left-wing politics, and pro-Irish unification sentiments. In contrast, Rangers are traditionally linked to Protestantism, Loyalism, and conservative values. During the 2014–15 season, Celtic ranked among the top clubs in the UK for home attendance, reflecting their significant fanbase and cultural importance in Scottish football.

The Goodwood circuit's iconic chicane was added in 1952 to address safety concerns, and its current configuration remains unchanged. During the Revival weekend, modern vehicles are prohibited within the perimeter, except for emergency services. Over 250 vintage vehicles and period costumes enhance the event's immersive historical atmosphere.

Clotilde von Derp, born Clotilde Margarete Anna Edle von der Planitz on November 5, 1892, in Berlin, was the daughter of Major Hans Edler von der Planitz (1863–1932) and Margarete von Muschwitz (1868–1970). She married Alexander Sakharoff on July 25, 1919.

Folly Farm began as a working dairy farm run by Glyndŵr Williams and his wife Anne. Observing families stopping to watch and pet their cattle, they decided to diversify into tourism. In 1988, the farm was converted to welcome visitors, who could see cows being milked and learn about farming. Over 35 years of reinvestment have followed, with the first zoo animals arriving in 2002. Initially, the attraction focused on providing an educational farming experience for guests.

As of the 2020 U.S. Census, there were 123,851 people and 49,948 households residing in the city. The population density was 4,435.9 inhabitants per square mile (1,712.7/km2), making it less densely populated than Detroit proper and its inner-ring suburbs like Oak Park and Ferndale, but more densely populated than outer-ring suburbs like Livonia and Troy. The racial makeup of the city was 67.6% White, 6.8% Black, 0.2% Native American, 15.7% Asian, 0.1% Native Hawaiian or Pacific Islander, 1.8% from other races, and 7.9% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino residents of any race made up 5.5% of the population. Ann Arbor has a small population of Arab Americans, including students as well as local Lebanese and Palestinians.

It is also possible to measure the role of attention within visual search experiments by calculating the slope of reaction time over the number of distractors present. Generally, when high levels of attention are required when looking at a complex array of stimuli (conjunction search), the slope increases as reaction times increase. For simple visual search tasks (feature search), the slope decreases due to reaction times being fast and requiring less attention. However, the use of a reaction time slope to measure attention is controversial because non-attentional factors can also affect reaction time slope.

Development of the AGS-40 started in 1980 but stalled after the dissolution of the Soviet Union and the subsequent economic decline in Russia. However, the project was revived in the early 1990s under the codename "Balkan", and was put into operational evaluation in 2018. State tests have been successfully completed and the weapon has been recommended for introduction into service as of March 2021. The first serial batch was delivered in January 2022. AGS-40 can be installed on armored vehicles. The AGS-40 can be integrated with Kalashnikov MBDU remote weapon station.

Members of the Venezuelan government were also accused of providing financial aid to Hezbollah by the United States Department of the Treasury, which included Charge d' Affaires of the Venezuelan Embassy in Damascus, Syria Ghazi Nasr Al-Din. According to the testimony of a former Assistant Secretary of State for Western Hemisphere Affairs of the United States Department of State Roger Noriega, Hugo Chávez's government gave "indispensable support" to Iran and Hezbollah in the Western Hemisphere. In an article by the conservative think tank American Enterprise Institute, Noriega states that two witnesses have told him that Ghazi Nasr Al-Din, a Venezuelan diplomat in Syria, was an operative of Hezbollah who used Venezuelan entities to launder money for Hezbollah with President Maduro's personal approval.

The United States Post Office and Courthouse was the first monumental structure in Oklahoma City, serving as an anchor for future federal development. Plans for its construction began in 1903 when Congress appropriated funds for a downtown facility. However, due to the region's rapid growth, the original funding was insufficient, requiring additional appropriations in 1906, 1908, and 1910. Completed in 1912, five years after Oklahoma achieved statehood, it became the first federal building constructed in the state.

Born in Seville, he was the son of Brigadier José de Gayangos, intendente of Zacatecas, in New Spain (Mexico). After completing his primary education in Madrid, at the age of thirteen he was sent to school at Pont-le-Voy near Blois. Then, he began the study of Arabic in the École spéciale des Langues orientales of Paris under Silvestre de Sacy.

To be eligible to play for their association in ITTF world title events, players must register with their national association. If a player chooses to represent a new association, they must register through the new association and meet ITTF eligibility requirements. Players under 15 must wait three years after registration, those under 18 must wait two years, and those under 21 must wait one year. Players aged 21 or older are immediately eligible upon registration. These timelines ensure compliance with ITTF regulations and maintain fairness in international competition.

In the 1996 *American Cowboy* article titled "The Debonair Killer," author David P. Grady described Marion Hedgepeth as a dark-complexioned, wavy-haired six-footer with a fastidious, gentlemanly appearance. Known as a hired gun, Hedgepeth maintained a dandy-like image, sporting a bowler hat, diamond stickpin, and polished shoes, as noted in WANTED posters. Grady emphasized that while Hedgepeth looked like a refined "dude," he was better characterized as "dangerous" and "deadly."

Virginia Wainscott, Robert Newsom's daughter, was called to the stand by the prosecution during the trial. She testified that during the investigation into her father's disappearance, she learned on June 25, 1855, where his bones had been placed. Virginia discovered the bones in the ashes near the fireplace on the family property. Among the remains, she found a buckle, buttons, and a knife, which she identified as belonging to her father. Her testimony provided critical evidence linking the remains to Newsom and supporting the prosecution's case against Celia.

The 1991 Indian general election was held after the dissolution of the eleventh Lok Sabha, which lasted only one-and-a-half years under Prime Minister Chandra Shekhar. Over 500 million eligible voters participated in the election, conducted amidst economic recession and political instability. Key issues included economic policies, with debates on welfare costs and reforms, and the aftermath of political tensions following Chandra Shekhar's brief and unstable tenure.

The results from research on sex differences in memory are mixed and inconsistent, as some studies show no difference, and others show a female or male advantage. Females tend to perform better in episodic memory tasks and access their memories faster than males and use more emotional terms when describing memories. Females also outperform men in random word recall, semantic memory and autobiographical memory. Men are more likely to get the gist of events rather than be aware of specific details. Men also recall more factual information like childhood memories better than females, and also have increased spatial based memories. Men use strategies where they use mental spatial maps and are better at knowing absolute direction, like north and south. Women use landmarks and directional cues for spatial navigation Also, estradiol, a hormone found in women, affects learning and memory. Estradiol affects the function related to memory in the brain because it maintains cognitive function by increases nervous tissue growth in the brain to help maintain memory. Though women experience brain fog when they go through menopause, it has been attributed to stress and processes in frontal neural networks instead.

Only 77 original S.E.5 aircraft had been completed prior to production settling upon an improved model, designated as the S.E.5a. The initial models of the S.E.5a differed from late production examples of the S.E.5 only in the type of engine installed – a geared 200 hp Hispano-Suiza 8b, often turning a large clockwise-rotation four-bladed propeller, replacing the 150 hp H.S. 8A model. In total 5,265 S.E.5s were constructed by six manufacturers: Austin Motors (1,650), Air Navigation and Engineering Company (560), Curtiss (1), Martinsyde (258), the Royal Aircraft Factory (200), Vickers (2,164) and Wolseley Motors Limited (431).

In March, Fa prepared for the vacant New Zealand Professional Boxing Association heavyweight title against Daniel Tai, at the ABA Stadium in Auckland. It would be his first title shot. Tai, an Auckland-based fighter, had been fighting professionally since the year 2000, whilst he boasted a record of 22 wins and 9 losses. With only one loss coming by knockout, in 2001, by legendary Australian champion Danny Green. With the title being vacant for almost five years and last held by Sonny Bill Williams, this was Fa's most difficult fight of his career to note as he was hampered by a back injury and forced to live off the jab. Tai applied pressure and landed several clean blows throughout the bout Tai, however, Fa did enough to win most of the rounds. The judges were divided, however, with one judge scoring a draw in a disappointing majority decision win to Fa.

In recent years, the theme park industry has begun using AGVs for rides. One of the earliest AGV ride systems was for Epcot's Universe of Energy, opened in 1982. The ride used wired navigation to drive the 'Traveling Theatre' through the ride. Many rides use wired navigation, especially when employees must frequently walk over the ride path such as at (the now-closed attraction) The Great Movie Ride at Disney's Hollywood Studios. Another ride at Hollywood Studios that uses wired navigation is The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror, a combined drop tower/dark ride. The elevator cars are AGVs that lock into place inside separate vertical motion cabs to move vertically. When it reaches a floor requiring horizontal movement, the AGV unlocks from the vertical cab and drives itself out of the elevator.

"Killing All the Right People," an Emmy-nominated episode of *Designing Women*, was written by series creator Linda Bloodworth-Thomason. Bloodworth-Thomason developed the show to feature intelligent, outspoken women addressing social issues. The episode was inspired by her personal tragedy: her mother contracted AIDS in 1986 from a contaminated blood transfusion, despite the availability of screening methods that could have identified unsafe blood. Infected blood was unknowingly spread due to inadequate testing and reliance on high-risk donors. While caring for her mother, Bloodworth-Thomason witnessed severe prejudice, particularly against AIDS patients, including medical staff's dehumanizing behavior. The episode's title came from a comment she overheard in the hospital: "It's killing all the right people."

As a member of an Islamic youth organization named Milli Gençlik Vakfı, Tekir joined the group of Shamil Basayev in the Georgian–Abkhazian conflict with code name Hadjarat, in the summer of 1992. He wanted to go to Chechnya in 1994 to participate in the Chechen War, when he was a sergeant in the Abkhazian Army. Since he could not pass to Chechenya from Abkhazia, he used the way from Azerbaijan to Chechnya. He did not manage to go to Chechnya, as he was handed over to Russians and detained in a prison. However, he saved him from the prison and returned to Turkey where he participated in hijacking the ferryboat MV Avrasya in 1996. He was sentenced to eight years in prison in Turkey, but was released after serving three and a half years.

Tariq Fancy, former BlackRock CIO of sustainable investing, wrote an op-ed stating that "the financial services industry is duping the American public with pro-environment, sustainable investing practices." Stating there is no evidence ESG investing has any social impact via a CNBC Exclusive In the USA Today article 'Financial world greenwashing the public with deadly distraction in sustainable investing practices' he claims "Wall Street is greenwashing the financial world, making sustainable investing merely PR, which is a distraction from the problem of climate change." He subsequently wrote a longer, three-piece article, published by Medium, elaborating and substantiating further the points he sketched in his op-ed and providing details of the interactions he had while he worked at Blackrock with different parties in the sustainable investment world.

Alan M. Krensky, MD, was born in 1953 in Boston, Massachusetts. He attended high school in the Boston area before earning both his Bachelor of Arts and Doctor of Medicine degrees from the University of Pennsylvania. Dr. Krensky completed his residency and fellowship in Pediatrics at Boston Children’s Hospital and served as a research fellow at the National Institutes of Health (NIH). In 1984, he joined Stanford University, where he developed the Children’s Health Initiative at Lucile Packard Children’s Hospital. This initiative aimed to advance child health services and policy research, eventually leading to the establishment of the Center for Policy, Outcomes and Prevention (CPOP) in 2004.

The Carver Creative and Performing Arts Center (CCPAC), developed in 1983, was the first magnet school in the Montgomery Public Schools district. Initially located at Carver High School, CCPAC was created to provide specialized arts education for students across Montgomery. Students attended part-day magnet classes at CCPAC and were bused from their home schools. As the program expanded, it became clear that the growing enrollment required a dedicated campus, leading to the eventual establishment of Booker T. Washington Magnet High School.

During a rugby football game in 2007, Cizikas tackled Manny Castillo in what was described by Cizikas as a "headlock." Castillo died of a head injury two days later; it was noted by physicians that two weeks prior to the battle with Cizikas, Castillo suffered a major concussion. One doctor testified that Castillo's concussion was severe enough that it should have kept him out of competition with physical contact for a full year. The Castillo family supported Cizikas throughout his ordeal and believed the death to have been accidental, asking that Cizikas be spared punishment. Their request could not be met, however, because Peel Regional Police supported Cizikas' conviction, as the tackle led to a death. Castillo's father provided a statement that called for fighting in youth hockey to be abolished, but again did not blame Cizikas for his son's death.

The Soft Configuration Model (SCM) in the context of random graphs generates an ensemble of graphs with a probability distribution constrained by specified degree sequences. The ensemble average of the degree of each vertex is set to a designated value, ensuring consistency with the imposed constraints. The SCM is fully parameterized by these degree constraints, which are associated with each vertex. Conditions on the ensemble are imposed using a maximum entropy principle, resulting in a canonical ensemble. This approach ensures that the ensemble adheres to the specified degree constraints while maintaining randomness in other graph properties, yielding a flexible yet controlled framework for studying network structures.

Eisenhower delegated the selection of his cabinet to two close associates, Lucius D. Clay and Herbert Brownell Jr. Brownell, a legal aide to Dewey, became attorney general. The office of Secretary of State went to John Foster Dulles, a long-time Republican spokesman on foreign policy who had helped design the United Nations Charter and the Treaty of San Francisco. Dulles would travel nearly 560,000 miles (901,233 km) during his six years in office. Outside of the cabinet, Eisenhower selected Sherman Adams as White House Chief of Staff, and Milton S. Eisenhower, the president's brother and a prominent college administrator, emerged as an important adviser. Eisenhower also elevated the role of the National Security Council, and designated Robert Cutler to serve as the first National Security Advisor.

Molecular solids may consist of single atoms, diatomic, and/or polyatomic molecules. The intermolecular interactions between the constituents dictate how the crystal lattice of the material is structured. All atoms and molecules can partake in van der Waals and London dispersion forces (sterics). It is the lack or presence of other intermolecular interactions based on the atom or molecule that affords materials unique properties.

In the lead-up to the 2016 United Kingdom European Union membership referendum, the Scottish National Party (SNP) was the only party to oppose the parliamentary bill authorising the referendum, reflecting its pro-European stance. Historically, the SNP had supported continued membership in the European Communities during the 1975 referendum. In 2016, the SNP joined other parties, including Labour, the Liberal Democrats, and the Green Party, in campaigning for Remain. Despite the Conservative Party’s official neutrality, Prime Minister David Cameron and Chancellor George Osborne strongly backed the Remain campaign, while UKIP was the only major party in Great Britain to campaign for Leave. The referendum resulted in a 52% vote for Leave across the UK, with England’s 53.4% Leave vote proving pivotal. In contrast, Scotland voted decisively to Remain, with 62% of voters supporting continued EU membership, and every council area in Scotland returning a Remain majority. The SNP’s 2016 Scottish Parliament election manifesto had explicitly stated that "Scotland being taken out of the EU against our will" would constitute a "significant and material change" justifying another independence referendum. Following the referendum, the SNP argued that the result, which contrasted sharply with Scotland’s pro-Remain stance, reinforced its mandate to pursue a second vote on Scottish independence.

The National ABU Robocon 2015 was held on 7 March 2015 at the Badminton Hall of the Shree Shiv Chhatrapati Sports Complex, Balewadi, Pune. The theme, declared by Televisi Republik Indonesia (TVRI), was "Robominton: Badminton Robo-Game." The Institute of Technology, Nirma University, Ahmedabad won the contest, while Muffakham Jah College of Engineering and Technology (MJCET) secured third place and was awarded the 'Best Design and Aesthetics' robot at the Asia Pacific Broadcasting Union Robotics Contest (ABU Robocon) 2015. U.V. Patel College of Engineering, Mehsana, Gujarat, received the Best IDEA Award.

In 1992 director Barry Cockcroft once again ventured into Hauxwell's life making a documentary series which followed Hauxwell on her first trips outside of the UK. Hauxwell visited France, Germany, Austria, Switzerland and Italy, during which she met the Pope. The series proved so popular it was followed by another trip, this time to the United States in 1993, filmed as Hannah:USA.

Born in San Jose, California, Coleman graduated from Lowell High School, then spent his entire playing career with the New York Yankees. He played six years in the Yankees' minor league system before reaching the big club in 1949. Coleman hit .275 in his first year and led all second basemen in fielding percentage. He was the Associated Press rookie of the year in 1949 and finished third in balloting by Baseball Writers' Association of America.

Gordon Churchill served overseas as a machine gunner during World War I from 1916 to 1919. During World War II, he did not serve in a military unit but was appointed in 1945 as a lecturer at Khaki University in England, an institution established to provide education to Canadian military personnel.

In 1950, the United States authorized Puerto Rico to draft its own constitution through Public Law 600, aiming to establish a republican form of government and a Bill of Rights. The Constitutional Assembly met from 1951 to 1952 to draft the document, which was approved by the U.S. Congress, creating the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.
